requirements.deadline_display: PAUSED for Fall 2026 — Fall 2027 TBD (historically Jan 3)
requirements.english: TOEFL/IELTS/DET for non-native speakers, no minimum; exempt if undergrad instruction was entirely in English (NYU qualifies)
requirements.admission_model_note: Direct program admission with no rotations; admission is not coupled to specific advisers — students choose an adviser (Princeton faculty or GFDL scientists) during year 1 while on a university fellowship.

funding.note: Minimum 5 years stipend + tuition; year 1 on a Science & Engineering fellowship, years 2-5 via CIMES, grants, or AI.

requirements.contact_note: Explicitly encouraged: 'contact potential advisers when preparing the application'; an Applicant Mentorship Program pairs applicants with current students.

programs.data_currency: Data reflective of Fall 2026 cycle pages, retrieved 2026-07-05. CRITICAL: program paused for Fall 2026 — verify whether it reopens for Fall 2027 in Sept 2026; Geosciences Dept (Dec 15) is the backup route. GFDL lecturer roster in flux.

faculty.name: Alistair Adcroft
faculty.title: Research Oceanographer (Senior Research Scholar), AOS/GFDL
faculty.summary: Co-architect of the MOM6 ocean model and lead of the ocean working group for GFDL's OM4/CM4/ESM4 coupled models; AGU 2021 Ocean Sciences Award. His flagship current activity is the Princeton/GFDL arm of M²LInES — co-led from NYU Courant by Laure Zanna — training machine-learned ocean and sea-ice subgrid parameterizations on data-assimilation increments and LES, and implementing ML mesoscale-eddy closures in operational-class models. Also known for iceberg modeling and finite-volume generalized-vertical-coordinate numerics. A built-in bridge for NYU applicants.

faculty.name: Robert Hallberg
faculty.title: Lecturer in AOS; GFDL Senior Scientist
faculty.summary: Lead developer of MOM6, the community ocean model used across NOAA for climate and operational forecasting. Research spans the numerics of generalized vertical coordinates via the vertical Lagrangian-remap method, energetically consistent mesoscale-eddy parameterizations (MEKE), dense overflow and boundary-layer mixing processes, and how ocean-model formulation shapes climate and sea-level projections. An ideal advisor for rigorous GFD plus numerical-methods training with direct impact on national modeling infrastructure.

faculty.name: Rong Zhang
faculty.title: Lecturer in AOS; GFDL Senior Scientist
faculty.summary: A leading researcher on the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation: developing observational AMOC 'fingerprints' for monitoring and prediction, and tracing AMOC impacts on Atlantic hurricane activity, Sahel and Indian monsoon rainfall, Arctic sea-ice decline, and the Northeast US shelf. Recent work addresses AMOC stability, the North Atlantic warming hole, and mechanisms of Atlantic multidecadal variability in coupled models. Strong fit for large-scale ocean-climate dynamics combining theory with coupled modeling.

faculty.name: Stephen Griffies
faculty.title: Lecturer in AOS (departed — retired from GFDL May 2025, now LOCEAN/IPSL Paris)
faculty.summary: World authority on ocean climate-model fundamentals and author of the standard textbook. Research covered regional and global sea-level change, ocean heat uptake, water-mass transformation frameworks, and mesoscale-eddy effects on tracer transport. His own site states he retired from GFDL in May 2025 after 29 years and took a position at LOCEAN/IPSL in Paris beginning February 2026 — the AOS page still listing him is stale. Do not target as a Fall 2027 advisor.

faculty.name: Stephan Fueglistaler
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences; Director of AOS; Director of CIMES
faculty.summary: The central Princeton-side figure in AOS — program director, director of graduate studies, and CIMES director. His group studies the tropical tropopause layer and the processes setting stratospheric water vapor, including satellite-constrained estimates of its response to warming and the roles of QBO, ENSO, and Walker-circulation variability. A second major thread concerns tropical SST patterns and their control on radiative feedbacks and apparent climate sensitivity. Theory plus model-hierarchy orientation, well matched to a fluid-dynamics background.

faculty.name: Leo Donner
faculty.title: Lecturer in AOS; GFDL Physical Scientist
faculty.summary: Longtime GFDL scientist who develops the deep-convection and cloud physics in GFDL's atmosphere models (AM4 and successors). Recent work spans convective parameterization with explicit vertical velocities enabling aerosol activation physics, aerosol-cloud interaction uncertainty as a driver of spread in effective radiative forcing and climate sensitivity, and evaluation of simulated precipitation extremes. Students typically work at the interface of process-level cloud physics and global modeling with GFDL supercomputing.

faculty.name: Olga Sergienko
faculty.title: Research Glaciologist, AOS/GFDL
faculty.summary: Princeton-based theoretical glaciologist and GFDL's contact for ice-sheet dynamics. Recent work revisits marine ice-sheet instability theory — showing grounding-line flux and stability results are richer than classic MISI assumptions — plus damage-mechanics simulation of ice-shelf rift propagation, subglacial lakes, and coupling ice sheets into GFDL climate models. Mathematically oriented, using asymptotics and variational methods — an excellent match for a student trained in fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Gabriel Vecchi
faculty.title: Knox Taylor Professor of Geosciences; Director, High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Former GFDL scientist, now Princeton's most prominent climate professor. His group studies hurricanes in a changing climate — frequency, intensification rates, and historical-record reconstruction — along with ENSO and Walker-circulation change, volcanic influences, and seasonal-to-decadal prediction using GFDL's high-resolution FLOR and SPEAR models, plus global rainfall and drought attribution. Runs a large, active group and regularly advises AOS and Geosciences students, with deep GFDL ties on both sides.

requirements.english: TOEFL/IELTS for non-native speakers; exemption policies apply — Verify
requirements.admission_model_note: Applicants are typically admitted with an advisor match in mind across MIT EAPS and WHOI PO; contacting potential advisors before applying is common practice.

funding.note: Joint Program students are fully supported (stipend + tuition) throughout — Verify current terms.

requirements.contact_note: Prospective students are commonly advised to reach out to WHOI/MIT faculty whose research interests them — Verify current guidance.

programs.data_currency: MOCK entry for UI testing — values illustrative; verify against mit-whoi.edu before use.

faculty.name: Raffaele Ferrari
faculty.title: Cecil & Ida Green Professor of Oceanography, MIT EAPS
faculty.summary: Studies the fluid dynamics of the ocean and its role in climate: turbulent mixing in the abyssal ocean and its control on the meridional overturning circulation, submesoscale dynamics, Southern Ocean upwelling, and the coupling of ocean physics to biogeochemistry and ice ages. Recent work combines theory, idealized simulation, and observations to rethink how the deep ocean overturns, with implications for heat and carbon uptake. A classic destination for students with strong fluids and applied-math training.

faculty.name: Amala Mahadevan
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer studying how submesoscale flows and upper-ocean fronts shape stratification, mixed-layer dynamics, and the delivery of nutrients and carbon: process-oriented modeling tightly coupled to field observations. Recent themes include frontal instabilities, the role of small-scale physics in phytoplankton blooms and export, and monsoon-region air-sea interaction. Suits students who want idealized fluid-dynamical process studies with direct observational grounding.

faculty.name: John Marshall
faculty.title: Cecil & Ida Green Professor of Oceanography, MIT EAPS
faculty.summary: Studies the general circulation of the ocean and its role in global climate, known for the MITgcm model and for idealized aquaplanet studies of coupled climate. Recent interests include Southern Ocean dynamics and sea-ice interaction, the ocean's role in setting climate sensitivity and ITCZ position, and geoengineering-relevant transport questions. His group blends theory, hierarchy modeling, and observations in the classic GFD tradition.

faculty.name: Mick Follows
faculty.title: Professor, MIT EAPS
faculty.summary: Leads the Darwin Project, building trait-based models of marine microbial communities embedded in ocean circulation models to understand how physics, chemistry, and ecology jointly set ocean productivity and carbon cycling. Recent work spans metabolic constraints on microbial growth, nutrient-cycle coupling, and using idealized theory to interpret global biogeochemical patterns. Good fit for students who want physics-grounded ecosystem and carbon-cycle modeling.

requirements.english: TOEFL (recommended min 100 iBT) or IELTS (recommended min 7.0) required for non-native English speakers; waived if a prior degree was earned where the language of instruction is English.
requirements.admission_model_note: Students are admitted to the AOSM program through GSAS (applying via Mathematics) rather than to a specific advisor. Upon entry each student is assigned an initial academic advisor and then identifies a research advisor by mutual agreement during the first fall, subject to Director of Graduate Studies approval and funding availability.

funding.note: All AOSM PhD students receive MacCracken Fellowships for up to five years, covering full tuition, registration fees, comprehensive NYU health insurance, and a nine-month living stipend; summer support is typically provided through faculty research grants.

requirements.contact_note: Admission is committee-based and advisor matching happens after enrollment, so pre-application faculty contact is not required, though reaching out can help gauge research fit.

programs.data_currency: Researched 2026-07-05 from official pages — verify before applying.

faculty.name: Oliver Bühler
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Bühler is a geophysical fluid dynamicist whose research centers on the interaction of waves and mean flows in the atmosphere and ocean. His work spans internal gravity waves, wave-vortex decomposition, wave turbulence, and statistical fluid dynamics, including methods for extracting internal-wave and vortical signals from sparse observations such as ship and float data. He contributes to fundamental applied mathematics and stochastic modeling of geophysical flows and authored a widely used textbook on waves and mean flows. Recent work develops spectral and data-analysis techniques for characterizing geophysical turbulence and wave fields.

faculty.name: Edwin Gerber
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Gerber studies large-scale atmospheric dynamics and climate using hierarchies of idealized numerical models. His research illuminates the internal variability of the atmosphere, the dynamics of jet streams and annular modes, and stratosphere-troposphere coupling, including how the stratospheric polar vortex and sudden warmings influence surface weather and predictability. He works on the atmospheric circulation response to climate change, gravity-wave driving of the middle atmosphere, and reduced models that connect theory with comprehensive simulations. Recent projects also apply machine learning and idealized frameworks to understand and predict circulation regimes.

faculty.name: David Holland
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Holland investigates the coupled dynamics of the ocean, atmosphere, and polar ice sheets, focusing on how warm ocean waters drive melting of Antarctic and Greenland glaciers and ice shelves. Combining field observations, remote sensing, and geophysical fluid dynamics modeling, he studies ice-ocean interactions near glacier grounding lines and their implications for future sea-level rise. He has led major field campaigns, including work at Thwaites Glacier, deploying instruments beneath ice and in fjords. His research connects small-scale ocean turbulence and melt processes to global climate and sea-level projections.

faculty.name: Zongyi Li
faculty.title: Incoming Assistant Professor (joint appointment, Center for Data Science and Mathematics)
faculty.summary: Li develops machine-learning methods for scientific computing and is best known for pioneering neural operators, including the Fourier Neural Operator, which learn mappings between function spaces to solve families of partial differential equations far faster than traditional solvers. His research targets fast, generalizable surrogate models for problems in fluid dynamics, weather and climate, and multiphysics simulation, blending deep learning with numerical analysis. As an incoming faculty member jointly appointed in data science and mathematics, he aims to accelerate scientific discovery through operator learning, differentiable simulation, and physics-informed machine learning.

faculty.name: Olivier Pauluis
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Pauluis studies the dynamics and thermodynamics of moist atmospheric convection and the general circulation. His research examines how phase changes of water, entropy production, and irreversible processes shape thunderstorms, hurricanes, monsoons, and the tropical atmosphere, treating the atmosphere as a thermodynamic engine. He develops theoretical frameworks such as the isentropic analysis of the atmospheric circulation and mean-flow decompositions, and uses high-resolution cloud-resolving and global simulations to connect convective-scale processes to climate. Recent work addresses moist convection, atmospheric energetics, and the representation of convection and precipitation in weather and climate models.

faculty.name: Sara Shamekh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Shamekh researches atmospheric and oceanic turbulence, moist convection, and clouds, combining physical reasoning, statistics, and machine learning. Her work develops data-driven parameterizations and interpretable machine-learning models that capture how convective organization, clouds, and turbulent transport influence climate, aiming to improve their representation in coarse climate models. She studies convective aggregation and the coupling between small-scale processes and the large-scale circulation, and uses high-resolution simulations to learn physically meaningful reduced descriptions. Recent projects extract emergent variables and closures for subgrid processes using neural networks while preserving physical constraints.

faculty.name: K. Shafer Smith
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Smith is a physical oceanographer and geophysical fluid dynamicist who studies mesoscale and submesoscale ocean turbulence. His research addresses baroclinic instability, geostrophic turbulence, and the eddy fluxes that transport heat, momentum, and tracers, shaping ocean stratification and climate. He develops theory together with idealized and realistic numerical models to understand energy pathways, eddy mixing, and the parameterization of unresolved eddies in ocean and climate models. Recent work examines submesoscale dynamics, the vertical structure of ocean turbulence, and the interaction of eddies with the large-scale circulation and tracer transport.

faculty.name: Esteban G. Tabak
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Tabak is an applied mathematician working at the interface of fluid dynamics, data analysis, and machine learning. He is known for developing optimal-transport and density-estimation methods and for data-driven approaches to problems such as attribution, clustering, and conditional density estimation. His research applies these mathematical tools to weather prediction, climate variability, and the analysis of geophysical and physical datasets, alongside work on stratified and rotating fluid flows. Recent projects advance explainable machine learning, factor discovery, and simulation-based approaches to sampling and transport with applications across the sciences.

faculty.name: Laure Zanna
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Zanna studies ocean dynamics and its role in climate variability, predictability, and change. Her research combines theory, observations, and computational and machine-learning methods to understand ocean turbulence, heat and carbon uptake, and sea-level rise. She develops data-driven and hybrid parameterizations of unresolved ocean processes such as mesoscale eddies to improve climate models, and works on climate-model interpretability and uncertainty. She leads efforts, including the M2LInES project, to reduce climate-model biases by learning from data, bridging physical oceanography and scientific machine learning.

faculty.name: Yi Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mathematics and Atmosphere/Ocean Science
faculty.summary: Zhang studies large-scale atmospheric dynamics and the interactions among circulation, thermodynamics, and radiation that shape climate. Her research examines tropical convection and precipitation, the structure and variability of the intertropical convergence zone and Hadley circulation, and how moisture and radiative processes couple to the atmospheric circulation. Using idealized models, cloud-resolving simulations, and theory, she investigates how the tropical atmosphere responds to warming and what controls the distribution of rainfall. Recent work explores convective organization, atmospheric energetics, and the dynamics of tropical rainbands in a changing climate.

faculty.name: Tapio Schneider
faculty.title: Theodore Y. Wu Professor of Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads the Climate Modeling Alliance (CliMA), building a next-generation Earth system model that learns systematically from observations and high-resolution simulations: ML-calibrated turbulence and cloud parameterizations, uncertainty quantification via ensemble Kalman methods, and the dynamics of subtropical clouds and their role in climate sensitivity. Earlier foundational work on atmospheric macroturbulence and the general circulation. A flagship destination for ML-plus-climate-dynamics research.

faculty.name: Andrew Thompson
faculty.title: John S. & Sherry Chen Professor of Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer studying Southern Ocean dynamics: mesoscale eddies and their role in the overturning circulation, exchange across the Antarctic margins and beneath ice shelves, and the coupling of physics to biogeochemical cycles. His group pairs theory and idealized modeling with autonomous observing platforms — gliders and floats — deployed in polar oceans. Good fit for students combining GFD with a taste for observational campaigns.

faculty.name: Christian Frankenberg
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Uses satellite and airborne remote sensing to constrain the global carbon cycle: solar-induced chlorophyll fluorescence as a proxy for photosynthesis, CO2 and methane retrievals (OCO-2/3, TROPOMI), and detection of methane point sources. Combines radiative-transfer modeling, retrieval algorithm development, and flux inversions to link observations to process understanding. Suits students interested in the data/inverse-problem side of Earth observation.

requirements.english: IELTS ≥6.5 / TOEFL ≥93 unless prior English-medium education — Verify
requirements.admission_model_note: Admission on academic background (physics/math heavy); research projects at IMAU chosen during the program.

requirements.contact_note: Not required for admission; useful when choosing an IMAU research group for the thesis year.

faculty.name: Henk Dijkstra
faculty.title: Professor of Dynamical Oceanography, IMAU
faculty.summary: Applies dynamical-systems theory to climate: stability and tipping behavior of the Atlantic overturning circulation, transition probabilities computed with rare-event techniques, ENSO and multidecadal variability as bifurcation phenomena, and complex-network approaches to climate data. Author of standard texts on nonlinear climate dynamics. Recent work couples machine learning with dynamical-systems analysis of tipping points — a distinctive, theory-first take on climate physics.

faculty.name: Anna von der Heydt
faculty.title: Professor of Climate Dynamics, IMAU
faculty.summary: Studies climate sensitivity and variability across timescales, using paleoclimate records and model hierarchies to constrain how sensitivity depends on the background state, and analyzing tipping elements and their interactions in the Earth system. Coordinates European projects on safe operating spaces and tipping-point early warning. Combines conceptual models, GCM experiments, and data analysis — well suited to physics-trained students.

faculty.name: Roderik van de Wal
faculty.title: Professor of Sea Level Change & Ice Sheets, IMAU
faculty.summary: Works on ice sheets and sea-level change: surface mass balance of Greenland from long-running observational transects, ice-dynamical modeling across glacial cycles, and regional-to-global sea-level projections including high-end scenarios used in coastal planning. Contributing author roles in IPCC assessments. His group links cryospheric process understanding to societally relevant sea-level products, with openings for both modeling- and data-oriented theses.

faculty.name: Ethan Baxter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Geochemist who studies processes within and between Earth's crust, mantle, and surface by analyzing the chemistry of rocks and minerals to constrain geologic timescales, rates, and mechanisms. His work integrates isotope geochemistry, field geology, petrology, and numerical modeling across global field sites.

faculty.name: Mark D. Behn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates active tectonic and magmatic processes using geophysical techniques to understand rock and ice deformation, melt generation, and volcanic activity in glacial, marine, and terrestrial environments. His group develops geodynamic models addressing faulting, mantle convection, ice-sheet response to climate change, and earthquake and volcanic hazards.

faculty.name: Seth C. Kruckenberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Investigates how rock properties and pressure-temperature conditions affect petrofabric development and lithospheric deformation, using a multi-disciplinary approach combining field mapping, electron backscatter diffraction, geochronology, and magnetic fabric studies across diverse orogenic systems.

faculty.name: Yi Ming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Physical climate scientist who investigates how Earth's climate system operates, with particular focus on aerosol-monsoon interactions and regional precipitation patterns under climate change, using climate models and observations across scales.

faculty.name: Hilary Palevsky
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how the ocean absorbs atmospheric carbon dioxide through biological, chemical, and physical processes, combining field measurements, autonomous sensor data, satellite observations, and climate models to improve understanding of ocean carbon cycling.

faculty.name: Juliet Ryan-Davis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates igneous and ore-forming processes with emphasis on subduction zones, integrating geological field observations with geochemistry, geochronology, and petrologic modeling to understand crustal-scale interactions and mineral formation over time.

faculty.name: Jeremy Shakun
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Paleoclimatologist who uses the geologic record to decipher the patterns and mechanisms of past changes in climate and ice sheets on decade to million-year time scales, analyzing geochemical records and developing paleoclimate datasets to understand climate sensitivity and ice-sheet dynamics.

faculty.name: Noah P. Snyder
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies how rivers respond to environmental changes by connecting high-resolution elevation data with field measurements, focusing on river management challenges that balance human needs such as water supply and flood control with ecological functions like fish migration.

faculty.name: Laura J. Steinberg
faculty.title: Professor; Executive Director, Schiller Institute for Integrated Science and Society
faculty.summary: Focuses on infrastructure management, disaster preparedness and response, and environmental modeling, drawing on a background in environmental and civil engineering and prior service as an engineering dean and EPA advisor.

faculty.name: Hanqin Tian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Global Change Ecology & Earth System Modeling
faculty.summary: Earth system scientist studying the Earth as a complex, interconnected system, focusing on how the terrestrial biosphere interacts with atmospheric and climate systems. Employs modeling, satellite data, and ground observations to examine biogeochemical cycles and their responses to climate change and human activities.

faculty.name: Xingchen (Tony) Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His lab investigates biogeochemical cycles in modern and ancient oceans using stable isotope analysis, with applications toward understanding Earth's habitability and the evolution of life.

faculty.name: Kat Wilson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how sedimentary processes in modern and ancient coastal systems respond to climate-driven changes, with particular focus on storm impacts and sea-level fluctuations, integrating morphodynamic analysis with coastal hazard assessment and community resilience strategies.

faculty.name: Lucy Hutyra
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Chair
faculty.summary: Studies the terrestrial carbon cycle and the impact of humans on carbon pools and fluxes, integrating atmospheric, biometric, and climatological data to examine carbon exchange between the atmosphere and biosphere.

faculty.name: Dan Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Associate Chair of Faculty Actions
faculty.summary: Investigates urban microclimate, urban hydrology, environmental fluid mechanics, turbulence, the atmospheric boundary layer, multi-scale climate modeling, and land-atmosphere interactions, focusing on how cities interact with their physical environment.

faculty.name: Michael Dietze
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Leads the Ecological Forecasting Laboratory, using iterative forecasts confronted with new data to better understand and predict ecological systems, integrating field research, statistical methods, numerical models, and ecoinformatics with applications to nature-based climate solutions.

faculty.name: Cédric G. Fichot
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Center for Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Marine scientist focused on estuarine and coastal biogeochemistry and water quality, combining marine optics, remote sensing, organic geochemistry, and photochemistry to understand terrestrial-marine interactions and their disruption by climate change and human activities.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Geddes
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: His group studies reactive trace gases in Earth's atmosphere, from urban air quality to biosphere-atmosphere interactions, using satellite remote sensing combined with atmospheric chemistry modeling and field measurements, including the atmospheric impacts of land-use change.

faculty.name: Anne Short Gianotti
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Chair of Curriculum
faculty.summary: Focuses on the social and political dimensions of conservation, natural resource management, and climate change, integrating natural resource management, institutional ecology, and political ecology to understand how socio-political and ecological processes shape human-nature interactions and policy.

faculty.name: Bruce Anderson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches global and regional climate change, atmospheric dynamics and hydrology, large-scale ocean/atmosphere interactions, regional impacts of climate variability, and climate/land-surface/vegetation interactions and monitoring.

faculty.name: James Lawford Anderson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Igneous petrologist studying granitic magma genesis and the evolution and construction of the Earth's crust, spanning Proterozoic North American crustal development and Mesozoic-Tertiary magmatism in the western U.S., using field, geochemical, and structural methods.

faculty.name: Elizabeth A. Barnes
faculty.title: Dalton Family Chair in Environmental Data Science & Sustainability; Professor, Computing & Data Sciences and Earth & Environment
faculty.summary: Her group emphasizes understanding Earth system variability, predictability, and change while developing interpretable AI tools, aiming to responsibly harness AI to anticipate human-Earth system futures in support of a thriving society.

faculty.name: Sergio Fagherazzi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates geomorphology, hydrology, and coastal and marine geology, with a focus on continental shelf morphology, salt marsh dynamics, and numerical modeling of coastal processes and hydrology across spatial scales.

faculty.name: Duncan M. FitzGerald
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on shallow marine geology, tidal inlet hydraulics, and coastal sediment dynamics, with recent projects on sea-level rise impacts on marshes, bedform evolution, wave-current interactions, and sedimentary signatures of coastal transgression and regression.

faculty.name: Mark Friedl
faculty.title: Professor, William Goodwin Aurelio Professor of Mathematics and Science
faculty.summary: Examines biogeophysical patterns and processes at the Earth's surface using remote sensing, focusing on how land cover affects climate, how surface biophysics influences weather systems, and how human activities shape the planet and biosphere.

faculty.name: Robinson W. Fulweiler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ecosystems ecologist and biogeochemist studying energy flow and biogeochemical cycling of nutrients (nitrogen, phosphorus, silica), carbon, and oxygen, with current focus on nitrogen transformations in marine settings and climate change effects on benthic-pelagic interactions.

faculty.name: Sucharita Gopal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on spatial analysis and modeling, GIS, data mining, information visualization, and artificial neural networks, applied to environmental science, public health, and biology, including marine decision analysis, malaria risk mapping, and climate impacts on food security.

faculty.name: Robert K. Kaufmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Examines global climate change, world oil markets, and land-use transformations, investigating whether humans are driving climate shifts and their ecosystem effects, geological and economic factors in oil supply and pricing, and how socioeconomic development influences land-use patterns.

faculty.name: Patrick Keys
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Explores how society will navigate climate and societal turbulence toward a sustainable and just future, with work spanning climate change futures, moisture recycling and society, and anticipating Anthropocene change using climate data, models, and futures methods.

faculty.name: Sasha Kramer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Ocean Color Remote Sensing & Phytoplankton Ecology
faculty.summary: Characterizes surface ocean phytoplankton communities using in-water samples and remote sensing data, examining how changing phytoplankton composition affects biogeochemical cycles and responds to extreme events such as coastal wildfires.

faculty.name: Andrew C. Kurtz
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of BU Marine Program
faculty.summary: Applies trace element and isotope geochemistry to Earth surface processes, Earth history, and biogeochemical cycles, focusing on connections between terrestrial and marine environments through silicate weathering and its effects on nutrient release and elemental cycling.

faculty.name: Ranga Myneni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches vegetation remote sensing with satellite data and climate-vegetation interactions, examining how vegetation patterns relate to climate dynamics through analysis of satellite imagery.

faculty.name: Christoph Nolte
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Conservation scientist studying where terrestrial ecosystem protection and restoration efforts occur, what they cost, and what difference they make, using spatial datasets, satellite imagery, and econometrics, with current work on conservation land acquisitions in the U.S., Chile, and Colombia.

faculty.name: Nathan Phillips
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Physiological ecologist examining land-climate interactions in terrestrial and human-dominated ecosystems, focusing on exchanges of energy, water, and greenhouse gases including methane and carbon dioxide, and collaborating with advocates and policymakers on sustainable communities.

faculty.name: Xiaozhou Ruan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Seeks to understand small-scale ocean dynamics and their roles in large-scale coastal and global ocean circulation, examining submesoscale currents, boundary layers, and turbulent mixing using theoretical fluid dynamics, numerical modeling, and observational analysis.

faculty.name: Guido Salvucci
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on water vapor dynamics, soil moisture-flux relationships, irrigation impacts on the atmospheric boundary layer, and groundwater influences on land-atmosphere interactions, employing remote sensing, mesoscale modeling, and energy budget constraints.

faculty.name: Ian Sue Wing
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in economic analysis of energy and environmental policy and computational general equilibrium (CGE) economic modeling, analyzing how natural hazards and climate change impacts affect regional economies and examining adaptation strategies.

faculty.name: Curtis E. Woodcock
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on monitoring forest change and its implications for terrestrial carbon budgets, urbanization as a component of global change, effects of forest canopy structure on gap patterns and radiation, and validation of remote sensing products.

programs.program_name: Ph.D. in Civil and Environmental Engineering

requirements.fee_display: Unknown/Verify (waivers available for GEM, McNair, hardship)

faculty.name: Amit Acharya
faculty.title: Paul P. Christiano Professor
faculty.sub_field: Continuum mechanics and theoretical materials science
faculty.summary: Acharya specializes in continuum mechanics and theoretical materials science, spanning fundamental theory to computational modeling. His work has contributed to finite element methods, including widely used nonlinear material models in commercial FE software.

faculty.name: Peter Adams
faculty.title: Thomas Lord Professor
faculty.summary: Adams develops chemical transport models focusing on aerosol microphysical processes, ultrafine particles, and cloud condensation nuclei formation in global climate models. He also studies climate change impacts on air quality and particulate matter formation.

faculty.name: Burcu Akinci
faculty.title: Hamerschlag University Professor; Dean, College of Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Advanced infrastructure systems and building information modeling
faculty.summary: Akinci develops approaches to model and reason about information-rich histories of facilities to streamline construction and facility management processes, integrating building information models with data capture technologies such as 3D imaging and embedded sensors.

faculty.name: Mario Bergés
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Smart infrastructure and building energy analytics
faculty.summary: Bergés works on making the built environment more operationally efficient and robust through information and communication technologies. His current research develops approximate inference techniques to extract actionable insights from infrastructure sensor data, particularly for building energy efficiency.

faculty.name: Kaushik Dayal
faculty.title: Walter J. Blenko, Sr. Professor
faculty.summary: Dayal develops theoretical and computational multiscale methods for problems in materials science, focusing on bridging atomic to continuum scales in the context of functional behavior, non-equilibrium response, and electromagnetic effects.

faculty.name: Sarah Fakhreddine
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Fakhreddine develops water management solutions addressing both quantity and quality by investigating the hydrologic and biogeochemical processes controlling contaminant and nutrient fate in complex watersheds, with focus on how climate-driven changes and water management alter water quality outcomes.

faculty.name: Katherine Flanigan
faculty.title: Henry Posner, Anne Molloy, and Robert and Christine Pietrandrea Career Development Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Flanigan transforms traditional infrastructure into intelligent cyber-physical systems by integrating sensing, computing, and actuation technologies. Her work extends to cyber-physical-social systems that account for human-infrastructure interactions and community-driven data collection.

faculty.name: James Garrett
faculty.title: Thomas Lord Professor; Provost and Chief Academic Officer
faculty.sub_field: Sensing and data analytics for infrastructure
faculty.summary: Garrett applies sensing technologies and data analytics to improve infrastructure condition assessment and management, bridging sensor systems, machine learning, and intelligent decision support for civil engineering applications.

faculty.name: Kelvin Gregory
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gregory investigates the microbiology, ecology, and fundamental interactions between bacteria and their physical and chemical environment. His work encompasses produced water management, environmental nanotechnology, and geologic carbon sequestration.

faculty.name: Corey Harper
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Harper directs the Future Mobility Systems Lab and applies modeling, simulation, and data analytics to assess how emerging technologies impact transportation equity, environment, and congestion, including work on climate resilience and vehicle automation.

faculty.name: Su Jiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Jiang combines artificial intelligence with domain expertise to model subsurface flow processes, with applications to geological carbon storage, hydrocarbon production, enhanced geothermal systems, and seawater intrusion.

faculty.name: Gregory Lowry
faculty.title: Interim Department Head, Hamerschlag University Professor
faculty.summary: Lowry's environmental nanotechnology and chemistry research aims to enhance agricultural efficiency, environmental cleanup processes, and water purification systems, exploring how nanoparticles can be engineered for practical environmental applications.

faculty.name: Destenie Nock
faculty.title: Alex Hills Career Development Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nock uses mathematical modeling to address sustainability planning and energy policy challenges, focusing on energy systems modeling and multi-criteria decision analysis to help policymakers understand energy planning options with attention to equity and vulnerable populations.

faculty.name: Matteo Pozzi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pozzi focuses on risk analysis for civil infrastructure systems using engineering models and sensor data, with emphasis on probabilistic approaches to seismic vulnerability, infrastructure deterioration, and maintenance optimization.

faculty.name: Sean Qian
faculty.title: H.J. Heinz III Professor
faculty.sub_field: Transportation network modeling and data analytics
faculty.summary: Qian specializes in large-scale dynamic network modeling and large-scale data analytics for multi-modal transportation systems, developing intelligent transportation systems and studying infrastructure interdependencies.

faculty.name: David Rounce
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of CERCA
faculty.summary: Rounce's research seeks to quantify the response of glaciers, water resources, and hazards to climate change to inform adaptation and mitigation efforts, using computational models integrated with remote sensing and fieldwork.

faculty.name: Costa Samaras
faculty.title: Trustee Professor; Director, Wilton E. Scott Institute for Energy Innovation
faculty.summary: Samaras examines pathways to clean, climate-safe, equitable, and secure energy and infrastructure systems, analyzing how technologies and policies affect energy use, climate resilience, and environmental outcomes across electric vehicles, renewable electricity, and climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Pingbo Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Remote sensing and human-cyber-physical systems for infrastructure
faculty.summary: Tang explores remote sensing, human systems engineering, data analytics, and information modeling to support spatiotemporal analyses for predictive management of constructed facilities, examining sensing methods for human-cyber-physical systems in construction and infrastructure operations.

faculty.name: Gerald Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Nanoscale transport phenomena and the energy-water nexus
faculty.summary: Wang applies mechanics, statistical physics, and high-performance computing to understand nanoscale structural and transport phenomena, developing innovations for challenges at the intersection of water and energy systems.

funding.note: Guaranteed 12-month fellowship for 5 yrs + Columbia housing + $1,000/yr research allowance

faculty.name: Sonya Dyhrman
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine phytoplankton and microbial biogeochemistry, understanding how marine microbes influence ocean nutrient cycles and respond to environmental change using molecular approaches.

faculty.name: Göran Ekström
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on global seismology, earthquake source processes, glacial earthquakes and glacier dynamics, and the structure and dynamics of Earth's interior using broadband seismic data.

faculty.name: Pierre Gentine
faculty.title: Maurice Ewing and J. Lamar Worzel Professor of Geophysics; Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences and Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on land-atmosphere interactions, the hydrologic cycle in a changing climate, machine learning for climate science and Earth system modeling, and atmospheric boundary layer processes.

faculty.name: Steven L. Goldstein
faculty.title: Higgins Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on isotope geochemistry applied to mantle evolution, continental crust formation, ocean circulation changes, and paleoclimate reconstruction using geochemical proxies in marine sediments.

faculty.name: Kevin L. Griffin
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences and Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology; Chair of DEES
faculty.summary: Research on plant and ecosystem ecology, physiological responses of plants to climate change, terrestrial carbon cycling, and the role of vegetation in Earth's climate system.

faculty.name: Sidney R. Hemming
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on paleoclimatology and geochronology, using radiogenic isotopes to trace sediment provenance, ice-rafted debris records, and deep ocean circulation changes through glacial cycles.

faculty.name: Bärbel Hönisch
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research on paleoceanography and paleoclimate, reconstructing past ocean carbonate chemistry and atmospheric CO2 using boron isotopes in foraminifera, and ocean acidification through Earth history.

faculty.name: Peter B. Kelemen
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on igneous and metamorphic petrology, fluid-rock interactions in subduction zones, carbon mineralization and carbon capture via natural weathering, and mantle geochemistry.

faculty.name: Galen McKinley
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on the ocean carbon cycle and its role in climate, air-sea CO2 exchange, variability and change in ocean carbon sinks, and coupled climate-carbon cycle modeling.

faculty.name: Jerry F. McManus
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on paleoceanography and paleoclimatology, the role of ocean circulation in abrupt climate change, Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation variability, and glacial-interglacial climate transitions.

faculty.name: Faye McNeill
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry and multiphase chemical processes, aerosol formation and transformation, cloud chemistry, and linkages between atmospheric composition, climate, and air quality.

faculty.name: William H. Menke
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on geophysical inverse theory, seismic imaging of Earth structure, and the development of computational methods for geophysical data analysis and interpretation.

faculty.name: Meredith Nettles
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on cryospheric seismology, glacial earthquakes and ice sheet dynamics, glacier-ocean interactions, and using seismic methods to study ice loss and cryospheric change.

faculty.name: Terry A. Plank
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Co-Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on volcanology and geochemistry of subduction zones, volatile cycling between Earth's surface and interior, explosive volcanism, and the role of water in arc magmatism.

faculty.name: Lorenzo M. Polvani
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric and climate dynamics, stratosphere-troposphere coupling, the role of ozone depletion in Southern Hemisphere climate change, and large-scale atmospheric circulation.

faculty.name: Maureen Raymo
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on paleoclimatology focusing on past sea level changes, ice sheet stability, Pliocene warmth as an analog for future climate, and the relationship between climate forcing and ice volume.

faculty.name: Adam H. Sobel
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences and Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric dynamics of tropical meteorology, tropical cyclones, monsoons, extreme weather events, and the intersection of climate science and societal risk.

faculty.name: Marc Spiegelman
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on geodynamics, computational modeling of magma migration and fluid flow in Earth's interior, subduction zone processes, and the development of numerical methods for multiphase flow in porous media.

faculty.name: Maya Tolstoy
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine geophysics, mid-ocean ridge processes and seafloor volcanism, hydroacoustic monitoring, and the interactions between tectonic and magmatic processes at ocean spreading centers.

faculty.name: Renata Wentzcovitch
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences and Applied Physics and Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Research on computational mineral physics, first-principles calculations of material properties at extreme conditions, composition and dynamics of Earth's deep interior, and planetary materials science.

faculty.name: Jacqueline Austermann
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on sea level change, solid Earth deformation, glacial isostatic adjustment, and interactions between ice sheets and the solid Earth in understanding past and future sea level.

faculty.name: Sarah Aarons
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on the relationship between climate and Earth's surface using radiogenic and stable isotopes to study paleoclimate, dust sources and transport, and ice sheet dynamics.

faculty.name: Folarin Kolawole
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on structural geology and tectonics, continental rifting processes, fault growth and reactivation, and links between surface and deep Earth processes at plate boundaries.

programs.program_name: Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology PhD

requirements.admission_model: Direct admission to department; partner institutions incl. AMNH

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS fellowship: tuition, fees, stipend)

faculty.name: Maria Uriarte
faculty.title: Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology; Chair of E3B
faculty.summary: Research on tropical forest ecology, the effects of natural and anthropogenic disturbances (hurricanes, land use) on forest dynamics, and the resilience of tropical ecosystems to climate change.

faculty.name: Kevin L. Griffin
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences and E3B; Chair of DEES
faculty.summary: Research on plant and ecosystem ecology, physiological responses of plants to climate change, terrestrial carbon cycling, and canopy-atmosphere interactions in forests.

faculty.name: Ruth DeFries
faculty.title: University Professor; Denning Family Professor of Sustainable Development; Professor of E3B
faculty.summary: Research on land use change and its impacts on ecosystem services and biodiversity, using remote sensing and field-based approaches to understand human-environment interactions in the tropics.

faculty.name: Shahid Naeem
faculty.title: Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on biodiversity and ecosystem functioning, the consequences of biodiversity loss for ecosystem services, and the role of biodiversity in sustainable development and ecological restoration.

faculty.name: Marina Cords
faculty.title: Professor of E3B and Anthropology
faculty.summary: Research on primate behavior and behavioral ecology, social systems and mating strategies of forest primates, and conservation of tropical forest mammals.

faculty.name: Dustin R. Rubenstein
faculty.title: Professor of E3B; Provost's Senior Faculty Teaching Scholar
faculty.summary: Research on behavioral ecology and social evolution, the evolution of cooperative breeding and complex animal societies, and environmental determinants of social behavior.

faculty.name: Maria Diuk-Wasser
faculty.title: Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on disease ecology, ecology of ticks and tick-borne pathogens, the effects of land use and climate on vector-borne disease dynamics, and urban ecology of zoonotic diseases.

faculty.name: Andres Bendesky
faculty.title: Associate Professor of E3B (Zuckerman Institute)
faculty.summary: Research on the genetic and neural basis of behavioral evolution, using comparative genomics and neuroscience approaches to understand how genes shape behavior across species.

faculty.name: Kira Delmore
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on evolutionary biology and speciation, the genetic basis of migratory behavior in songbirds, and how migration influences population divergence and speciation processes.

faculty.name: Deren Eaton
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on phylogenetics and evolutionary biology, comparative genomic methods for studying plant evolution, and the development of computational tools for phylogenetic inference.

faculty.name: Duncan Menge
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Ecology, Evolution, and Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry, nitrogen fixation and cycling in terrestrial ecosystems, plant-microbe symbiotic interactions, and mathematical models of nutrient dynamics.

faculty.name: Marcia Macedo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of E3B and Columbia Climate School
faculty.summary: Research on tropical ecology and hydrology, the effects of deforestation and land use change on water resources in the Amazon, and the intersection of ecosystem function and water cycling.

requirements.admission_model: Departmental committee admission (SEAS); MS/PhD track available

funding.note: Comprehensive doctoral funding package: stipend, full tuition, health insurance and fees

faculty.name: Daniel Steingart
faculty.title: Chair, Department of Earth and Environmental Engineering; Stanley-Thompson Professor of Chemical Metallurgy; Professor of Chemical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on electrochemical energy storage systems, battery science and engineering, chemical metallurgy for sustainable manufacturing, and design of next-generation energy storage technologies.

faculty.name: Kartik Chandran
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental biotechnology, biological wastewater treatment, nitrogen cycling in engineered and natural systems, greenhouse gas emissions from wastewater, and resource recovery from waste streams.

faculty.name: Greeshma Gadikota
faculty.title: Lenfest Earth Institute Professor of Climate Change; Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on carbon capture, utilization and storage, fluid-surface interactions in energy and environmental systems, sustainable resource recovery, and reactive transport processes for decarbonization.

faculty.name: Pierre Gentine
faculty.title: Maurice Ewing and J. Lamar Worzel Professor of Geophysics; Professor of EEE and Earth and Environmental Sciences; LEAP Director
faculty.summary: Research on the hydrologic cycle and land-atmosphere interactions, using machine learning to improve climate and Earth system models, and understanding water cycle changes under climate change.

faculty.name: Ngai Yin Yip
faculty.title: La Von Duddleson Krumb Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on advanced separation processes for water treatment, desalination and brine management, membrane-based resource recovery from waste streams, and zero-liquid-discharge technologies.

faculty.name: Adeyemi Adeleye
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental chemistry of contaminants, heavy metal mobility and remediation, PFAS fate and transport, phytomining for metal recovery, and sustainable environmental nanotechnologies.

faculty.name: Shaina Kelly
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on subsurface environmental processes, reactive transport in porous media, and physicochemical phenomena governing contaminant fate and resource extraction in geological systems.

faculty.name: Oscar Nordness
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental engineering processes, sustainable material and chemical manufacturing, and technologies for environmental sustainability.

faculty.name: Bolun Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable energy systems, electricity market design for high renewable penetration, energy storage optimization, and strategies for deep decarbonization of power grids.

faculty.name: Galen McKinley
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Affiliate Professor of Earth and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on the ocean carbon cycle, variability and change in ocean carbon sinks, air-sea CO2 exchange, and coupled climate-carbon cycle modeling for understanding future climate trajectories.

faculty.name: George Deodatis
faculty.title: Vice Dean for Research; Professor of Civil Engineering and Engineering Mechanics; Associated Faculty in EEE
faculty.summary: Research on risk and resilience of infrastructure systems, stochastic mechanics for uncertainty quantification, and probabilistic methods for natural hazard risk assessment and climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Vijay Modi
faculty.title: Professor of Mechanical Engineering; Associated Faculty in Earth and Environmental Engineering; Director, Laboratory for Sustainable Energy Solutions
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable energy solutions, energy access and rural electrification in developing regions, distributed energy systems, and infrastructure planning for energy transitions.

faculty.name: Alan West
faculty.title: Samuel Ruben-Peter G. Viele Professor of Electrochemistry; Professor of Chemical Engineering; Associated Faculty in EEE
faculty.summary: Research on electrochemical engineering, electrodeposition and battery materials, electrochemical energy conversion and storage, and corrosion science.

funding.note: Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Beth A. Ahner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ahner investigates how organisms adapt to trace metal stress and influence metal forms in the environment. Her lab studies plant metal solubilization, uptake, detoxification, and sequestration, with applications in phytoremediation of contaminated soils.

faculty.name: C. Lindsay Anderson
faculty.title: Department Chair, Professor
faculty.summary: Anderson works on energy system decarbonization at the interface of environmental and systems engineering, electric power systems, applied optimization, and decision science. Projects include renewable energy integration, wind generation uncertainty mitigation, and biofuels production optimization.

faculty.name: Buz M. Barstow
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Barstow is a physicist using synthetic biology to build sustainable energy technologies. His work spans systems and synthetic biology for sustainable energy and molecular/cellular bioengineering.

faculty.name: Natalie Cápiro
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Cápiro examines the biotransformation of emerging (e.g., PFAS) and legacy (e.g., chlorinated solvents) organic contaminants in the environment. Her research explores degradation pathways, microbial community dynamics, and biological-chemical interactions relevant to groundwater remediation and water quality protection.

faculty.name: Michael Charles
faculty.title: Cornell Provost's New Faculty Fellow and Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Computational sustainability and Indigenous knowledge systems
faculty.summary: Charles develops computational sustainability frameworks combining dynamic ecological models with community-centered relationships. His work focuses on the role landscapes can play in addressing sustainability challenges and on interfaces between institutionalized science and Indigenous knowledge systems.

faculty.name: Ashim K. Datta
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Datta develops mechanistic understanding of food processes under various heating modes to enhance food safety and quality. His group uses a fundamentals-based approach applying multiphase and multicomponent porous media analysis across scales and physics domains.

faculty.name: Jacqueline Gerson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Gerson investigates how human activities alter nutrient and contaminant cycling through watersheds, focusing on the fate and transport of these elements in terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems. She studies biogeochemical cycles in socioenvironmental systems and their implications for people and animals.

faculty.name: Peter Hess
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hess focuses on understanding atmospheric chemistry within the context of the Earth's climate system. He examines how atmospheric composition may shift during the 21st century and investigates coupled interactions between climate change and air quality.

faculty.name: Sunghwan (Sunny) Jung
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Jung studies physics and biology problems arising from biological systems interacting with their environments, using mathematical modeling, fluid dynamics, and experiments. Topics include animal drinking mechanics, bird diving, plant spore dispersal, and raindrop impacts on biological surfaces.

faculty.name: Manoj Karkee
faculty.title: The Norman R. and Sharon R. Scott Professor of Agriculture and Life Sciences
faculty.summary: Karkee focuses on sensing, machine vision, AI, and robotics in agriculture. His program develops AI, machine learning, and robotics solutions to reduce resource consumption and address farming challenges.

faculty.name: Dan Luo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: DNA materials and nucleic acid engineering
faculty.summary: Luo's work focuses on DNA and RNA as both generic and genetic materials. His lab develops novel DNA materials such as tree-shaped DNA, DNA gels, and DNA-nanoparticle hybrids, with applications in diagnostics, pharmaceutics, protein production, drug delivery, cell culture, and optoelectronics.

faculty.name: Minglin Ma
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Biomaterials for biomedical and agricultural applications
faculty.summary: Ma's group develops advanced biomaterials for agricultural and biomedical applications, with emphasis on cell replacement therapies for type 1 diabetes. The work spans polymers, hydrogels, inorganics, and composites that interact with biological systems.

faculty.name: John March
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: March focuses on reconfiguring biological systems for improved performance in biomedicine and sustainability. His lab concentrates on signal transduction, metabolism, and eukaryotic-prokaryotic interactions, with emphasis on subcellular-level events.

faculty.name: Chris Roh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Roh studies insect adaptations in fluid environments and applies biomechanics findings to agricultural, biomedical, and aeronautical engineering. Research areas include fluid mechanics, entomology, comparative biomechanics, bio-inspired engineering, and in vivo engineering.

faculty.name: Rosa Santomartino
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Santomartino investigates microbial behavior in space conditions to advance sustainable space exploration and address environmental challenges on Earth. Her work covers biotechnologies for in situ resource utilization, bioregenerative life support systems, and waste upcycling, including plastic-degrading and bioleaching microorganisms.

faculty.name: Vivek Srikrishnan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Srikrishnan focuses on quantifying impacts from climate change and identifying and evaluating strategies for climate risk management. His research uses systems engineering, data science, decision science, and economics to enhance societal resilience, spanning flood management to energy investments.

faculty.name: Tammo Steenhuis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Steenhuis investigates landscape processes and water movement across the northeastern US, Ethiopia, and China, with focus on sustainable agricultural practices, distributed hydrological processes, pesticide transport, and gully formation prevention.

faculty.name: Scott Steinschneider
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Steinschneider specializes in statistical hydroclimatology, examining how climate and hydrologic systems affect water resources planning. His work addresses hydroclimate variability, risks to water infrastructure from climate processes, and adaptation strategies for sustainable water management.

faculty.name: M. Todd Walter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Walter focuses on interactions between hydrology, ecology, and biogeochemistry. Current research encompasses linkages between hydrology and biogeochemical hotspots, plus nano-biotechnology applications to ecohydrological systems.

faculty.name: Mingming Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wu's lab develops micro-scale devices and computational tools for measuring cell-generated forces and exploring cellular growth dynamics in relation to environmental factors. Current work focuses on tumor mechanics and invasion, motivated by health and environmental challenges.

faculty.name: Lirong Xiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Xiang develops smart cyber-physical systems integrating robotics, computer vision, and machine learning for agriculture. Focus areas include AI and robotic sensing for controlled environment agriculture, bio-inspired robotic systems, high-throughput phenotyping, and digital twins for agricultural automation.

funding.note: Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: John D. Albertson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Albertson studies the exchange rates of mass (water, CO2, pollutants), energy, and momentum between the land and atmosphere, using integrated measurement and modeling with applications in hydrometeorology and air quality.

faculty.name: Edwin (Todd) Cowen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cowen studies environmental fluid mechanics with a focus on sustainability, renewable energy, and water systems, pairing laboratory experiments with field studies of natural and anthropogenic flows including surface turbulence, wave energy, and environmental DNA tracking.

faculty.name: Ricardo Daziano
faculty.title: Director of Graduate Studies, Professor
faculty.summary: Daziano works on engineering decision making, specifically theoretical and applied econometrics of consumer behavior and discrete choice models applied to technological innovation in transportation and energy.

faculty.name: Fani Derveni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Derveni develops multiscale smart metastructures for sustainable and resilient infrastructure, integrating experimental and computational mechanics with work on renewable materials and lightweight metamaterials with high energy dissipation.

faculty.name: Pete J. Diamessis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Diamessis develops high-accuracy spectral computational methods and parallel simulations to study stratified turbulence, internal waves, and mixing in oceanographic and environmental fluid systems.

faculty.name: Christopher J. Earls
faculty.title: J. Preston Levis Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Earls develops mathematical and computational approaches for complex systems, focusing on Scientific Artificial Intelligence and inverse problems, including uncertainty quantification and sparse sensing in engineering and applied science.

faculty.name: Stefano Galelli
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Galelli studies interactions between critical infrastructure systems and the natural environment, focusing on how hydro-climatic variability affects interconnected water-energy infrastructures and developing adaptive management for reliability, resilience, and sustainability.

faculty.name: H. Oliver Gao
faculty.title: Howard Simpson 1942 Professor of Engineering; Director, Systems Engineering Program
faculty.summary: Gao works at the intersection of environmental health, sustainability, and systems engineering, developing computational and systems solutions integrating engineering, data science, and policy for climate-smart mobility, low-emission infrastructure, and data-enabled health systems.

faculty.name: Andrea Giometto
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Giometto investigates the spatial growth dynamics of microbial communities, examining how ecological and evolutionary processes play out in spatially extended populations through statistical physics, experiments, and genetic engineering.

faculty.name: Mircea Dan Grigoriu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Grigoriu specializes in random vibration, stochastic calculus, numerical methods for stochastic problems, probabilistic microstructure models, and wind and earthquake engineering, spanning computational solid mechanics and structural engineering.

faculty.name: April Gu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gu applies microorganisms and biotechnology to address environmental pollutants in water systems, with work on sustainable wastewater treatment, nutrient recovery, climate change impacts on phosphorus cycling, and toxicogenomics-based water quality assessment.

faculty.name: Damian E. Helbling
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Helbling studies organic chemicals in water systems using high-resolution mass spectrometry, examining fate and transformation processes and remediation technologies to understand how chemical contaminants impact freshwater resources and global health.

faculty.name: Andreas Malikopoulos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Control and learning for autonomous systems
faculty.summary: Malikopoulos works at the intersection of learning and control to enable autonomous systems—vehicles, robots, and infrastructure—to operate optimally while safely adapting to dynamic environments, with applications in connected and automated mobility.

faculty.name: Jacob Mays
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Mays applies optimization and statistical learning to energy systems, with a focus on electricity market design and analysis, drawing on prior experience at the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

faculty.name: Gregory McLaskey
faculty.title: Director of Undergraduate Studies, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: McLaskey studies earthquake mechanics, fracture, and friction using seismic waves and piezoelectric sensors, applying these techniques to structural health monitoring and damage detection in buildings, bridges, and civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Sriramya Duddukuri Nair
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nair advances cementitious materials for infrastructure, energy, and automated construction, studying how composition and processing affect cement-based materials, with emphasis on alternative formulations, subsurface well cementing, and 3D concrete printing.

faculty.name: Linda K. Nozick
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Professional Programs, Professor
faculty.summary: Nozick focuses on quantitative modeling of risk, resilience, and complex infrastructure systems through optimization, game theory, and stochastic methods, with applications to transportation networks, hazardous materials, disaster recovery, and insurance mechanisms.

faculty.name: Patrick Michael Reed
faculty.title: Director of the School; Joseph C. Ford Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Reed advances multiobjective systems engineering and decision analytics for complex water systems facing climate change risks, bridging sustainability science, economics, operations research, and high-performance computing to design and manage engineered systems.

faculty.name: Matthew Charles Reid
faculty.title: Director of Undergraduate Studies for Environmental Engineering Major, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Reid combines molecular-level experimental studies with modeling to understand aquatic contaminant dynamics for sustainable ecosystem management, focusing on nutrients in agricultural watersheds and geogenic metal(loid)s affected by climate change.

faculty.name: Ruth E. Richardson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Richardson applies molecular biological tools—genomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics—to study microbial communities involved in bioremediation, sustainable wastewater treatment, and bioenergy, including organisms that remediate chlorinated contaminants and uranium.

faculty.name: Samitha Samaranayake
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Samaranayake focuses on the analysis and control of networked cyber-physical systems with applications to transportation and urban infrastructure, using optimization, algorithms, and real-time data to enable sustainable mobility including mobility-on-demand platforms.

faculty.name: Chloé Arson
faculty.title: Professor and M.Eng. Director
faculty.summary: Arson specializes in damage and healing rock mechanics, poromechanical modeling of porous media, and computational geomechanics, developing numerical tools to evaluate underground storage, simulate rock fracturing, and design bio-inspired geotechnical systems.

faculty.name: Nikolaos Bouklas
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bouklas develops theoretical frameworks and computational methods to study soft, active, and biomaterials with applications to fracture, instabilities, and multiscale coupled systems, integrating machine learning with physics for constitutive modeling and PDE solutions.

funding.note: Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Toby R. Ault
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Studies for Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies climate risks including drought projections under climate change, seasonal (spring) dynamics, and variations in the tropical Pacific on decadal to centennial timescales and their influence on global climate. Leads the Emerging Climate Risk Lab, using data synthesis and statistical modeling.

faculty.name: Rebecca J. Barthelmie
faculty.title: Professor, Sibley School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates wind energy resources and wind turbine wake dynamics within and downwind of large wind farms, both onshore and offshore. Her work combines remote sensing (including lidar), modeling, and analysis of large wind farm datasets.

faculty.name: Gregory P. Bewley
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Sibley School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates turbulence phenomena across multiple contexts, from cloud droplet collisions to quantized vortex dynamics in superfluid helium, using novel experimental techniques and interpretation methods to inspire theoretical understanding.

faculty.name: Arthur T. DeGaetano
faculty.title: Professor; Director of Undergraduate Studies for Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Focuses on applied climatology, developing methods and datasets that provide climatological information to decision-makers. His work spans climate modeling, climate data quality, and climate impact assessment; he directs the NOAA Northeast Regional Climate Center.

faculty.name: Peter G. Hess
faculty.title: Professor, Biological and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric chemistry and its relationship to climate change, examining how atmospheric composition may shift through the 21st century. Integrates chemical models and atmospheric measurements to project future air quality and understand impacts on human health and ecosystems.

faculty.name: Peter Hitchcock
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates Earth's global atmospheric circulation from the perspective of the stratosphere, focusing on stratospheric dynamics, predictability, and interactions with other parts of the climate system. Uses dynamical models across a range of complexity together with observational datasets.

faculty.name: David L. Hysell
faculty.title: Thomas R Briggs Professor in Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates ionospheric plasma physics, focusing on instabilities and irregularities affecting radio wave propagation. His work combines experimental, theoretical, and computational approaches, with significant research conducted at the Jicamarca Radio Observatory in Peru.

faculty.name: Flavio Lehner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates how internal and externally-forced climate variability affects our understanding of climate change across scales. Works to improve climate projections by understanding the physical processes governing variability and regional changes in temperature, precipitation, and extreme events.

faculty.name: Nikole K. Lewis
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Astronomy
faculty.summary: Investigates exoplanet atmospheres through observational and theoretical methods, including space- and ground-based spectroscopy and photometry, one- to three-dimensional atmospheric modeling, and laboratory studies of cloud and haze formation processes.

faculty.name: Jonathan Lin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies tropical meteorology, including hurricanes, equatorial waves, and the Madden-Julian Oscillation. His work focuses on improving hurricane forecasting, understanding their predictability, and projecting how weather hazards associated with hurricanes might change with warming.

faculty.name: Douglas MacMartin
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Sibley School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on climate engineering and solar geoengineering, particularly stratospheric aerosol injection, applying engineering control and feedback-analysis methods to critical climate science questions and to support informed policy decisions.

faculty.name: Natalie M. Mahowald
faculty.title: Irving Porter Church Professor in Engineering; Chair, Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches feedbacks in the Earth system that impact climate change, including atmospheric transport of biogeochemically important species such as desert dust and interactions with the carbon cycle, using global transport models and satellite data analysis.

faculty.name: Una K. Miller
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates air-sea interactions, upper ocean turbulence, and high-latitude environments, as listed in the Cornell Atmospheric Science graduate field faculty roster.

faculty.name: Angeline G. Pendergrass
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
