faculty.summary: Investigates extreme precipitation and its response to climate variability and change. Her work employs theory, observational analysis, and climate modeling to understand how precipitation patterns are shifting and what drives these changes.

faculty.name: Sara C. Pryor
faculty.title: Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: An atmospheric scientist using field measurements and numerical modeling to understand global climate change and variability at regional scales, atmosphere-surface exchange, and formation and removal processes for atmospheric aerosols, with applications to climate and wind energy science using big data analytics.

faculty.name: Vivek Srikrishnan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Biological and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on quantifying impacts from climate change and identifying and evaluating strategies for climate risk management, drawing on systems engineering, data science, decision science, and economics to improve resilience to deeply uncertain and dynamic environmental risks.

faculty.name: Scott Steinschneider
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Biological and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in statistical hydroclimatology, characterizing water-related climate variability and analyzing hydroclimate impacts on water systems. His work addresses sustainable water resource planning and management through assessing climate risks and identifying adaptation strategies.

faculty.name: Daniele Visioni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies interactions between stratospheric aerosols, chemistry, and climate, focusing on volcanic eruptions and sunlight reflection methods (geoengineering). Explores how climate models simulate these atmospheric-surface interactions and their impacts on fisheries, biodiversity, and human health.

faculty.name: K. Max Zhang
faculty.title: Irving Porter Church Professor of Engineering, Sibley School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates energy-environment interactions through numerical modeling and experimental techniques, focusing on air pollution, renewable energy, and deep decarbonization. His research has informed major legislation and programs in New York, the U.S., and internationally.

funding.note: Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Geoffrey Abers
faculty.title: William and Katherine Snee Professor in Geological Sciences
faculty.summary: Geophysicist who uses seismic arrays to study earthquake dynamics, crustal structure, and material exchange between Earth's surface and deep interior. His work focuses on subduction zones and their role in continental crust growth and volcanic activity.

faculty.name: Chloé Arson
faculty.title: Professor and M.Eng. Director, Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies damage and healing rock mechanics, micro-macro modeling of porous media, and computational geomechanics. Her group develops tools to evaluate underground storage performance, model rock weathering, and create bio-inspired geotechnical systems, with emerging applications of artificial intelligence.

faculty.name: Toby R. Ault
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Director of Graduate Studies for Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies emergent climate risks including prolonged drought under climate change, seasonal dynamics, and tropical Pacific variations affecting global climate. Combines observational data synthesis with numerical and statistical modeling; field concentrations include paleoclimatology and Quaternary geology.

faculty.name: Grace Barcheck
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Employs seismology, geophysics, and machine learning to investigate sliding and deformation processes in ice sheets and fault zones, with applications to understanding sea-level rise and earthquake hazards.

faculty.name: Riley Culberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies near-surface hydrology and the internal structure of ice sheets using ice-penetrating radar, focusing on how shallow water systems affect sea-level contributions from the Greenland and Antarctic ice sheets. Leads the Cornell Hydrology, Ice, and Radar Physics (CHIRP) Lab.

faculty.name: Louis A. Derry
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies for Geological Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates biogeochemical and Critical Zone processes at multiple time scales, from modern soil-plant-water systems to the evolution of coupled biogeochemical cycles and long-term atmospheric CO2 change over Earth history.

faculty.name: Patrick Fulton
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Croll Sesquicentennial Fellow
faculty.summary: Investigates heat and fluid in fault zones using observation, quantitative analysis, and numerical modeling to understand earthquake physics and subsurface processes. His work combines hydrogeology, thermal geophysics, and geomechanics with applications to earthquake behavior and geothermal energy.

faculty.name: Esteban Gazel
faculty.title: Charles N. Mellowes Professor in Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates processes in Earth's interior that produce magmas and volcanoes, including intraplate volcanism and subduction processes, integrating geochemical cycles to understand planetary evolution and past environmental impacts.

faculty.name: Alexander Hayes
faculty.title: Jennifer and Albert Sohn Professor; Director, Cornell Center for Astrophysics and Planetary Science
faculty.summary: Investigates the geological and physical processes shaping planetary surfaces and atmospheres using spacecraft-based remote sensing, with recent focus on Titan's hydrocarbon lakes, early Mars sedimentology, and Comet 67P. Directs the Spacecraft Planetary Image Facility.

faculty.name: Megan Holycross
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts laboratory experiments to understand how Earth's interior chemically differentiates, focusing on 'crystal clock' diffusion chronometry and trace element partitioning in subduction zones to quantify the rates and conditions of magmatic and metamorphic processes.

faculty.name: David Lee Hysell
faculty.title: Thomas R. Briggs Professor in Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates ionospheric plasma physics, focusing on plasma instabilities and irregularities that affect radio wave propagation. His work combines experimental, theoretical, and computational approaches, with significant research conducted at the Jicamarca Radio Observatory in Peru.

faculty.name: Kade Keranen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses a wide range of geophysical methods to study the shallow subsurface, with field faculty concentrations in tectonics at plate boundaries and induced earthquakes, and integrates scientific studies with work on equity in science.

faculty.name: Rowena B. Lohman
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies for Earth and Atmospheric Sciences Major
faculty.summary: Investigates earthquake nucleation and fault dynamics using satellite-based remote sensing, particularly interferometric synthetic aperture radar (InSAR), combined with geodetic modeling to understand ground deformation in tectonically active regions.

faculty.name: Natalie M. Mahowald
faculty.title: Irving Porter Church Professor in Engineering; Chair, Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric transport of biogeochemically important species such as desert dust and their role in carbon cycling, examining climate feedbacks through global transport models and satellite data analysis.

faculty.name: Gregory McLaskey
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies (Civil and Environmental Engineering)
faculty.summary: Studies the mechanics of earthquakes, fracture, friction, impact, and other processes that generate sounds and vibrations in solids, using seismic waves and acoustic emission sensors, including large-scale laboratory earthquake experiments.

faculty.name: Carolina Muñoz-Saez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Investigates geological processes involving fluids, with a focus on hydrothermal environments such as geysers and geothermal systems, combining field observations with geochemical and physical analysis.

faculty.name: Matthew Pritchard
faculty.title: Sidney Kaufman Professor of Geophysics; Director, Institute for the Study of the Continents (INSTOC)
faculty.summary: Measures changes in Earth's shape to model processes including earthquakes, volcanoes, groundwater, landslides, and glaciers, applying optical and radar satellite remote sensing along with analysis of subsurface energy systems.

faculty.name: Britney Schmidt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops robotic instruments and uses spacecraft observations to study planetary systems, focusing on Earth's ice shelves, glaciers, and subsurface oceans as analogs for Ocean Worlds like Europa, combining climate impact assessment with the search for extraterrestrial habitability.

funding.note: Fully funded (tuition, stipend, health insurance) for PhD students

faculty.name: Ginger Allington
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Allington studies how ecosystems respond to management and climate change in socio-environmental systems, with emphasis on arid rangelands in Central Asia. Her work integrates ecology, geography, remote sensing, and modeling across multiple scales.

faculty.name: Bernd Blossey
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.sub_field: Invasive plant ecology and biological control
faculty.summary: Blossey directs the Ecology and Management of Invasive Plants Program and the New York Invasive Species Research Institute. His work develops biological weed control using specialized insect herbivores and examines how multiple environmental stressors affect native ecosystems.

faculty.name: Evan Cooch
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Cooch is an evolutionary ecologist who applies theoretical and quantitative methods to the management and conservation of natural resources. His work emphasizes quantitative population analysis for wildlife populations.

faculty.name: Courtney Davis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Davis develops quantitative methods to understand biodiversity status and trends, integrating population ecology, conservation biology, and information science. She also studies bias and accessibility in scientific peer review.

faculty.name: Reem Hajjar
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Hajjar is an interdisciplinary conservation social scientist who uses an environmental justice lens to make forest governance processes more just and effective. Her work examines tropical forest management in Latin America, West Africa, Southeast Asia, and the U.S. Pacific Northwest.

faculty.name: Matthew Hare
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section; Director, Environment and Sustainability
faculty.summary: Hare investigates population genetics and evolutionary processes in marine organisms and how genetic markers can inform aquatic population management and restoration. His work examines adaptive evolution and the genomic consequences of domestication and wild-farmed interbreeding.

faculty.name: Steven Mana'oakamai Johnson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Johnson studies how conservation and climate change affect coastal communities in the Pacific Islands, using social, environmental, and climate data to develop equitable solutions. His work emphasizes human-environment geography, social-ecological systems, and indigenous knowledge.

faculty.name: Karim-Aly Kassam
faculty.title: International Professor of Environmental and Indigenous Studies, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.sub_field: Indigenous ecological knowledge and human-environment relations
faculty.summary: Kassam's research examines the connectivity of human and environmental relations, addressing indigenous ways of knowing, food sovereignty, sustainable livelihoods, stewardship, and climate change. His work is conducted in partnership with indigenous communities in the Arctic, Boreal Forest, and Pamir Mountains.

faculty.name: Clifford Kraft
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Kraft examines ecosystem interactions affecting freshwater fish populations in lakes and rivers, with emphasis on thiamine deficiency-related fish mortality. He also investigates climate change impacts on coldwater fish and invasive species effects on fish communities.

faculty.name: Marianne Krasny
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Krasny's work centers on climate education and advocacy, exploring connections between community environmental stewardship, individual environmental actions, and wider systemic change. She directs the Cornell Civic Ecology Lab.

faculty.name: Peter McIntyre
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: McIntyre is an aquatic conservation ecologist who develops management approaches balancing human interests with biodiversity protection. His work uses field studies across multiple continents to achieve sustainable freshwater resource use while maintaining river and lake ecosystem integrity.

faculty.name: Amelia Moore
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Moore is an environmental anthropologist studying island and coastal politics, the social effects of transnational environmental science and technology, and inequities exacerbated by global environmental change. Her work emphasizes environmental justice and how historical injustices shape relationships between scientists and impacted communities.

faculty.name: Amanda Rodewald
faculty.title: Garvin Professor and Senior Director of Center for Avian Population Studies, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Rodewald leads an international research program at the intersection of ecology and conservation addressing challenges in working landscapes. She uses field studies and participatory science, collaborating with practitioners and policymakers to balance social and ecological needs.

faculty.name: Lars Rudstam
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section; Director, Cornell Biological Field Station
faculty.summary: Rudstam specializes in fisheries and aquatic sciences, focusing on food web dynamics and interactions between fisheries management and ecosystem processes. His current work examines invasive species impacts and lower trophic level dynamics in the Great Lakes and Oneida Lake.

faculty.name: Rebecca Schneider
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Section Chair, Natural Resources and the Environment
faculty.sub_field: Watershed hydrology and water resource management
faculty.summary: Schneider's work centers on sustainable water resource management under climate change, with emphasis on how roadside ditch networks affect watershed hydrology and water quality. Her secondary research investigates organic matter restoration in wetland and terrestrial soils to enhance hydrologic functions.

faculty.name: Richard Stedman
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section; Interim Director, Ashley School of Global Development and the Environment
faculty.summary: Stedman studies social-ecological systems with emphasis on sense of place, meanings and attachments held for landscapes, and environmental quality. His recent work addresses energy transitions, water systems, wildlife, and fisheries in resource-dependent communities experiencing rapid change.

faculty.name: Nina Overgaard Therkildsen
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Therkildsen studies how species adapt to their environment and how quickly they can respond to altered conditions caused by selective harvest, climate change, or other anthropogenic pressures. Her work focuses on marine and anadromous fish populations using genomic approaches.

faculty.name: Steven Wolf
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Wolf's scholarship examines environmental governance and the interplay of state and non-state actors in environmental management, with emphasis on institutional analysis applied to agriculture, forests, and environmental change. His current work centers on accountability relations in environmental management.

faculty.name: Joseph Yavitt
faculty.title: Professor, Natural Resources and the Environment Section
faculty.summary: Yavitt investigates the composition and functioning of wetland and forest ecosystems. He maintains collaborative research networks examining peatland ecosystems across North America, tropical forests in Panama, and local forest and wetland sites near Cornell.

programs.program_name: Ecology, Evolution, Environment and Society PhD

faculty.name: Matthew P. Ayres
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Ayres studies the population ecology of forest insects, investigating why some species become high-abundance pests with dramatic population fluctuations while most remain stable. His work is basic, applied, collaborative, and often interdisciplinary.

faculty.name: Emily L. Behrman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Behrman investigates how animal behaviors evolve at the genetic and neural levels using fruit flies (Drosophila), complemented with field ecology to understand how behavior affects evolution in natural populations.

faculty.name: Ryan Calsbeek
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Calsbeek studies how males and females face different evolutionary pressures despite sharing genetic material, leading to sexual conflict, and uses wood frogs to examine how selection shapes distinct life stages and local adaptation.

faculty.name: Kathryn L. Cottingham
faculty.title: Dartmouth Professor in the Arts & Sciences; Professor, Department of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Cottingham investigates the dynamics of lake plankton communities, linkages between terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems, and applications of ecological principles to public and environmental health, using observations, experiments, and models.

faculty.name: Mark Alan McPeek
faculty.title: David T. McLaughlin Distinguished Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: McPeek integrates empirical and theoretical approaches to examine the ecological processes determining organism distributions and abundances, spanning population and community ecology, molecular systematics, comparative biology, and paleontology to understand adaptation and diversification.

faculty.name: Jessie Mutz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Mutz studies feedbacks between individual traits (such as defenses and movement) and population characteristics to understand ecological and evolutionary dynamics, emphasizing plant interactions with herbivorous insects and belowground fungi using field studies, experiments, and mathematical modeling.

faculty.name: Kevin J. Peterson
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Peterson investigates the Cambrian explosion and the origins of animal body plans, focusing on the evolution of novel developmental regulatory circuitry by integrating molecular paleobiology with evolutionary developmental biology.

faculty.name: Caitlin E. Hicks Pries
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Ecosystem ecology and terrestrial carbon cycle
faculty.summary: Hicks Pries investigates the terrestrial carbon cycle and how ecosystem carbon balance is determined by the interplay of soil and plant processes with climate, with recent work on deep soil organic carbon stability and how climate change affects it.

faculty.name: Olga Zhaxybayeva
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Zhaxybayeva investigates microbial evolution through large-scale genomic data analysis, examining the impact of horizontal gene transfer on microbial populations and using metagenomic datasets to characterize communities and identify signatures of adaptive evolution.

faculty.name: Douglas Thomas Bolger
faculty.title: Myers Family Professor; Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Bolger studies how human land use affects animal and plant populations, examining the impacts of residential development in California's coastal sage scrub ecosystem and agricultural intensification near African national parks.

faculty.name: Bala Chaudhary
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Chaudhary studies plant-soil-microbial interactions and mycorrhizal symbioses, focusing on microbial dispersal, trait-based ecology, and using microbes to address global challenges like climate change and sustainable agriculture across multiple ecosystems.

faculty.name: Michael Cox
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies; Chair, Department of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Cox investigates environmental governance with an emphasis on community-based natural resource management, examining how property rights shape environmental policy across disciplines and cultures, with fieldwork in the Southwestern United States and Dominican Republic.

faculty.name: Richard B. Howarth
faculty.title: Richard and Jane Pearl Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Howarth studies the interplay between economic analysis and the biophysical, moral, and social dimensions of environmental policy and governance, focusing on ecosystem service valuation, intergenerational justice, and climate stabilization. He co-chairs the EEES PhD program.

faculty.name: Lydia L. Jennings
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.sub_field: Environmental soil science and Indigenous science
faculty.summary: Jennings is an environmental soil scientist focused on soil health, environmental remediation, Indigenous science, Indigenous data sovereignty, and science communication, integrating Indigenous knowledge systems with environmental science.

faculty.name: Theresa Ong
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Ong investigates how complexity in agricultural systems across space, time, and species diversity affects food production and resilience to ecological and political disruptions, integrating theory with empirical studies of agroecology, urban farming, and biological control.

faculty.name: Shersingh Joseph Tumber-Dávila
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.sub_field: Plant ecology and the global carbon cycle
faculty.summary: Tumber-Davila investigates how plants influence the global carbon cycle through studies of plant allometry, climate change impacts on forest carbon sequestration, and nature-based climate solutions, using regional and global experiments.

faculty.name: Justin S. Mankin
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Geography
faculty.summary: Mankin investigates climate variability and change using observations and models to constrain uncertainties in future climate impacts and to attribute impacts already occurring, focusing on drought, ecohydrology, and socioeconomic consequences.

faculty.name: Jonathan M. Winter
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Geography
faculty.summary: Winter investigates climate prediction and its impacts on human health, water resources, and agriculture, focusing on drivers of tickborne disease expansion, future irrigation water availability, and the predictability of climate extremes in the Northeastern United States.

faculty.name: Abigail H. Neely
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Geography
faculty.summary: Neely investigates how material and social forces shape health and development outcomes in Africa, examining interactions among local people, development workers, and non-human actors like crops and nutrients, using ethnographic and quantitative methods.

faculty.name: Christopher S. Sneddon
faculty.title: Professor of Geography and Environmental Studies; Chair, Department of Geography
faculty.summary: Sneddon investigates how to balance human activities with the resilience of ecological systems, focusing on water use and river basin transformation, social conflicts over water resources, and river restoration through political ecology and environmental history frameworks.

programs.program_name: Ph.D. in Earth and Climate Sciences

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Nicolas Cassar
faculty.title: Professor of Biogeochemistry, Lee Hill Snowdon Bass Chair
faculty.summary: Cassar's research focuses on environmental biogeochemistry and physiology, with the objective of constraining the mechanisms governing carbon cycling and climate. Current interests include ocean carbon cycles and productivity, carbon acquisition mechanisms in marine phytoplankton, and global carbon cycle and ocean-atmosphere fluxes, integrating field observations, laboratory experiments, modeling and theory.

faculty.name: Sarah Cooley
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Planetary Health
faculty.sub_field: Arctic hydrology and cryosphere remote sensing
faculty.summary: As PI of the Cryo-hydrO Observation Leaders (COOL) Lab at Duke, Cooley's group investigates how climate change is affecting dynamic cryospheric and hydrologic processes in the Arctic and globally. Her research primarily uses satellite data to understand fine-scale hydrologic change, complemented by field campaigns in Alaska, Northern Canada, and Greenland.

faculty.name: Shineng Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Climate Dynamics
faculty.summary: Hu is a climate dynamicist whose lab combines observations, theory, and climate modeling to study climate dynamics on a broad range of timescales, with a particular focus on ocean-atmosphere interactions. Research topics include El Nino, ocean inter-basin interactions, global warming, and paleoclimate.

faculty.name: Michael Kipp
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in the Division of Earth and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Kipp's GAIA Lab uses elemental and isotopic techniques to study the rock record and reconstruct changes in environmental conditions through Earth history. Research foci include tracking ocean (de-)oxygenation through Earth history, nutrient cycling in ancient ecosystems, and developing new geochemical analytical methods.

faculty.name: Wenhong Li
faculty.title: Professor of Climate in the Division of Earth and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Li's research interests focus primarily on climate dynamics, land-atmosphere interaction, hydroclimatology, and climate modeling. Her current research seeks to understand how the hydrological cycle changes in the current and future climate and the impacts on ecosystems, subtropical high variability and change, unforced global temperature variability, and climate and health issues.

faculty.name: Brian McAdoo
faculty.title: Truman & Nellie Semans/Alex Brown & Sons Associate Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: McAdoo studies the effects of disasters triggered by natural hazards, asking how humans impact the physical systems that keep us alive and how marginalized populations are specifically affected. Current projects in Nepal (earthquakes, landslides and road development) and in Borneo and Brazil (deforestation, ecosystem services and community health) apply a Planetary Health framework to coupled human-environment systems and geohazards.

faculty.name: A. Brad Murray
faculty.title: Professor of Geomorphology and Coastal Processes
faculty.summary: Murray, a geomorphologist, studies how Earth-surface environments are shaped and how they change over time, especially in response to changing forcing, with most recent research focusing on coastal environments. Much of his work addresses couplings between physical and ecological processes and between natural and human dynamics, using perspectives and techniques from nonlinear dynamics and complex systems. He is Director of Graduate Studies for Earth & Climate Sciences.

faculty.name: Lincoln Pratson
faculty.title: Gendell Family Professor of Energy and Environment
faculty.summary: Pratson is a geologist/geophysicist by training who has served as Chair of the Earth & Ocean Sciences division, Director of the Duke University Energy Hub, and Associate Director of the Gendell Center for Engineering, Energy & the Environment. His work spans marine geology and energy systems, and he co-founded the Sustainable Energy Fellowship.

faculty.name: Daniel Richter
faculty.title: Theodore S. Coile Distinguished Professor of Soils and Forest Ecology
faculty.sub_field: Soil science and Critical Zone science
faculty.summary: Richter's research and teaching links soils with ecosystems and the wider environment, most recently the Earth scientists' Critical Zone. He focuses on how humanity is transforming Earth's soils from natural to human-natural systems, specifically how land uses alter soil processes and properties on time scales of decades, centuries, and millennia, including the long-term Calhoun soil experiment.

faculty.name: Jonathan Ryan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Ice and Climate Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Glaciology and ice sheet remote sensing
faculty.summary: Ryan is a glaciologist interested in ice sheet surface processes, investigating components that influence the accumulation and ablation of ice sheets including snowfall, clouds, melt-albedo feedbacks, and supraglacial hydrology. He relies primarily on satellite remote sensing, serves on NASA's ICESat-2 and Terra/Aqua/Suomi Science Teams, and conducts field surveys of ice sheets and glaciers with uncrewed aerial systems.

faculty.name: Drew Shindell
faculty.title: Nicholas Distinguished Professor of Earth Science
faculty.summary: Shindell studies climate change, air quality, and links between science and policy. Formerly at the NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (1995-2014), he has authored more than 250 peer-reviewed publications, is a fellow of AGU and AAAS, and has testified on climate issues before both houses of the US Congress.

faculty.name: Avner Vengosh
faculty.title: Nicholas Distinguished Professor of Environmental Quality
faculty.sub_field: Water quality and energy-water nexus geochemistry
faculty.summary: Vengosh, chair of the Division of Earth and Climate Sciences, and his team study the energy-water nexus, conducting pioneering research on the impact of hydraulic fracturing and coal ash disposal on the quantity and quality of water resources in the U.S. and China. He has also investigated sources and mechanisms of water contamination worldwide, including salinity and radioactivity in the Middle East.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: James S. Clark
faculty.title: Nicholas Distinguished Professor of Environmental Science
faculty.summary: Clark's lab uses long-term experiments and monitoring studies to understand disturbance and climate controls on ecosystem dynamics. He is a Fellow of the Ecological Society of America and a recipient of the Humboldt Research Prize.

faculty.name: Stuart L. Pimm
faculty.title: Doris Duke Distinguished Professor of Conservation Ecology
faculty.summary: Pimm is a world leader in the study of present-day extinctions and what can be done to prevent them, researching why species become extinct, how fast they do so, and the global patterns of habitat loss and species extinction. He is one of the most highly cited environmental scientists and author of over 350 scientific papers.

faculty.name: James Brendan Heffernan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Ecosystem Ecology and Ecohydrology
faculty.summary: Heffernan studies major changes in ecosystem structure, particularly in streams, rivers and wetlands, focusing on feedbacks among ecological, physical, and biogeochemical processes. He increasingly applies tools and theories developed for local ecosystems to understand ecological patterns at regional and continental scales.

faculty.name: Liyin He
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Ecology
faculty.summary: He researches the relationship between ecosystem, air, and water in the face of climate change, translating fundamental ecological knowledge into applied practices such as forest management for carbon sequestration and mitigating urban heat. She uses spatiotemporal data analysis, machine learning, and process-based ecological models.

faculty.name: Tong Qiu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Ecology
faculty.sub_field: Global change and terrestrial ecosystem ecology
faculty.summary: Qiu is an ecologist interested in how global change affects terrestrial ecosystem functions and services across scales, and how climate and habitat change jointly drive biodiversity shifts in natural and urban systems. He develops data-model synthesis frameworks integrating remote sensing, field sampling, and ecological monitoring with statistical and Earth System models.

faculty.name: Jason Donaldson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in the Division of Environmental Natural Sciences
faculty.summary: Donaldson is a wildlife ecologist investigating how interactions between plants, animals, and environmental drivers such as rainfall and soils impact ecosystem processes across savanna landscapes. His lab integrates disease, consumer, and community ecology to examine how predators and disease affect fire-herbivore-vegetation interactions, combining landscape experiments with modeling.

faculty.name: Jennifer Jo Wernegreen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental and Evolutionary Genomics
faculty.summary: Wernegreen's lab centers on environmental and evolutionary genomics, primarily in bacteria, exploring the mechanisms shaping genetic and functional variation in microbes that play important roles in the natural environment. Her work integrates evolutionary, population genetic, computational, and molecular approaches to clarify how bacterial genomes change over time and how ecological interactions such as symbiosis influence genome content.

faculty.name: Heather M. Stapleton
faculty.title: Ronie-Richele Garcia-Johnson Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Stapleton is an environmental chemist and exposure scientist whose research focuses on identifying halogenated and organophosphate chemicals in building materials, furnishings, and consumer products and estimating human exposure, particularly in vulnerable populations such as pregnant women and children. Her lab uses mass spectrometry to characterize chemical burdens in environmental and biological samples, and she serves as Director of the Duke Superfund Research Center.

faculty.name: Joel Meyer
faculty.title: Sally Kleberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Meyer studies the effects of toxic agents and stressors on human and wildlife health, particularly the mechanisms by which environmental agents cause DNA damage and the processes organisms use to prevent and repair it, with a focus on mitochondrial DNA. He studies these effects in the nematode C. elegans, cell culture, other model organisms, and human populations.

faculty.name: Nishad Jayasundara
faculty.title: Juli Plant Grainger Assistant Professor of Global Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Jayasundara studies adverse human and ecological health outcomes of anthropogenic environmental change, primarily emphasizing environmental drivers of kidney development and health, including aquatic contaminants and heat stress. His lab integrates environmental epidemiology with mechanistic toxicology, examining mitochondrial function in the context of chronic kidney disease.

faculty.name: Prasad S. Kasibhatla
faculty.title: Professor in Environmental Chemistry
faculty.summary: Kasibhatla works to develop a fundamental, quantitative understanding of the factors that determine the chemical composition of the atmosphere, delineating natural and anthropogenic impacts and how these affect natural ecosystems. His research uses numerical models together with remote and in-situ measurements of atmospheric composition.

faculty.name: William Kuang-Yao Pan
faculty.title: Professor of Global Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Pan is a biostatistician with expertise in spatial analysis, demography, land use science, infectious disease epidemiology, and environmental health. He has over 20 years of experience leading large, interdisciplinary research teams studying the impact of human-environment dynamics on human health in low- and middle-income countries.

faculty.name: Junfeng Zhang
faculty.title: Professor of Global and Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Zhang is a professor of environmental and global health with more than 290 peer-reviewed publications who directs an Environmental and Biomarkers Analysis Laboratory. His early work characterizing sources of non-methane greenhouse gases made him an officially recognized contributor to the 2007 Nobel Peace Prize.

faculty.name: Lori Snyder Bennear
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Economics and Policy
faculty.summary: Bennear's research focuses on evaluating environmental policies and improving the methods and techniques for conducting these evaluations, using rigorous quantitative methods. Her work includes estimating the effectiveness of environmental policies such as information disclosure and management-based regulations.

faculty.name: Dalia Patino-Echeverri
faculty.title: Gendell Family Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Patino-Echeverri's research focuses on public policy design for energy systems, with a particular emphasis on managing risks arising from uncertainties in government actions. Much of her work addresses policies affecting capital investment decisions in the electricity industry and the societal costs of electricity and air-emissions levels.

faculty.name: Erika S. Weinthal
faculty.title: John O. Blackburn Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Weinthal specializes in global environmental politics and environmental security with a particular emphasis on water and energy, including global environmental governance, environmental conflict and peacebuilding, the resource curse, climate change adaptation, and energy transitions. She is an award-winning author of several books on environmental cooperation and resources.

faculty.name: Jeffrey R. Vincent
faculty.title: Korstian Distinguished Professor in Forest Economics and Management
faculty.summary: Vincent is an economist who works mainly on forest policy issues in low- and middle-income countries, blending academic research, teaching, administration, and donor-funded policy-advising. His current research focuses on the economics of forest restoration.

faculty.name: Martin Doyle
faculty.title: Professor in the Division of Environmental Social Systems
faculty.summary: Doyle focuses on the science and policy of rivers and water in the US, with work ranging from fluid mechanics and sediment transport to infrastructure finance and federal water policy. He is the author of three books on America's rivers, ecosystem markets, and water infrastructure finance.

faculty.name: Ryan Emanuel
faculty.title: Professor of Hydrology in the Division of Environmental Social Systems
faculty.summary: Emanuel is a hydrologist and community-engaged scholar studying ecohydrology, biogeosciences, environmental justice, and Indigenous rights. His group partners with Native American Tribes and other communities to understand environmental change through the lenses of environmental justice and Indigenous rights.

faculty.name: Anais Roque
faculty.title: John Hope Franklin Assistant Professor of Environmental Justice
faculty.summary: Roque is an environmental social scientist and disaster anthropologist who studies resource insecurity and health in the Anthropocene, examining how households and communities experience, prepare for, and respond to food, energy, and water insecurity in the wake of hazards or disasters. She conducts mixed-methods research in Puerto Rico, Phoenix, and the U.S.-Mexico Borderlands.

faculty.name: Yichun Fan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Climate Economics in Environmental Social Systems
faculty.summary: Fan is an applied economist specializing in Environmental and Urban Economics whose research focuses on urban resilience to environmental risks. Her recent work integrates applied economics with new data and data science technologies to understand how adaptations of people and places shape the economic and distributional impacts of climate change.

faculty.name: Kimberly Marion Suiseeya
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Policy in the Division of Environmental Social Systems
faculty.sub_field: Environmental justice and global environmental politics
faculty.summary: Marion Suiseeya is an environmental social scientist with expertise in environmental justice, global environmental politics, Indigenous politics, and community-driven research. Her research examines how Indigenous communities shape and are impacted by multilateral environmental agreements such as the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change and the Convention on Biological Diversity.

programs.program_name: Ph.D. in Marine Science and Conservation

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Andrew J. Read
faculty.title: Stephen A. Toth Distinguished Professor of Marine Biology in the Nicholas School of the Environment
faculty.summary: Read studies the conservation biology of long-lived marine vertebrates, particularly marine mammals, seabirds, and sea turtles. His work documents the effects of human activities on these populations through fieldwork, experimentation, and modeling.

faculty.name: Brian R. Silliman
faculty.title: Rachel Carson Professor of Marine Conservation Biology
faculty.summary: Silliman focuses on community ecology, food webs, conservation, restoration, and the ecological consequences of cooperative behavior in marine environments. His work examines how species interactions and mutualisms shape coastal ecosystems and inform strategies for protecting and restoring marine habitats.

faculty.name: Douglas P. Nowacek
faculty.title: Randolph K. Repass and Sally-Christine Rodgers University Distinguished Professor of Conservation Technology in Environment and Engineering
faculty.summary: Nowacek investigates the link between acoustic and motor behavior in marine mammals, primarily cetaceans and manatees, focusing on echolocation, foraging behaviors, and the impacts of human-generated noise on marine species.

faculty.name: Patrick N. Halpin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Halpin's work centers on marine geospatial analysis and the ecological applications of geographic information systems and remote sensing to marine conservation and ecosystem-based management. He leads the Marine Geospatial Ecology Lab and applies spatial technologies to inform conservation for marine species and habitats.

faculty.name: James L. Hench
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Oceanography
faculty.summary: Hench's laboratory investigates fluid dynamics in the coastal ocean and its effects on transport processes through field measurements, computational models, and theory. His work emphasizes coral reef hydrodynamics and how water motion influences geochemistry, biology, and ecology in shallow marine environments.

faculty.name: Dana E. Hunt
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Microbial Ecology
faculty.summary: Hunt studies bacterial ecology and diversity, examining how microbes interact with their environments across temporal and spatial scales. Her lab investigates bacterial responses to environmental factors such as temperature change and emerging pollutants to understand the forces driving microbial diversity in nature.

faculty.name: Zackary I. Johnson
faculty.title: Juli Plant Grainger Associate Professor of Biological Oceanography and Marine Biotechnology
faculty.summary: Johnson's group investigates the distribution, diversity, and function of marine microorganisms, with particular focus on Prochlorococcus, the most abundant phytoplankton in the open oceans. Their work also explores biotechnological applications of marine microalgae for food and fuel.

faculty.name: Grant D. Murray
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Policy in the Marine Science and Conservation Division
faculty.summary: Murray investigates the social and economic dimensions of marine resource management, with a focus on aquaculture conflicts and coastal community impacts. His current work examines oyster mortality events, shellfish aquaculture expansion, and the competing values shaping policy debates in coastal waters.

faculty.name: Lisa M. Campbell
faculty.title: Rachel Carson Distinguished Professor of Marine Affairs and Policy
faculty.summary: Campbell investigates ocean governance across issues including fisheries, marine protected areas, and the blue economy, drawing on political ecology and science studies to examine how science and state and non-state actors shape governance outcomes. She also develops participatory and collaborative research methodologies.

faculty.name: Daniel Rittschof
faculty.title: Norman L. Christensen Distinguished Professor of Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Rittschof studies marine ecology, including larval biology and chemical, behavioral, and spatial ecology as well as environmental toxicology. His work spans macroinvertebrate ecology (blue crabs and mud snails), barnacle biology relevant to fouling prevention and bioadhesives, and xenobiotic impacts on marine organisms.

faculty.name: David A. Gill
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in the Division of Marine Science and Conservation
faculty.summary: Gill examines marine coupled human-natural systems with an emphasis on tropical coral reef management and conservation. His research provides evidence-based insights into how marine management and conservation can lead to equitable and sustainable outcomes, drawing on economics, ecology, and political science.

programs.program_name: Ph.D. in Civil and Environmental Engineering

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Mike Bergin
faculty.title: Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Bergin investigates how atmospheric particles affect Earth's climate and human health, studying the emission, transformation, and deposition of particulate matter across polluted and pristine environments.

faculty.name: Fred Boadu
faculty.title: CEE Director of Master's Studies, Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Boadu investigates how the engineering, environmental, and petrophysical properties of porous media (soils, fractured rock, biological tissues) affect measurable geophysical responses and develops methods to obtain these properties through non-invasive geophysical measurements.

faculty.name: Mark Borsuk
faculty.title: James L. and Elizabeth M. Vincent Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Environmental systems modeling and risk analysis
faculty.summary: Borsuk develops mathematical models for integrating scientific information across natural, technical, and social systems, specializing in Bayesian network modeling applied to environmental and human health decision-making.

faculty.name: Andy Bragg
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Bragg studies the physics and modeling of turbulence and turbulent transport, theoretical and computational fluid dynamics, and applied mathematics, focusing on complex turbulent flows in natural and engineered systems.

faculty.name: David Carlson
faculty.title: Yoh Family Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Carlson develops machine learning and artificial intelligence techniques to accelerate scientific discovery, with applications in environmental health, mental health, and neuroscience.

faculty.name: Nathaniel Chaney
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Chaney harnesses global environmental data to improve understanding of the terrestrial water cycle, focusing on quantifying the role of multi-scale spatial organization over land in Earth systems.

faculty.name: Laura Dalton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Reactive multiphase transport in porous media
faculty.summary: Dalton is an experimentalist studying reactive, multiphase transport in porous media, working to understand and manipulate the chemical and physical processes that occur in engineered (cementitious) and natural (geological) porous materials.

faculty.name: Jeseth Delgado Vela
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Delgado Vela leverages environmental biotechnology to improve urban water infrastructure, integrating molecular tools and modeling to understand microbial community interactions in engineered water treatment systems.

faculty.name: Marc Deshusses
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Deshusses focuses on the design, analysis, and application of remediation, waste-to-energy, and decentralized sanitation processes, developing novel reactors for treating air, water, and solid wastes.

faculty.name: Lee Ferguson
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Ferguson develops analytical methods for measuring organic pollutants, with a major focus on high-resolution mass spectrometry to detect emerging contaminants such as PFAS in wastewater and drinking water.

faculty.name: Henri P. Gavin
faculty.title: W.H. Gardner Jr. Chair of Civil and Environmental Engineering, Professor
faculty.summary: Gavin's work spans computational mechanics, structural dynamics, and control systems, with emphasis on earthquake engineering and semi-active vibration isolation technologies.

faculty.name: Leanne M. Gilbertson
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Gilbertson develops sustainable materials to address environmental challenges, integrating molecular design with life cycle assessment to minimize impacts while solving environmental and public health problems.

faculty.name: Claudia Gunsch
faculty.title: Muriel Theodorsen Williams E'46 Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Gunsch's research bridges environmental engineering and molecular biotechnology, investigating the ecological impacts of emerging contaminants on environmental microbiomes and developing microbiome engineering approaches for bioremediation and water treatment.

faculty.name: Heileen Hsu-Kim
faculty.title: Director of Graduate Studies, Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Hsu-Kim specializes in environmental aquatic chemistry and geochemistry, applying geochemical principles to understand the persistence, mobility, and bioavailability of pollutant metals in natural and built environments.

faculty.name: Andrew D. Jones
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Transport at biological interfaces and biofilms
faculty.summary: Jones's work focuses on transport mechanisms at biological interfaces, developing tools to address biofilms responsible for vascular graft infections, skin infections, waterborne diseases, and post-flood mold contamination.

faculty.name: Zbigniew Kabala
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Kabala's work focuses on groundwater hydrology, contaminant transport, and fluid dynamics through porous media, with applications to environmental remediation and aquifer characterization.

faculty.name: Gaby Katul
faculty.title: George Pearsall Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Katul conducts research on hydrology and fluid dynamics, focusing on turbulent transport processes in atmospheric boundary layers and canopy flows, with applications to water-plant-atmosphere interactions and ecosystem fluxes.

faculty.name: Jerome Lynch
faculty.title: Vinik Dean of Engineering; Fitzpatrick Family University Distinguished Professor of Engineering; Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineer
faculty.sub_field: Structural health monitoring and cyber-physical systems
faculty.summary: Lynch advances cyber-physical system architectures combining sensing, computing, and control for intelligent civil infrastructure, and is recognized for structural health monitoring research that assesses infrastructure performance to enhance safety and resilience.

faculty.name: Nicole C. Rockey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Rockey's work focuses on reducing infectious disease burden by better understanding the persistence of viral pathogens in the built environment, using laboratory and modeling methods to assess transmission risks and develop engineering solutions.

faculty.name: Hossein (Amir) Salahshoor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Salahshoor develops data-driven and computational frameworks to predict, control, and engineer the behavior of complex material systems, with applications to sustainability and health challenges including brain mechanics and advanced metamaterials.

faculty.name: Guglielmo Scovazzi
faculty.title: Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Scovazzi's work focuses on finite element and advanced numerical methods for computational fluid and solid mechanics, emphasizing multiphase porous media flows, transient compressible flows, turbulent flows, and fluid-structure interaction.

faculty.name: Manolis Veveakis
faculty.title: Professor in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Veveakis focuses on theoretical and applied mechanics, geomechanics, and irreversible thermodynamics, with emphasis on multiphysical modeling of plasticity of solids, solid-fluid interactions, friction laws, and rheology of geomaterials.

faculty.name: Mark Wiesner
faculty.title: James B. Duke Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Wiesner's research encompasses membrane processes, nanostructured materials, and the transport and fate of nanomaterials in the environment, along with related colloidal and environmental systems analysis.

faculty.name: Marta Zaniolo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Water resources modeling and machine learning
faculty.summary: Zaniolo addresses water sustainability challenges by combining hydrology and water resources modeling with machine learning, focusing on ensuring reliable water access amid scarcity, competing demands, and climate change.

faculty.name: Yang Liu
faculty.title: Professor, Chair & Gangarosa Distinguished Professor of Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Develops data-driven methods, including satellite remote sensing, to understand how environmental exposures such as air pollution and extreme weather affect health outcomes like cancer, cardiovascular disease, and mental health.

faculty.name: Howard Chang
faculty.title: Professor, Vice Chair for Education
faculty.summary: Develops statistical methods for spatial-temporal exposure and health data, and conducts epidemiologic research on air pollution, heat, and built-environment effects.

faculty.name: Donghai Liang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Exposure scientist and molecular epidemiologist characterizing the human exposome and the molecular mechanisms of health responses to air pollution and persistent organic pollutants, spanning maternal/child health, cancer, and Alzheimer's disease.

faculty.name: Carmen Marsit
faculty.title: Professor, Rollins Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular and epigenomic mechanisms by which environmental exposures affect disease, including cancer, pregnancy outcomes, and childhood conditions, within the exposome framework.

faculty.name: Dana Barr
faculty.title: Professor, Rollins Endowed Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Uses analytical chemistry to assess exposure to environmental toxicants and evaluate exposure sources, with a maternal-child health focus on prenatal exposures and neurodevelopment.

faculty.name: William Caudle
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor, Director of Graduate Studies (PhD Program)
faculty.summary: Integrates neuroscience and toxicology to study how environmental chemicals contribute to neurological disease and neurodegeneration.

faculty.name: Stefanie Ebelt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ambient air quality, climate, and health using exposure science and epidemiologic methods, including population-based studies of vulnerable groups drawing on administrative health data.

faculty.name: Stephanie Eick
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, EH-EPI MPH Program Director
faculty.summary: Environmental and reproductive epidemiologist studying the health effects of chemical and psychosocial stressors during pregnancy, with a focus on chemical mixtures and biomarkers of oxidative stress and inflammation.

faculty.name: Christine Ekenga
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Rollins Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines how environmental and occupational factors drive health disparities using a strengths-based, community-engaged epidemiologic approach, with recent focus on cancers and respiratory disease.

faculty.name: Todd Everson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how early-life exposures shape epigenomic and transcriptomic patterns and long-term health, through epigenome-wide association studies and multi-cohort collaborations on children's health.

faculty.name: Audrey Gaskins
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Rollins Endowed Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Investigates how environmental, dietary, and lifestyle factors relate to reproductive health and fertility, often using assisted-reproductive-technology cohorts.

faculty.name: Xin Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses high-resolution mass spectrometry and omics to study how environmental contaminants affect health, focusing on prenatal exposure impacts on fetal respiratory development and early-life lung disease.

faculty.name: Anke Hüls
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Rollins Endowed Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Studies how air pollution, social factors, and molecular epigenetic mechanisms affect cognitive health across the lifespan, from cognitive decline in adults to development in children.

faculty.name: Longxiang Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assesses human exposure to environmental pollutants and associated health effects, focusing on unconventional oil and gas development, and develops satellite-based machine-learning models and high-resolution radon maps.

faculty.name: Maya Nadimpalli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Food-borne pathogen transmission & gut microbiome epidemiology
faculty.summary: Combines environmental microbiology, epidemiology, and genomics to study how food affects health via the gut microbiome, focusing on antibiotic-resistant pathogens in food systems.

faculty.name: Amina Salamova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Vice Chair
faculty.summary: Uses analytical chemistry and exposure assessment to study exposures to harmful chemical contaminants, especially semi-volatile organic compounds, in vulnerable populations and to develop interventions.

faculty.name: Jeremy Sarnat
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Graduate Studies (MPH Programs), Rollins Endowed Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Characterizes human exposure to urban air pollution and identifies the pollutant sources and chemical components responsible for adverse health effects, including the impact of exposure measurement error.

faculty.name: Noah Scovronick
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the links between human health, climate change, and climate policy, and also investigates exposure to harmful industrial chemicals in Georgia.

faculty.name: Lisa Thompson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on environmental health and nursing with emphasis on sustainability and environmental public health, leading environmental-health evaluation and implementation-science research.

faculty.name: Douglas Walker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Environmental engineer and analytical chemist developing high-throughput exposome methods and exposome-wide association study approaches to measure unidentified exposures and mechanisms of environment-related disease.

faculty.name: Qiang Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mechanistic quantitative adverse outcome pathway (qAOP) and physiologically based toxicokinetic models to understand how environmental chemicals, supplements, and drugs alter molecular pathways affecting health.

faculty.name: Thomas Gillespie
faculty.title: Professor & Chair
faculty.sub_field: Disease ecology and zoonotic pathogen emergence
faculty.summary: Studies interactions among anthropogenic environmental change, biodiversity, and the ecology and emergence of pathogens affecting people, wildlife, and domestic animals, with fieldwork in Africa and Latin America.

faculty.name: Jola Ajibade
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Environmental/human geography focused on climate justice and equitable adaptation
faculty.summary: Uses environmental justice and political ecology frameworks to study climate risk, urban disasters, climate migration, and adaptation, focusing on how exclusionary policies reproduce structural inequalities.

faculty.name: Megan Mucioki
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Interdisciplinary scholar examining food sovereignty, traditional foods, and ecocultural restoration in partnership with Indigenous communities, integrating ethnoecology, botany, applied anthropology, and Indigenous studies.

faculty.name: Stacy-ann Robinson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Climate change adaptation and policy in small island developing states
faculty.summary: Examines the human, social, and policy dimensions of climate change adaptation in small island developing states across multiple scales, using qualitative and mixed methods.

faculty.name: Emily Burchfield
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Her FACES lab integrates social and environmental data to study agricultural sustainability, modeling land-use impacts on agroecological outcomes and drivers of farm-level behavior across scales.

faculty.name: Xiao Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Coordinator of Undergraduate Research
faculty.sub_field: Geospatial analysis of human-environment systems and disaster studies
faculty.summary: Examines human-environment interactions and urban vulnerabilities via geospatial techniques and big data, applying GeoAI and deep learning to disaster monitoring, assessment, and mitigation.

faculty.name: Eric Lonsdorf
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Applied ecosystem services and conservation science
faculty.summary: Conducts applied research incorporating ecosystem services into land management decisions and policy in agricultural and urban systems, bridging environmental science, economic valuation, and decision-making.

faculty.name: Eri Saikawa
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Emory Climate Hub
faculty.summary: Interdisciplinary work spanning atmospheric chemistry, environmental health, biogeochemistry, and climate science, studying emission sources tied to air pollution, ozone depletion, and climate change and their societal consequences.

faculty.name: Gonzalo Vazquez-Prokopec
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Graduate Studies, Winship Distinguished Research Professor in Environmental Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Ecology and spatial epidemiology of vector-borne infectious diseases
faculty.summary: Studies vector-borne diseases at the intersection of ecology and spatial epidemiology, focusing on transmission and geographic propagation and how urban landscapes influence disease spread.

faculty.name: Anthony Martin
faculty.title: Professor of Practice, Honors Program Coordinator
faculty.sub_field: Ichnology and paleontological trace fossil analysis
faculty.summary: Specializes in ichnology, the study of modern and fossil animal traces such as tracks and burrows; his discoveries include the only known burrowing dinosaur and the oldest dinosaur burrows in the record.

requirements.admission_model: Dec 1 for fall; Aug 1 for spring (spring mostly PhD)

funding.note: RA/TA available; avg doctoral GRA stipend $2,835/mo + tuition

faculty.name: Lauren Stewart
faculty.title: Professor, Williams Family Professor; Associate Chair for Graduate Programs
faculty.summary: Designs, tests, and analyzes structures subjected to extreme and high-rate loading including blast, shock, and impact. Directs the Structural Engineering and Materials Laboratory, with focus areas in mass timber systems, ultra-high performance concrete, residual capacity of connections, and protective design.

faculty.name: Donald W. White
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the design and behavior of steel and composite steel-concrete structures, computational mechanics, nonlinear analysis methods, and structural stability design. Focus areas include computer-aided engineering, bridge engineering, and steel building design.

faculty.name: Ryan J. Sherman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches steel structures, fatigue and fracture, large-scale structural experimentation, field monitoring, and structural system redundancy, with outcomes informing AASHTO specifications. Also leads work in large-format metallic additive manufacturing for structural applications.

faculty.name: Danny Smyl
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops tools to monitor and characterize structural life-cycle behavior by integrating tomographic sensing, inverse problems, and machine learning with uncertainty quantification. His group works on damage imaging in self-sensing materials, learned model-error compensation, and ML-driven structural design with physics constraints.

faculty.name: Arash Yavari
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical and computational mechanics with emphasis on elasticity, anelasticity, viscoelasticity, defects, and the geometric structure of continuum and discrete mechanical systems. Focus areas include geometric formulations of mechanics, nonlinear elasticity, growth and remodeling, and defect theories with applications in transformation cloaking and active matter.

faculty.name: Kimberly E. Kurtis
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Faculty Development and Scholarship, Raymond Allen Jones Chair & Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the multi-scale structure, properties, and performance of cement-based materials, integrating advanced experimental characterization, materials processing, and data-driven approaches to improve durability, sustainability, and resilience of concrete infrastructure. Core areas include low-clinker cement systems, durability under coupled loading, and machine learning for materials design.

faculty.name: Abdul-Hamid Zureick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: An authority on innovative materials in civil engineering, with research dedicated to formulating structural engineering criteria and specifications governing the design, testing, and construction of polymer composite structural systems, including testing protocols for new construction and infrastructure rehabilitation.

faculty.name: Edvard P.G. Bruun
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how large-scale robotic automation can transform the design, assembly, and disassembly of building and infrastructure systems. Develops computational methods for material-efficient structural components requiring robotic fabrication, using a Cooperative Multi-Robot Gantry facility for cooperative manipulation and fabrication-informed structural design.

faculty.name: Rafi L. Muhanna
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in computational solid and structural mechanics, uncertainty modeling, reliable engineering computing, structural reliability, and finite elements, with emphasis on uncertainty quantification and methods for dependable engineering computations.

faculty.name: Phanish Suryanarayana
faculty.title: Professor, Group Coordinator
faculty.summary: Focuses on multiscale modeling of materials and structures with particular emphasis on ab initio methods, developing computational tools for characterizing materials across length and temporal scales to design materials with tailored properties for technological applications.

faculty.name: Laurie A. Garrow
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies advanced data-driven methods to understand air travel behavior and forecast demand, with expertise in discrete choice modeling, econometrics, and aviation planning. Leads the Air Transportation Lab and is affiliated with the Center for Urban and Regional Air Mobility.

faculty.name: Randall L. Guensler
faculty.title: Guy J. Lookabaugh Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on transportation systems analysis, travel behavior modeling, and sustainable transportation strategies, developing data-driven methodologies to improve mobility, traffic operations, and environmental impacts. Research includes monitoring air quality impacts of transportation policies, vehicle emissions assessment, and road pricing mechanisms.

faculty.name: Michael P. Hunter
faculty.title: Professor, Group Coordinator
faculty.summary: Specializes in transportation operations and design, including adaptive signal control, traffic simulation, and arterial corridor operations. Current research covers simulation, digital twins, connected and autonomous vehicles, AI-based signal control optimization, and smart cities.

faculty.name: Jorge A. Laval
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies urban traffic congestion by merging traffic flow theory, complex systems theory, and machine learning. Work addresses urban network control, autonomous vehicle stability, macroscopic fundamental diagrams for urban networks, and stochastic car-following models.

faculty.name: Patricia L. Mokhtarian
faculty.title: Clifford and William Greene, Jr. Professor, Regents Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the impact of telecommunications technology on travel behavior, particularly teleworking adoption and impacts. Also researches built environment influences on travel, attitudes toward travel, autonomous vehicles, and subjective well-being.

faculty.name: Srinivas Peeta
faculty.title: Frederick R. Dickerson Chair & Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced optimization and control methods, AI/ML techniques, econometric and behavioral approaches, and system-of-systems paradigms for complex transportation systems. Key areas include dynamic traffic assignment, real-time traffic control, autonomous and connected vehicles, and intelligent transportation systems; Co-Editor-in-Chief of Transportation Research Part B.

faculty.name: Sofía Pérez-Guzmán
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Advances freight transportation and supply chain systems through a human-centered lens, focusing on urban freight, emergency logistics, and urban food systems. Integrates optimization, simulation, and behavioral modeling to address accessibility, sustainability, and operational resilience in logistics.

faculty.name: Yi-Chang James Tsai
faculty.title: Professor, CISE Group Coordinator
faculty.summary: Directs the Smart City Infrastructure Lab, which leverages advanced sensing (3D laser scanning, LiDAR, smartphones, UAVs, eye-tracking), AI, computer vision, and GIS spatiotemporal analysis for sustainability and resilience research. Focus areas include smart infrastructure asset management, intelligent transportation safety, safe mobility for seniors, and transportation energy and emissions reduction.

faculty.name: Susan E. Burns
faculty.title: Interim Associate Vice President for Research Operations and Infrastructure, Dwight H. Evans Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on geoenvironmental engineering, including sustainability and beneficial use of waste materials, bio-mediated ground improvement, and the fundamental chemical and engineering behavior of soils.

faculty.name: Sheng C. Dai
faculty.title: Georgia Mining Association Early Career Professor, Group Coordinator
faculty.summary: Addresses resilience, sustainability, and adaptability in energy and the natural environment through energy geotechnics and nature-inspired engineering. His Subsurface Processes Laboratory studies subsurface geomechanics with applications including gas hydrates, geothermal energy, geological carbon sequestration, nuclear waste disposal, and compressed air energy storage.

faculty.name: J. David Frost
faculty.title: Elizabeth and Bill Higginbotham Professor, Regents' Entrepreneur
faculty.summary: Studies natural and man-made disasters and their impacts on infrastructure at multiple scales using digital data collection systems. Maintains an active, externally funded research program and has graduated 48 PhD students.

faculty.name: Haiying Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches rock mechanics and geomechanics, particularly theoretical and numerical modeling of quasi-brittle material behavior, rock cutting and indentation, poroelastic contact mechanics, and coupled multiphysics processes for subsurface energy recovery and storage.

faculty.name: Jorge Macedo
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Frederick L. Olmsted Early-Career Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on extreme events engineering, with emphasis on earthquakes, mining geotechnics, and AI-driven, physics-informed engineering to advance performance-based assessments of critical infrastructure.

faculty.name: J. Carlos Santamarina
faculty.title: G. Wayne Clough Chair and Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates experimental and numerical approaches to investigate the behavior of geomaterials, including sediments, fractured rocks, and complex fluids, with applications in geotechnical engineering and energy geotechnology.

faculty.name: Iris Tien
faculty.title: Williams Family Professor
faculty.summary: Develops probabilistic methods for modeling and reliability assessment of civil infrastructure systems, spanning risk analysis of complex systems, structural and infrastructure health monitoring, sensing and data analytics, machine learning, and decision making under uncertainty, with applications to smart cities, interdependent systems, and resilient communities.

faculty.name: Isaiah Bolden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies biogeochemical cycling in coral reef and coastal ecosystems, focusing on carbonate mineralization, marine carbonate chemistry, and climate change resilience. His CO3@GT lab uses geochemical methods and community-participatory approaches to study elemental cycling in coastal and marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Annalisa Bracco
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her group investigates climate modes of variability and the multiscale dynamics of geophysical flows and their interactions with biological and chemical tracers, using climate and ocean models and data science tools. Specializes in geophysical fluid dynamics, transport and mixing in the ocean, and climate dynamics.

faculty.name: Jennifer Bowen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the biogeochemistry of wetland and aquatic ecosystems along the land-ocean continuum, examining organic matter decomposition across redox gradients and how disturbance mobilizes stored carbon, using tools including ultra-high resolution mass spectrometry.

faculty.name: Winnie Chu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates climate-cryosphere interaction and glacier and ice sheet dynamics, using ice-penetrating radar and electromagnetic methods, geophysical data analysis, and numerical ice sheet modeling with data assimilation. Her Glacier Geophysics group combines geophysics, machine learning, and modeling to study ice sheet hydrology and ice-ocean interactions.

faculty.name: Yi Deng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on hydroclimate variability at regional scales, polar-tropical interaction feedbacks of ENSO and the Annular Modes, and applications of probabilistic graphical models and climate networks to the climate system.

faculty.name: Lily Dove
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies polar oceanography and oceanic biophysical interactions, including ocean ventilation and circulation, using ocean observations, emerging technology, and numerical modeling; her GATO lab connects fluid dynamics and biogeochemistry in marine ecosystems with an emphasis on polar regions.

faculty.name: Jennifer Glass
faculty.title: Associate Chair for Undergraduate Affairs, Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates environmental controls on greenhouse gas cycling and anaerobic metabolisms in marine ecosystems, including anaerobic oxidation of methane and how trace metal bioavailability influences microbial carbon and nitrogen cycling.

faculty.name: Jie He
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: He leads the Climate Dynamics and Modeling lab group and studies climate change and variability with a focus on climate dynamics and modeling, air-sea interaction, and tropical meteorology.

faculty.name: Felix Herrmann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Herrmann's research covers computational seismic imaging, inverse problems, and machine learning, carried out through his SLIM group at Georgia Tech.

faculty.name: Greg Huey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Huey works in atmospheric chemistry, including measurement of trace gases using chemical ionization mass spectrometry, chemical kinetics of ions and neutrals, heterogeneous chemistry, and aircraft sampling of condensable gases.

faculty.name: Taka Ito
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ito studies ocean circulation, marine biogeochemical cycles, and climate.

faculty.name: Jennifer Kaiser
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Kaiser's research addresses atmospheric composition and chemistry, biogenic and anthropogenic emissions, global chemistry-transport modeling, and in-situ and remote sensing.

faculty.name: Joel Kostka
faculty.title: Professor
