faculty.summary: Kostka studies microbial processes that impact Earth's biogeochemical cycles and ecosystem services, including microbial decomposition of organic matter, greenhouse gas cycling (CO2, CH4), nitrogen fixation, methanogenesis, plant/animal-microbe associations, and hydrocarbon degradation.

faculty.name: Karl Lang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lang studies surface processes, including geochronology and thermochronology methods, thermo-kinematic evolution of convergent orogens, sedimentary provenance and records of landscape change, interactions between climate, tectonic and surface processes, and the formation and preservation of topographic relief.

faculty.name: Pengfei Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric chemistry and aerosols, including aerosol-climate interactions, laboratory studies of organic particulate matter, and biomass burning across multiple timescales.

faculty.name: Susan Lozier
faculty.title: Professor, Dean and Betsy Middleton and John Clark Sutherland Chair
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer whose research focuses on the ocean's role in climate variability and change; she is the international project lead for the Overturning in the Subpolar North Atlantic Program (OSNAP), an observing system measuring meridional overturning in the subpolar North Atlantic.

faculty.name: Jean Lynch-Stieglitz
faculty.title: Chair, Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the behavior of the ocean and Earth's climate over the last 100,000 years, combining geochemical methods for reconstructing the state of the past ocean with the analytical tools and approaches of modern oceanography to understand which parts of the ocean/climate system may be vulnerable to future change.

faculty.name: Samer Naif
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses magnetotelluric, controlled-source electromagnetic, and controlled-source seismic imaging to study hydration of the oceanic lithosphere, fluid-tectonic interactions at plate margins, and the structure of the lithosphere-asthenosphere system, integrating petrologic, seismic, and electromagnetic data.

faculty.name: Andrew Newman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies active deformation and failure of the Earth's lithosphere in seismic and volcanic provinces and its impact on society, focusing on earthquake faulting, failure mechanics, recurrence of large events, and magmatic source physics, with application to understanding natural geologic hazards.

faculty.name: Zhigang Peng
faculty.title: Associate Chair for Research and Faculty Development, Professor
faculty.summary: Seismologist working on data science, high-resolution imaging of fault zone structures, temporal changes of material properties, earthquake source properties, spatio-temporal seismicity patterns and earthquake triggering, and seismological studies of the Earth's interior.

faculty.name: Chris Reinhard
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the biogeochemistry of oxygen-deficient aqueous environments, carbon cycle dynamics and geoengineering, the chemical evolution of Earth's oceans and atmosphere, and planetary habitability and atmospheric biosignatures.

faculty.name: Alexander Robel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies glaciers and ice sheets, sea level change, and climate-cryosphere interaction using computational and mathematical modeling, dynamical systems, and statistical physics. He leads the Ice, Climate and Coasts Lab, which investigates how ice sheets and coastal regions change in response to climate.

faculty.name: Ali Sarhadi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches hurricane dynamics and risk assessment, climate risk and resiliency, compound and cascading dynamics, and tropical hydrometeorology using physics-based and machine learning approaches. His Climate Risk & Extreme Dynamics Lab focuses on tropical cyclones and associated storm surge, extreme rainfall, and compound flooding in warming climates.

faculty.name: Joyce Sim
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies fluid dynamics, mathematics, and numerical modeling to geologic problems, including melt transport beneath mid-ocean ridges, fluid transport at subduction zones, the evolution of tectonic boundaries through Earth's history, and coupled magma ocean-thermal-atmospheric dynamics in early planetary formation.

faculty.name: Martial Taillefert
faculty.title: Associate Chair for Graduate Studies, Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the geochemistry of freshwater and marine environments and geomicrobiology, focusing on interactions between chemical and biological processes at redox interfaces via in situ sediment measurements, analytical speciation of trace metals, and dynamic biogeochemical (reactive transport) modeling.

faculty.name: Yuhang Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches remote sensing of tropospheric oxidants and their precursors, multiscale atmospheric chemistry modeling, reaction kinetics, and the impacts of air pollution on trace gases and aerosols.

faculty.name: Rodney Weber
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric aerosols, including their sources and processing, air quality, and the mechanisms and conditions for new particle formation and particle growth, through instrument development and airborne and ground-based field studies (e.g., wildfire particle emissions and Arctic aerosol chemistry).

requirements.admission_model: Dec 1 for fall; Aug 1 for spring (spring mostly PhD)

funding.note: RA/TA available; avg doctoral GRA stipend $2,835/mo + tuition

faculty.name: Adjo A. Amekudzi-Kennedy
faculty.title: Associate Chair for Academic Innovation; Frederick Law Olmsted Professor
faculty.summary: Studies complex real-world systems with a focus on integrated built, natural, and social environments, emphasizing infrastructure and community resilience, transportation system performance, and decision-making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Joe F. Bozeman III
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Industrial ecologist working on circularity, food-energy-water systems, and urbanization; develops datasets and policy proposals using AI/machine learning, life cycle assessment, and scenario analysis to address socioecological degradation.

faculty.name: Rafael L. Bras
faculty.title: K. Harrison Brown Family Chair and Professor; Regents' Professor
faculty.summary: Hydrologist, hydrometeorologist, and ecohydrologist studying land-atmosphere interactions through soils and vegetation using satellite observations; contributions span hydro-climatic uncertainty, flood and drought forecasting, landscape evolution modeling, and climate change impacts.

faculty.name: Yongsheng Chen
faculty.title: Bonnie W. and Charles W. Moorman IV Professor; Director, The Nutrients-Energy-Water (N.E.W.) Center for Agriculture Technology
faculty.summary: Applies AI and machine learning-assisted tools to integrate decentralized urban resource recovery with precision agriculture, including inverse design of novel materials, computer vision, and digital twin techniques for agricultural productivity.

faculty.name: Aris P. Georgakakos
faculty.title: Turnipseed Family Professor; Group Coordinator, Water Resources Engineering Program; Director, Georgia Water Resources Institute
faculty.summary: Focuses on hydrologic and environmental systems analysis, integrating climate and hydrologic science, earth observations, and water and power system engineering; develops integrated river basin management models and decision support tools for flood/drought management and agricultural planning.

faculty.name: Katherine Graham
faculty.title: Susan G. and Christopher D. Pappas Early-Career Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the fate and transport of pathogens in built and natural environments, including wastewater-based epidemiology, quantitative microbial risk assessment, and pathogen removal in green stormwater infrastructure.

faculty.name: Ching-Hua Huang
faculty.title: Turnipseed Family Chair & Professor
faculty.summary: Expertise encompasses environmental chemistry, advanced water treatment technologies, contaminants of emerging concern, and resource recovery of critical minerals.

faculty.name: Jennifer Kaiser
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Greene Early Career Professor
faculty.summary: Examines the formation and impacts of air pollutants, using aircraft-based measurement campaigns, long-term monitoring networks, and ground-based field studies paired with computational modeling to assess air quality implications.

faculty.name: Kostas T. Konstantinidis
faculty.title: Richard C. Tucker Professor
faculty.summary: Aims to advance understanding of how microorganisms adapt to human-induced environmental perturbations and cause disease, developing culture-independent approaches to study microbial communities in natural and human-associated systems.

faculty.name: Chris Chungkei Lai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the role of fluid turbulence in environmental processes and applications, spanning marine outfall design, oil spill modeling, and turbulence effects on droplet/bubble breakup, glacier ice loss, and water hammer in fluid transport systems.

faculty.name: Jian Luo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research encompasses subsurface flow and reactive transport, computational methods for contamination assessment and groundwater remediation design, geostatistical parameter estimation, coastal hydrogeology and seawater intrusion, and coastal urban flood modeling.

faculty.name: Da Pan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Carlton S. Wilder Early-Career Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates trace gas sensing, multi-scale monitoring/modeling, and machine learning to study atmospheric carbon and nitrogen cycles and their impacts on air quality, climate, and environmental policy, including nitrogen deposition, VOC emissions, and secondary inorganic aerosol formation.

faculty.name: Ameet Pinto
faculty.title: Professor, Carlton S. Wilder Early-Career Professor, Associate Director of Interdisciplinary Research and Collaboration, Brook Byers Institute for Sustainable S
faculty.summary: Research focuses on characterizing and managing microbial communities in the engineered water cycle; the group develops experimental and computational approaches to translate ecological and physiological theory into industry practice.

faculty.name: Armistead G. Russell
faculty.title: Howard T. Tellepsen Chair, Regents' Professor
faculty.summary: Research aims to understand the dynamics of air pollutants at urban and regional scales and assess their health and environmental impacts to develop air quality improvement strategies, spanning air quality modeling, aerosol dynamics, atmospheric chemistry, and environmental economics.

faculty.name: Shane Snyder
faculty.title: José Domingo Pérez Foundation Chair and Professor
faculty.summary: Work emphasizes water quality, treatment, and sustainability, using in vitro bioassays to assess mixture toxicity combined with high-resolution mass spectrometry to identify contaminants, and evaluating physical-chemical treatment processes for organic chemical removal.

faculty.name: Yuanzhi Tang
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Courtesy Appointment
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the fundamentals and applications of molecular-scale geochemical reactions at mineral-water interfaces in natural and engineered systems.

faculty.name: Jingfeng Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research fields include foundations and models of global water-energy-carbon cycles, non-equilibrium thermodynamics of heat and radiation transfer, Amazon deforestation and regional climate dynamics, and Bayesian probability and statistics.

faculty.name: Xing Xie
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Carlton S. Wilder Early-Career Professor, Group Coordinator
faculty.summary: Research centers on applications of environmental biotechnology and materials science at the nexus of water, food, energy, and health, including water treatment and reuse, pollutant detection and quantification, and energy and resource recovery.

faculty.name: Annalisa Bracco
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geophysical fluid dynamics, transport and mixing in the ocean, bio-physical interactions, and climate dynamics using modeling. Projects include phytoplankton blooms in the Tropical Atlantic and physical/biological transport of petrocarbon in the Gulf of Mexico.

faculty.name: Isaiah Bolden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches coral reef and coastal ecosystem biogeochemical cycling, carbonate (bio)mineralization, sclerochronology and paleoclimatology, and marine carbonate chemistry, trace element, and nutrient cycling using traditional and non-traditional stable isotopes and inorganic mass spectrometry.

faculty.name: Sheng Dai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies methane gas hydrates and seafloor sediments through ocean drilling, pressure core testing, and in-situ borehole characterization, with projects including microbes at high pressure in methane hydrate cores.

faculty.name: Thomas DiChristina
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in environmental microbiology, geomicrobiology, and biogeochemistry, focusing on microbial degradation of pollutants and microbial metal reduction. Listed projects include iron-driven hydrocarbon degradation from the Deepwater Horizon spill and coastal ocean acidification.

faculty.name: Lily Dove
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab (GATO Lab) studies the connections between fluid dynamics and biogeochemistry that shape marine ecosystems and influence global climate, with emphasis on Earth's polar regions.

faculty.name: Hermann Fritz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the fluid dynamics of natural hazards including tsunamis, hurricane storm surges, and landslides, along with coastal systems. Current research focuses on the response of coastal defense structures to sea-level rise.

faculty.name: Kevin Haas
faculty.title: Professor & Associate Chair, CE and co-Director and Chair GC, OSE
faculty.summary: Works on coastal engineering, ocean energy, ocean and coastal modeling, and wave mechanics, with current projects on impacts of energy extraction from ocean currents and modeling river-ocean linkages in Southeast Asia.

faculty.name: Mark Hay
faculty.title: Regents Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans marine ecology, chemical ecology, coral reefs, consumer-prey interactions, and marine conservation. Listed available projects include phytoplankton blooms in the Tropical Atlantic and effects of reef degradation on coral microbiomes.

faculty.name: Ellery Ingall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies phosphorus and nutrient cycling in marine environments, including Antarctic waters, and aerosol chemistry using synchrotron techniques; his work addresses dissolved organic phosphorus enriched in phosphonates and marine polyphosphates in geologic phosphorus sequestration.

faculty.name: Takamitsu Ito
faculty.title: Professor & Chair of OSE Graduate Admissions
faculty.summary: Works on computational modeling of climate and biogeochemical processes, focusing on the ocean oxygen and carbon cycle; a listed project involves modeling and observing river-ocean linkages in Southeast Asia.

faculty.name: Joel Kostka
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies microbial carbon and nitrogen cycles, cultivation of marine microbes, biodegradation and bioremediation. Work includes climate-change impacts on microbial ecosystems (e.g., the SPRUCE bog warming experiment) and microbial responses to stressors such as oil spills and plastic degradation.

faculty.name: Julia Kubanek
faculty.title: Professor & Vice President for Interdisciplinary Research
faculty.summary: Researches aquatic chemical ecology and chemical signaling in marine environments, including marine natural products chemistry, metabolomics, and drug discovery, focusing on how organisms use chemical cues and how toxins are regulated in ocean ecosystems.

faculty.name: Chris Lai
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the role of fluid turbulence in environmental processes, including mass transport in coastal vegetation and forest canopies, mixing in hyporheic zones and stratified water bodies, combining experiments, theory, and modeling. Background includes marine outfall design and oil spill modeling.

faculty.name: Susan Lozier
faculty.title: Professor & Dean
faculty.summary: Studies the ocean's role in climate variability and change, with a focus on large-scale ocean circulation. She is the international project lead for the Overturning in the Subpolar North Atlantic Program (OSNAP).

faculty.name: Jean Lynch-Stieglitz
faculty.title: Professor, ADVANCE Professor
faculty.summary: Studies paleoceanography, ocean circulation and climate, and isotope geochemistry, using fossilized shells to reconstruct ocean circulation during past climate periods, including how icebergs may have influenced ice age ocean circulation.

faculty.name: Joseph Montoya
faculty.title: Director of OSE, Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on nutrient and stable isotope biogeochemistry, plankton biology, and oil impacts in offshore ecosystems. Available projects include phytoplankton blooms in the Tropical Atlantic and physical/biological transport of petrocarbon in the Gulf of Mexico.

faculty.name: Zhigang Peng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research includes high-resolution imaging of fault zone structures, temporal changes of the Earth's properties, spatio-temporal seismicity patterns, earthquake triggering, non-volcanic tremor and slow earthquakes, and seismological studies of the Earth's interior.

faculty.name: Alex Robel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies climate and the cryosphere, including glaciology through theory, experiments and observations, computational and mathematical modeling of ice sheets, oceans and atmospheres, ice sheet instabilities, and sea level change.

faculty.name: Karim Sabra
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests include wave propagation, underwater acoustics, and geophysics. His listed OSE project, PAMASON (Passive Monitoring with Ambient Seismic and Ocean Noise), focuses on monitoring acoustic waves generated by ocean noise.

faculty.name: Ali Sarhadi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Climate Risk & Extreme Dynamics Lab, which integrates climate science, physics, engineering, and machine learning to advance understanding and mitigation of risks posed by extreme weather and compound, cascading hazards, including tropical cyclones in warming climates and multidimensional risk assessment.

faculty.name: Lauren Speare
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in Ocean Science & Engineering whose lab (Speare Lab) focuses on marine microbial ecology and host-microbe interactions. The OSE profile provides no detailed research narrative beyond lab links.

faculty.name: Martial Taillefert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans chemical oceanography, sediment biogeochemistry, geomicrobiology, kinetics of biogeochemical processes, and technology development for in-situ measurements. Listed projects include climate change effects on trace metal inputs and coastal ocean acidification driven by urbanization.

faculty.name: Donald Webster
faculty.title: Karen & John Huff School Chair and Professor
faculty.summary: Areas of specialization are ecological fluid mechanics and environmental fluid mechanics. Available research projects include work on harmful algal toxins and marsh attenuation of wave energy during storm events.

faculty.name: Marc Weissburg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research areas are chemical communication, marine community ecology, and predator-prey dynamics. Lists an available project on resilient and sustainable coastal infrastructure.

faculty.name: Jeannette Yen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Area of specialization is small-scale biological-chemical-physical interactions in marine zooplankton.

funding.note: Fully funded (SEAS PhD guarantee: tuition + stipend)

faculty.name: Daniel Jacob
faculty.title: Vasco McCoy Family Professor of Atmospheric Chemistry and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Jacob's group uses atmospheric observations from satellites and other platforms combined with advanced models to understand the chemical composition of the atmosphere, its perturbation by human activity, and the implications for climate and life on Earth.

faculty.name: James G. Anderson
faculty.title: Philip S. Weld Professor of Atmospheric Chemistry
faculty.summary: The Anderson Group develops lasers, lidars, radars, hyperspectral imaging, and in-situ instruments deployed on airborne stratospheric platforms to study free-radical kinetics, catalytic processes controlling ozone, and global climate change.

faculty.name: Frank N Keutsch
faculty.title: Stonington Professor of Engineering and Atmospheric Science and Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology
faculty.summary: Research in the Keutsch group aims to improve understanding of the photochemical oxidation of volatile organic compounds that produce tropospheric ozone and secondary organic aerosol, which affect human health and comprise the largest uncertainties in estimates of anthropogenic climate forcing.

faculty.name: Scot T. Martin
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering and Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Martin studies the chemistry of the atmosphere and environmental systems, including aerosol particles, atmosphere-biosphere interactions, and biogeochemical processes, with field and laboratory work spanning tropical forest and urban environments.

faculty.name: Steven C. Wofsy
faculty.title: Abbott Lawrence Rotch Professor of Atmospheric and Environmental Science
faculty.summary: Wofsy studies the chemistry of the atmosphere on global and regional scales, including stratospheric and tropospheric chemistry, and leads forest and atmospheric measurement programs tracking greenhouse gases and the carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Zhiming Kuang
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Atmospheric and Environmental Science and of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Kuang's research seeks to understand and simulate how tropical convection interacts with the large-scale flow, a key control on tropical circulation, rainfall distribution, and its variability, with implications for monsoon droughts and floods.

faculty.name: Peter John Huybers
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences and of Environmental Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Huybers studies how and why climate changes and the implications for human wellbeing, spanning paleoclimate, ice dynamics and sea level, ocean processes, and climate impacts on agriculture and society.

faculty.name: Eli Tziperman
faculty.title: Pamela and Vasco McCoy, Jr. Professor of Oceanography and Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Tziperman's research addresses ocean, atmosphere, and climate dynamics, seeking to understand physical processes that affect Earth's climate on time scales from a few years to millions of years, in the past, present, and future.

faculty.name: Kaighin A McColl
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences and of Environmental Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: McColl's work focuses on understanding the terrestrial water cycle and its changes in a warming world, using theory, numerical models, and observations of land-atmosphere interactions.

faculty.name: Fiamma Straneo
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering and Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Straneo's research focuses on the climate of the polar regions, including the interactions of the ocean, ice sheets, and sea ice, and their role in climate change and sea-level rise.

faculty.name: Robin Wordsworth
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering and Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Wordsworth's research focuses on planetary climate, atmospheric evolution, and habitability, both in the solar system and around other stars, using fluid dynamics and radiative modeling.

faculty.name: Elsie M Sunderland
faculty.title: Fred Kavli Professor of Environmental Chemistry and Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Research in the Sunderland Lab examines how biogeochemical processes affect the fate, transport, and food-web bioaccumulation of trace metals and organic chemicals, developing models across scales to characterize how changes in climate and emissions affect human and ecological health.

faculty.name: Ann Pearson
faculty.title: Murray and Martha Ross Professor of Environmental Sciences; Affiliate in Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Pearson's research applies analytical chemistry, isotope geochemistry, and molecular biology to biogeochemical oceanography and Earth history, reconstructing carbon and nitrogen cycling in modern and ancient environments.

faculty.name: Daniel P. Schrag
faculty.title: Sturgis Hooper Professor of Geology and Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Schrag studies climate and climate change over Earth history and works on technological and policy approaches to reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including energy systems and carbon capture and storage; he co-directs the Science, Technology and Public Policy Program.

faculty.name: John H. Shaw
faculty.title: Harry C. Dudley Professor of Structural and Economic Geology and Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Shaw's research integrates structural geology, geophysics, and subsurface imaging to study earthquake hazards, fault systems, and energy resources, including carbon sequestration and subsurface energy systems.

faculty.name: Jerry X. Mitrovica
faculty.title: Frank B. Baird, Jr. Professor of Science; Affiliate in Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Mitrovica studies the Earth's response to external and internal forcings across time scales from seconds to billions of years, including ice-age geodynamics, the rotational stability of planets, and the geophysical signatures of ice-sheet mass change and sea-level rise.

faculty.name: Paul R Moorcroft
faculty.title: Professor of Organismic and Evolutionary Biology; Affiliate in Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Moorcroft's research focuses on the ecological dynamics of terrestrial plant communities and ecosystems and their interactions with the atmosphere, developing mathematical and statistical models of vegetation dynamics and biosphere-atmosphere exchange.

faculty.name: Noel Michele Holbrook
faculty.title: Charles Bullard Professor of Forestry; Affiliate in Environmental Science & Engineering
faculty.summary: Holbrook studies the physics and physiology of vascular transport in plants to understand how constraints on the movement of water and solutes between soil and leaves influence ecological and evolutionary processes, including stomatal regulation and xylem embolism during drought.

faculty.name: Hannah Greenwald Healy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Health and Exposure Science
faculty.summary: Healy's research studies pathogens and microbiomes of engineered water systems to inform water management and treatment, infectious disease surveillance, and healthcare infection prevention; she leads the Healy Health 'Omics (H2O) Lab.

faculty.name: Peter DeCarlo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the chemical composition of gas and particles in the air to improve understanding of climate, air quality, and the health impacts of pollutants.

faculty.name: J. Hugh Ellis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: An expert in environmental systems analysis whose models and analyses inform policy-shaping research studies ranging from climate issues to public health.

faculty.name: Paul Ferraro
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: A Bloomberg Distinguished Professor (joint with the Carey Business School) known for research on behavioral economics and the design and estimation of impacts of environmental programs.

faculty.name: Ciaran Harman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Russell Croft Faculty Scholar in landscape hydrology who studies how the structure of landscapes controls the movement of water from rainfall to streams and how that structure evolves over time.

faculty.name: Benjamin F. Hobbs
faculty.title: Theodore M. and Kay W. Schad Professor of Environmental Management
faculty.summary: Uses systems analysis and economics to improve electric utility planning, operations, and policy, as well as the management of environmental and water resources systems.

faculty.name: Venkataraman "Venkat" Lakshmi
faculty.title: B. Howell Griswold, Jr. Professor in Geography and International Affairs
faculty.summary: Studies how water, land, and climate interact across landscapes using Earth observations, physical models, and AI/ML methods to better understand water resources and environmental impacts on human health.

faculty.name: Scot Miller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines satellite data and statistics to understand greenhouse gas emissions across the globe.

faculty.name: Carsten Prasse
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the occurrence and fate of organic contaminants in the urban water cycle and their impact on environmental and human health.

faculty.name: Sarah Preheim
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the microorganisms and environmental drivers of impairments to inland and coastal water bodies, such as oxygen depletion and algal blooms.

faculty.name: Ruggero Rossi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of electrochemistry, microbiology, and material science to achieve sustainable energy-water infrastructure and environmentally sustainable production of chemicals.

faculty.name: Shilva Shrestha
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on innovative biotechnologies to produce bioenergy and biochemicals that substitute for fossil-fuel-based products, address environmental problems, and strengthen the bioeconomy.

faculty.name: Alan Stone
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies chemistry fundamentals to biogeochemical processes to address issues of anthropogenic chemicals in the environment and engineered systems.

faculty.name: Steven Hanke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: An expert in financial statecraft, including water resource economics.

faculty.name: Tanya Alderete
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the effects of environmental exposures on human health throughout the life course, integrating clinical, epidemiological, and multiomics research.

faculty.name: Shyam Biswal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the mechanisms by which environmental factors drive chronic health conditions such as diabetes and cardiovascular diseases.

faculty.name: Joseph Bressler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how environmental pollutants affect neurodevelopment, with specific interests in autism and fetal alcohol syndrome.

faculty.name: Giehae Choi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies environmental epidemiology to characterize exposures during vulnerable life stages and their health impacts, focusing on chemical exposure and children's health.

faculty.name: Meghan Davis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the interface of bacteria and hosts to reduce microbe-mediated diseases in people and animals.

faculty.name: Natalie Exum
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Bridges public health and sanitary engineering to understand how environmental infectious pathogens are transmitted to humans.

faculty.name: Louis Fazen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: An occupational medicine physician interested in preventing occupational illness and enabling workers to achieve better health outcomes.

faculty.name: John Groopman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research involves the development and application of molecular biomarkers of exposure, dose, and effect from environmental carcinogens.

faculty.name: Thomas Hartung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Toxicology and alternatives to animal testing
faculty.summary: Leads efforts to move toxicology away from decades-old animal tests toward organoid cultures and the use of artificial intelligence.

faculty.name: Christopher Heaney
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads research on environmental exposures and infectious disease; his team developed a saliva-based test that detects antibodies to SARS-CoV-2 from small saliva samples.

faculty.name: Benjamin Huynh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses data science and AI to address public health issues related to environmental injustices and disasters.

faculty.name: Kirsten Koehler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches ways to improve exposure assessment methods to inform occupational and environmental public health policy.

faculty.name: Mark Kohr
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches how male and female hearts differ in their response to injury and environmental factors.

faculty.name: Paul Locke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A scientist and lawyer who researches, designs, and implements evidence-based policies to improve public health and protect the environment.

faculty.name: Jaime Madrigano
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Emphasizes environmental justice and aims to reduce population-wide environmental health disparities by producing results that inform equitable policies.

faculty.name: Alexandra Maertens
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses machine learning to study how chemicals perturb cellular signaling and cause toxicity, and how to design safer chemicals.

faculty.name: Keeve Nachman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Exposure and risk science in food systems
faculty.summary: Aims to generate scientific evidence to support decisions that mitigate human exposures to chemical and microbial hazards associated with food production.

faculty.name: Lesliam Quirós-Alcalá
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leverages expertise in exposure science and environmental epidemiology to investigate the potential health impacts of chemical exposures on high-risk populations.

faculty.name: Gurumurthy Ramachandran
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops effective and accurate methods to assess health-related human exposure in occupational and non-occupational settings.

faculty.name: Ana Rule
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on developing and evaluating novel sampling and analysis strategies for assessing exposure to biological aerosols, e-cigarette aerosols, and particulate matter.

faculty.name: Kellogg Schwab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates engineering, public health, and human behavior by researching water and wastewater contaminants of public health concern.

faculty.name: Brian Schwartz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses environmental epidemiology to evaluate the drivers of sustainability health impacts, such as energy, agriculture, and built environments.

faculty.name: Fenna Sillé
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: An immunologist studying the exposome, assessing how a lifetime of physical, chemical, biological, and psychosocial factors interact with the genome and impact human health.

faculty.name: Lena Smirnova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches new methodologies for developmental neurotoxicity testing and gene-environment interactions in autism.

faculty.name: Genee Smith
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses environmental epidemiology, exposure assessment, and spatial analysis to promote environmental justice and alleviate place-based health disparities.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded)

faculty.name: Paolo Bocchini
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the Center for Catastrophe Modeling and Resilience
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic methods and computational techniques to civil engineering, with recent focus on catastrophe modeling and community resilience under natural disasters. His work supports decisions on design, retrofit, preparedness, damage mitigation, and recovery of critical infrastructure.

faculty.name: Dan M. Frangopol
faculty.title: Fazlur R. Khan Endowed Chair of Structural Engineering and Architecture, Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: A pioneer in life-cycle civil engineering and life-cycle cost optimization. Applies probabilistic methods to assess and optimize the long-term performance and management of civil and marine infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: Muhannad T. Suleiman
faculty.title: Professor of Geotechnical Engineering and Deputy Director of ATLSS
faculty.summary: Research emphasizes sustainable and resilient civil infrastructure and renewable energy systems, including soil-structure interaction of deep foundations supporting offshore wind turbines and bridges. Leads a DOE-funded project establishing a National Offshore Wind Energy Testing and Hybrid Simulation Facility at Lehigh.

faculty.name: Sibel Pamukcu
faculty.title: Professor, Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: A recognized expert in electroremediation of soils and groundwater, with over two decades of research on electrochemical decontamination methods. Also develops distributed fiber-optic and wireless sensor systems for monitoring subsurface changes in geo-media and infrastructure.

faculty.name: Shamim Pakzad
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Focuses on the use of wireless sensor networks to monitor and detect damage in bridges and other large structures. Expertise spans structural identification, signal processing, and probabilistic methods in civil engineering.

faculty.name: Spencer Quiel
faculty.title: Associate Chair, Associate Professor of Structural Engineering and Associate Director of ICPIE
faculty.summary: His work focuses on how buildings, bridges, and tunnels resist extreme loads such as fire and blast. His group conducts experimental testing of large-scale assemblies, material characterization, and numerical analyses with funding from NSF, USDOT, and others.

faculty.name: Clay J. Naito
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental and analytical evaluation of reinforced and prestressed concrete structures subjected to extreme events including earthquakes, tsunamis, and intentional blast demands. His work also covers forensic assessment of buildings and bridges for remaining service-life evaluation.

faculty.name: James M. Ricles
faculty.title: Bruce G. Johnston Professor of Structural Engineering, Director of ATLSS Center and NSF NHERI Lehigh Experimental Facility
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental and analytical research on innovative seismic-resistant structures, large-scale multi-directional real-time cyber-physical simulation, offshore wind turbine structures, and coastal civil infrastructure resiliency. Has led over 62 funded research projects and published 187+ peer-reviewed journal articles.

faculty.name: Diego Isidoro Heredia Rosa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational models and structural design methods to enhance the resilience and sustainability of the built environment. Expertise includes multiaxial constitutive formulations and finite element methodologies to simulate inelastic structural behavior under multidirectional cyclic loading.

faculty.name: Cláudia Reis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: A structural engineer researching fluid-solid interaction in the framework of coastal and offshore engineering and multi-risk management. Focused on modeling critical infrastructure across multi-scale and multi-physics domains in multi-hazard prone regions.

faculty.name: Faegheh (Farrah) Moazeni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor and PhD Program Director
faculty.summary: Directs the CONCISE laboratory, focusing on cybersecurity, optimization, and control of interconnected water and energy systems. Emphasizes the water-energy nexus and mathematical optimization frameworks for resilient and efficient infrastructure operations.

faculty.name: Y. C. Ethan Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Director of CatModeling Center
faculty.summary: Research evaluates climate change impacts on the water-energy-food-environment nexus for major river basins, combining system modeling to quantitatively answer questions of uncertain climate change impact and anthropogenic influence. Interests include stormwater management and smart cities.

faculty.name: Maryam Rahnemoonfar
faculty.title: Tenured Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering, joint appointment in Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: AI and remote sensing for infrastructure and disasters
faculty.summary: Directs the Computer Vision and Remote Sensing Laboratory (Bina lab), developing machine learning and computer vision algorithms for diverse sensor types including radar, sonar, multi-spectral, and optical. Her work addresses natural disaster resiliency and environmental challenges through actionable data insights.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded)

faculty.name: James M. Ricles
faculty.title: Bruce G. Johnston Professor of Structural Engineering, Director of ATLSS Center and NSF NHERI Lehigh Experimental Facility
faculty.sub_field: Seismic-resistant steel structures and hybrid simulation
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental and analytical research on innovative seismic-resistant steel and composite structures and large-scale multi-directional real-time cyber-physical simulation. Directs the ATLSS Center and NSF NHERI Lehigh Experimental Facility, one of the largest structural testing labs in the world.

faculty.name: Stephen Pessiki
faculty.title: Professor of Structural Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Structural behavior under fire and earthquake
faculty.summary: Investigates structural performance under extreme conditions including fire exposure and seismic events. Work spans nondestructive testing methodologies and development of innovative structural materials and systems.

faculty.name: Clay J. Naito
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Concrete structures under seismic and extreme loads
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental and analytical evaluation of reinforced and prestressed concrete structures subjected to extreme events including earthquakes, tsunamis, and intentional blast demands. Also performs forensic assessment of buildings and bridges for service-life evaluation.

faculty.name: Spencer Quiel
faculty.title: Associate Chair, Associate Professor of Structural Engineering and Associate Director of ICPIE
faculty.sub_field: Structural resistance to fire and blast
faculty.summary: His work focuses on how buildings, bridges, and tunnels resist extreme loads such as fire and blast. His group conducts experimental testing of large-scale assemblies, material characterization, and numerical analyses.

faculty.name: Dan M. Frangopol
faculty.title: Fazlur R. Khan Endowed Chair of Structural Engineering and Architecture, Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: A pioneer in life-cycle civil engineering and probability-based assessment, design, and optimization of structures. Applies probabilistic mechanics to the long-term performance and management of civil and marine structural systems.

faculty.name: Diego Isidoro Heredia Rosa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational models and structural design methods to enhance resilience under extreme hazards such as earthquakes and wind. Expertise includes multiaxial constitutive formulations and finite element methods for inelastic structural behavior under multidirectional cyclic loading.

faculty.name: Shamim Pakzad
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.sub_field: Structural health monitoring and system identification
faculty.summary: Focuses on the use of wireless sensor networks to monitor and detect damage in bridges and other large structures. Expertise spans structural identification, signal processing, random vibrations, and structural reliability.

faculty.name: Paolo Bocchini
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the Center for Catastrophe Modeling and Resilience
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic methods and computational stochastic mechanics to structural engineering, including regional seismic and hurricane analysis. Recent focus on catastrophe modeling and community resilience of critical infrastructure under natural disasters.

faculty.name: Cláudia Reis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: A structural engineer researching fluid-solid interaction in the framework of coastal and offshore engineering and multi-risk management. Models critical infrastructure across multi-scale and multi-physics domains in hazard-prone regions.

faculty.name: Muhannad T. Suleiman
faculty.title: Professor of Geotechnical Engineering and Deputy Director of ATLSS
faculty.summary: Studies soil-structure interaction of deep foundations supporting offshore wind turbines and bridges, emphasizing sustainable and resilient civil infrastructure. Leads a DOE-funded National Offshore Wind Energy Testing and Hybrid Simulation Facility at Lehigh.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded)

faculty.name: Derick G. Brown
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean for Undergraduate Education
faculty.summary: Investigates interactions between microorganisms and surfaces, focusing on how adhesion affects microbial processes. Explores microbial fate and transport through porous media with applications to bioremediation and energy recovery.

faculty.name: Kristen Jellison
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean for Faculty Development
faculty.summary: Works to prevent waterborne disease through two approaches: protecting drinking water sources from pathogenic contamination and designing household water treatment technologies suitable for developing countries. Research addresses the fate and transport of contaminants and pathogens.

faculty.name: John T. Fox
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Concentrates on preventing pollution from industrial activities and advancing water treatment technologies. Work includes biomaterials for air pollution control, industrial waste reclamation, and novel water treatment materials.

faculty.name: Jishan Wu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced membrane platforms addressing challenges at the water-energy-materials nexus, including hypersaline brine management, critical mineral recovery, and ultrapure water production. Bridges molecular-scale membrane design with system-level separation performance.

faculty.name: Gabrielle String
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on closing evidence gaps for WASH programs in humanitarian and development contexts through laboratory efficacy studies and field effectiveness evaluations. Emphasis on rural Water Safety Plans, household water storage, and treatment methods.

faculty.name: Sibel Pamukcu
faculty.title: Professor, Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: A recognized expert in electroremediation of soils and groundwater, with over two decades of research on electrochemical decontamination methods. Also develops distributed sensor systems for monitoring subsurface environmental change.

faculty.name: Hong-Yi Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the climate-water-energy-environment nexus and develops advanced modeling tools to examine how coupled natural and human systems respond to environmental changes. Emphasizes sustainable water resource management.

faculty.name: Y. C. Ethan Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Director of CatModeling Center
faculty.summary: Research evaluates climate change impacts on the water-energy-food-environment nexus for major river basins, combining system modeling to answer questions of uncertain climate impact and anthropogenic influence. Interests include stormwater management and smart cities.

faculty.name: Faegheh (Farrah) Moazeni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor and PhD Program Director
faculty.summary: Directs the CONCISE laboratory, focusing on optimization, control, and cybersecurity of interconnected water and energy systems. Emphasizes the water-energy nexus and mathematical optimization frameworks for resilient, efficient operations.

faculty.name: Jordan Abell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests span paleoclimatology, geochemistry, paleoceanography, and geoarcheology, reconstructing past environmental and climate conditions.

faculty.name: John Paul Balmonte
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on aquatic microbial ecology and microbial carbon cycling in marine environments.

faculty.name: Gray Bebout
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates cycling of volatiles (CO2, N2) among Earth's major reservoirs, emphasizing metamorphic processes at subduction zones. Also studies altered volcanic glasses as Mars analogs for astrobiology.

faculty.name: Robert Booth
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies ecological responses to environmental change using paleoecological records from peatlands and lakes coupled with contemporary studies. Emphasizes testate amoebae as tools for global change questions.

faculty.name: Benjamin Felzer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Terrestrial ecosystem and carbon cycle modeling
faculty.summary: Investigates how land-use change, rising CO2, climate change, and air pollution affect the land's capacity to absorb carbon, using models at continental and global scales. Recent focus on forest persistence in the American West.

faculty.name: Jill McDermott
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Lab investigates biogeochemistry of deep seawater, seafloor hot springs, and ancient terrestrial fracture waters, including subsurface microbial communities, abiotic organic synthesis, and environmental DNA persistence in marine waters.

faculty.name: Anne Meltzer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies continental lithosphere structure and crustal deformation using seismic arrays in tectonically active regions, using earthquakes to image Earth's interior. Also applies reflection seismology and ground-penetrating radar to near-surface processes.

faculty.name: Frank Pazzaglia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Field-oriented geologist investigating long-term landscape evolution via fluvial deposits, river terraces, and geomorphic markers using thermochronology and cosmogenic nuclide dating. Focuses on tectonic and climatic shaping of the Appalachians, Rockies, Apennines, and central Asia.

faculty.name: Stephen Peters
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates low-temperature geochemical processes using analytical and isotope measurements. Interests include mineral weathering, mercury photochemistry, and trace metal fate and transport in groundwater.

faculty.name: Joan Ramage
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing to detect snowmelt patterns and monitor environmental change in arctic and sub-arctic regions, with applications to wildlife migration and wetland vegetation dynamics.

faculty.name: Dork Sahagian
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Work spans models of volcanic eruption mechanisms and human factors affecting modern sea level. Has integrated multiple disciplines to develop environmental science programs on people-environment interactions.

faculty.name: Michelle Spicer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Plant community ecologist studying plant diversity distribution, epiphytic plant ecology and evolution, and how disturbances affect community dynamics and resilience in forested ecosystems.

faculty.name: Meredith Townsend
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies magma chamber dynamics and volcanic processes, including how thermal and mechanical processes in magma chambers influence surface deformation and eruption triggers. Integrates thermo-mechanical modeling with geophysical observations.

faculty.name: Peter Zeitler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops geochronology-based methods to measure rock temperature histories applied to crustal and landscape co-evolution. Decades of fieldwork in the Himalaya, Tibet, and Mongolia, plus recent deep-time work on continental interiors and the Great Unconformity.

funding.note: SM/PhD track receive RA or fellowship (tuition, stipend, insurance)

faculty.name: Elfatih Eltahir
faculty.title: H. M. King Bhumibol Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how global climate change may impact patterns of water availability, extreme weather, and the spread of disease through hydrologic and climate modeling.

faculty.name: Dara Entekhabi
faculty.title: Bacardi and Stockholm Water Foundations Professor
faculty.summary: Expert in remote sensing techniques for soil and water, focusing on Earth's water cycle and the modeling of land-atmosphere interactions.

faculty.name: Charles F. Harvey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hydrologist and biogeochemist leading field projects in Bangladesh and Borneo, with expertise in carbon storage, peatlands, coastal groundwater, and hydrogeology.

faculty.name: Heidi Nepf
faculty.title: Donald and Martha Harleman Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Fluvial and coastal hydrodynamicist studying how vegetation impacts currents and waves, sediment transport, and ecosystem function.

faculty.name: Ruben Juanes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Computational geoscientist and engineer studying the physics of multiphase flow in porous media with energy and environmental applications.

faculty.name: Jesse Kroll
faculty.title: Peter de Florez Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the chemical transformations and impacts of organic species in the Earth's atmosphere.

faculty.name: Oliver Jagoutz
faculty.title: Cecil and Ida Green Professor of Geology
faculty.summary: Geologist specializing in magmatic processes, oceanic crust evolution, and the formation and evolution of the ocean-continent transition zone.

faculty.name: Desirée Plata
faculty.title: School of Engineering Distinguished Climate and Energy Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in environmental chemistry for novel materials and industrial process optimization, carbon and energy systems, resource efficiency, and sustainable design.

faculty.name: Haruko Murakami Wainwright
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Computational hydrogeologist and geostatistician advancing AI/ML methods for monitoring and predicting water quality and water resources.

faculty.name: Andrew Whittle
faculty.title: Edmund K. Turner Professor in Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Expert in modeling geomaterial behavior, developing analysis methods for predicting soil-structure interactions, and monitoring underground infrastructure.

faculty.name: Franz-Josef Ulm
faculty.title: Class of 1922 Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the nano- and micro-mechanics of porous materials such as concrete, rocks, and bones.

faculty.name: César Terrer
faculty.title: TianFu Group Career Development Professor
faculty.summary: Examines Earth system challenges and climate change with satellite and field data, assessing terrestrial ecosystems, carbon dynamics, and climate solutions.

faculty.name: Michael F. Howland
faculty.title: Jeffrey Cheah Career Development Professor, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies environmental flow physics and the modeling, optimization, and control of renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Otto Cordero
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how social and ecological interactions at micro-scales impact the global productivity, stability, and evolutionary dynamics of microbial ecosystems.

faculty.name: Darcy McRose
faculty.title: Thomas D. and Virginia W. Cabot Career Development Professor
faculty.summary: Uses secondary metabolites to understand microbial ecology, biogeochemistry, and plant-microbe interactions.

faculty.name: Michael Follows
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Models marine ecosystems to understand relationships between microbial biodiversity and carbon and nutrient cycling in the ocean.

faculty.name: David L. Des Marais
faculty.title: Amgen Career Development Professor, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses molecular, quantitative, and population genetics to identify the physiological and genetic basis of plant response to environmental cues.

faculty.name: Serguei Saavedra
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the feasibility of observing the emergence, transformation, and regeneration of ecological systems.

programs.program_name: MIT-WHOI Joint Program in Oceanography / Applied Ocean Science and Engineering (PhD/ScD)

funding.note: Admitted with full funding (stipend, health insurance, tuition)

faculty.name: Raffaele Ferrari
faculty.title: Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer building next-generation numerical models to understand ocean circulation and its role in climate.

faculty.name: Glenn R. Flierl
faculty.title: Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Oceanographer exploring planetary-scale turbulence and the fluid dynamics of atmospheres and oceans.

faculty.name: John Marshall
faculty.title: Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Oceanographer known for breakthroughs in modeling ocean circulation and for insights into the role of the oceans in climate.

faculty.name: Michael Follows
faculty.title: Professor, MIT EAPS; Director, MIT-WHOI Joint Program
faculty.summary: Models marine ecosystems to understand relationships between microbial biodiversity and carbon and nutrient cycling in the ocean.

faculty.name: Andrew Babbin
faculty.title: Associate Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Marine biogeochemist studying microbial communities and their chemical interactions, including nutrient and nitrogen cycling in the ocean.

faculty.name: Kristin Bergmann
faculty.title: Associate Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Reconstructs early Earth environmental changes through the analysis of sedimentary and carbonate records.

faculty.name: Tanja Bosak
faculty.title: Professor, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Experimental geobiologist exploring modern processes in microbial systems to interpret the early Earth record.

faculty.name: Stephanie Dutkiewicz
faculty.title: Senior Research Scientist, MIT Department of Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences (EAPS)
faculty.summary: Biogeochemist studying the marine plankton ecosystem using numerical simulations of ocean biogeochemistry.

faculty.name: Pierre Lermusiaux
faculty.title: Professor, MIT Department of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops models and methods for multiscale ocean modeling, uncertainty quantification, data assimilation, and the guidance of autonomous vehicles, applied to physical, acoustic, and biological ocean dynamics.

faculty.name: Otto Cordero
faculty.title: Associate Professor, MIT Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies how social and ecological interactions at micro-scales impact the productivity, stability, and evolutionary dynamics of marine microbial ecosystems.

faculty.name: Amy S. Bower
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Physical Oceanography Department
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer investigating large-scale ocean circulation and thermohaline transport, deploying subsurface floats to study deep currents in regions such as the North Atlantic and Arctic.

faculty.name: J. Thomas Farrar
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Physical Oceanography Department
faculty.summary: Investigates atmosphere-ocean exchange of heat and freshwater and upper-ocean dynamics, leading research on ocean observing systems, satellite oceanography, and submesoscale processes.

faculty.name: Ken O. Buesseler
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Marine Chemistry and Geochemistry Department
faculty.summary: Marine chemist specializing in natural and man-made radionuclides in the ocean and the ocean's biological carbon pump; directs the WHOI Center for Marine and Environmental Radioactivity.

faculty.name: Christopher German
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Department of Geology and Geophysics
faculty.summary: Investigates geologic controls on seafloor fluid flow at vents and seeps and how hydrothermal activity influences ocean chemistry and chemosynthetic ecosystems, with applications to deep-ocean exploration.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Kujawinski
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Marine Chemistry and Geochemistry Department
faculty.summary: Studies the structural characterization of dissolved organic matter and how microorganisms transform organic compounds in aquatic environments at the molecular level.

faculty.name: Jeffrey P. Donnelly
faculty.title: Senior Scientist, WHOI Department of Geology and Geophysics
faculty.sub_field: Marine geology / coastal systems and paleoclimate
faculty.summary: Studies how climate change has altered terrestrial and coastal systems, reconstructing long-term hurricane activity and coastal change from sediment cores to project future responses.

programs.program_name: Ph.D. in Environmental Health Sciences (tracks: Exposure Assessment & Health Effects; Molecular Toxicology/Carcinogenesis; Systemic Toxicology)

funding.note: Fully funded via MacCracken (full tuition + stipend + health insurance, 4-5 yrs + 3 summers)

faculty.name: Max Costa
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine; Professor, Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Pharmacology
faculty.summary: Costa investigates the molecular mechanisms of metal carcinogenesis, focusing on how nickel and cadmium ions affect histone demethylases and epigenetic regulation, leading to cellular transformation and cancer.

faculty.name: Suresh Cuddapah
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Cuddapah studies epigenetic regulation of chromatin structure and gene expression programs during differentiation and disease, including tumor suppressor gene silencing and the epigenetic effects of heavy metals.

faculty.name: Wei Dai
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine; Professor, Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Pharmacology
faculty.summary: Dai's research focuses on the surveillance mechanisms that regulate cell cycle progression and how defects in these checkpoints contribute to genomic instability and cancer development.

faculty.name: Terry Gordon
faculty.title: Research Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Gordon's research program employs human and animal models to study susceptibility factors, including genetics, age, and gender, underlying the adverse pulmonary and cardiac effects of environmental and occupational air pollutants.

faculty.name: Chunyuan Jin
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Jin investigates how environmental exposures such as aldehydes and heavy metals compromise chromatin assembly pathways, with emphasis on histone variants and their roles in development, aging, and cancer.

faculty.name: Catherine G. Klein
faculty.title: Research Associate Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Using molecular biologic and cytogenetic techniques, Klein studies the mutagenic and epigenetic mechanisms that silence gene expression leading to cancer and other diseases, including effects of metals, formaldehyde, and e-cigarettes. She is listed as an advisor in the Environmental Health Sciences Graduate Program.

faculty.name: Angelica Ortiz
faculty.title: Research Assistant Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Ortiz studies extracellular vesicles as biomarkers and mediators of inflammatory diseases, carcinogenesis, and metastasis, examining how toxic metal exposure alters vesicle composition to influence cancer progression.

faculty.name: Hong Sun
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Sun examines epigenetic regulation of cell proliferation, differentiation, and malignant transformation by environmental toxic metals, with current work on hexavalent chromium's effects on histone modification enzymes and carcinogenesis.

faculty.name: Moon-Shong Tang
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine; Professor, Department of Pathology
faculty.summary: Tang studies the mechanisms by which environmental agents such as tobacco smoke and sunlight induce carcinogenesis, with recent work on the health effects of electronic-cigarette aerosols, nicotine, and cotinine.

faculty.name: George D. Thurston
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine; Professor, Department of Population Health
faculty.summary: Thurston investigates the human health effects of exposure to air pollutants including particulate matter and ozone, encompassing PM2.5 mortality research, source apportionment, and the global burden of disease from outdoor air pollution.

faculty.name: Arul Veerappan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Veerappan's laboratory investigates environmental and occupational exposures using human and animal models, including World Trade Center and ambient particulate matter, wildfire, heavy metals, and endocrine disruptors, and their pulmonary, cardiac, renal, and vascular effects.

faculty.name: Judith T. Zelikoff
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Medicine
faculty.summary: Zelikoff's laboratory focuses on the effects of inhaled toxicants on immune system function and the reproductive process, including studies of particulate matter, nanoparticles, cigarette smoke, and alternative tobacco products.

faculty.name: Maurizio Porfiri
faculty.title: Institute Professor; Interim Chair, Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering; Director, Center for Urban Science + Progress
faculty.summary: Institute Professor with appointments in Mechanical and Aerospace, Biomedical, and Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering, and director of CUSP and the Urban Institute. He conducts and supervises research on complex systems, with applications ranging from mechanics to behavior, public health, and robotics.

faculty.name: Magued Iskander
faculty.title: Professor; Senior Director of Strategic Initiatives
faculty.summary: Professor of Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering whose research covers modeling transparent soil-structure interaction and flow, sustainable piling made of recycled polymers, geotechnical instrumentation, foundation engineering, high strain rate behavior of soils, and offshore and marine geotechnology foundations.

faculty.name: Mohamad Awada
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor integrating sensing systems, immersive technologies, and artificial intelligence to enhance human livability by optimizing the design and operation of built environments. He directs the Smart Innovations in Built Environment Research (SIBER) Lab, with work spanning healthy and sustainable infrastructure and biosensing solutions on construction sites.

faculty.name: Semiha Ergan
faculty.title: Professor; Associate Department Chair
faculty.summary: Professor and Associate Chair of Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering with a courtesy appointment in Computer Science and Engineering. Her research spans AR/VR, BIM and digital twins, computer vision/AI/ML, data analytics and visualization, and building/urban informatics.

faculty.name: Chen Feng
faculty.title: Institute Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Institute Associate Professor affiliated with Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, and Computer Science and Engineering, directing the AI4CE lab. His group develops algorithms and systems for intelligent agents to understand and interact with dynamic, unstructured environments, addressing infrastructure challenges in construction robotics, manufacturing automation, and autonomous vehicles.

faculty.name: Masoud Ghandehari
faculty.title: Professor of Urban Systems Engineering
faculty.summary: Professor of Urban Systems Engineering whose work focuses on climate resilience of infrastructure systems using sensing, data acquisition, and modeling, aimed at a fundamental understanding of the health and performance of the built environment. Research interests include infrastructure asset management and data-driven characterization of systems interdependencies.

faculty.name: Debra Laefer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering and CUSP, leading the NYU Urban Modeling Group. Her research spans urban informatics, remote sensing, geospatial data management, geotechnical engineering, and disaster mitigation, including technical solutions for protecting architecturally significant buildings from sub-surface construction.

faculty.name: Bridger Ruyle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor studying how human activity, the biosphere, and climate change affect water quality, including aquatic contaminant biogeochemistry and mechanistic and statistical modeling. His lab develops tools to quantify chemical contamination such as fluorinated chemicals (PFAS) and pharmaceuticals and integrates remote sensing data to assess climate impacts on water systems.

faculty.name: Andrea Silverman
faculty.title: Institute Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Institute Associate Professor in Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering and CUSP whose research focuses on water quality, wastewater treatment, and urban flooding, with an overarching goal to protect public health and environmental quality. Specific areas include detection and disinfection of waterborne pathogens, wastewater-based epidemiology, and design of natural wastewater treatment systems; she co-directs the FloodNet project.

faculty.name: Eleonora Maria Tronci
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering and CUSP. Her research focuses on structural damage assessment using machine learning, multi-domain transfer learning, offshore wind structure performance, and structural informatics.

faculty.name: Omar Wani
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor whose group combines statistical and computational methods to study the dynamics of hydroclimatic risk in built and natural environments, which are often interconnected and coupled systems. Research interests include statistical hydrosciences, urban water, and fluvial geomorphology.

faculty.name: Tarek Abdoun
faculty.title: Global Network Professor, NYU Tandon; Professor and Program Head of Civil and Urban Engineering, NYU Abu Dhabi
faculty.summary: Global Network Professor of Civil and Urban Engineering at NYU Tandon and Program Head at NYU Abu Dhabi. His research covers geotechnical systems and soil-structure interaction, soil dynamics and earthquake engineering, geo-centrifuge and full-scale testing, and AI sensor-based infrastructure health monitoring.

faculty.name: Kemal Celik
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil Engineering, NYU Abu Dhabi; Global Network Assistant Professor, NYU Tandon
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor at NYU Abu Dhabi and Global Network Assistant Professor at NYU Tandon, directing the AMBER Lab. His work centers on construction materials and building energy efficiency to develop sustainable and multi-functional structural materials, including low-carbon cements and alkali-activated cementitious materials.

faculty.name: Mostafa Mobasher
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Urban Engineering, NYU Abu Dhabi; Associated Faculty, NYU Tandon
faculty.summary: Professor at NYU Abu Dhabi leading the Computational Solid Mechanics Lab. His research focuses on modeling fracture and multi-physical response of manmade and natural materials across spatial and temporal scales, integrating machine learning and AI with computational methods such as the finite element method, with applications in infrastructure resilience and energy.

faculty.name: Rita Leal Sousa
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering. Her research interests include tunneling automation, natural threat risk assessment, soil erosion, and stochastic modeling.

faculty.name: Borja Garcia de Soto
faculty.title: Associate Professor, NYU Abu Dhabi; Global Network Associate Professor, NYU Tandon
faculty.summary: Associate Professor at NYU Abu Dhabi and Global Network Associate Professor in Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering at NYU Tandon, leading the S.M.A.R.T. Construction Research Group. His research covers construction automation and robotics, digital twins for the built environment, AEC cybersecurity, AI and multimodal LLMs in construction, lean project delivery, and BIM.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Hénaff
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Technology, Culture and Society (affiliated with Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering)
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in Technology, Culture and Society and CUSP, affiliated with the Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering department, leading the Laboratory for Living Interfaces. Her work explores the way living beings interact with their environment, with recent emphasis on microbial communities in urban settings.

faculty.name: Kaan Ozbay
faculty.title: Professor; Director of C2SMART (Tier 1 USDOT University Transportation Center)
faculty.sub_field: Traffic simulation, sensing, and real-time traffic control
faculty.summary: Research interests include simulation models, advanced technology and sensing applications, traffic incident and emergency management, feedback-based online real-time traffic control techniques, operations research, humanitarian inventory control, and transportation economics. He directs C2SMART, a Tier 1 University Transportation Center funded by USDOT.

faculty.name: Joseph Chow
faculty.title: Institute Associate Professor; Deputy Director of C2SMART Center
faculty.sub_field: Multimodal transportation systems and behavioral urban logistics
faculty.summary: His expertise lies in transportation systems, with emphasis on multimodal networks, behavioral urban logistics, smart cities, and transport economics. He heads BUILT@NYU, the Behavioral Urban Informatics, Logistics, and Transport Laboratory.

faculty.name: Linda Ng Boyle
faculty.title: Vice Dean for Research; Professor of Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research interests include driving behavior, crash countermeasures, crash and safety analysis, and statistical modeling. She previously served as Professor and Chair of Industrial and Systems Engineering at the University of Washington and holds an NSF CAREER Award.

faculty.name: Daniel Vignon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Transportation economics and emerging mobility services
faculty.summary: His work focuses on informing the design, regulation, and operation of emerging mobility services and smart infrastructure systems, combining engineering and economics to model their interactions with markets, study their impact on social welfare, and design policies to regulate them. He leads FEATS, the laboratory For the Economic Analysis of Transportation Systems.

faculty.name: Eugene Vinitsky
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Reinforcement learning for autonomous vehicles and traffic control
faculty.summary: Research interests are reinforcement learning, autonomous vehicles, and traffic control, centering on enabling complex, human-like behavior to emerge through unsupervised multi-agent learning with applications to autonomous vehicle operation.

faculty.name: Samer Madanat
faculty.title: Dean of Engineering, NYU Abu Dhabi; Xenel Distinguished Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Research interests are transportation infrastructure management and transportation systems analysis, including modeling facility performance, optimal management policies under uncertainty, and sustainable transportation solutions.

faculty.name: Zhibin Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Engineering, NYU Shanghai; Global Network Assistant Professor, NYU
faculty.summary: Research interests are transportation network modeling and optimization, intelligent transportation systems, and discrete optimization.

faculty.name: Saif Eddin Jabari
faculty.title: Associate Professor, NYU Abu Dhabi; Global Network Associate Professor, NYU
faculty.sub_field: Traffic flow theory and urban network traffic management
faculty.summary: Research interests include traffic flow theory, algorithms for urban network traffic management, and applied probability and statistical mechanics, focusing on uncertainty modeling and emergent phenomena with applications to traffic state estimation, prediction, distributed control, and cybersecurity.

faculty.name: Monica Menendez
faculty.title: Professor, NYU Abu Dhabi; Global Network Professor of Civil, Urban and Environmental Engineering, NYU
faculty.sub_field: Multimodal transportation systems monitoring and control
faculty.summary: Her research focuses on monitoring, modeling, and control of multimodal transportation systems, considering new technologies and data sources. She is Director and Lead PI of the NYUAD Research Center for Interacting Urban Networks (CITIES).

faculty.name: Ali Diabat
faculty.title: Professor, NYU Abu Dhabi; Global Network Professor, NYU
faculty.summary: Research interests are logistics and supply chain management, within the Department of Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering.

faculty.name: Zhong-Ping Jiang
faculty.title: Institute Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering; affiliated transportation faculty, Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Learning-based control for connected and autonomous vehicles
faculty.summary: Research covers stability theory; robust, adaptive, and distributed nonlinear control; robust adaptive dynamic programming; and reinforcement learning, with applications including connected and autonomous vehicles.
