He is listed as affiliated faculty on the department's transportation faculty page and leads the Control and Networks (CAN) Lab.

faculty.name: Maurizio Porfiri
faculty.title: Institute Professor; Interim Chair, Civil, Urban, and Environmental Engineering; Director, Center for Urban Science + Progress (CUSP)
faculty.sub_field: Urban science and complex systems (mobility networks)
faculty.summary: Research interests include collective behavior, complex systems, network science, theoretical mechanics, and urban science; his profile highlights work on how factors like income inequality, poverty, and housing costs shape commuting flows between counties. He directs the Dynamical Systems Laboratory and the Center for Urban Science + Progress.

faculty.name: Akram N. Alshawabkeh
faculty.title: George A. Snell Professor of Engineering and University Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Alshawabkeh works on geoenvironmental engineering, including soil and groundwater remediation and the transport and remediation of subsurface contaminants. He leads the PROTECT center studying environmental exposures and health.

faculty.name: R. Edward Beighley
faculty.title: COE Distinguished Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Beighley researches hydrologic and hydraulic modeling, remote sensing of water, and flood hazard assessment across watershed to continental scales. His work supports water resources management and flood risk analysis.

faculty.name: Qin "Jim" Chen
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Civil and Environmental Engineering & Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Chen develops and applies advanced numerical models for coastal resiliency and sustainability, addressing hurricane impacts, storm surge, and nature-based solutions for sea-level rise. His group focuses on adaptive coastal intelligence.

faculty.name: Enrique M. del Castillo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Del Castillo studies computational geomechanics and the behavior of granular materials and flows. His work applies computational methods to understand the mechanics of geomaterials.

faculty.name: Matthew J. Eckelman
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair for Faculty, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Eckelman applies life cycle assessment to evaluate sustainable production, materials, and energy systems. His research quantifies the environmental impacts of infrastructure, technologies, and human systems.

faculty.name: Loretta A. Fernandez
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Civil and Environmental Engineering & Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Fernandez studies environmental organic chemistry, passive sampling methods, and the transport and transformation of contaminants in built and natural environments. Recent work includes passive samplers to detect degraded compounds from unexploded ordnance on the seafloor.

faculty.name: Auroop R. Ganguly
faculty.title: College of Engineering Distinguished Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Ganguly develops physics-integrated data science for weather, climate, and infrastructure, with emphasis on climate extremes and resilience. His work combines physical modeling with machine learning for environmental and infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: Khaled Ghannam
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Ghannam studies boundary-layer meteorology and air quality modeling, focusing on turbulent transport and land-atmosphere interactions. His work informs understanding of atmospheric processes affecting air quality.

faculty.name: Philip Larese-Casanova
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Larese-Casanova investigates the transformation processes of water pollutants and aquatic geochemistry. His research examines the chemical reactions that govern the fate of contaminants in water.

faculty.name: Shang Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Liu studies the origin and transport of air pollutants and aerosol chemistry. His research addresses the chemical processes shaping atmospheric particulate matter and air quality.

faculty.name: Yu (Rain) Miao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, jointly appointed in Civil and Environmental Engineering & Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Miao researches environmental biotechnology for groundwater remediation, wastewater treatment, and ecological health, combining meta-omics and machine learning with single-microbe experiments. Her work spans microbial ecology principles across multiple scales.

faculty.name: Amy Mueller
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Civil and Environmental Engineering & Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Mueller develops sensor technologies and physics-informed machine learning for environmental monitoring, including ultra-low-cost monitoring of urban water systems. Her lab works at the intersection of engineering, science, and the environment.

faculty.name: Samuel Munoz
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Munoz studies hydrological extremes and their links to the natural and built environment, reconstructing environmental change over hundreds to thousands of years from sedimentary records. His group combines geological, instrumental, and historical data in statistical and computational models of flood risk.

faculty.name: Cristina Schultz
faculty.title: Foley Family Assistant Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Schultz investigates how climate change affects ocean biogeochemistry using regional ocean models, Earth System models, and observational data. Her work focuses on feedbacks among ocean ecosystems, sediment, and circulation and their influence on carbon and oxygen cycles.

faculty.name: Aron Stubbins
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences, Chemistry and Chemical Biology & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Stubbins studies the natural carbon cycle and human alterations to it, with a focus on plastics as both a novel component of the carbon cycle and an environmental pollutant. His work examines how microplastics affect ocean biogeochemical processes.

faculty.name: Yang Zhang
faculty.title: COE Distinguished Professor and Associate Chair for Research, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Zhang works on air quality, atmospheric chemistry, and climate modeling, including health impact assessment. Her research develops and applies chemical transport and coupled climate-air quality models.

faculty.name: Mark Patterson
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Patterson directs the Field Robotics Laboratory, designing autonomous underwater vehicles and sensors for marine research, and studies environmental fluid mechanics and the physical biology of marine organisms. His work also addresses infrastructure sensing.

faculty.name: Serena Alexander
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in School of Public Policy and Urban Affairs & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Alexander researches climate change, sustainable and resilient transportation systems, and environmental policy. Her work connects engineering with public policy for sustainable urban systems.

faculty.name: Jerome F. Hajjar
faculty.title: CDM Smith Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering (Interim Dean, College of Engineering)
faculty.summary: Hajjar researches sustainable steel and composite structures and earthquake engineering. His work advances the design and behavior of resilient structural systems.

faculty.name: Andrew T. Myers
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair for Graduate Studies, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Myers develops probabilistic simulation methods for resilient and sustainable structures, including offshore wind infrastructure. His research addresses structural reliability under environmental hazards.

faculty.name: Mehrdad Sasani
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Sasani researches progressive collapse of structures, earthquake engineering, and building resilience. His work examines the robustness of buildings under extreme loading.

faculty.name: Qi "Ryan" Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Vice Chair for Research, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Wang studies urban and social resilience, disaster response, and urban computing. His research uses large-scale data to understand and improve the resilience of cities and communities.

faculty.name: Haris Koutsopoulos
faculty.title: COE Distinguished Professor and Associate Chair for Undergraduate Studies, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Koutsopoulos researches urban transportation networks and public transit operations. His work uses data-driven methods to model and improve transportation systems.

funding.note: Assistantships, stipend positions, and fellowships (per College of Science)

faculty.name: Geoffrey C. Trussell
faculty.title: Chair and Doherty Professor of Marine and Environmental Sciences; Director, Marine Science Center and Coastal Sustainability Institute
faculty.summary: Trussell studies evolutionary, community, and ecosystem ecology, emphasizing the significance of predation risk and the evolution of phenotypic plasticity and inducible defenses. His work spans rocky intertidal shores and freshwater systems and examines climate change impacts on food webs.

faculty.name: Jennifer Bowen
faculty.title: Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Bowen investigates how human activities alter microbial communities in coastal environments, including nutrient impacts on salt marshes and effects of oyster aquaculture on microorganisms. Her work explores how microbial communities can help ameliorate pollution.

faculty.name: Jonathan Grabowski
faculty.title: Professor and Assistant Director, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Grabowski studies ecological dynamics in estuarine and marine ecosystems, examining how habitat structure and predation shape populations of economically important species such as lobster and cod. His research spans habitat restoration, fisheries management, and climate-driven range shifts.

faculty.name: Brian Helmuth
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & School of Public Policy and Urban Affairs
faculty.summary: Helmuth predicts the ecological impacts of climate change on coastal ecosystems and the people who depend on them. His lab uses sensor technology, physiological experiments, and ecological mapping to assess organism vulnerability and inform marine spatial planning.

faculty.name: Tarik Gouhier
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Gouhier uses mathematical modeling to study how processes across organizational scales interact to shape species distributions. His work focuses on population dynamics to predict and reduce vulnerability to environmental change.

faculty.name: Randall Hughes
faculty.title: Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Hughes investigates the ecological and social causes and consequences of biodiversity change, applying these insights to conservation and restoration of marine systems. Her work spans coastal ecosystems from West Coast eelgrass beds to East Coast salt marshes.

faculty.name: Katie Lotterhos
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Lotterhos uses eco-evolutionary genomics to understand how climate has shaped biodiversity and how climate change will affect it. Her work combines theoretical and experimental approaches with novel statistical methods, focusing on marine invertebrate responses to ocean acidification and fisheries population dynamics.

faculty.name: Justin Ries
faculty.title: Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Ries investigates oceanic change across geologic timescales, including global climate change, paleoceanography, biomineralization, and carbon sequestration. He combines field studies with laboratory experiments to explore biogeochemical processes affecting ocean conditions.

faculty.name: David Kimbro
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Kimbro studies population dynamics, community ecology, invasion biology, ecosystem science, and coastal oceanography to understand why coastal habitats change and how those shifts affect human communities.

faculty.name: Remy Gatins
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Gatins investigates the ecological and evolutionary processes shaping marine biodiversity at genomic, population, and species levels. Her focus areas include genetic connectivity in marine fishes, genomic signatures of speciation and hybridization, and climate-driven range expansions.

faculty.name: Chengfei He
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: He is a climate modeler investigating the causes of past and future changes in oceanic and atmospheric circulation and climate variability. His work uses climate models, isotope-enabled modeling, statistical and machine learning methods, and paleoclimate data.

faculty.name: Samuel Munoz
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Munoz studies hydrological extremes and their links to natural and built environments, reconstructing environmental change over hundreds to thousands of years from sedimentary records. His group combines geological, instrumental, and historical data in statistical and computational models of flood risk.

faculty.name: Cristina Schultz
faculty.title: Foley Family Assistant Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Schultz investigates how climate change affects ocean biogeochemistry using regional ocean models, Earth System models, and observational data. Her work focuses on feedbacks among ocean ecosystems, sediment, and circulation and their influence on carbon and oxygen cycles.

faculty.name: Aron Stubbins
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences, Chemistry and Chemical Biology & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Stubbins studies the natural carbon cycle and human alterations to it, focusing on plastics as both a novel component of the carbon cycle and an environmental pollutant. His work examines how microplastics affect ocean biogeochemical processes.

faculty.name: Amy Mueller
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Mueller develops sensor technologies and physics-informed machine learning for environmental monitoring, including ultra-low-cost monitoring of urban water systems. Her lab works at the intersection of engineering, science, and the environment.

faculty.name: Mark Patterson
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Patterson directs the Field Robotics Laboratory, designing autonomous underwater vehicles and sensors for marine research, and studies the physical biology of marine invertebrates, plants, and fishes. His work includes coral resilience under environmental stress and machine learning for underwater imagery.

faculty.name: Dan Distel
faculty.title: Research Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences; Director, Ocean Genome Legacy Center
faculty.summary: Distel studies the evolution, physiology, genomics, and metabolism of marine bacteria and bacteria-animal symbioses, particularly in bivalve mollusks such as shipworms. As director of the Ocean Genome Legacy Center he maintains a public-access DNA bank for marine biodiversity research.

faculty.name: Yu (Rain) Miao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Miao researches environmental biotechnology for groundwater remediation, wastewater treatment, and ecological health, combining meta-omics and machine learning with single-microbe experiments. Her work addresses microbial ecology principles across scales including saltmarsh ecosystems.

faculty.name: Loretta Fernandez
faculty.title: Associate Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Fernandez studies environmental organic chemistry, passive sampling methods, and the transport and transformation of contaminants in built and natural environments. Recent work includes passive samplers to detect degraded compounds from unexploded ordnance on the seafloor.

faculty.name: Peter "James" Dennedy-Frank
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Dennedy-Frank studies watershed management that benefits both people and nature, focusing on ecohydrological systems and how water flows through watersheds. He uses computational simulation at varying complexity to work with colleagues and stakeholders on watershed function.

faculty.name: Qin "Jim" Chen
faculty.title: Professor, jointly appointed in Marine and Environmental Sciences & Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Chen develops and applies advanced numerical models for coastal resiliency and sustainability, addressing hurricane impacts, storm surge, and nature-based solutions for sea-level rise.

faculty.name: Malcolm Hill
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Hill conducts geochemical analysis of igneous and metamorphic rocks using trace element and isotopic (strontium and neodymium) methods. His research spans regions from Alaska's accretionary prism to California's Sierra Nevada and South African granites.

faculty.name: Damon Hall
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Director, MS Environmental Science & Policy, Marine and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Hall examines how environmental governance failures stem from misidentifying social problems as technical ones, developing frameworks around social-ecological system resonance. His empirical work centers on the long-term Yellowstone River Cultural Inventory.

funding.note: Financial support packages begin in fall quarter

faculty.name: Ludmilla Aristilde
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Chemical and Biological Engineering (by courtesy); Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences (by
faculty.summary: Aristilde combines experimental and computational tools in chemistry and biochemistry to gain molecular insights into natural mineral and biological catalysts in environmental organic processes, with work on cellular carbon metabolism in environmental bacteria, extracellular enzyme dynamics, and the physical chemistry of organic contaminants.

faculty.name: Oluwaseyi Balogun
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Balogun's group studies the propagation of elastic vibrations in materials using optical and scanning probe techniques to characterize thermal conductivity, vibrational properties, and elastic properties of thin films, interfaces, and biological tissues, and to understand coupling of optical and acoustic oscillations in nanostructures.

faculty.name: Zdeněk Bažant
faculty.title: McCormick Institute Professor and Walter P. Murphy Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Mechanical Engineering and Materials Scien
faculty.summary: Bažant works on interdisciplinary problems in the mechanics of solids and structures, including nonlinear fracture mechanics, size effects and scaling of failure, damage micromechanics, creep in nanoporous materials, hydraulic fracturing of shale, and probabilistic mechanics of quasibrittle structures.

faculty.name: Neal Blair
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Blair studies the behavior of carbon on the Earth's surface, focusing on pathways of organic carbon decomposition and sequestration mediated by biological and physical processes in soils and sediments, integrated into conceptual models of carbon transformation across landscapes and the seafloor.

faculty.name: Giuseppe Buscarnera
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Buscarnera works on constitutive modeling of geomaterials, mechanics of unsaturated soils, chemo-mechanics of porous rocks, material stability theory, fracture and crushing in granular materials, and applications to natural hazards, slope stability, and landslide forecasting.

faculty.name: Giorgia Chinazzo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director of the Architectural Engineering and Design Program
faculty.summary: Chinazzo's work focuses on intelligent buildings that sense, adapt, and improve occupant quality of life while reducing environmental impact, blending engineering, architecture, data science, and health and social sciences to study how buildings influence people's health, well-being, and behavior.

faculty.name: Federico Ciardo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Ciardo's research interests span geomechanics and applied mechanics for geophysical, geomechanical, and engineering problems, with an emphasis on computational mechanics.

faculty.name: Gianluca Cusatis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Cusatis works on the mechanics of infrastructure materials and constitutive modeling of concrete and cementitious composites, developing multiscale computational models for their deformation and failure.

faculty.name: Pablo Durango-Cohen
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Durango-Cohen develops and analyzes optimization and econometric models to support monitoring, management, and operation of transportation infrastructure systems, and has published in transportation economics and policy and life-cycle assessment, including work on decarbonization of freight transportation.

faculty.name: Jean-François Gaillard
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Gaillard's group works to understand the molecular and biogeochemical processes affecting the fate of metals in aquatic systems, emphasizing how chemical speciation controls bioavailability, using field studies, laboratory experiments, and spectroscopic modeling of metals such as As, Ni, and Hg.

faculty.name: Kimberly Gray
faculty.title: Stanley F. Pepper Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Chemical and Biological Engineering (by courtesy)
faculty.summary: Gray's expertise is in environmental chemistry and applied ecology in natural and engineered systems, studying TiO2-based nanocomposites for resource recovery and water/air treatment, nature-based stormwater strategies, ecotoxicology of nanomaterials, and resilient design of urban systems in response to climate change.

faculty.name: Erica Hartmann
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Medicine (Pulmonary Division)
faculty.summary: Hartmann's research investigates at the molecular level how microbial communities respond to anthropogenic chemicals, using field sampling, shotgun DNA sequencing, and laboratory model systems to control the spread of undesirable traits such as antibiotic resistance in the built environment.

faculty.name: Jackson Jewett
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Jewett's work focuses on computational design for the built environment, exploring how optimization methods improve material utilization in building-scale structures under construction constraints, complemented by physical experiments and novel digital fabrication methods for civil engineering applications.

faculty.name: Sinan Keten
faculty.title: Jan and Marcia Achenbach Professor; Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Biomedical Engineering
faculty.summary: Keten's group establishes theory, multiscale simulation, and machine learning approaches to understand the mechanical behavior of soft materials, focusing on nanostructured polymers, biomolecular building blocks such as proteins, and bioinspired systems, to accelerate design of materials for infrastructure, manufacturing, and therapeutics.

faculty.name: Yu (Marco) Nie
faculty.title: Roxelyn and Richard Pepper Family Chair in Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Nie studies how to understand, predict, and improve the behavior of transportation networks, formulating design and control strategies for mobility, reliability, and sustainability using tools from optimization, network science, traffic flow theory, and economics, including routing algorithms and Pareto-improving congestion pricing.

faculty.name: Aaron Packman
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and (by courtesy) Mechanical Engineering and Chemical and Biological Engineering
faculty.summary: Packman's research focuses on water, sediments, and microorganisms, particularly the intersection of physical transport with biological and biogeochemical processes, studying environmental interfaces, surface-groundwater interactions, and biofilms with applications to water sustainability, urban flooding, and contaminant transport.

faculty.name: Alessandro Rotta Loria
faculty.title: Louis Berger Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Rotta Loria's work lies at the intersection of mechanics, energy, and electrochemistry focused on the subsurface, using theoretical, computational, and experimental studies to understand the impacts of energy transfers on geological and granular materials, addressing subsurface urban heat islands, geological energy storage, and sustainable construction.

faculty.name: John Rudnicki
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Rudnicki studies inelastic behavior and failure of geomaterials, particularly deformation instabilities in brittle rocks and granular media and their interactions with pore fluids, with applications to fault instability, earthquake energy radiation, and resource-related geomechanics.

faculty.name: Amanda Stathopoulos
faculty.title: William Patterson Junior Professor; Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Stathopoulos's research examines human-centered sustainable mobility, studying how lifestyles, values, and priorities of travelers and decision-makers shape adoption of sustainable transportation practices, using discrete choice modeling, statistical analysis, qualitative fieldwork, and large-scale surveys.

faculty.name: Lucia Stein-Montalvo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Stein-Montalvo's research focuses on the mechanics of shape-shifting, compelling structures to change configuration on demand or in response to environmental stimuli, bridging fundamental studies of nonlinear deformations and instabilities with adaptive architectural solutions for the built environment.

faculty.name: George Wells
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Wells works on environmental biotechnology and microbial ecology of engineered and impacted natural systems, including microbial nitrogen and phosphorus cycling, shortcut biological nutrient removal, sustainable wastewater treatment, resource and energy recovery from waste, and mathematical modeling of biofilms and bioprocesses.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition, health plan, and base stipend for all Ph.D. students throughout regular enrollment

faculty.name: Sigrid Adriaenssens
faculty.title: Ritter Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director, Program in Mechanics, Materials and Structures
faculty.summary: Adriaenssens's research focuses on lightweight surface systems and how they can be optimized and realized to interact with extreme structural or environmental loading, spanning mechanics, materials, and structures.

faculty.name: Joshua Atkinson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Omenn-Darling Bioengineering Institute
faculty.summary: Atkinson's lab uses synthetic biology, protein engineering, biophysics, and electrochemistry to understand and control how microbes and proteins transport electrons, engineering biotechnologies for sustainability, environmental monitoring and remediation, chemical synthesis, and resource recovery.

faculty.name: Elie Bou-Zeid
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Bou-Zeid integrates theory, numerical simulations, and experimental observations to study flow and transport in the atmospheric boundary layer, with applications to the built environment, energy efficiency in buildings, urban sensor networks, wind energy, and surface-atmosphere exchange over polar sea ice.

faculty.name: Ian Bourg
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Bourg examines the fundamental properties of water at interfaces and their impact on environmental systems, including the effect of clay minerals on hydrology and mechanics of sedimentary media, mineral-organic interactions in contaminant fate and soil carbon storage, and hygroscopicity of natural systems.

faculty.name: Maria Garlock
faculty.title: Daniel Tsui Professor in Engineering; Co-Director, Program in Architecture and Engineering; Head of Forbes College
faculty.sub_field: Resilient structural design for extreme loads
faculty.summary: Garlock's scholarship focuses on creative and resilient structural design for extreme loads such as fires, earthquakes, and storm surges, studying these hazards both as isolated and cascading multi-hazard events while bridging academia and engineering practice.

faculty.name: Branko Glisic
faculty.title: Chair and Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Glisic works in structural health monitoring, smart structures, and heritage structures, developing advanced sensing technologies (fiber optic sensors, sensing sheets, RF-tags, GPR) and data-analysis methods for structural and material identification and prediction across bridges, buildings, pipelines, and historical monuments.

faculty.name: Jürgen Hackl
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Hackl's research lies at the interface of network science and complex infrastructure systems, focusing on spatial-temporal networks, uncertainty quantification, digital twin technologies, and intelligent risk and resilience assessment of cities under climate change.

faculty.name: Peter Jaffé
faculty.title: William L. Knapp '47 Professor of Civil Engineering; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Jaffé's research addresses the physical, chemical, and biological processes governing transport and transformation of pollutants in the environment and their application to remediation of contaminated systems, including biogeochemical reaction-transport in soils and sediments.

faculty.name: Ryan Kingsbury
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Andlinger Center for Energy and the Environment
faculty.summary: Kingsbury's research accelerates development of electrochemical technologies for clean water and energy by advancing understanding of ion-selective materials such as membranes and electrodes, seeking a molecular-level understanding of ion selectivity to engineer higher-performing materials for desalination, fuel cells, and flow batteries.

faculty.name: Liuchi Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Li's research focuses on the mechanics and physics of complex material systems involving multi-body contact, friction, and fracture, developing computational and experimental approaches to characterize macroscopic behavior of granular media and crack propagation in heterogeneous materials for resilient infrastructure.

faculty.name: Ning Lin
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director of Graduate Studies; Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Lin's research areas include natural hazards and risk analysis, wind engineering, coastal engineering, and climate change impact and adaptation, with a primary focus on hurricane risk analysis integrating science, engineering, and policy to study weather extremes and mitigation.

faculty.name: Jyotirmoy Mandal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Mandal's research involves understanding and controlling nano-to-macro scale radiative heat flows in natural environments and artificial surfaces, at the intersection of optics and materials science, designing scalable materials that radiatively thermoregulate to make human environments more sustainable and climate resilient.

faculty.name: Denise Mauzerall
faculty.title: William S. Tod Professor of Public and International Affairs and Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Air quality, energy and climate policy
faculty.summary: Mauzerall analyzes the impacts of energy technology choices on air quality, public health, food security, and greenhouse gas mitigation, conducting policy-relevant research using regional and global atmospheric chemistry/climate models and atmospheric, agricultural, and epidemiological data.

faculty.name: Reed Maxwell
faculty.title: William and Edna Macaleer Professor of Engineering and Applied Science; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the High Meadows Environmental Inst
faculty.summary: Maxwell's research centers on understanding how much terrestrial freshwater exists and how fast it is being replenished or depleted, focusing on groundwater, evapotranspiration, and snow, and connections within the hydrologic cycle under anthropogenic stress using integrated hydrologic modeling, field observations, and remote sensing.

faculty.name: Reza Moini
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Moini's research focuses on bio-inspired design and development of architected materials using additive manufacturing and robotic technologies for civil infrastructure, studying the mechanics of intrinsically brittle engineering materials and developing ductile, flaw-tolerant responses through biomimetic design.

faculty.name: Glaucio H. Paulino
faculty.title: Margareta Engman Augustine Professor of Engineering; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of the Princeton Materials Institute
faculty.summary: Paulino works in applied mechanics, developing methodologies to characterize deformation and fracture of existing and emerging materials, topology optimization for large-scale multiscale and multiphysics problems, computational mechanics, variational methods, and deployable structures and origami engineering.

faculty.name: Catherine Peters
faculty.title: George J. Magee Professor of Geosciences and Geological Engineering; Director, Program in Geological Engineering
faculty.summary: Peters works in environmental chemistry and geochemistry, focusing on the environmental challenges of subsurface energy technologies such as geologic carbon dioxide sequestration, geothermal energy production, and hydraulic fracturing for shale.

faculty.name: Amilcare Porporato
faculty.title: Thomas J. Wu '94 Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Porporato's research focuses on quantitative description and prediction of the terrestrial water cycle, with special interest in the impact of the hydrologic cycle on ecosystem processes (ecohydrology) and related energy, carbon, and nutrient cycles, studying soil moisture-microbe-plant dynamics through theory and experiment.

faculty.name: Anu Ramaswami
faculty.title: Sanjay Swani '87 Professor of India Studies; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, Princeton Institute for International and Regional Studies, and t
faculty.summary: Ramaswami is an interdisciplinary environmental engineer and leader on sustainable urban infrastructure systems, exploring how sectors providing water, energy, food, buildings, mobility, connectivity, waste, and green spaces shape human and environmental wellbeing from local to global scales.

faculty.name: Zhiyong Jason Ren
faculty.title: Eugene Higgins Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Andlinger Center for Energy and the Environment; Associate Director for Research, Andlin
faculty.summary: Ren's lab works on the water-energy nexus, especially water sector decarbonization and resource recovery through processes such as water and CO2 electrolysis, wastewater treatment and reuse, desalination, and carbon capture and utilization, using electrochemistry, microbiology, and AI/machine learning.

faculty.name: Gabriele Villarini
faculty.title: Theodora Shelton Pitney Professor of Environmental Studies; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the High Meadows Environmental Institute; Vice
faculty.summary: Villarini's research interests focus broadly on flood hydrology, extreme events, hydroclimatology, and climate predictions and projections, and he serves as Editor-in-Chief for Advances in Water Resources.

faculty.name: Claire White
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Andlinger Center for Energy and the Environment
faculty.summary: White's research focuses on understanding and optimizing engineering and environmental materials, emphasizing the chemical mechanisms responsible for formation and long-term degradation of sustainable cements, along with conventional cements and new materials for carbon dioxide capture.

faculty.name: Mark Zondlo
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Zondlo's research focuses on the sources, sinks, and distributions of trace gases important for air quality, the carbon and nitrogen cycles, and climate change, developing and deploying optical-based sensors for field measurements bridging in-situ and remote sensing scales.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition, health plan, and base stipend for all Ph.D. students throughout regular enrollment

faculty.name: Jie Deng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Deng uses atomistic simulations, machine learning, and laboratory experiments to study material properties and key processes underlying the formation and evolution of planetary bodies, including phase transitions, element partitioning, and isotope fractionation, recently modeling melting and crystallization of bridgmanite in magma oceans.

faculty.name: Curtis A. Deutsch
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Deutsch works on oceanography, biogeochemical cycles and the climate system, biodiversity and physiological adaptation to environmental change, the oceanic nitrogen cycle, and the response of ocean circulation and carbon cycling to climate change.

faculty.name: Thomas S. Duffy
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences; Chair, Department of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Duffy's program focuses on understanding the large-scale behavior of Earth and other planets through experimental study of geological materials under extreme pressure and temperature, exploring crystal structures, phase relations, equations of state, and deformation using diamond anvil cells, gas guns, and high-powered lasers.

faculty.name: Stephan A. Fueglistaler
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences; Director, Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences (AOS); Director, Cooperative Institute for Modeling the Earth System (CIMES)
faculty.summary: Fueglistaler is a climate scientist in the Department of Geosciences who directs the Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences program and the Cooperative Institute for Modeling the Earth System, working in climate science and atmospheric processes.

faculty.name: Christopher T. Griffin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Griffin's work bridges vertebrate paleontology, quantitative evolutionary analysis, and developmental biology to investigate the evolution of vertebrate form across deep time, uniting paleontological fieldwork, museum collections, and developmental biology techniques to study how animal form evolves.

faculty.name: John A. Higgins
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Higgins's primary research interest is the evolution of the carbon cycle and global climate system over Earth history, focusing on processes controlling seawater chemical composition and the post-depositional chemistry of carbonate sediments, using numerical models and stable and novel isotope systems.

faculty.name: Adam C. Maloof
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Maloof's Earth history group relies on original field observations at the scale of maps, outcrops, and thin sections, currently using sedimentary and volcanic rocks to extract information about Earth's ancient magnetic field, continental motion, perturbations to the global carbon cycle, and the coevolution of life and climate.

faculty.name: Satish C. Myneni
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Myneni's group works on molecular environmental geochemistry, including the chemistry of mineral-water and bacteria-water interfaces, aqueous speciation, ion solvation and complexation, iron chemistry in terrestrial and marine systems, organic biogeochemistry, and natural organohalogens.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Niespolo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Niespolo combines field work with isotope geochemistry to anchor climatic, fossil, and archaeological records to precise timescales, emphasizing radioisotopic dating alongside light stable isotope geochemistry, petrology, and field geology, with foci on the timing of human evolution and crustal processes.

faculty.name: Michael Oppenheimer
faculty.title: Albert G. Milbank Professor of Geosciences and International Affairs and the High Meadows Environmental Institute; Director, Center for Policy Research on Energ
faculty.summary: Oppenheimer's interests include the science and policy of the atmosphere, particularly climate change, the risks and impacts it entails, and adaptation and other human responses, aiming to understand the potential for dangerous outcomes of increasing greenhouse gases.

faculty.name: Laure Resplandy
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Geosciences and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Resplandy is a biogeochemical oceanographer whose research aims to understand how climate and ocean physics influence marine biogeochemistry and ecosystems and how these changes impact Earth's climate, designing numerical models and statistical tools to interpret in-situ and satellite observations.

faculty.name: Allan M. Rubin
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Rubin is a geophysicist who combines seismic and geodetic observations with numerical models to improve understanding of brittle deformation of the crust, with applications primarily to regions of active volcanism and faulting.

faculty.name: Blair Schoene
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Schoene is a geologist who uses high-precision U-Pb geochronology to understand Earth history, focusing on how magmatic processes build continental crust and affect the biosphere, atmosphere, and oceans, combining field mapping with thermochronology, radiogenic isotope tracing, and structural analysis.

faculty.name: Daniel M. Sigman
faculty.title: Dusenbury Professor of Geological and Geophysical Sciences
faculty.summary: Sigman leads a geochemistry and paleoclimate research laboratory in the Department of Geosciences, studying biogeochemical cycles and climate history through isotope geochemistry.

faculty.name: Frederik J. Simons
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences; Associate Chair, Department of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Simons studies the physical properties of the terrestrial lithosphere, focusing on elastic and thermo-mechanical properties of continents via seismic tomography and spectral analysis of gravity and topography, and has developed mathematical methods for localized analysis of fields on the sphere and oceanic seismic instrumentation.

faculty.name: Jeroen Tromp
faculty.title: Blair Professor of Geology; Professor of Geosciences and Applied and Computational Mathematics; Director, Princeton Institute for Computational Science and Engi
faculty.summary: Tromp's primary research areas are in theoretical and computational seismology, developing methods for simulating seismic wave propagation and imaging Earth's interior through adjoint-based tomography.

faculty.name: Gabriel A. Vecchi
faculty.title: Knox Taylor Professor of Geosciences; Director, High Meadows Environmental Institute; Deputy Director, Cooperative Institute for Modeling the Earth System
faculty.summary: Vecchi works on climate science and modeling, including extreme weather events, hurricanes, mechanisms of precipitation variability and change, ocean-atmosphere interaction, and detection and attribution of climate change.

faculty.name: Bess B. Ward
faculty.title: William J. Sinclair Professor of Geosciences and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Ward's laboratory studies the marine and global nitrogen cycle using molecular biological investigations of marine bacteria and processes such as nitrification and denitrification, measuring rates of N transformation with isotope approaches across suboxic ocean zones and coastal systems.

faculty.name: Xinning Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Geosciences and the High Meadows Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Zhang is an environmental microbiologist whose research aims to provide a mechanistic understanding of how microbial metabolism influences biogeochemical cycling and climate in modern and ancient environments, applying microbial physiology, molecular ecology, and stable isotope geochemistry in lab and field.

faculty.name: Ernest (Chip) Blatchley III
faculty.title: Lee A. Rieth Professor in Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies physico-chemical processes of environmental engineering, with current foci on disinfection of water and wastewater, UV-based advanced oxidation/reduction processes, swimming pool chemistry, UV disinfection of air and surfaces, and water supply in developing countries.

faculty.name: Hua Cai
faculty.title: Thomas and Jane Schmidt Rising Star Associate Professor (Industrial Engineering); Associate Professor of Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineerin
faculty.summary: Uses agent-based modeling, life cycle assessments, system dynamics, big data analytics, and GIS to study environmental implications of emerging technologies. Research interests include the energy-water nexus, emerging transportation systems, energy policy, and urban sustainability.

faculty.name: Rebecca Ciez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research spans techno-economic and environmental analysis of energy technologies, including lithium-ion battery cost and recycling analysis and evaluation of fuel economy and greenhouse gas standards. Published on battery recycling and energy storage cost modeling.

faculty.name: Carol Handwerker
faculty.title: Reinhardt Schuhmann Jr. Distinguished Professor of Materials Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on Pb-free interconnects for high-reliability electronic systems, next-generation electronic packaging, and incorporating sustainability into the design of new electronic materials, processes, and products; director of the NSF IGERT on sustainable electronics.

faculty.name: Brady Hardiman
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Forestry and Natural Resources and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the interaction of vegetation structure and function in coupled natural and human ecosystems, including the role of vegetation in urban carbon cycling and the influence of forest canopy structure on long-term carbon sequestration to improve forest management for ecosystem services.

faculty.name: John Howarter
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Materials Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research group focuses on surface and interfacial phenomena of soft materials, investigating synthesis and processing methods to improve desalination membranes, functional polymer-clay nanocomposites, polymer degradation in aqueous environments, and selective ion-transport coatings.

faculty.name: Inez Hua
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Construction Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Expertise in aquatic chemistry, environmental sustainability, water pollution control, and the environmental fate of organic contaminants, with recent work on the photochemistry of emerging contaminants, water consumption in supply chains, and sustainable electronics.

faculty.name: Linda Lee
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Agronomy; Professor of Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering; Program Head, Ecological Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the occurrence, mobility, transformation, plant uptake, and remediation of PFAS 'forever' chemicals and unregulated organic chemicals in wastewater and biosolids, developing decision tools and management guidelines for industry and agriculture.

faculty.name: Heather Liddell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research areas include sustainable manufacturing, environmental life cycle assessment, mechanics of multilayered systems, adhesion in paints and coatings, and lightweighting strategies for transportation.

faculty.name: Loring (Larry) Nies
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Construction Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research interests include optimizing interdependent urban infrastructure systems and the molecular genetic characterization of microbial community structure and function, developing molecular tools to assess anthropogenic chemical effects and monitor bioremediation.

faculty.name: Caitlin Proctor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Agricultural and Biological Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Premise plumbing microbiology and drinking water
faculty.summary: Directs the Proctor Lab studying microbiology of household and premise plumbing; her doctoral and master's research addressed causes and consequences of plumbing microbial communities and factors affecting Legionella proliferation in drinking water systems.

faculty.name: Amisha Shah
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Construction Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Aqueous chemical transformations in water treatment
faculty.summary: Research evaluates how chemicals transform in complex aqueous matrices using advanced analytical tools to study reaction kinetics and mechanisms, targeting disinfection, disinfection by-product formation, environmental photochemistry, and critical materials recovery from aqueous waste streams.

faculty.name: Shweta Singh
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Agricultural and Biological Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research spans systems-scale modeling, coupled natural and human systems modeling, complex systems theory for sustainability assessment, industrial ecology, and urban sustainability, enhancing methodologies for sustainability assessment.

faculty.name: John Sutherland
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Fehsenfeld Family Head of Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmentally responsible design and manufacturing, including closed-loop material cycles through remanufacturing and recycling, energy-efficient manufacturing, biofuel production logistics, and indicators for corporate social sustainability.

faculty.name: Andrew J. Whelton
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Construction Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Water quality, infrastructure, and public health
faculty.summary: Investigates problems affecting natural and built environments, with expertise in environmental chemistry and engineering, disasters, polymer science and engineering, water quality, infrastructure, and public health, including drinking-water contamination after chemical spills and cured-in-place-pipe safety.

faculty.name: Fu Zhao
faculty.title: Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering; Co-Director, Critical Materials Pillar, Purdue Institute for E
faculty.summary: Research addresses sustainability of manufacturing processes and product life cycles, including sustainability of solar thermal systems, integrating sustainability into product/process design, water footprint quantification of manufacturing, and biofuel supply chain dynamics.

faculty.name: Zhi (George) Zhou
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Construction Engineering and Sustainability Engineering and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental microbiology and application of biotechnologies in engineered systems, including molecular techniques to evaluate antibiotic-resistant bacteria, cost-effective biofuels, nanomaterials for water purification, and potable water reuse.

faculty.name: Philip Bedient
faculty.title: Herman Brown Professor of Engineering; Director, SSPEED Center
faculty.sub_field: Surface water hydrology and flood modeling
faculty.summary: Teaches and performs research in surface water hydrology, disaster management, and flood modeling and prediction systems, including radar-based flood alert systems and regional storm-surge mitigation strategies through the SSPEED Center.

faculty.name: James Doss-Gollin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Hydroclimatic risk and water systems design
faculty.summary: Research focuses on how assumptions and uncertainties about the future shape design and investment in water systems and climate adaptation, using space-time statistical modeling, operations research, and physics-based understanding of the climate system.

faculty.name: Leonardo Duenas-Osorio
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops analytical and computational methods, including quantum-classical optimization algorithms, for performance assessment of structural and infrastructure systems, network reliability and resilience quantification, and seismic and hurricane risk assessment of interdependent networks.

faculty.name: Kai Gong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research lies at the intersection of civil engineering, materials science, environmental sustainability, and energy, aiming to decarbonize cities and infrastructure through durable sustainable materials, waste encapsulation and conversion, and carbon mineralization using advanced characterization and computation.

faculty.name: Avantika Gori
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on understanding and quantifying coastal flood risk under evolving climate and landscape conditions, combining physics-based models and multivariate statistics to study tropical cyclone hazards, compound flood delineation, and interactions of coastal hazards with the built and natural environment.

faculty.name: Satish Nagarajaiah
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research resides at the intersection of computational structural dynamics, seismic isolation, adaptive structures and sensing, and sparse structural system identification with machine learning, developing physics-informed methods and digital twins for urban infrastructure.

faculty.name: Larissa Novelino
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Situates her research at the intersection of art and engineering, using origami to create compact deployable structures that change shape and to tailor mechanical metamaterial properties such as switchable Poisson's ratio, anisotropy, and tunable stiffness.

faculty.name: Jamie Padgett
faculty.title: Stanley C. Moore Professor in Engineering; Chair, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research applies probabilistic methods to risk assessment of infrastructure and the quantification of resilience and sustainability, emphasizing regional structural portfolios such as bridges or oil storage tanks exposed to earthquakes, hurricanes, and deterioration.

faculty.name: Xinwu Qian
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in mathematical modeling and data-driven methods for transportation problems, focusing on transportation electrification, public transportation, and shared mobility applications, along with human mobility and urban computing.

faculty.name: Pol D. Spanos
faculty.title: Lewis B. Ryon Professor in Mechanical & Civil Engineering; Professor of Materials Science and NanoEngineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on dynamics and vibrations of structural and mechanical systems under varied loads, particularly nonlinear and hazard/risk-exposed systems, developing analytic and numerical methods involving stochastic differential equations, Monte Carlo simulation, and signal processing.

faculty.name: Sang-ri Yi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops new computational methods for uncertainty quantification tailored for complex large-scale multi-hazard simulation models, integrating algorithmic innovations with high-performance computing to support natural-hazard risk and resilience assessment of urban infrastructure.

faculty.name: Pedro Alvarez
faculty.title: George R. Brown Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director, NEWT Center; Director, Rice WaTER Institute
faculty.summary: Research addresses environmental sustainability through bioremediation of contaminated aquifers, fate and transport of toxic chemicals, water footprint of biofuels, water treatment and reuse, and environmental implications and applications of nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Daniel Cohan
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research specializes in the development of atmospheric models and their application to air quality management, energy policy, and health studies, including photochemical modeling, pollutant impacts on health and vegetation, and climate change mitigation.

faculty.name: Menachem Elimelech
faculty.title: Nancy and Clint Carlson Professor, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Department of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering
faculty.summary: Research is in the water-energy nexus, involving membrane-based processes for energy-efficient desalination and wastewater reuse, advanced materials for environmental separation and water decontamination, and environmental applications of nanomaterials.

faculty.name: Bezawit Getachew
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in Environmental Engineering; Research Faculty, NSF NEWT Center
faculty.summary: Research focuses on understanding the performance of stimuli-responsive 'smart materials' in water treatment technologies, evaluating them under environmentally relevant conditions, integrating them into treatment systems, and designing new composite structures for novel functionalities.

faculty.name: Qilin Li
faculty.title: Karl F. Hasselmann Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Co-Director, NEWT Center
faculty.summary: Conducts research on physical and chemical processes affecting water quality and on advanced materials and novel processes for desalination, water purification, wastewater reuse, and resource recovery, developing technologies using renewable energy and unconventional water sources.

faculty.name: Shihong Lin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research interests are in water separation processes for sustainability challenges, including desalination and water treatment, resource extraction and recovery, and brine management, advancing membrane and electrochemical separation technologies.

faculty.name: Lauren Stadler
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Research Faculty, NEWT
faculty.summary: Research focuses on wastewater-based epidemiology, environmental antibiotic resistance, wastewater treatment and resource recovery, and environmental synthetic biology, reimagining wastewater systems as a source of information for public health.

faculty.name: Mason Tomson
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Fate and transport of chemicals in aquatic systems
faculty.summary: Specializes in the fate and transport of organic and inorganic chemicals with a focus on aquatic processes, including enhanced transport via molecular and nanoparticle carriers, irreversible adsorption to solids, and mechanisms of mineral deposition and threshold inhibition.

faculty.name: Perumalsamy N. Balaguru
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Balaguru specializes in reinforced and prestressed concrete structures, studying strength and serviceability behavior under static and fatigue loading, and develops innovative materials such as fiber-reinforced concrete and high-performance composites.

faculty.name: Vassiliki Demetracopoulou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Demetracopoulou works on improving infrastructure quality in urban environments and optimizing the delivery of infrastructure systems under the risks and complexities posed by the current climate.

faculty.name: Nicole Fahrenfeld
faculty.title: Professor and Environmental Engineering Undergraduate Director
faculty.summary: Fahrenfeld studies contaminant fate and transport, environmental microbiology and organic chemistry, and water quality, with emphasis on antibiotic resistance genes, wastewater treatment effects, and microbial communities in contaminated environments.

faculty.name: Jie Gong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gong investigates community resilience and sustainability through geospatial big data analytics for disaster response and critical infrastructure protection, and through sensing and building data modeling for high-performance building systems.

faculty.name: Nenad Gucunski
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Gucunski specializes in seismic methods and nondestructive evaluation techniques, dynamic soil-structure interaction, and numerical modeling, applying advanced inspection methods to assess transportation infrastructure.

faculty.name: Qizhong (George) Guo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Guo specializes in hydraulics and hydrology, urban stormwater and flood management, inland and coastal water environment restoration, and green and sustainable water infrastructure.

faculty.name: Jing (Peter) Jin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Jin researches mobile sensor data modeling and applications and vehicle characteristics and control in connected vehicle environments, with emphasis on transportation analytics and intelligent traffic management systems.

faculty.name: Yalin Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Li advances the sustainability of water and energy infrastructure through thermochemical and catalytic technologies for bioprocess engineering and resource recovery, using sustainable design approaches that weigh economic, environmental, and social tradeoffs.

faculty.name: Meiyin Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Liu focuses on leveraging automation and robotics to improve construction jobsite performance, especially safety, integrating expertise from surveying, electrical engineering, and construction management.

faculty.name: Xiang Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Liu applies artificial intelligence and big data technologies to enhance rail transportation safety and efficiency, and explores emerging technologies such as IoT and automation for connected multimodal transportation and infrastructure asset management.

faculty.name: Ali Maher
faculty.title: Professor and Director, Center for Advanced Infrastructure and Transportation
faculty.summary: Maher specializes in ground improvement, soil dynamics, infrastructure asset management, nondestructive testing, and environmental geotechnology, with emphasis on advancing transportation infrastructure and bridge asset management.

faculty.name: Monica Mazurek
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Mazurek focuses on air quality engineering and the chemical analysis of aerosol particles in the atmosphere.

faculty.name: Robert Miskewitz
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Miskewitz works on contaminated sediment management and surface water quality in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Husam Najm
faculty.title: Professor and Undergraduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Najm specializes in concrete materials, infrastructure repair, prestressed concrete, and earthquake engineering, including design and construction of pre-tensioned and post-tensioned concrete bridges and high-performance fiber-reinforced concrete.

faculty.name: Hani Nassif
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nassif specializes in structural health monitoring and weigh-in-motion technologies along with reliability-based analysis of bridges, developing advanced concrete materials and monitoring systems to evaluate structural safety and performance.

faculty.name: Efthymios Nikolopoulos
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Environmental Engineering Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Nikolopoulos investigates hydrometeorological and hydrological extremes such as floods and droughts, aiming to improve prediction and develop mitigation strategies for these hazards and their environmental impacts.

faculty.name: Tyler Oathes
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Oathes investigates the interplay between fundamental soil behavior and the dynamic and static response of geotechnical infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: Hao Wang
faculty.title: Professor and Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Wang develops sustainable, resilient, and intelligent pavement and transportation infrastructure through advanced materials, multi-scale computational modeling, non-destructive testing, AI-enabled management systems, and life-cycle assessment.

faculty.name: Ruo-Qian (Roger) Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wang develops numerical models to connect big data and decision-making in coastal engineering, natural hazards, water resources, and renewable energy systems.

faculty.name: Yook-Kong Yong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yong specializes in numerical modeling of bulk acoustic wave and surface acoustic wave piezoelectric resonators and filters, including their thermal, frequency, and noise characteristics.

faculty.name: Anthony J. Broccoli
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Environmental Sciences (GPAS Director)
faculty.summary: Broccoli studies climate dynamics, paleoclimate, and climate model feedbacks and sensitivity.

faculty.name: Chi Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Ecology, Evolution, and Natural Resources
faculty.summary: Chen studies ecosystem and vegetation dynamics, remote sensing, and the terrestrial carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Enrique Curchitser
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Curchitser works on oceanic general circulation and coupled biophysical and numerical modeling of the Earth system.

faculty.name: Steven J. Decker
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Decker specializes in numerical weather prediction and forecasting and data assimilation.

faculty.name: Xiaomeng Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Jin studies atmospheric chemistry, air quality modeling, and satellite remote sensing of air pollution.

faculty.name: Robert E. Kopp
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Kopp works on paleoclimate data integration and sea level change modeling.

faculty.name: Benjamin R. Lintner
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Lintner studies tropical climate dynamics and the interactions between large-scale dynamics and convection in the climate system.

faculty.name: Monica A. Mazurek
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Mazurek focuses on air quality engineering and chemical analysis of aerosol particles.

faculty.name: Travis Miles
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine and Coastal Sciences
faculty.summary: Miles studies atmosphere-ocean-cryosphere interactions across multiple scales, including storm dynamics.

faculty.name: James R. Miller
faculty.title: Professor, Marine and Coastal Sciences
faculty.summary: Miller works on climate modeling and climate change, with emphasis on the polar-region hydrologic cycle.

faculty.name: Mark Miller
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Miller studies cloud physics, atmospheric radiation transfer, remote sensing, and atmospheric observing systems.

faculty.name: Ying Fan Reinfelder
faculty.title: Professor, Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Fan Reinfelder studies hydrology and the impacts of climate change on freshwater resources.

faculty.name: Asa Rennermalm
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Geography
faculty.summary: Rennermalm studies Arctic hydroclimatology, high-latitude surface hydrology, and ice sheet dynamics.

faculty.name: David Robinson
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Geography
faculty.summary: Robinson studies climate and climate change, with emphasis on hemispheric snow cover kinematics and dynamics.

faculty.name: Alan Robock
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Robock studies climate change detection, attribution, and impacts, including effects of volcanic eruptions, geoengineering, and nuclear winter.

faculty.name: Mary Whelan
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Whelan studies biochemical cycling, biosphere-atmosphere interactions, and atmospheric chemistry, including carbon uptake.

faculty.name: Lili Xia
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor, Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Xia works on climate modeling and climate change with a focus on aerosols, environment, and atmospheric pollution.

faculty.name: Donna Fennell
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Fennell researches bioremediation of contaminated environments, bioaerosols, and bioenergy.

faculty.name: Weilin Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Huang studies physicochemical processes governing contaminant behavior, water treatment, and soil remediation.

faculty.name: Elodie Passeport
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Passeport studies emerging contaminants, environmental remediation technologies, and stable isotope analysis of pollutant degradation.

faculty.name: John Reinfelder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Reinfelder studies biogeochemistry in aquatic systems, including the cycling and bioaccumulation of trace elements and contaminants.

faculty.name: Nathan Yee
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yee studies microbial geochemistry and the interactions between microorganisms and metals in the environment.

faculty.name: Lily Young
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Young studies anaerobic microbial metabolism and microbial ecology, including biodegradation of environmental contaminants.

faculty.name: Siddhartha Roy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Roy studies water quality, public health, and environmental epidemiology, including contaminants in drinking water systems.

faculty.name: Lisa Rodenburg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rodenburg studies the sources, fate, and transport of organic pollutants such as PCBs in aquatic and atmospheric environments.

faculty.name: Gediminas Mainelis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mainelis studies biological and non-biological aerosols, including their measurement, behavior, and exposure implications.

faculty.name: Daniel Gimenez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gimenez studies soil science and soil physics, including soil structure and the movement of water through soils.

faculty.name: Uta Krogmann
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Krogmann studies solid waste management, green buildings, and life-cycle assessment of environmental systems.

faculty.name: Jeffra Schaefer
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Schaefer studies environmental microbiology and biogeochemistry, including microbial transformation and cycling of metals such as mercury.

faculty.name: Robert Miskewitz
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Miskewitz researches contaminated sediment management and surface water quality in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Taewon Han
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Han studies aerosol mechanics, including the behavior and measurement of airborne particles.

faculty.name: Karen Bemis
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Bemis studies geologic visualization, hydrothermal plumes, and volcanology of the seafloor.

faculty.name: Kay Bidle
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bidle studies microbial oceanography, phytoplankton physiology and mortality, and host-virus interactions.

faculty.name: Benedict Borer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Borer studies microbial biogeochemistry and the interactions between microbes and particles in the ocean.

faculty.name: David Bushek
faculty.title: Professor and Director, Haskin Shellfish Research Laboratory
faculty.summary: Bushek studies bivalve host-parasite interactions and oyster ecology.

faculty.name: Robert Chant
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chant studies estuarine physics, turbulent mixing, and sediment transport in coastal and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Paul Falkowski
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Falkowski studies the origins of life, electron transfer, biogeochemistry, and biological oceanography.

faculty.name: Heidi Fuchs
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Fuchs studies larval biophysics and predator-prey and plankton-fluid interactions in marine populations.

faculty.name: Scott Glenn
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Co-Director, Center for Ocean Observing Leadership
faculty.summary: Glenn develops autonomous ocean observing systems and studies extreme events in the coastal ocean.

faculty.name: Max Gorbunov
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Gorbunov studies coral reefs, photosynthesis, and benthic ecosystems using bio-optical approaches.

faculty.name: Joseph Gradone
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Gradone studies physical oceanography and water mass transport in the coastal and open ocean.

faculty.name: Thomas Grothues
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor and Director, Rutgers University Marine Field Station
faculty.summary: Grothues studies fish distribution and movement ecology using tracking technologies.

faculty.name: Ximing Guo
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Guo studies mollusk genetics, aquaculture, and selective breeding of shellfish.

faculty.name: Lee Kerkhof
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kerkhof studies microbial diversity, biogeochemistry, and the activity of microbes in marine environments.

faculty.name: Josh Kohut
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kohut studies coastal ocean variability and ocean observing systems.

faculty.name: Julia Levin
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Levin works on ocean modeling and data assimilation for coastal ocean prediction.

faculty.name: Richard Lutz
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Lutz studies deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystems and larval ecology.

faculty.name: Travis Miles
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Miles studies physical oceanography, storm dynamics, and ice-ocean interactions.

faculty.name: Daphne Munroe
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Munroe studies invertebrate larval dispersal, population connectivity, and shellfisheries.

faculty.name: Luis Fernando Pareja Roman
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor
faculty.summary: Pareja Roman studies coastal ocean dynamics, air-sea interaction, and offshore wind interactions.

faculty.name: Fiorella Prada
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Prada studies coral biomineralization and the effects of ocean acidification on corals.

faculty.name: Yair Rosenthal
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Rosenthal studies paleoceanography and the development of geochemical proxies for past ocean and climate conditions.

faculty.name: Grace Saba
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Saba studies organismal ecology and physiological responses of marine organisms to climate change and ocean acidification.

faculty.name: Danielle Santiago Ramos
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Santiago Ramos studies silicate weathering, ocean chemistry, and geochemical cycling.

faculty.name: Oscar Schofield
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor, Chair, and Co-Director, Center for Ocean Observing Leadership
