faculty.summary: Schofield studies phytoplankton physiology and ecology and develops ocean observing technologies.

faculty.name: Corday Selden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Selden studies marine biogeochemistry, microbial metabolism, and nutrient cycling in the ocean.

faculty.name: Myla Aronson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Aronson studies patterns and drivers of global biodiversity, urban ecology, and plant ecology.

faculty.name: Christina Bergey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Bergey uses population, evolutionary, and functional genomic approaches to study adaptation and evolution.

faculty.name: Debashish Bhattacharya
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Bhattacharya studies the genomics of algal origin and evolution.

faculty.name: Juan Bonachela
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bonachela develops theoretical models spanning microbial ecology and evolution to terrestrial vegetation patterns.

faculty.name: Joanna Burger
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Burger studies avian and reptile behavior and ecology, salt marsh ecology, and the behavioral effects of heavy metals.

faculty.name: Chi Chen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Chen studies ecosystem and vegetation dynamics, remote sensing, and the terrestrial carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Siobain Duffy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Duffy studies emerging viruses, experimental evolution, phylogenetics, and bacteriophages.

faculty.name: Dina Fonseca
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fonseca studies the genetic epidemiology of disease vectors, especially introduced species.

faculty.name: Robert Goodman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Goodman studies the diversity and function of microorganisms in natural environments.

faculty.name: Julie Lockwood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lockwood studies conservation biology, wildlife management, and biological invasions.

faculty.name: Michael Manhart
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Manhart studies microbial communities and their ecological and evolutionary dynamics using systems biology approaches.

faculty.name: Brooke Maslo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Maslo studies conservation biology, population dynamics, and ecosystem services, with work on bats and shorebirds.

faculty.name: Marci Meixler
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Meixler studies aquatic ecology and ecological modeling of freshwater systems.

faculty.name: Peter J. Morin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Morin studies biodiversity and ecosystem functioning, ecological networks, and community ecology.

faculty.name: Tetsuya Nakamura
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nakamura studies fish diversity and the genetics of the fish-to-tetrapod evolutionary transition.

faculty.name: Kimberly Russell
faculty.title: Associate Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Russell studies community ecology, urban ecology and novel ecosystems, and arthropod conservation.

faculty.name: Lena Struwe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Struwe studies plant systematics and evolution, biogeography, ethnobotany, and biodiversity conservation.

faculty.name: Erin Vogel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vogel studies food acquisition and diet selection in nonhuman primates.

faculty.name: John Wiedenmann
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wiedenmann studies marine population and ecosystem dynamics and fisheries management.

faculty.name: Rachael Winfree
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Winfree studies biodiversity, pollination ecology, and ecosystem services.

faculty.name: Sarah Billington
faculty.title: The Gary Retelny and Family Chair of the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering, UPS Foundation Professor, Professor of Civil and Environmental Engin
faculty.summary: Billington's group studies how built environments impact human wellbeing and how human-centered engineering design can improve those impacts, developing tools and design interventions; her prior work centered on sustainable, durable construction materials including bio-based and ductile cement-based composites.

faculty.name: Alexandria Boehm
faculty.title: Richard and Rhoda Goldman Professor of Environmental Studies, Professor of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Boehm studies pathogens in natural and engineered environments, including their sources, fate, and transport, and how they are transmitted to humans through water and contaminated surfaces, with the goal of designing interventions to reduce disease burden; she also works broadly on coastal water quality and nitrogen fate.

faculty.name: Ronaldo Borja
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Borja works in computational mechanics, geomechanics, and geosciences, developing strain localization and failure models for soils and rocks, modeling coupled solid deformation and fluid flow in porous materials, and finite element modeling of faulting and fracturing.

faculty.name: Jenna Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, of Environmental Social Sciences and Higgins-Magid Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute
faculty.summary: Davis heads the Stanford Program on Water, Health & Development, with research and teaching focused at the nexus of water, economic development, and public health, particularly in low- and middle-income countries, with field research in more than 20 countries.

faculty.name: Gregory Deierlein
faculty.title: John A. Blume Professor in the School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Deierlein's research focuses on improving limit-states design of constructed facilities through nonlinear structural analysis methods and performance-based design criteria, including seismic behavior of steel and reinforced concrete structures and fracture in welded steel connections.

faculty.name: Sarah Fletcher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Center Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: The Fletcher lab advances water resources management to promote resilient and equitable responses to a changing world, integrating methods from hydrology, policy analysis, and data science to inform environmental decision-making.

faculty.name: Oliver Fringer
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and of Oceans
faculty.summary: Fringer develops and applies numerical models and high-performance computational techniques to study fundamental processes influencing the dynamics of the coastal ocean, rivers, lakes, and estuaries.

faculty.name: Catherine Gorle
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and, by courtesy, of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Predictive flow simulation for sustainable cities
faculty.summary: Gorle's research focuses on developing predictive flow simulations to support the design of sustainable buildings and cities, including coupling large- and small-scale models and experiments to quantify uncertainties in urban wind and environmental flows.

faculty.name: Lynn Hildemann
faculty.title: Wayne Loel Professor of Sustainability and Senior Associate Dean for Education
faculty.summary: Hildemann's research addresses the sources and dispersion of airborne particulate matter indoors and the assessment of human exposure to air pollutants.

faculty.name: Mark Z. Jacobson
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Institute for Energy
faculty.summary: Jacobson develops and applies three-dimensional atmosphere-biosphere-ocean computer models to simulate air pollution, weather, climate, and renewable energy, and has developed roadmaps to transition states and countries to 100% clean, renewable energy.

faculty.name: Rishee Jain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Jain's research develops data-driven and socio-technical solutions to sustainability problems facing the urban built environment, at the intersection of civil engineering, data analytics, and social science, including commercial building energy usage and urban sustainability planning.

faculty.name: Peter K. Kitanidis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Kitanidis develops methods for interpolation and inverse problems using observations and mathematical models of flow and transport, studying dilution and mixing in heterogeneous geologic formations, in situ pollutant decay, and hydrologic forecasting.

faculty.name: Christian Linder
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Linder directs the Computational Mechanics of Materials Lab, developing computational methods to understand micromechanically originated multi-scale and multi-physics mechanisms in solid materials undergoing large deformations and fracture, with applications including sustainable energy storage materials.

faculty.name: Meagan Mauter
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering, of Photon Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Instit
faculty.summary: Mauter directs the Water and Energy Efficiency for the Environment Lab (WE3Lab), aiming to provide sustainable water supply in a carbon-constrained world through innovation in water treatment technology, optimization of water management, and redesign of water policy.

faculty.name: Eduardo Miranda
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Miranda specializes in structural engineering with emphasis on performance-based earthquake engineering, studying how structures respond to earthquakes and developing ways to design and build structures with improved seismic performance.

faculty.name: William Mitch
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Mitch employs a fundamental understanding of environmental chemistry to study contaminants in water, including disinfection byproduct formation and control relevant to drinking water and potable water reuse.

faculty.name: Stephen Monismith
faculty.title: Obayashi Professor in the School of Engineering and Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Monismith applies fluid mechanics principles to flow processes in rivers, lakes, estuaries, and oceans, with particular interest in ecological impacts, studying estuarine hydrodynamics and mixing, flows over coral reefs, and stratified turbulence.

faculty.name: Khalid Osman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Osman uses mixed quantitative-qualitative methods to assess public perceptions of water infrastructure and conservation, focusing on operationalizing equity in water-sector infrastructure, water affordability, and stakeholder engagement for environmental justice.

faculty.name: Iro Armeni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Machine perception for the built environment
faculty.summary: Armeni works at the intersection of architecture, civil engineering, and visual machine perception, developing data-driven methods that learn from real-world visual data to generate, predict, and simulate sustainable, inclusive, and adaptive built environments.

faculty.name: Christine M Baker
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and, by courtesy, of Oceans
faculty.summary: Baker studies processes at the land-ocean interface, integrating laboratory experimentation, numerical modeling, and remote sensing to understand coastal hydrodynamics and inform predictions of coastal water quality, shoreline evolution, and coastal resiliency.

faculty.name: Yaochun Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Yu's research integrates high-resolution mass spectrometry, meta-omics, molecular microbiology, and computational modeling to identify functional microbes, genes, and enzymes driving chemical biotransformation, aiming to develop biosolutions for bioremediation and resource recovery.

faculty.name: Jack Baker
faculty.title: William Alden Campbell and Martha Campbell Professor and Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Baker uses probabilistic and statistical tools to quantify and manage disaster risk and resilience, contributing to risk analysis of spatially distributed systems, characterization of earthquake ground motions, and simulation of post-disaster recovery.

funding.note: Generally full financial support for first five years

faculty.name: Kevin Arrigo
faculty.title: Donald and Donald M. Steel Professor of Earth Sciences and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Arrigo studies polar marine ecosystems and biogeochemistry, using satellite and field data to understand phytoplankton productivity and biogeochemical cycling in the ocean, particularly in polar regions.

faculty.name: Jen Burney
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Social Sciences, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Freeman Spogli Institute for International Studies
faculty.sub_field: Climate, air pollution and food security
faculty.summary: Burney's research focuses on the coupled relationships between climate and food security, measuring air pollutant emissions, quantifying climate and pollution effects on food systems, and designing and evaluating adaptation and mitigation strategies for farmers.

faculty.name: Karen Casciotti
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Facilities and Shared Labs, Professor of Oceans, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Casciotti studies the marine nitrogen cycle using stable isotope geochemistry, geochemical modeling, and molecular biology to understand how nitrate, nitrite, and nitrous oxide are produced and consumed in ocean waters, particularly in oxygen-deficient zones.

faculty.name: Steven J. Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science, Senior Fellow at the Precourt Institute for Energy and Professor, by courtesy, of Energy Science and Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Emissions, energy scenarios and climate solutions
faculty.summary: Davis leads the Sustainable Solutions Lab, quantifying how human activities affect climate and air quality, how those changes jeopardize human wellbeing, and the relative priority of solutions; he studies emissions and energy scenarios and climate mitigation.

faculty.name: Giulio De Leo
faculty.title: Professor of Oceans, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: De Leo is a theoretical ecologist investigating processes driving the dynamics of natural and harvested populations, focusing on resilience of ecosystems to natural and anthropogenic stressors, including parasitic and infectious diseases and sustainable management of marine populations.

faculty.name: Anne Dekas
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth System Science and, by courtesy, of Oceans
faculty.summary: Dekas is a geomicrobiologist interested in how microbial life affects the chemistry and climate of the planet today and throughout time, studying microbial metabolisms and their biogeochemical impacts.

faculty.name: Noah Diffenbaugh
faculty.title: William Wrigley Professor and Kimmelman Family Senior Fellow
faculty.summary: Diffenbaugh's research focuses on understanding what aspects of the climate system most directly and acutely impact people and ecosystems; he has served as a Lead Author for the IPCC and studies climate variability, change, and extremes.

faculty.name: Rodolfo Dirzo
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Integrative Initiatives in Environmental Justice, Bing Professor in Environmental Science, Professor of Earth System Science and Senior Fello
faculty.summary: Dirzo studies species interactions in tropical ecosystems, with recent research highlighting the decline of animal life (defaunation) and how it affects ecosystem processes and services such as disease regulation.

faculty.name: Scott Fendorf
faculty.title: Terry Huffington Professor, Senior Associate Dean for Research, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and Professor of Photon Science
faculty.summary: Fendorf examines the coupled chemical, biological, and hydrological processes controlling the cycling of elements from arsenic to carbon, iron, and uranium in soils and sediments, with field work informing how contaminants enter groundwater and food.

faculty.name: Chris Field
faculty.title: Melvin and Joan Lane Professor of Interdisciplinary Environmental Studies, Director of the Woods Institute for the Environment, Professor of Earth System Scienc
faculty.summary: Field's research focuses on climate change, especially solutions that improve lives now, decrease future warming, and support vibrant economies, with recent projects emphasizing reduced risks from coastal flooding and wildfires; he co-chaired IPCC Working Group II.

faculty.name: Christopher Francis
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science, of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Francis studies the molecular, biogeochemical, and ecological aspects of microbially-mediated cycling of nitrogen and metals, examining the diversity and activity of microorganisms involved in nitrification, denitrification, and manganese cycling in coastal and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Steven Gorelick
faculty.title: Cyrus Fisher Tolman Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Gorelick directs the Global Freshwater Initiative and has published extensively on groundwater management, water security, water resources vulnerability in developing regions, remediation design, and ecohydrology.

faculty.name: Sebastian Heilpern
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Heilpern is an ecologist and sustainability scientist studying the causes of biodiversity change and its consequences for ecosystems and human well-being, with emphasis on aquatic ecosystems, aligning conservation with public health, food systems, and renewable energy objectives.

faculty.name: Alison Hoyt
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Hoyt's work focuses on understanding how biogeochemical cycles respond to human impacts, with a particular focus on the most vulnerable and least understood carbon stocks in the tropics and the Arctic.

faculty.name: Rob Jackson
faculty.title: Michelle and Kevin Douglas Provostial Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Institute for Energy
faculty.sub_field: Global change biology and carbon cycle
faculty.summary: Jackson and his lab examine the many ways people affect the Earth, producing scientific knowledge to shape policies and reduce environmental footprints; he chairs the Global Carbon Project and works to measure and reduce methane emissions and air pollution.

faculty.name: Alexandra Konings
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth System Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and, by courtesy, of Geophysics
faculty.summary: Konings leads the Remote Sensing Ecohydrology group, studying interactions between the global carbon and water cycles, including transpiration, root water uptake, photosynthesis, and fire processes, primarily using model development and microwave remote sensing data.

faculty.name: Eric Lambin
faculty.title: George and Setsuko Ishiyama Provostial Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.sub_field: Land use change and environmental geography
faculty.summary: Lambin, a geographer and environmental scientist, studies the causes and impacts of land use changes worldwide; he chaired the international Land Use and Land Cover Change project and has received the Volvo Environment Prize and Blue Planet Prize.

faculty.name: David Lobell
faculty.title: Benjamin M. Page Professor, William Wrigley Senior Fellow at the Freeman Spogli Institute, at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Stanford Instit
faculty.summary: Lobell directs the Center on Food Security and the Environment; his research focuses on agriculture and food security, generating and using unique datasets to study rural areas and climate change risks and adaptations in cropping systems.

faculty.name: Katharine (Kate) Maher
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and Professor, by courtesy, of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Maher's research examines the carbon cycle through multiple lenses, combining computer models with field and laboratory measurements to link hydrologic, chemical, and biological processes; current projects include soil carbon cycling, water quality, and carbon dioxide removal.

faculty.name: Adam Pellegrini
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Pellegrini studies how biogeochemical cycles respond to disturbances such as wildfires, droughts, and disease, and what this means for climate change; his work uses global field experiments to examine how repeated fires alter soil carbon and nitrogen storage.

faculty.name: Aditi Sheshadri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Sheshadri studies atmosphere and ocean dynamics, climate variability, and general circulation, addressing fundamental questions in atmospheric dynamics using theory, observations, and both idealized and comprehensive numerical experiments, with focus on the mid-latitude jet.

faculty.name: Leif Thomas
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science and, by courtesy, of Oceans
faculty.summary: Thomas is a physical oceanographer studying the dynamics of upper-ocean fronts, eddies, and submesoscale processes and their roles in exchanging heat, nutrients, and carbon between the ocean surface and interior, with implications for climate.

faculty.name: Yuan Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Wang's group studies the physical and chemical interactions between atmospheric constituents and climate change, including aerosol-cloud-radiation interactions, haze formation, and cloud microphysics, using multiscale numerical and AI models with satellite and in situ measurements.

faculty.name: Paula V. Welander
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Research and Professor of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Welander is a microbiologist whose research focuses on understanding the biosynthesis and physiological function of molecular fossils (biomarkers) in extant bacteria.

faculty.name: Elliott White Jr.
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: White is a coastal ecosystem scientist studying the effects of saltwater intrusion and sea level rise on vegetation in the coastal land margin, using an interdisciplinary approach drawing from ecology, hydrology, biogeochemistry, and remote sensing.

faculty.name: Earle Wilson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth System Science, by courtesy, of Geophysics, of Oceans and Center Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Wilson is a physical oceanographer studying ocean dynamics at high latitudes and their impacts on global climate, particularly the circulation of the Southern Ocean and its interactions with sea ice and marine-terminating glaciers, using in situ observations and idealized models.

faculty.name: Gabrielle Wong-Parodi
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Wong-Parodi studies the human dimensions of environmental change, focusing on how people perceive and respond to environmental and climate risks and how to support individual and collective decision-making and behavior.

faculty.name: Halleh Balch
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Oceans and, by courtesy, of Electrical Engineering, and HHMI Hanna Gray Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Balch is an experimental physicist whose research focuses on advancing water sustainability and ocean exploration through new technologies based on nanoscale control over light-matter interactions and engineered materials.

faculty.name: Barbara Block
faculty.title: Charles and Elizabeth Prothro Professor of Marine Sciences, Professor of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Block, based at Hopkins Marine Station, studies the physiology and ecology of large pelagic marine predators such as tunas and sharks, using electronic tagging and physiological research to inform ocean conservation.

faculty.name: Alexandria Boehm
faculty.title: Richard and Rhoda Goldman Professor of Environmental Studies, Professor of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Boehm studies pathogens in natural and engineered environments and their transmission to humans through water, and works broadly on coastal water quality including the sources, transport, and ecology of fecal indicator organisms, pathogens, and nitrogen.

faculty.name: Karen Casciotti
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Facilities and Shared Labs, Professor of Oceans, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Casciotti studies the marine nitrogen cycle using stable isotope geochemistry, geochemical modeling, and molecular biology to understand how nitrate, nitrite, and nitrous oxide are produced and consumed in ocean waters, particularly in oxygen-deficient zones.

faculty.name: Larry Crowder
faculty.title: Edward Ricketts Provostial Professor, Professor of Oceans, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and Professor, by courtesy, of Environmental
faculty.summary: Crowder's research centers on predation and food web interactions, recruitment variation in fishes, population and food web modeling in conservation biology, and interdisciplinary approaches to marine conservation.

faculty.name: Kristen Davis
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Oceans and, by courtesy, of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Davis is an engineer and oceanographer studying how physical processes shape coastal waters, combining principles of fluid mechanics, oceanography, and ecology using field observations and numerical tools to examine ocean circulation and its influence on marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Giulio De Leo
faculty.title: Professor of Oceans, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: De Leo is a theoretical ecologist investigating processes driving population dynamics, focusing on resilience of ecosystems to natural and anthropogenic stressors, including parasitic and infectious diseases and sustainable management of marine populations.

faculty.name: Rob Dunbar
faculty.title: W.M. Keck Professor in the School of Earth Sciences, Professor of Oceans, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and Professor, by courtesy, o
faculty.summary: Dunbar's research studies global environmental change with a focus on air-sea interactions, tropical marine ecosystems, polar climate, and biogeochemistry, and he is interested in environmental policy directed toward problem-solving.

faculty.name: Christopher Francis
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science, of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Francis studies the molecular, biogeochemical, and ecological aspects of microbially-mediated cycling of nitrogen and metals, examining microorganisms involved in nitrification, denitrification, and manganese cycling in coastal and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Oliver Fringer
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and of Oceans
faculty.summary: Fringer develops and applies numerical models and high-performance computational techniques to study fundamental processes influencing the dynamics of the coastal ocean, rivers, lakes, and estuaries.

faculty.name: William Gilly
faculty.title: Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Gilly, based at Hopkins Marine Station, studies the neurobiology and physiology of marine organisms, including the behavior and biology of Humboldt squid and other cephalopods.

faculty.name: Jeremy Goldbogen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Goldbogen, based at Hopkins Marine Station, is a comparative physiologist studying the integrative biology of marine organisms, with a particular focus on the feeding biomechanics, energetics, and foraging ecology of baleen whales.

faculty.name: Fiorenza Micheli
faculty.title: David and Lucile Packard Professor of Marine Science, Professor of Oceans and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Micheli, chair of the Oceans Department and co-director of the Center for Ocean Solutions, studies the processes shaping marine communities and coastal social-ecological systems, including climatic impacts, marine predator ecology, small-scale fisheries sustainability, and marine protected areas.

faculty.name: Stephen Monismith
faculty.title: Obayashi Professor in the School of Engineering and Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Monismith applies fluid mechanics principles to flow processes in rivers, lakes, estuaries, and oceans with particular interest in ecological impacts, studying estuarine hydrodynamics and mixing, flows over coral reefs, and stratified turbulence.

faculty.name: Stephen Palumbi
faculty.title: Jane and Marshall Steel Jr. Professor of Marine Sciences, Professor of Oceans and of Biology
faculty.summary: Palumbi studies the genomics of marine organisms to address basic evolutionary questions and practical conservation, including human-induced evolutionary change and developing genomic methods to identify ocean species and corals resistant to climate change.

faculty.name: Brooke Weigel
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Oceans
faculty.summary: Weigel is a marine ecologist whose research focuses on the ecology, physiology, and genomics of marine algae and microbial communities, particularly the feedbacks between climate change, kelp forests, microbes, and carbon cycling in the ocean; her lab is based at Hopkins Marine Station.

faculty.name: Gretchen C. Daily
faculty.title: Bing Professor of Environmental Science and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and, by courtesy, at the Freeman Spogli Institute for Inter
faculty.summary: Daily is co-founder and Faculty Director of the Stanford Natural Capital Project; her research quantifies the production and value of ecosystem services, harmonizes biodiversity conservation with agriculture, and develops policies for integrating nature into societal decisions.

faculty.name: Eric Lambin
faculty.title: George and Setsuko Ishiyama Provostial Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.sub_field: Land use change and environmental geography
faculty.summary: Lambin, a geographer and environmental scientist, studies the causes and impacts of land use changes worldwide; he chaired the international Land Use and Land Cover Change project and has received the Volvo Environment Prize and Blue Planet Prize.

faculty.name: Chris Field
faculty.title: Melvin and Joan Lane Professor of Interdisciplinary Environmental Studies, Director of the Woods Institute for the Environment, Professor of Earth System Scienc
faculty.summary: Field's research focuses on climate change, especially solutions that improve lives now, decrease future warming, and support vibrant economies, with recent projects emphasizing reduced risks from coastal flooding and wildfires; he co-chaired IPCC Working Group II.

faculty.name: Rob Jackson
faculty.title: Michelle and Kevin Douglas Provostial Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Institute for Energy
faculty.sub_field: Global change biology and carbon cycle
faculty.summary: Jackson and his lab examine the many ways people affect the Earth, producing scientific knowledge to shape policies and reduce environmental footprints; he chairs the Global Carbon Project and works to measure and reduce methane emissions and air pollution.

faculty.name: David Lobell
faculty.title: Benjamin M. Page Professor, William Wrigley Senior Fellow at the Freeman Spogli Institute, at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Stanford Instit
faculty.summary: Lobell directs the Center on Food Security and the Environment; his research focuses on agriculture and food security, generating and using unique datasets to study rural areas and climate change risks and adaptations in cropping systems.

faculty.name: Noah Diffenbaugh
faculty.title: William Wrigley Professor and Kimmelman Family Senior Fellow
faculty.summary: Diffenbaugh's research focuses on understanding what aspects of the climate system most directly and acutely impact people and ecosystems; he has served as a Lead Author for the IPCC and studies climate variability, change, and extremes.

faculty.name: Katharine (Kate) Maher
faculty.title: Professor of Earth System Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and Professor, by courtesy, of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Maher's research examines the carbon cycle through multiple lenses, combining computer models with field and laboratory measurements to link hydrologic, chemical, and biological processes; current projects include soil carbon cycling, water quality, and carbon dioxide removal.

faculty.name: Steven Gorelick
faculty.title: Cyrus Fisher Tolman Professor and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Gorelick directs the Global Freshwater Initiative and has published extensively on groundwater management, water security, water resources vulnerability in developing regions, remediation design, and ecohydrology.

faculty.name: Jenna Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, of Environmental Social Sciences and Higgins-Magid Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute
faculty.summary: Davis heads the Stanford Program on Water, Health & Development, with research and teaching focused at the nexus of water, economic development, and public health, particularly in low- and middle-income countries, with field research in more than 20 countries.

faculty.name: Meagan Mauter
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering, of Photon Science, Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and at the Precourt Instit
faculty.summary: Mauter directs the Water and Energy Efficiency for the Environment Lab (WE3Lab), aiming to provide sustainable water supply in a carbon-constrained world through innovation in water treatment technology, optimization of water management, and redesign of water policy.

faculty.name: William Abraham Tarpeh
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical Engineering and, by courtesy, of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Tarpeh's lab reimagines liquid waste streams as resources, developing and evaluating novel approaches to resource recovery from wastewaters at molecular, unit-process, and systems scales using electrochemistry, separations, and reactor design.

faculty.name: Sally Benson
faculty.title: Precourt Family Professor, Professor of Energy Science Engineering and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment and the Precourt Institute for E
faculty.summary: Benson studies technologies and pathways to reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including geologic storage of CO2 in deep underground formations and energy systems analysis for a low-carbon future; she co-founded the Stanford Center for Carbon Storage.

faculty.name: Adam Brandt
faculty.title: Professor of Energy Science Engineering and Senior Fellow at the Precourt Institute for Energy
faculty.sub_field: Energy systems and life cycle assessment
faculty.summary: Brandt is interested in reducing the environmental impacts of energy systems, focusing on understanding, measuring, and reducing greenhouse gas emissions from fossil energy sources using life cycle assessment and process optimization.

faculty.name: Ines M. L. Azevedo
faculty.title: Professor of Energy Science Engineering, Senior Fellow at the Precourt Institute for Energy and Professor, by courtesy, of Civil and Environmental Engineering a
faculty.summary: Azevedo works on problems that combine environmental, technical, economic, and policy issues, assessing how energy systems are likely to evolve, the technologies that can address future energy needs, and the decision-making of agents in the economy.

faculty.name: Marshall Burke
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Social Sciences, Senior Fellow at the Freeman Spogli Institute for International Studies, at the Woods Institute for the Environment,
faculty.summary: Burke uses tools from the social and natural sciences to measure environmental change and its impacts on society, spanning climate change, air pollution, food security, and poverty measurement, combining economics, statistics, remote sensing, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Jen Burney
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Social Sciences, of Earth System Science and Senior Fellow at the Freeman Spogli Institute for International Studies
faculty.sub_field: Climate, air pollution and food security
faculty.summary: Burney's research focuses on the coupled relationships between climate and food security, measuring air pollutant emissions, quantifying climate and pollution effects on food systems, and designing and evaluating adaptation and mitigation strategies for farmers.

faculty.name: Nicole Ardoin
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Social Sciences and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Ardoin and her Social Ecology Lab research motivations for and barriers to environmental behavior at individual and collective scales, using mixed methods to study place-based connections, environmental learning, and social-ecological interactions in sustainability decision-making.

faculty.name: Barton Thompson
faculty.title: Robert E. Paradise Professor of Natural Resources Law, Professor of Environmental Social Sciences and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Thompson, a global expert on water and natural resources, focuses on how to improve resource management through legal, institutional, and technological innovation, and directs the Water in the West program at the Woods Institute.

faculty.name: Erin Mordecai
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biology and Senior Fellow at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Mordecai's research focuses on the ecology of infectious disease, studying how climate, species interactions, and global change drive infectious disease dynamics in humans and natural ecosystems, combining mathematical modeling and empirical work.

faculty.name: Jenny Suckale
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Geophysics and, Senior Fellow, by courtesy, at the Woods Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Suckale's group studies disasters to reduce the risk they pose, developing customized mathematical models tested against observational data and informed by community needs, spanning volcanic eruptions, ice-sheet instability, permafrost, induced seismicity, and flood-risk mitigation.

faculty.name: Jane Kathryn Willenbring
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences and, by courtesy, of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Willenbring is a geologist who studies the evolution of the Earth's surface, especially how landscapes are affected by tectonics, climate change, and life, using geochemical techniques including cosmogenic nuclides, high-resolution topography, and landscape evolution models.

faculty.name: Patrick Bassal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Combines nonlinear dynamic geotechnical modeling with subsurface characterization, geostatistics, and remote sensing (InSAR, LiDAR) to understand ground failures from natural hazards. Conducted post-earthquake reconnaissance including the 2023 Turkiye-Syria earthquakes.

faculty.name: Min Liew
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in permafrost and cold regions geotechnics, climate change-related geohazards, and climate-resilient sustainable civil infrastructure. Uses numerical modeling of coupled thermal-hydraulic-mechanical physics validated with laboratory, field, and sensing methods.

faculty.name: Halil Sezen
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches structural analysis, design, and performance assessment of structures under extreme loading conditions, including dynamic behavior and collapse of buildings and rehabilitation of concrete buildings and bridges. Has published over 150 peer-reviewed articles with funding from NSF, FHWA, and DOE.

faculty.name: Abdollah Shafieezadeh
faculty.title: Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Abba G. Lichtenstein Professorship in Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads the RAMSIS Lab advancing uncertainty quantification, reliability, resilience, and life-cycle performance assessment methods for structural and infrastructure systems ranging from buildings and bridges to power grids and flood defenses, addressing both acute hazards and chronic deterioration.

faculty.name: Lisa Burris
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on increasing the sustainability of concrete construction through novel supplementary and alternative cementitious materials for traffic infrastructure, characterizing material properties and linking hydration to concrete strength and durability.

faculty.name: Ethan Kubatko
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops and applies computational models of fluid flow and transport processes, with applications from hurricane storm surge simulation to sea-level rise impacts on estuarine systems and tidal turbine performance. Directs the Computational Hydrodynamics and Informatics Laboratory.

faculty.name: Benjamin Coifman
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on intelligent transportation systems, developing new approaches to traffic surveillance that lead to better traffic control, spanning traffic flow theory and driver dynamics while bridging academic research with practical implementation.

faculty.name: Rabi Mishalani
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic modeling, statistical inference, experimental design, and optimization to public transportation planning and operations, real-time network monitoring, and infrastructure resilience decision-making, using OSU's campus transit system as a living laboratory.

faculty.name: Andre Carrel
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Associate Professor, Knowlton School of Architecture
faculty.summary: Directs the Travel Behavior Research Group, studying the dynamics of travel behavior, travelers' adaptation to experienced service quality, and the impact of emerging mobility technologies (autonomous, shared, alternative-fuel) on travel demand to inform sustainable transportation policy.

faculty.name: Kelsea Best
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Assistant Professor, Knowlton School of Architecture
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate change interacts with human societies and infrastructure, and how adaptation strategies can be implemented equitably, using computational methods such as machine learning and agent-based modeling of coupled human-natural systems.

faculty.name: Jim Stagge
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs the Hydrologic Extremes Research Laboratory, investigating the frequency and severity of droughts and floods, climate change impacts on water resources, and management strategies to protect society and the environment.

faculty.name: Linda Weavers
faculty.title: Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; John C. Geupel Chair, College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops water and hazardous waste treatment technologies, investigating ultrasound for treatment of emerging contaminants such as PFAS, harmful algal bloom control, and membrane defouling. Has secured over $35 million in sponsored research funding.

faculty.name: John Lenhart
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Studies reactions and processes at important natural and engineered interfaces to understand contaminant fate, combining experiments with mathematical modeling on topics including cyanotoxins, microplastics, arsenic in Ohio groundwater, and rare earth element recovery from acid mine drainage.

faculty.name: Karen Dannemiller
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Directs the Indoor Environmental Quality research group, studying chemical and microbial processes in indoor environments that impact human health, with funding from NSF, NIH, HUD, and NASA.

faculty.name: Rongjun Qin
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for accurately measuring static and moving objects in urban contexts using aerial/UAV imagery, LiDAR, and satellite multispectral/hyperspectral images. Author of the RSP and MSP software tools for reconstructing 3D information from 2D images.

faculty.name: Alper Yilmaz
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Computer Science and Engineering (courtesy)
faculty.summary: Leads the Photogrammetric Computer Vision Lab, focusing on biomimetic navigation systems for unmanned systems, anomaly mining in multi-physics data, and learning geospatial information for scene understanding, with over $13M in extramural funding and 190+ publications.

faculty.name: Lei Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in acquiring, analyzing, and interpreting geodetic data, developing high-precision GNSS positioning algorithms with applications to tectonic and geodynamic deformations and interactions between environmental changes and the solid Earth.

funding.note: OSU fellowship deadline Dec 1 for U.S. applicants

faculty.name: Douglas Alsdorf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates tropical wetland systems in the Congo and Amazon Basins, focusing on water flux dynamics and their connections to flooding, ecology, and climate change using satellite-based measurements of water surface elevations.

faculty.name: Loren Babcock
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the Orton Geological Museum
faculty.summary: Studies the early history of multicellular animals (notably trilobites), biotic changes across the Ediacaran-Cambrian transition, Cambrian global chronostratigraphy, and processes of fossilization.

faculty.name: Michael Barton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates magma generation, movement, and evolution in the Earth's interior using petrology, thermodynamics, geochemistry, and geophysics, with projects on Iceland volcanism, crustal formation, magma degassing, and mantle source characteristics.

faculty.name: Emilie Beaudon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates paleoclimate signals preserved in ice cores from polar and alpine regions, using geochemical analysis of mineral dust to examine changes in pollution transport pathways, atmospheric contamination, paleofire histories, and climate variability.

faculty.name: David Cole
faculty.title: Professor, Ohio Research Scholar
faculty.summary: Investigates geochemical processes including reaction mechanisms in minerals and fluids, isotopic climate proxies, CO2 sequestration in geological formations, and molecular-level behavior of carbon-bearing fluids in nanopores; directs the SEMCAL subsurface characterization laboratory.

faculty.name: Ann Cook
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates subseafloor natural gas hydrate systems using petrophysics, geophysics, and mathematical modeling, focusing on identifying and quantifying gas hydrate deposits, with additional work on marine geology and carbon sequestration.

faculty.name: Brendan Crowell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of seismology and geodesy to advance earthquake and tsunami early warning systems and hazard mitigation, with expertise in seismogeodesy and natural hazards assessment.

faculty.name: Thomas Darrah
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the Office of Industry Partnerships and Commercialization
faculty.summary: Investigates geological processes controlling fluid migration in Earth's crust and mantle, develops geochemical techniques for energy exploration, and applies isotope geochemistry to assess environmental and health impacts of energy activities; leads the Water, Energy, and Life Laboratory (WELL).

faculty.name: Melisa Diaz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies geochemical methods to understand physical systems and their interactions with biology and society, focusing on salt and nutrient transport in Antarctic and Arctic environments as well as urban geochemistry and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Graduate Students
faculty.summary: Investigates land surface hydrology using spaceborne remote sensing to address questions in snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology, including changes in snowmelt runoff patterns and remote estimation of river discharge.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Griffith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses non-traditional stable isotopes (Ca, Sr) in combination with traditional paleoclimate proxies to study biogeochemical cycling in modern and ancient oceans, reconstructing past environmental conditions.

faculty.name: W. Ashley Griffith
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Administration
faculty.summary: Investigates structural geology and rock mechanics with a focus on fracture behavior in the Earth's crust, examining how fractures influence subsurface fluid movement, earthquake hazards, and engineering challenges.

faculty.name: Andrea G. Grottoli
faculty.title: College of Arts and Sciences Distinguished Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies the drivers of coral resilience under climate change, develops blue technology for coral restoration, and reconstructs past oceanographic conditions using coral skeletal records; directs the NSF-funded Coral Bleaching Research Coordination Network.

faculty.name: Ian Howat
faculty.title: School Director, ENGIE-Axium Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: A glaciologist whose research centers on ice sheet and glacier dynamics, hydrology, and remote sensing of Earth's surface processes; internationally recognized for satellite-based mapping of ice flow and elevation change in polar regions.

faculty.name: Motomu Ibaraki
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates water contamination and insufficient water supplies caused by human activities and their impacts on health and ecosystems, including hydrological influences on disease transmission, wetland hydrology using radar altimetry, and water-energy sustainability.

faculty.name: Jill Leonard-Pingel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: An invertebrate paleontologist examining how the environment shapes biological communities across geologic timescales and in modern conservation contexts, with current focus on Neogene mollusks affected by the formation of the Isthmus of Panama and changing reef communities.

faculty.name: Steven Lower
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works across geomicrobiology, mineralogy, and biophysics, with recent projects on biomineralizing magnetic particles, cancer caused by nanoscale minerals, acidophilic sulfide-oxidizing bacteria, and infectious biofilms on medical implants.

faculty.name: Alex Michaud
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates microbial biogeochemistry at the intersection of microbial ecology and glaciology, focusing on how the loss of ice impacts the role of microorganisms in globally relevant element cycles (C, Fe, S) in polar and alpine regions.

faculty.name: Joachim Moortgat
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the BuckAI Observatory
faculty.summary: Develops computational methods for multiphase flow in fractured porous media with applications to CO2 sequestration and hydrogen reservoirs, and applies deep learning to satellite imagery for river mapping, coastal bathymetry, and deforestation tracking.

faculty.name: Matthew Saltzman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His research group investigates the role of the carbon cycle in climate change and biotic events over the past half billion years of Earth history, using stable isotope analysis of sedimentary rocks to understand organic matter burial and continental weathering rates.

faculty.name: Lonnie Thompson
faculty.title: Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: Specializes in ice core paleoclimatology, extracting climate histories from tropical and subtropical ice fields in the Andes, Himalayas, and Kilimanjaro; his work has advanced understanding of El Nino and monsoon patterns and documented glacier retreat outside the range of climate variability for several millennia.

faculty.name: Zhengyu Liu
faculty.title: Max Thomas Professor of Climate Dynamics, Atmospheric Sciences Program Director
faculty.summary: Models the global climate system and ocean-atmosphere-land interaction, with application to climate change and climate variability. His research spans climate dynamics from seasonal to interdecadal timescales, past glacial-interglacial cycles, and future warming scenarios.

faculty.name: David Bromwich
faculty.title: Research Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates global climate change in polar regions using climate models and atmospheric reanalyses, emphasizing local and tropical influences. He directs development of polar versions of the WRF and MPAS regional weather prediction models and leads the Arctic System Reanalysis project on the coupled atmosphere-land-ocean-sea ice system.

faculty.name: Man-Yau ("Joseph") Chan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Data Assimilation and Earth System Prediction
faculty.summary: A statistical meteorologist focused on improving Earth system forecasts through ensemble data assimilation methods that integrate measurements with computer models. His work addresses error growth in Earth system models to enhance weather, ocean, and land forecasting systems for disaster mitigation.

faculty.name: Jana Houser
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates tornado formation and evolution using mobile radar observations, with emphasis on how rotation develops within thunderstorms. Her work also examines how topography and land cover influence tornado intensity and path.

faculty.name: Bryan Mark
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Glacier Environmental Change research group at the Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center, studying climate-glacier-hydrologic dynamics over different time scales with emphasis on climate change, glaciers, and water in mountain regions. Field research spans the Peruvian Andes, Nevada's Great Basin, the Hindu Kush Himalaya, and Africa.

faculty.name: Alvaro Montenegro
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates paleoclimate modeling with archaeological and biogeographic questions, using agent-based simulations to investigate historical human mobility. His research examines scenarios such as the prehistoric colonization of remote Oceania and how climate variability influenced past human dispersal.

faculty.name: Ellen Mosley-Thompson
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes chemical and physical properties preserved in ice cores from polar ice sheets and high mountain glaciers to reconstruct Earth's climate history. She has led multiple expeditions to Antarctica and Greenland and has shown that current climate conditions exceed the range of natural variability of the past 2,000 years.

faculty.name: Steven Quiring
faculty.title: Professor, Promotion and Tenure Chair
faculty.summary: A climatologist specializing in hydroclimatology, synoptic climatology, and applying climate data to societally relevant problems. His research focuses on land-atmosphere interactions to improve drought prediction and on assessing the impacts of hurricanes, thunderstorms, and winter storms on power infrastructure.

faculty.name: Aaron Wilson
faculty.title: State Climatologist of Ohio; Assistant Professor, Ag Weather and Climate Field Specialist
faculty.summary: An atmospheric scientist at the Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center who assesses hydrologic conditions throughout Ohio and coordinates the state's contributions to the U.S. Drought Monitor. His work emphasizes how changing weather extremes impact urban and rural communities.

faculty.name: Lonnie Thompson
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor, School of Earth Sciences; Senior Research Scientist, Byrd Polar and Climate Research Center
faculty.summary: Specializes in ice core paleoclimatology, developing lightweight solar-powered drilling equipment to recover climate records from tropical and subtropical ice fields in the Andes, Himalayas, and Kilimanjaro. His decades of glacier observations document worldwide glacier retreat and show current warming exceeds natural climate variability over millennia.

faculty.name: Douglas Alsdorf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies tropical wetlands and water systems in the Congo and Amazon Basins using satellite measurements to understand water flux, flooding, ecology, and global change impacts.

faculty.name: Michael Bevis
faculty.title: Professor, Ohio Eminent Scholar
faculty.summary: Uses geodetic techniques to investigate crustal motion, earthquake deformation, mountain building, sea-level change, and atmospheric water vapor, with GPS networks established across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Brendan Crowell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on seismogeodesy and early warning systems, studying earthquakes and natural hazards through integrated geophysical and geodetic approaches.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Director for Graduate Students
faculty.sub_field: Remote sensing of snow and rivers
faculty.summary: Investigates land surface hydrology using spaceborne remote sensing, with emphasis on snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology, including snow-runoff dynamics, snow microstructure effects on satellite measurements, and river discharge estimation.

faculty.name: Demian Gomez
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: A geophysicist and geometrical geodesist researching regional and global reference frame realizations, which he uses to investigate geodynamic processes such as the earthquake cycle and periodic loading and unloading of the Earth's crust.

faculty.name: Shin-Chan Han
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Analyzes space geodetic measurements and models geophysical processes governing mass changes on Earth and terrestrial planets, with applications to the solid Earth, water systems, ice mass, oceans, and planetary bodies.

faculty.name: Ian Howat
faculty.title: School Director, ENGIE-Axium Professor, Distinguished University Scholar
faculty.summary: Studies ice sheet dynamics and glacier behavior using satellite-based Earth observation, focusing on how polar regions respond to climate change through advanced mapping technologies.

faculty.name: Joachim Moortgat
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the BuckAI Observatory
faculty.sub_field: AI-powered Earth observation and computational Earth science
faculty.summary: Works on computational Earth science, combining finite element methods for multiphase flow in porous media with AI-powered Earth observation using deep learning and physics-informed neural networks, applied to problems such as CO2 sequestration and satellite-based environmental monitoring.

faculty.name: In-Young Yeo
faculty.title: Professor (on leave)
faculty.summary: Listed under the Geodetic Science division with research interests in geodetic science and water, climate and the environment; currently on leave per the OSU directory.

faculty.name: Rongjun Qin
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in computational image analysis and 3D vision, focusing on accurate localization and detection of objects using aerial/UAV imagery, LiDAR, and satellite data for urban measurement and monitoring applications.

faculty.name: Lei Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil, Environmental and Geodetic Engineering
faculty.sub_field: GNSS positioning and geodetic data analysis
faculty.summary: Specializes in geodetic data acquisition and analysis, including high-precision static and kinematic GNSS positioning algorithms and applications, tectonic and geodynamic deformations, and the interaction between environmental changes and the solid Earth.

faculty.name: Alper Yilmaz
faculty.title: Professor, Civil Environmental and Geodetic Engineering; Professor, Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Photogrammetric computer vision and geospatial AI
faculty.summary: Researches biomimetic navigation systems for unmanned systems, mining anomalies in multi-physics and multi-dimensional data for surveillance, and learning geospatial information for scene understanding, using deep learning, computer vision, photogrammetry, and indoor positioning.

faculty.name: Nicholas Basta
faculty.title: Co-Director, Professor
faculty.summary: Basta specializes in risk-based soil environmental chemistry emphasizing bioavailability, exposure, and impact to human, agronomic, and ecological receptors. His work also covers soil remediation technologies and environmental risk assessment of land-applied agricultural and industrial by-products.

faculty.name: Gil Bohrer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bohrer works on atmospheric modeling and biosphere-atmosphere interactions, focusing on eddy flux measurements, forest canopy structure and function, forest fire modeling, ecological dispersal, and land surface heterogeneity.

faculty.name: Michael Durand
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Durand studies land surface hydrology processes, using data assimilation methods and spaceborne remote sensing measurements to address open questions in snow hydrology and fluvial hydrology.

faculty.name: Rachel Gabor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Gabor's research focuses on watershed hydrology, examining water movement and management within watershed systems.

faculty.name: Steven W. Lyon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lyon investigates how landscapes and water interact across scales, focusing on improving water quality management and ecosystem functionality. His work combines hydrological process analysis with community engagement to address coupled climate and land-use change impacts on water resources.

faculty.name: Jay Martin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Martin investigates ecosystems for waste utilization and treatment, sustainable agriculture, ecosystem modeling, riparian ecosystem restoration, wetlands, and aquaculture.

faculty.name: Virginia Rich
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rich investigates microbial biogeochemistry using molecular ecology techniques to understand how microbes respond to anthropogenic environmental change. Her flagship research examines permafrost collapse in Arctic Sweden and microbially-mediated ecosystem functions at the Catalina-Jemez Critical Zone Observatory.

faculty.name: Matthew Sullivan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sullivan studies the co-evolution of microbes and viruses in environmental systems, emphasizing how marine phages influence microbial-driven global biogeochemistry. His work uses genomics and experimental approaches to investigate phage-host interactions in cultured and natural populations.

faculty.name: Jim Ippolito
faculty.title: Dr. Rattan Lal Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Ippolito studies environmental soil health across ecosystems, examining connections between soil nutrients, microbiology, and physical properties. His work explores how these factors influence ecosystem sustainability, food security, climate resilience, and environmental health.

faculty.name: Scott Demyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Demyan focuses on soil organic matter and mineral stabilization mechanisms, inorganic carbon dynamics, and ex situ mineral carbonation as a sequestration method, with emphasis on carbon permanence in soil and environmental mineralogy.

faculty.name: Richard Dick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Dick studies soil microbial ecology, specifically microbial diversity and function that affects ecosystem services and sustainability, and biogeochemical processes related to microorganisms.

faculty.name: James Stagge
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Stagge's work focuses on understanding hydrologic extremes with the goal of decreasing drought vulnerability. His prior research analyzed drought risk patterns across Europe through an EU-funded initiative.

faculty.name: Ryan Winston
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Winston investigates water resource management with particular focus on urban stormwater and the relationships between stormwater control measures and their broader impacts on society, including water quality improvement and climate change mitigation.

faculty.name: Steven Quiring
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quiring is a climatologist studying hydroclimatology, synoptic climatology, and applying climate data to solve societally-relevant problems. His work focuses on land-atmosphere interactions for drought prediction and modeling weather impacts on power infrastructure.

faculty.name: Yanlan Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Liu studies plant responses to climate variations and drought by combining physically-based models and data-driven analytics across ecosystems ranging from wetlands, forests, and deserts, at scales from a single site to continents.

faculty.name: Alexander Michaud
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Michaud is a microbial biogeochemist conducting research at the intersection of microbial ecology, biogeochemistry, and glaciology, focusing on how microorganisms contribute to biogeochemical cycles in ice-affected regions.

faculty.name: Sami Khanal
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Khanal's research interests span agroecosystems, climate change, mathematical modeling, remote sensing, and water quality.

faculty.name: Kaiguang Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Zhao employs geospatial tools and modeling approaches to map, monitor, model, and manage terrestrial environments and examine ecosystem responses to disturbances and climate change, emphasizing remote sensing of ecosystem structure and function.

faculty.name: John Lenhart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lenhart's work focuses on environmental chemistry, surface chemistry, colloidal processes, and contaminant fate and transport, exploring how contaminants move through and interact with environmental surfaces and systems.

faculty.name: Darren Drewry
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Drewry's research examines interactions of agricultural systems with the environment, spanning ecohydrology, terrestrial biophysics, and land-atmosphere interactions, using simulation and sensing techniques to improve vegetation resilience.

faculty.name: Kerry Ard
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental and Natural Resource Sociology
faculty.summary: Ard examines connections between environmental policy, inequality, and public health outcomes. Her work investigates how social and economic systems shape environmental risks and their effects on population health.

faculty.name: Nicholas Basta
faculty.title: Professor of Soil and Environmental Chemistry
faculty.summary: Basta's research focuses on environmental soil chemistry, examining biogeochemical processes affecting heavy metal bioavailability, human health risks in soil-water systems, and remediation technologies for contaminated soils.

faculty.name: Ramiro Berardo
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental and Natural Resources Policy
faculty.summary: Berardo studies how policy actors interact when addressing environmental problems, examining how social capital forms among stakeholders and affects their support for policy responses.

faculty.name: Jeremy Brooks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Brooks is an environmental social scientist investigating sustainability through cultural evolution theory, examining dynamics between individuals and the socio-cultural, physical, and natural environments.

faculty.name: Julia DeFeo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Silviculture & Forest Ecology
faculty.summary: DeFeo is a silviculturist and fire ecologist studying drivers of resilience in forest ecosystems, with current focus on biodiversity in two-aged silvicultural systems and Appalachian fuel complexes.

faculty.name: M. Scott Demyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Soil and Environmental Mineralogy
faculty.sub_field: Soil organic matter and carbon dynamics
faculty.summary: Demyan's work focuses on soil organic matter and mineral stabilization mechanisms, inorganic carbon dynamics, and ex situ mineral carbonation as a carbon sequestration method.

faculty.name: Alia Dietsch
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Parks, Protected Areas, Natural Resources Management
faculty.summary: Dietsch applies social science theories to conservation decision-making and natural resource management, focusing on how human thought affects public support for management actions and behaviors related to wildlife and environmental issues.

faculty.name: Rachel Gabor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Watershed Hydrology
faculty.summary: Gabor's research examines how environmental changes affect wetland ecosystems, with particular focus on microbial communities and mercury cycling in response to dissolved organic matter variations.

faculty.name: Robert Gates
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Wildlife Ecology and Management
faculty.summary: Gates investigates population-habitat relationships of migratory waterfowl, marsh birds, shorebirds, and upland game birds, along with wildlife diseases and threatened species conservation.

faculty.name: Shoshanah Inwood
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Inwood examines agriculture, environment, and society at the intersection of community development, focusing on health and well-being in the food and agriculture sector, farm growth, and food system resilience.

faculty.name: Douglas Jackson-Smith
faculty.title: Professor and Kellogg Endowed Chair of Agroecosystem Management
faculty.summary: Jackson-Smith investigates the social, economic, and environmental dynamics of agricultural systems, focusing on crop-livestock integration, farmer conservation behaviors, and participatory research methods.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Jacquet
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Rural Sociology
faculty.summary: Jacquet is a rural and natural resource sociologist studying energy development impacts, including social consequences from renewable and fossil fuel projects, plus rural community development and environmental change.

faculty.name: Jim Ippolito
faculty.title: Professor, Rattan Lal Endowed Professor of Soil Health and Soil Fertility
faculty.summary: Ippolito's research focuses on soil health and fertility, examining connections between soil nutrients, microbial activity, and contaminants to improve ecosystem sustainability and food security.

faculty.name: Stephen Matthews
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Wildlife Landscape Ecology
faculty.summary: Matthews studies landscape ecology and biodiversity responses to environmental change, focusing on climate and land-use impacts on tree and bird species across the Eastern United States and forest management effects on vertebrate habitat use.

faculty.name: Sayeed Mehmood
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Natural Resources Economics
faculty.summary: Mehmood applies economic and social science theories to understand human behavior related to natural resources and environmental management, with recent focus on forest landowner economics, ecosystem restoration, and biomass energy policy.

faculty.name: Risa Pesapane
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in Ecosystem and Wildlife Health
faculty.summary: Pesapane is a community disease ecologist with an emphasis on wildlife health and vector-borne disease, focusing on understanding how diseases spread through wildlife populations and ecosystems.

faculty.name: William Peterman
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Wildlife Ecology and Management
faculty.summary: Peterman studies landscape ecology and conservation biology, examining how climate change, habitat alteration, and urbanization affect animal populations, biodiversity, and dispersal across landscapes using field, experimental, and quantitative modeling approaches.

faculty.name: Lauren Pintor
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Aquatic Ecology and Associate Director of Undergraduate Education
faculty.summary: Pintor's work examines how species interactions structure ecological communities and ecosystem function, with particular focus on biological invasions and the mechanisms driving predator-prey dynamics.

faculty.name: Christopher Tonra
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Avian Wildlife Ecology and Chair, Schiermeier Olentangy River Wetland Research Park
faculty.summary: Tonra examines how individuals interact with their environments, focusing on avian systems, integrating behavior, physiology, and ecology to understand mechanisms shaping life history and population responses to environmental change.

faculty.name: Nicole Sintov
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Behavior, Decision Making and Sustainability
faculty.summary: Sintov is an environmental psychologist studying psychological and social factors influencing sustainable behavior, developing behavior-change interventions, and investigating factors affecting intervention success, particularly regarding household energy use and technology adoption.

programs.program_name: PhD in Civil and Environmental Engineering

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Laurie Gaskins Baise
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on natural hazards such as earthquakes and extreme wind, linking predictive models with observational data. Specializes in earthquake site response, regional seismic hazard mapping, and remotely sensed imagery for post-disaster damage assessment.

faculty.name: C. Andrew Ramsburg
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Dean of Undergraduate Education, School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies transport and reaction processes occurring in multiphase systems with applications to environmental contamination, including emulsion delivery in porous media and the fate of emerging contaminants in biological treatment systems.

faculty.name: Wayne Chudyk
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research centers on fiber optic sensors, groundwater monitoring, and water treatment for both drinking water and wastewater, with a focus on drinking water quality and monitoring of toxic materials.

faculty.name: Luis Dorfmann
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical models of material behavior, including nonlinear magneto- and electromechanical interactions and the biomechanics of soft materials, with applications in finite strain theory and constitutive modeling of highly deformable materials.

faculty.name: John Durant
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on air pollution monitoring, mobile monitoring platforms, air pollution modeling, and ambient air quality, together with exposure assessment for health studies.

faculty.name: David Gute
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of engineering and public health, spanning environmental and occupational epidemiology, environmental health and safety, water-borne disease control, and occupational health risks.

faculty.name: Shafiqul Islam
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Water Diplomacy, The Fletcher School
faculty.summary: Studies the availability, access, and allocation of water in the context of climate change, health, and geopolitics through an interdisciplinary approach combining science, engineering, policy, and negotiation.

faculty.name: Daniel Kuchma
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the design, behavior, and modeling of reinforced and prestressed concrete structures subject to complex states of stress, including strut-and-tie methods and shear design of high-strength concrete bridge girders. Also works on foundations for offshore wind turbines.

faculty.name: Jonathan Lamontagne
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research addresses hydrologic extremes, water resources in a changing world, energy systems modeling, and robust adaptive planning, centering on uncertainty quantification in environmental and water systems.

faculty.name: Babak Moaveni
