faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on Bayesian inference and inverse problems, structural health monitoring, and system and damage identification of civil structures, along with earthquake engineering and uncertainty quantification.

faculty.name: Masoud Sanayei
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in bridge structural health monitoring, developing methods for assessing structural condition using nondestructive test data, with additional work on train-induced building vibrations, fatigue prediction, and full-scale structural evaluation.

faculty.name: Lauryn Spearing
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Uses systems thinking to examine infrastructure management during uncertain contexts and sustainable water technology adoption, combining qualitative and quantitative methods at the intersection of technical, social, and natural systems.

faculty.name: Helen Suh
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Internationally recognized expert studying the impact of air pollutant exposures on dementia, cognitive performance, and cardiovascular health, and developing data modeling methods to predict air pollutant concentrations and assess extreme weather effects on health.

faculty.name: Deborah Sunter
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational modeling of electrical grid integration of renewable energy and storage, and explores the intersection of technology innovation and policy for environmental sustainability and energy justice.

faculty.name: Chris Swan
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Dean of Inclusive Excellence, School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Examines sustainable development and innovative engineering education, with focus on waste minimization and reuse of traditional waste materials and on assessing service-based learning in engineering programs.

faculty.name: Farshid Vahedifard
faculty.title: Professor and Louis Berger Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the resilience and adaptation of critical infrastructure such as levees and dams to extreme events in a changing climate, with emphasis on disadvantaged communities and multi-physics processes in geotechnical engineering.

faculty.name: Robert Viesca
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies theoretical mechanics to problems in earth sciences and engineering, examining processes including landsliding, fault rupture, and seafloor sedimentation through the lens of friction, fracture, and deformation mechanics.

programs.program_name: PhD in Offshore Wind Energy Engineering

funding.note: Full-time PhD students in School of Engineering often receive tuition scholarship

faculty.name: Eric Hines
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice and Kentaro Tsutsumi Faculty Fellow, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs Tufts' offshore wind energy graduate program and has over 25 years of experience engineering innovative infrastructure. His work spans offshore wind structural design, earthquake engineering, and systems-level design at the technology-policy interface, including transmission expansion planning.

faculty.name: Daniel Kuchma
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Offshore Wind Foundations and Concrete Structures
faculty.summary: Global leader in structural testing methods who heads projects on corrosion and fatigue of offshore wind foundations and leads Tufts' efforts on nature-inclusive design. Combines experience in earthquake engineering and offshore oil and gas to extend wind turbine foundation service lives.

faculty.name: Babak Moaveni
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Digital Twins and Structural Health Monitoring
faculty.summary: Leads Tufts' work on digital twinning of offshore wind turbines, combining physics-based and AI approaches. His broader research covers Bayesian inference and inverse problems, structural health monitoring, and damage identification of civil structures.

faculty.name: Barbara Kates-Garnick
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice in Energy Policy, The Fletcher School
faculty.summary: Leads the program's work on electricity transmission policy and researches energy transition, climate policy, and offshore wind policy. Former Undersecretary of Energy for Massachusetts, she collaborates with the School of Engineering on Offshore Wind Program research and state advisory efforts.

faculty.name: Beth Rosenberg
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Public Health and Community Medicine; Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Offshore Wind Occupational Health and Safety
faculty.summary: Leads Tufts' transatlantic offshore wind safety initiative, investigating occupational and environmental health with emphasis on safety systems and culture. Recent work includes improving safety conditions in the offshore wind industry and workforce diversity, equity, and inclusion.

faculty.name: Laurie Gaskins Baise
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on natural hazards such as earthquakes and extreme wind, linking predictive models with observational data. Specializes in site response, regional hazard mapping, and remotely sensed damage assessment relevant to siting and foundations.

faculty.name: Masoud Sanayei
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in structural health monitoring of bridges and large structures, developing methods to assess structural condition from nondestructive test data, including fatigue prediction and full-scale structural evaluations.

faculty.name: Jonathan Lamontagne
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research addresses energy systems modeling, hydrologic extremes, and robust adaptive planning under deep uncertainty, centering on uncertainty quantification in coupled environmental and energy systems.

faculty.name: Deborah Sunter
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on computational modeling of electrical grid integration of renewable energy and storage, and explores the intersection of technology innovation and policy for environmental sustainability and energy justice.

faculty.name: Farshid Vahedifard
faculty.title: Professor and Louis Berger Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies the resilience and adaptation of critical infrastructure to extreme events in a changing climate, with emphasis on multi-physics processes in geotechnical engineering relevant to energy infrastructure foundations.

faculty.name: Lisa Alvarez-Cohen
faculty.title: Fred and Claire Sauer Professor; Vice Provost for Academic Planning
faculty.summary: Studies environmental microbiology and the biotransformation of contaminants, including biological processes for treating pollutants in water and wastewater systems.

faculty.name: Joshua Apte
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches air quality and atmospheric aerosols, examining human exposure to air pollution from neighborhood to global scales and climate change mitigation.

faculty.name: Maya Carrasquillo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on urban water infrastructure and green stormwater systems with an emphasis on infrastructure justice and equitable community outcomes.

faculty.name: Fotini Katopodes Chow
faculty.title: Fred & Claire Sauer Chancellor's Chair; Professor
faculty.summary: Uses large-eddy simulation to study atmospheric flows, wind energy, urban pollutant dispersion, and air quality.

faculty.name: Paolo D'Odorico
faculty.title: Thomas J. Graff Professor of Natural Resources
faculty.summary: Studies surface hydrology, ecosystem ecology, aeolian processes, and desertification, linking water dynamics to ecological and environmental change.

faculty.name: Cynthia Gerlein-Safdi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates ecohydrology using remote sensing, satellite experiments, and stable isotopes to understand plant-water and land-surface interactions.

faculty.name: Allen Goldstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches atmospheric chemistry, terrestrial biogeochemistry, and biosphere-atmosphere exchange of trace gases and particles.

faculty.name: Robert Harley
faculty.title: Carl W. Johnson Professor in Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies air pollution and air quality, including atmospheric chemistry and the impact of motor vehicle emissions on urban air.

faculty.name: Arpad Horvath
faculty.title: Edward G. Cahill and John R. Cahill Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Applies life cycle assessment to infrastructure systems to evaluate and improve environmental sustainability.

faculty.name: Cesunica Ivey
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Vice Chair of Strategic Planning
faculty.summary: Focuses on air pollution assessment, community-centered resilience, and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Baoxia Mi
faculty.title: Wood-Calvert Chair in Engineering; Professor
faculty.summary: Develops membrane separation, desalination, and solar evaporation technologies for water purification.

faculty.name: Kara L. Nelson
faculty.title: Blum Chancellor's Chair in Development Engineering; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches pathogen detection and removal, water reuse, and nutrient recovery for sustainable water and sanitation systems.

faculty.name: Amy Pickering
faculty.title: Blum Center Distinguished Chair in Global Poverty and Practice; Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies environmental disease transmission, public health surveillance, and antimicrobial resistance related to water and sanitation.

faculty.name: David L. Sedlak
faculty.title: Plato Malozemoff Professor; Director, Berkeley Water Center
faculty.summary: Researches water reuse, urban water infrastructure, and treatment technologies including engineered treatment wetlands.

faculty.name: Mark Stacey
faculty.title: Henry and Joyce Miedema Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies coastal and estuarine physics and ecology, including climate adaptation of shoreline and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Evan A. Variano
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches environmental fluid dynamics, focusing on mixing and transport by turbulence in aquatic environments.

faculty.name: Jonathan D. Bray
faculty.title: Faculty Chair in Earthquake Engineering Excellence; Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geotechnical earthquake engineering using physical and numerical modeling of soil and foundation behavior under seismic loading.

faculty.name: Adda Athanasopoulos-Zekkos
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Vice Chair for Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Belonging
faculty.summary: Researches geotechnical engineering of infrastructure and soil dynamics, including seismic response of geosystems.

faculty.name: Dimitrios Zekkos
faculty.title: Professor; Vice Chair for Strategic Planning and Implementation
faculty.summary: Studies geotechnical engineering with a focus on landslides and natural hazards affecting infrastructure.

faculty.name: Matthew DeJong
faculty.title: Ray & Shirley Clough Presidential Chair; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches earthquake engineering, infrastructure monitoring, and soil-structure interaction.

faculty.name: Khalid M. Mosalam
faculty.title: Taisei Professor of Civil Engineering; Director, Pacific Earthquake Engineering Research Center
faculty.summary: Works on structural health monitoring, experimental methods, and earthquake engineering for resilient structures.

faculty.name: James W. Rector
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches exploration geophysics and applied seismology, incorporating machine learning for subsurface imaging.

faculty.name: Justin Remais
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Environmental Health Sciences Division
faculty.summary: Uses statistical and mathematical modeling to study how infectious diseases such as West Nile virus, TB, and schistosomiasis spread in response to urbanization, climate change, and shifting water resources.

faculty.name: Joshua Apte
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates human exposure to air pollution from neighborhood to global scales, including how historical policies such as redlining drive disparities in air quality exposure.

faculty.name: Rachel Morello-Frosch
faculty.title: Professor, Environmental Health Sciences; Community Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines the social determinants of environmental health disparities and how inequality and psychosocial stress interact with chemical exposures, often using community-based participatory research.

faculty.name: Ellen Eisen
faculty.title: Professor in Residence, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Applies biostatistical and epidemiological methods to occupational and environmental health, addressing healthy worker survivor bias and workplace contributions to chronic disease.

faculty.name: Jay Graham
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Residence, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Applies epidemiologic methods and DNA sequencing to understand antimicrobial resistance and zoonotic infectious disease transmission across global regions.

faculty.name: Laura Kwong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies environmental contamination and infectious disease exposure affecting child and maternal health in low-income countries, developing and evaluating engineering interventions through randomized controlled trials.

faculty.name: Ajay Pillarisetti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates how household energy consumption affects air pollution exposure, human health, and climate change, with a focus on low- and middle-income countries.

faculty.name: David J.X. González
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how pollution from extractive industries and climate-driven disasters affects public health, using population-level data and community-engaged methods to address health disparities.

faculty.name: Misbath Daouda
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines the distribution of health benefits from energy transitions in the context of climate change in the US and West Africa using environmental epidemiology and exposure modeling.

faculty.name: Rosemarie de la Rosa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how social and environmental factors in childhood become biologically embedded and affect susceptibility to pollutant exposures, including combined effects of psychosocial stress and air pollution.

faculty.name: Bambarendage Pinithi Upekka Perera
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies how environmental toxicants alter the epigenome during critical developmental stages, integrating molecular biology and toxicology to examine maternal-fetal interactions and sex-specific epigenetic regulation.

faculty.name: Carly Hyland
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Cooperative Extension, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate change affects agricultural and food systems workers through interconnected exposures like heat, wildfire smoke, and pesticides, using community-engaged and mixed methods.

faculty.name: David Ackerly
faculty.title: Dean of Rausser College of Natural Resources and Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates climate change effects on California biodiversity, including distribution modeling, long-term vegetation dynamics, drought tolerance of native trees, and post-fire forest dynamics.

faculty.name: Justin Brashares
faculty.title: Professor and G.R. & W.M. Goertz Chair
faculty.summary: Studies how wildlife consumption and habitat conversion affect animal communities and population persistence, integrating ecology with economic, political, and cultural factors for conservation.

faculty.name: Carl Boettiger
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on ecological forecasting and decision-making under uncertainty for conservation and natural resource management, especially in systems prone to sudden regime shifts.

faculty.name: Timothy Bowles
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates soil health, climate resilience, and sustainable farming systems, examining how biodiversity-based agroecological approaches affect soil and climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Benjamin Blonder
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies plant responses to climate change through forest ecophysiological and genetic analyses, community assembly theory, and responses to heatwaves across temporal scales.

faculty.name: Stephanie Carlson
faculty.title: Professor and A.S. Leopold Chair in Wildlife Biology
faculty.summary: Studies the evolutionary ecology and conservation of freshwater fishes, focusing on the dynamics of California's inland fishes and factors shaping their persistence.

faculty.name: Todd Dawson
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how plants interact with their environments using physiological plant ecology, stable isotope biogeochemistry, and ecohydrology, emphasizing woody plants and forest systems.

faculty.name: Iryna Dronova
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing and spatial analysis to study ecosystem and landscape ecology, with emphasis on wetland biodiversity, phenological patterns, and urban green spaces.

faculty.name: Alejandra Echeverri
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Conservation Science
faculty.summary: Investigates how human behaviors such as habitat conversion affect wildlife, combining ornithological fieldwork with social science to advance biocultural conservation in the Neotropics.

faculty.name: Trevor Keenan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how terrestrial ecosystems respond to climate change and variability, focusing on carbon cycling, water use, and atmosphere feedbacks using large datasets, models, and field studies.

faculty.name: Céline Pallud
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Examines the transport and fate of chemical species in soils and sediments, emphasizing microbial processes affecting contaminant mobility using field, laboratory, and modeling approaches.

faculty.name: Albert Ruhi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how freshwater ecosystems respond to global change, particularly hydrologic alteration and drought, studying community dynamics, biodiversity, and regime shifts in river food webs.

faculty.name: Christopher Schell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how urban carnivores such as coyotes and foxes adapt to city life, integrating behavioral and physiological approaches with research on how systemic racism shapes urban ecosystems.

faculty.name: Whendee Silver
faculty.title: Professor of Ecosystem Ecology and Biogeochemistry
faculty.summary: Studies how climate change and human activities affect biogeochemical cycling, focusing on carbon sequestration in grasslands, peatlands, and tropical forests, including the Marin Carbon Project.

faculty.name: Scott Stephens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies wildland fire's interactions with ecosystems, including prehistoric fire regimes, current impacts, fire policy, and Indigenous-led ecosystem stewardship under climate change.

faculty.name: Lucas Vargas Zeppetello
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates land-atmosphere interactions, climate variability, and ecosystem responses to climate change.

faculty.name: Ian Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how environmental variation drives genetic, phenotypic, and ecological diversity across space and time through landscape genomics and evolutionary biology.

faculty.name: Manuela Girotto
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies hydrologic response and human-natural process interactions using land surface remote sensing and data assimilation, including snow hydrology and hydrology's contribution to sea-level change.

faculty.name: Miranda Redmond
faculty.title: Associate Professor in Forest Science and Climate Change
faculty.summary: Investigates how climate and disturbances affect forest dynamics to inform land management, encompassing tree demography, forest disturbances, and ecological forecasting.

faculty.name: John Battles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates forest ecosystem dynamics through quantitative field studies, examining how disturbance and recovery cycles shape forest trajectories under anthropogenic change.

faculty.name: Cort Anastasio
faculty.title: Professor of Tropospheric Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies the photochemistry of tropospheric aerosol particles, cloud/fog drops, and snow. His group examines how sunlight-driven chemistry transforms pollutants in atmospheric condensed phases.

faculty.name: Brian Bailey
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Plant Sciences
faculty.summary: Develops high-resolution 3D plant simulation tools (the Helios framework) that combine engineering, computer science, atmospheric science, and biology to model plant biophysical processes from leaf to canopy scales. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Joseph Biello
faculty.title: Professor of Mathematics
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician who studies wave phenomena in rotating fluid systems and the dynamics of the atmosphere, including tropical wave dynamics.

faculty.name: Christopher D. Cappa
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Quantifies and characterizes the processes controlling the atmospheric evolution of the chemical, physical, and optical properties of aerosols through laboratory and field investigations. Prospective students can apply through either the CEE or Atmospheric Science graduate programs.

faculty.name: Shu-hua Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Atmospheric Science
faculty.summary: Mesoscale meteorologist whose research includes dust transport, hurricanes, and regional climate, using numerical weather prediction and mesoscale modeling.

faculty.name: Margaret Duffy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies equilibrium climate sensitivity, the sea-surface temperature pattern effect, and climate forcings and feedbacks to better constrain future climate change.

faculty.name: Ian C. Faloona
faculty.title: Professor and Biometeorologist
faculty.summary: Researches atmospheric photochemistry, marine biogeochemistry, and the turbulent dynamics of the planetary boundary layer, combining airborne and ground-based field observations.

faculty.name: Adele Igel
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Cloud Physics
faculty.summary: Studies cloud microphysics and aerosol-cloud-radiation interactions and their influence on weather and climate.

faculty.name: Yufang Jin
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Uses satellite and airborne remote sensing to study terrestrial ecosystems, crop monitoring, and fire disturbances, and their interactions with climate.

faculty.name: Michael Kleeman
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies urban and regional air quality with a focus on atmospheric particle size and composition and gas-to-particle conversion, including the health, visibility, and climate impacts of fine particles.

faculty.name: John Largier
faculty.title: Professor of Coastal Oceanography
faculty.summary: Coastal oceanographer whose research includes marine atmospheric boundary layers and transport processes connecting the coastal ocean and atmosphere.

faculty.name: Erwan Monier
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Climate Change Impacts
faculty.summary: Works on climate modeling and uncertainty quantification and on assessing the impacts of climate change on natural and human systems.

faculty.name: Terrence R. Nathan
faculty.title: Professor of Atmospheric Science
faculty.summary: Atmospheric dynamicist who studies large-scale atmospheric circulation systems and the dynamics of atmospheric waves.

faculty.name: Tran B. Nguyen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Chemistry, Department of Environmental Toxicology
faculty.summary: Atmospheric chemist and environmental mass spectrometrist studying atmospheric chemical reactions, atmospheric oxidation, and organic aerosol formation through laboratory and field research.

faculty.name: Holly J. Oldroyd
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Boundary-layer meteorology and environmental fluid mechanics
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric turbulent transport and land-water-atmosphere interactions, including surface exchanges of mass and energy and flows over heterogeneous and mountainous terrain.

faculty.name: Kyaw Tha Paw U
faculty.title: Professor of Atmospheric Science and Biometeorologist
faculty.summary: Specializes in biometeorology, micrometeorology, and boundary layer meteorology, including turbulent exchange between plant canopies and the atmosphere.

faculty.name: Maike Sonnewald
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Computer Science
faculty.sub_field: Machine learning for ocean and climate science
faculty.summary: Uses dynamical systems theory and machine learning to study the ocean and its role in climate.

faculty.name: Chijun Sun
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Paleoclimatology
faculty.summary: Studies past climate change using organic geochemical biomarkers combined with climate model simulations.

faculty.name: Paul A. Ullrich
faculty.title: Professor, Regional and Global Climate Modeling
faculty.summary: Works on regional climate change, variable-resolution global climate modeling, and extreme weather events, including development of numerical methods and analysis tools for climate models.

faculty.name: Anthony S. Wexler
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor; Director, Air Quality Research Center
faculty.summary: Directs the UC Davis Air Quality Research Center and studies particulate pollutant transport and urban and regional smog.

faculty.name: Qi Zhang
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Environmental Toxicology
faculty.summary: Investigates the chemical and physical properties and lifecycle processes of atmospheric aerosols and fog droplets.

faculty.name: Heather Bischel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental health engineering and water sustainability. Studies water and wastewater treatment for emerging contaminants, PFAS bioaccumulation, rapid virus detection, and resource-oriented sanitation. Led UC Davis wastewater-based SARS-CoV-2 epidemiology monitoring during COVID-19.

faculty.name: Christopher Cappa
faculty.title: Professor and Vice Chair
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric aerosols and air quality, investigating the physical, chemical, and optical properties of particulate matter. Studies how aerosols influence climate and develops improved representations of aerosol processes in climate models.

faculty.name: Anthony S. Wexler
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading researcher on air pollution aerosols and atmospheric chemistry. Studies the formation and evolution of particulate matter in urban environments, the chemical mechanisms of smog formation, and the human health effects of airborne particles.

faculty.name: Michael J. Kleeman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on urban and regional air quality, focusing on the characterization, modeling, and source apportionment of atmospheric aerosols. Studies how emissions from different sources contribute to particulate matter pollution and associated health impacts.

faculty.name: Frank J. Loge
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Center for Water-Energy Efficiency
faculty.summary: Research on water-energy efficiency and sustainable systems, studying water reuse technologies, urban and agricultural water demand management, and data-driven optimization of water distribution systems. NSF CAREER Award recipient.

faculty.name: Thomas M. Young
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental chemistry applied to water quality and contaminated site remediation. Studies the fate and transport of organic pollutants in environmental systems, with focus on sorption processes and the development of remediation technologies.

faculty.name: Jeannie L. Darby
faculty.title: Professor and Vice Chair
faculty.summary: Research on water and wastewater treatment processes, focusing on disinfection technologies and the removal of inorganic contaminants including arsenic and chromium from drinking water supplies.

faculty.name: Maureen Kinyua
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on biological wastewater treatment and resource recovery, with emphasis on anaerobic digestion and waste-to-energy technologies. Applies engineering approaches to improve sanitation and energy access in developing communities.

faculty.name: Fabian Bombardelli
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental fluid mechanics and multiphase flow, developing computational models for turbulent flows, sediment transport, and bubble plumes. Applications include reservoir oxygenation, river restoration, and coastal engineering.

faculty.name: Jonathan Herman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water resources planning and management under uncertainty. Develops multi-objective optimization and decision support frameworks to help water agencies adapt to climate change, manage reservoir operations, and balance competing water demands.

faculty.name: Holly Oldroyd
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental fluid dynamics at the land-water-atmosphere interface. Studies turbulent transport processes in lakes, reservoirs, and coastal waters, and their interactions with atmospheric boundary layer dynamics.

faculty.name: Jason T. DeJong
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in biogeotechnical engineering, developing biologically-mediated soil improvement methods such as microbial-induced carbonate precipitation (MICP) for ground improvement, liquefaction mitigation, and erosion control. Also advances in-situ site characterization methods.

faculty.name: Ross W. Boulanger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Internationally recognized expert in geotechnical earthquake engineering. Research focuses on liquefaction assessment, seismic soil-structure interaction, and ground improvement methods. His work has significantly influenced professional practice codes worldwide.

faculty.name: Katerina Ziotopoulou
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on geotechnical earthquake engineering, developing advanced constitutive models and numerical methods for simulating soil behavior during seismic loading, with focus on liquefaction and cyclic soil response.

faculty.name: Amit Kanvinde
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research on the seismic performance and design of steel structures, focusing on fracture and fatigue at extreme limit states. His work on column base connections and steel brace behavior has been incorporated into national design specifications (AISC). Recipient of the ASCE Norman Medal.

faculty.name: Michele Barbato
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on nonlinear structural analysis and dynamics, structural reliability under multiple natural hazards, climate adaptation of infrastructure, and sustainable construction materials including earth-block and bio-based building materials.

faculty.name: John E. Bolander
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on high-performance cement-based materials for civil infrastructure, including fiber-reinforced cement composites, durability assessment, structural health monitoring, and computational modeling of material behavior.

faculty.name: Alejandro Martinez
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on geotechnical engineering including bio-inspired approaches to soil-structure interface design, deep foundation systems, and innovative site characterization methods. Studies geomaterial behavior at multiple scales.

faculty.name: Alissa Kendall
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Institute of Transportation Studies
faculty.summary: Research on life cycle assessment and industrial ecology applied to transportation, energy, and infrastructure systems. Studies the environmental impacts of electric vehicles, bioenergy pathways, and sustainable materials for infrastructure.

faculty.name: Miguel A. Jaller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable urban freight systems, city logistics, and humanitarian logistics. Studies the impacts of e-commerce on transportation systems and develops strategies for reducing freight-related emissions and congestion in urban areas.

faculty.name: Kari Edison Watkins
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable transportation systems, focusing on public transit, bicycling, and pedestrian infrastructure. Studies how technology and information can improve transit accessibility and promote active transportation modes.

faculty.name: Sabbie Miller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable infrastructure materials and the built environment. Assesses the environmental impacts of construction materials using life cycle methods, and develops low-carbon alternatives including bio-derived and polymeric materials for infrastructure.

faculty.name: Yueyue Fan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on network optimization and stochastic modeling applied to transportation and infrastructure systems. Develops methods for planning and managing networks under uncertainty, with focus on resilience to disruptions and natural hazards.

faculty.name: Sashi K. Kunnath
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on structural engineering and earthquake engineering, focusing on nonlinear modeling of reinforced concrete and steel structures under extreme loading, progressive collapse analysis, and performance-based seismic design.

requirements.admission_model: Priority deadline Dec 15, final deadline Jan 15

faculty.name: Mo Li
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies unconventional infrastructure materials and their interfaces with structural engineering, advanced manufacturing, spatial damage sensing, and the environment.

faculty.name: Mohammad Javad Abdolhosseini Qomi
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches the mechanics and physics of multi-scale porous materials, nano- and meso-chemo-mechanics, and engineering advanced materials for sustainable infrastructures.

faculty.name: Amir AghaKouchak
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Director of the Center for Hydrometeorology and Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Works on hydrology, climatology, remote sensing of the environment, climate extremes, the water-energy nexus, climate change, stochastic modeling, and water resources management.

faculty.name: Tirtha Banerjee
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric boundary layer dynamics, turbulent fluid dynamics, land/water/vegetation-atmosphere interactions, wildfires, vegetation dynamics, carbon and water cycles, and wetlands.

faculty.name: Elisa Borowski
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches transportation equity and justice, shared mobility, community needs and impacts, and travel behavior during extreme events.

faculty.name: Matthew Dean
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on sustainable transportation and energy systems, transportation-energy modeling and decision-making, and shared automated vehicles.

faculty.name: Russell Detwiler
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies groundwater hydrology, contaminant fate and transport, subsurface process modeling, and groundwater/surface-water interaction.

faculty.name: Efi Foufoula-Georgiou
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches hydrology and geomorphology with emphasis on modeling the interactions between the atmosphere, land, and the terrestrial environment at plot to large-watershed scale.

faculty.name: Shakira Hobbs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Samueli Faculty Development Chair
faculty.summary: Focuses on global sustainability, resource recovery from anthropogenic waste, and life cycle thinking applied to engineering equity at the food-energy-water nexus.

faculty.name: Kuo-Lin Hsu
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on remote sensing of precipitation, hydrologic systems modeling, stochastic hydrology, and water resources systems planning.

faculty.name: Michael Hyland
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches the modeling, design, control, and analysis of smart city transportation systems, with emphases on shared-use autonomous mobility services and urban transit networks.

faculty.name: R. Jayakrishnan
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in transportation systems analysis.

faculty.name: Sunny Jiang
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies water pollution microbiology, environmental biotechnology, and aquatic microbial ecology.

faculty.name: Wenlong Jin
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches intelligent transportation systems, traffic flow theory, and transportation network analysis.

faculty.name: Anne Lemnitzer
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on geotechnical and earthquake engineering, soil-structure interaction, reinforced concrete design, and seismic monitoring.

faculty.name: Michael McNally
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Urban Planning and Public Policy
faculty.summary: Studies travel behavior and transportation systems analysis.

faculty.name: Ayman Mosallam
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Materials & Manufacturing Engineering Technology
faculty.summary: Researches advanced composites and hybrid systems, seismic repair and rehabilitation of structures, structural health monitoring, 3D printing in construction, and sustainable building technology.

faculty.name: Christopher Olivares Martinez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on environmental biotechnology, remediation, metabolomics, pollutant fate in natural and engineered systems, microbial toxicity, and environmental organic chemistry.

faculty.name: Diego Rosso
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Materials Science and Engineering; Director of the UCI WEX Center
faculty.summary: Studies environmental process engineering, mass transfer, wastewater treatment, and carbon- and energy-footprint analysis.

faculty.name: Brett Sanders
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Urban Planning and Public Policy
faculty.summary: Researches urban, coastal, and dam-break flooding, flood hazard modeling and risk management, sediment management, shallow-water hydrodynamics and morphodynamics, computational methods, and drone-based remote sensing.

faculty.name: Jean-Daniel Saphores
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Economics; Planning, Policy, and Design; Director, Institute of Transportation Studies-Irvine
faculty.summary: Studies transportation and environmental systems with a focus on air pollution and energy use, travel behavior analysis, alternative fuel and automated vehicles, transit use, sustainable infrastructure management, and decision making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Soroosh Sorooshian
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Samueli Endowed Chair in Engineering, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Earth System Science; Founding Director of th
faculty.summary: Works on hydrology, hydrometeorology and hydroclimate modeling, remote sensing, and water resources management.

faculty.name: Lizhi Sun
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches micro- and nano-mechanics, composites and nanocomposites, smart materials and structures, multiscale modeling, and elastography.

faculty.name: Jasper Vrugt
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Examines how (eco)systems work, why theories deviate from data, and how scientists diagnose change, as applied to biogeosciences, ecology, geomorphology, geophysics, hydrology, and soils.

faculty.name: Farzin Zareian
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on structural engineering, performance-based earthquake engineering, structural reliability, and structural control.

requirements.admission_model: Priority Dec 1, rolling through Jan 18; Fall entry only

faculty.name: Jane Wilson Baldwin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies how large-scale atmospheric dynamics influence regional climate and extreme events, including heat waves, tropical cyclones, and mountain effects on precipitation, using climate models and observations with applications to climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Claudia I Czimczik
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Investigates the carbon cycle and how terrestrial ecosystems regulate atmospheric composition and climate using field observations and radiocarbon, with a focus on permafrost soils, vegetation carbon allocation, aerosols, and urban and natural greenhouse gas emissions.

faculty.name: Henri Drake
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies the geophysical fluid dynamics of the ocean and its role in the climate system, using mathematical models and supercomputer simulations to address questions about sea level rise and ocean circulation.

faculty.name: Mark England
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Uses Earth System Models to study polar climate change and Arctic sea ice, and leads the MethaneMIP project quantifying the climate benefits of methane emission cuts.

faculty.name: Lyssa Freese
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Combines climate science, atmospheric chemistry, and social-systems research on climate and pollution, focusing on pollution during net-zero energy transitions and on climate attribution, reversibility, and uncertainty.

faculty.name: Michael L Goulden
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies how terrestrial ecosystems exchange gases and energy with the atmosphere and the controls on those exchanges across tropical, wetland, and boreal environments in relation to climate change.

faculty.name: Alex B Guenther
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His Biosphere Atmosphere Interactions group investigates biosphere-atmosphere interactions across scales to improve prediction of biogeochemical fluxes, atmospheric composition, air pollution, climate, and ecosystem health through field, laboratory, and modeling studies.

faculty.name: Kathleen Rose Johnson
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Reconstructs past climate from geochemical signatures in speleothems from Southeast Asia and North America, developing and testing proxies in modern cave systems and building high-resolution dated records for comparison with climate models.

faculty.name: Saewung Kim
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His group investigates atmospheric gas composition to assess air quality, evaluate human impacts on the atmosphere, and forecast future atmospheric change.

faculty.name: Gudrun Magnusdottir
faculty.title: Chancellor's Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Her group studies atmospheric circulation across time scales and its drivers, including internal dynamics and external influences such as the ocean, sea ice, volcanic eruptions, and greenhouse gases, as well as energy and water transport in the climate system.

faculty.name: Adam C Martiny
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His lab investigates how environmental change affects marine microbial diversity and its role in cycling carbon, nutrients, and oxygen in the ocean, combining culture experiments, genomics, big-data analysis, and modeling.

faculty.name: Jefferson Keith Moore
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies how marine phytoplankton and other ocean biota influence the cycling of carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, iron, and silicon using computer modeling and satellite data, linking ocean biogeochemistry to climate.

faculty.name: Leonard Ohenhen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His lab studies Earth surface stability and resilience, bridging geophysics, hydrology, and remote sensing, and integrates satellite geodesy, machine learning, and modeling to study natural hazards, environmental change, and societal vulnerability.

faculty.name: Michael J Prather
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Works in atmospheric chemistry, including ozone assessments and atmospheric modeling such as the Fast-J photolysis scheme, and has been involved in IPCC climate assessments.

faculty.name: Francois William Primeau
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Combines satellite, ship, and drifter observations with numerical ocean models and Bayesian methods to estimate ocean circulation and biogeochemistry, developing inverse models of nutrient cycling, the biological carbon pump, and marine nitrogen processes.

faculty.name: James T Randerson
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His Climate, Carbon, and Fire Dynamics Lab investigates how humans alter biogeochemical cycles and climate using satellite imagery, Earth system models, machine learning, and field observations, focusing on human-ecosystem-climate interactions.

faculty.name: Angela Rigden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Her group studies how water moves from soils through plants to the atmosphere and how this changes with warming, using satellite data, field measurements, and Earth system models to assess impacts on ecosystems, agriculture, and society.

faculty.name: Eric Rignot
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: His group studies the interactions of the Antarctic and Greenland ice sheets with climate to project sea level rise this century, using satellite remote sensing, airborne surveys, ocean modeling, and fieldwork.

faculty.name: Eric Steven Saltzman
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Investigates the global cycling and atmospheric impacts of trace gases through modern atmospheric measurements and analysis of ancient air trapped in polar ice cores, examining human alteration of atmospheric composition and links between trace gases and climate.

faculty.name: Isabella Velicogna
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Uses satellite remote sensing, especially GRACE time-variable gravity, together with GPS, altimetry, reanalysis, and regional climate models to study ice sheet mass balance and high-latitude hydrology.

faculty.name: Jin-Yi Yu
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies ENSO dynamics and diversity, including the formation, evolution, and interactions of different El Nino/La Nina types with monsoons, other ocean basins, and global climate, using coupled atmosphere-ocean modeling and analysis of tropical-extratropical teleconnections.

faculty.name: Charlie Zender
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Earth System Science
faculty.summary: Studies how trace gases, aerosols, and surface properties interact with Earth's radiation and climate, developing models of mineral dust, snow albedo, aerosol effects on oceans, and wind-driven energy exchange.

requirements.admission_model: Tracks in Environmental Toxicology and Environmental Epidemiology; GRE not required Fall 2026 only

faculty.name: Scott Bartell
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Vice Dean, Academic Affairs
faculty.summary: Has dedicated his research to quantifying human exposures and health effects caused by environmental contaminants, including perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), with a focus on contamination and epidemiology in U.S. water sources.

faculty.name: Stephen Bondy
faculty.title: Professor of Medicine, Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: His work focuses on brain aging and the environmental and dietary agents that promote or retard such aging.

faculty.name: Saurabh Chatterjee
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Professor of Medicine, UCI School of Medicine
faculty.summary: Investigates host-microbiome interactions with redox biology, neuroimmune pathology, and gut-brain interactions in pro-inflammatory disease processes, including Gulf War illness and chronic multi-symptom illnesses.

faculty.name: Andrea De Vizcaya Ruiz
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Graduate Program Director, Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: An environmental toxicologist who focuses on the effects of particulate matter, microplastics, and nanomaterials toxicity on the human body, using laboratory and animal models to evaluate tissue injury, inflammation, and oxidative stress from environmental and occupational exposures.

faculty.name: Rufus Edwards
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how emissions affect populations globally, examining exposures to combustion byproducts in industrialized nations and household solid fuel emissions in less industrialized regions; a widely cited expert on solid fuels used for cooking and heating.

faculty.name: Jill Johnston
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Her research centers on community-driven approaches to understand health impacts from industrial pollution and energy production, partnering with local organizations and residents on air pollution, oil and gas extraction, extreme heat, and environmental justice issues.

faculty.name: Masashi Kitazawa
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular pathogenesis of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease and how environmental toxicants affect neuroinflammation, examining cellular signaling pathways in brain immune cells using experimental models and cultured cells.

faculty.name: Karen Lincoln
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental and Occupational Health; Director of Center for Environmental Health Disparities Research
faculty.summary: Investigates psychosocial, behavioral, and biological mechanisms that link social determinants to health and well-being, focusing on improving outcomes for Black Americans, older adults, and marginalized populations.

faculty.name: Ulrike Luderer
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Director, Center for Occupational & Environmental Health
faculty.summary: A reproductive toxicologist focused on how toxicants and ionizing radiation disrupt reproductive function and on mechanisms that protect against such disruption, including effects on ovarian health across generations.

faculty.name: Marizen Ramirez
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Associate Dean for Diversity, Equity & Inclusion
faculty.summary: Specializes in injury and violence prevention, examining factors that influence injury rates among workers and exploring community-based approaches to preventing violence.

faculty.name: David Richardson
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Associate Dean of Research
faculty.summary: Designs studies to explore potential occupational health hazards so that employers and policymakers can construct safer working environments, with interests including radiation exposure and cancer risk, heat-related worker injury, and novel epidemiological methods.

faculty.name: Nicole Sparks
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how environmental toxicants, particularly tobacco product exposure, affect fetal development, using stem cell and animal models to examine changes in microRNA and mRNA expression that cause embryonic bone-cell defects.

faculty.name: Veronica Vieira
faculty.title: Chair & Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health
faculty.summary: Conducts epidemiologic analyses of health data to examine the contributions of known risk factors and environmental exposures to underlying patterns of disease risk, using geographic methods to model environmental exposures and identify communities facing elevated health risks.

faculty.name: Jun Wu
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental & Occupational Health; Director, Graduate Programs in Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Examines influences of various environmental exposures, such as air pollution, climate, and the built environment, on reproductive and children's health outcomes through population-based epidemiological studies.

funding.note: TA/RA and Graduate Division fellowships; most funding to PhD applicants

faculty.name: Yousef Bozorgnia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on earthquake engineering and engineering seismology, including ground motion hazard analysis, seismic hazard mapping, and performance-based seismic design of structures.

faculty.name: Scott J. Brandenberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in geotechnical earthquake engineering with focus on soil-structure interaction, liquefaction assessment, geophysical imaging for site characterization, and seismic performance of levee systems.

faculty.name: Jiann-Wen (Woody) Ju
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in damage mechanics, computational plasticity, micromechanics, and durability modeling of concrete and composite materials. Develops multiscale computational methods for structural materials.

faculty.name: Jonathan P. Stewart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research covers geotechnical earthquake engineering, soil-structure interaction, engineering seismology, site response analysis, and seismic hazard assessment for critical infrastructure.

faculty.name: Ertugrul Taciroglu
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in computational structural and solid mechanics, constitutive modeling, structural health monitoring, performance-based earthquake engineering, and soil-structure interaction. NSF CAREER Award recipient and ASCE Fellow.

faculty.name: John W. Wallace
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on earthquake engineering design methodologies, seismic evaluation and retrofit of existing structures, and large-scale experimental testing of structural components and systems.

faculty.name: Jian Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in earthquake engineering, structural dynamics and mechanics, seismic protective devices and strategies, soil-structure interaction, and bridge engineering performance assessment.

faculty.name: Sriram Narasimhan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on structural dynamics, system identification, uncertainty quantification, and sensing and monitoring of civil infrastructure systems for condition assessment and decision-making.

faculty.name: Eric M.V. Hoek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in physical and chemical environmental processes, colloidal and interfacial phenomena, membrane separations for water treatment and desalination, and environmental applications of nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Jennifer A. Jay
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies aquatic chemistry and environmental microbiology, with emphasis on mercury biogeochemistry, contaminant fate and transport in aquatic systems, and water quality in natural and engineered environments.

faculty.name: Michael K. Stenstrom
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on process development and control for water and wastewater treatment plants, including biological nutrient removal, activated sludge processes, and stormwater characterization and treatment.

faculty.name: David Jassby
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on water treatment and desalination technologies, membrane separation processes, electrochemical water treatment, and environmental applications of nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Shaily Mahendra
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies environmental microbiology, biodegradation of groundwater contaminants, microbial-nanomaterial interactions, nanotoxicology, and application of molecular and isotopic tools for environmental remediation.

faculty.name: Gaurav N. Sant
faculty.title: Professor and Pritzker Professor of Sustainability
faculty.summary: Pritzker Professor of Sustainability. Research on cementitious materials and porous media, emphasizing chemistry-structure-property relationships, interfacial thermodynamics, and sustainable infrastructure materials including carbon capture via mineralization.

faculty.name: Dennis P. Lettenmaier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: National Academy of Engineering member. Research in hydrologic modeling and prediction, hydrology-climate interactions, hydrologic change detection and attribution, and large-scale water resources assessment.

faculty.name: Steven A. Margulis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in surface hydrology, hydrometeorology, remote sensing of land surface states and fluxes, and data assimilation for hydrologic prediction, with emphasis on snow hydrology in mountain environments.

faculty.name: Ali Mosleh
faculty.title: Professor and Evalyn Knight Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: National Academy of Engineering member. Research on reliability engineering, physics of failure modeling, system life prediction, resilient systems design, and probabilistic risk assessment for complex engineered systems.

faculty.name: Enrique A. López-Droguett
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in risk and reliability sciences, Bayesian inference for structural health monitoring, prognostics and health management, digital twins, and machine learning for reliability and safety of engineered systems.

faculty.name: Henry V. Burton
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Presidential Endowed Professor of Structural Engineering
faculty.summary: Presidential Endowed Professor. Research in performance-based earthquake engineering, seismic design and retrofit, enhanced seismic performance systems, and building community resilience through integrated risk assessment.

faculty.name: Idil D. Akin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research in geotechnical engineering with focus on how wildfires alter soil properties and affect slope stability, unsaturated soil mechanics, and bio-inspired geotechnical design drawing from natural systems.

faculty.name: Mathieu Bauchy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on development of high-performance and sustainable glasses and cementitious materials using physics-based and data-driven multiscale simulations to understand structure-property relationships.

faculty.name: Timu W. Gallien
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on urban coastal flood prediction, wave runup and overtopping, coastal hazard assessment, sea level rise impacts, flood control infrastructure performance, and nearshore remote sensing.

faculty.name: Sanjay K. Mohanty
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water quality and quantity changes in urban environments, sustainable urban development at the water-energy nexus, contaminant transport, stormwater capture and treatment, and bioremediation technologies.

faculty.name: Daniele Bianchi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the UCLA Ocean Biogeochemistry and Ecosystem Group, which investigates ocean biogeochemistry and ecosystem dynamics, with research areas including the California Current ecosystem, oxygen minimum zones, and the mesopelagic ecosystem.

faculty.name: Jacob Bortnik
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Bortnik Group, which applies machine learning to study Earth's space environment as part of the department's space physics research division.

faculty.name: Marcelo Chamecki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates turbulence dynamics and transport processes in atmospheric and oceanic boundary layers, combining numerical simulations, field experiments, and analytical methods; topics include particle and pollutant dispersion, forest-canopy turbulence, and large-eddy simulation model development.

faculty.name: Gang Chen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies large-scale atmospheric circulation and transport that shape weather and climate extremes, bridging theory and modeling to understand jet stream behavior and how circulation patterns connect to extreme weather events.

faculty.name: Yue Dong
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies climate dynamics and change, with emphasis on large-scale interactions in the coupled climate system, such as atmosphere-ocean interactions and tropics-extratropics teleconnections, and how these processes relate to Earth's radiative responses and climate sensitivity.

faculty.name: Rob Eagle
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research examines how physical environments influence organism biology, and explores climate history alongside the evolution of life and biomineralization, including experimental studies of species vulnerability to changing ocean conditions.

faculty.name: Rong Fu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the WAVE Research Group studying mechanisms that control droughts, fire weather, and rainfall seasonality and variability over Amazonian and North American regions, using satellite remote sensing of atmosphere-land-vegetation interactions. Elected to the National Academy of Engineering in 2024 for methodologies using satellite data to characterize and predict land precipitation.

faculty.name: Alex Hall
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His group studies how global climate models project future climate impacts, with emphasis on extreme precipitation, snow and water resources, and drought, developing techniques including emergent constraints and downscaling methods to improve climate projections at policy-relevant spatial scales.

faculty.name: Jasper Kok
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His group studies how aerosols, particularly desert dust, interact with Earth's climate system through modeling and observations, examining cloud microphysics, Earth's energy balance, climate feedbacks, and the feasibility of climate intervention strategies.

faculty.name: Qinbin Li
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads UCLA's atmospheric chemistry modeling group, working on global and regional 3-D modeling and analysis of satellite, aircraft, and ground observations using GEOS-Chem, CMAQ, and WRF-Chem, with particular focus on chemical data assimilation, inverse modeling, and biomass-burning aerosols such as black carbon.

faculty.name: Karen McKinnon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research sits at the nexus of climate science and statistics, addressing questions that span the weather-climate divide, the interactions between natural variability and human-caused climate change, and links to downstream human and biological impacts; holds a joint appointment in Statistics and the Institute of the Environment and Sustainability.

faculty.name: James C. McWilliams
faculty.title: Louis B. and Martha B. Slichter Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research is broadly about natural geophysical fluid dynamics in the ocean and climate, encompassing theory, computational simulation, and data analysis; frequent subjects include the general circulation and its variability, mesoscale eddy processes, surface turbulent boundary layers, and submesoscale currents in the upper ocean. Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Jonathan Mitchell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His group investigates the atmospheric dynamics and climate of terrestrial planets, particularly Titan and Earth, drawing comparisons across planetary bodies and using climate models to understand extreme atmospheric conditions.

faculty.name: J. David Neelin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates coupled interactions within the climate system, particularly ocean-atmosphere dynamics and how moist convection processes affect tropical climate variability and precipitation responses to global warming.

faculty.name: Suzanne Paulson
faculty.title: Professor and Center for Clean Air Director
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric chemistry, urban air quality, and pollutant health effects, combining field and laboratory measurements, modeling, bibliometrics, and machine learning to study cloud droplet chemistry, particle sources in cities, and air pollution.

faculty.name: Pablo Saide
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His group investigates air quality and atmospheric composition modeling at local to regional scales, with emphasis on air quality forecasting, data assimilation techniques, and aerosol interactions affecting climate projections, including wildfire smoke modeling.

faculty.name: Ulli Seibt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab investigates how land ecosystems function and interact with the atmosphere, focusing on tropical, Mediterranean, boreal, and arctic systems vulnerable to climate extremes, combining process-based modeling with field observations using carbonyl sulfide and solar-induced chlorophyll fluorescence.

faculty.name: Andrew Stewart
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Dynamical Oceanography Group, which investigates ocean circulation physics with emphasis on high-latitude dynamics, particularly around Antarctic margins, examining how the ocean interacts with the atmosphere, sea ice, and ice sheets using simulation tools.

faculty.name: Jochen Stutz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His group uses atmospheric spectroscopy and numerical modeling to examine air pollution, the impact of the ground and snow on atmospheric composition, wildfires, and plant photosynthesis, and develops spectroscopic instruments for environmental monitoring.

faculty.name: Roger Varney
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Varney Geospace Research Group, which investigates the physics of Earth's space environment, specifically the ionosphere and interactions between the ionosphere, upper atmosphere, and magnetosphere, contributing to understanding of space weather phenomena.

faculty.name: A. Park Williams
faculty.title: Professor (Department of Geography; affiliated faculty in Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences)
faculty.summary: Studies the causes and consequences of hydrological extremes such as drought, focusing on hydroclimatology and bioclimatology and the effects of human-caused climate change on extreme events including heat waves, wildfires, and flooding; his work showed that climate change contributed substantially to the southwestern North American megadrought. 2023 MacArthur Fellow.

faculty.name: Craig E. Manning
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Elected to the National Academy of Sciences in 2026. Leads the UCLA PTX Lab studying high-pressure, high-temperature processes in Earth's crust and upper mantle. Research focuses on experimental petrology, deep carbon cycling, subduction zone fluids, and fluid-rock interactions at mid-crustal to upper-mantle conditions.

faculty.name: Gilles Peltzer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Geology and Geophysics, also a Research Scientist at NASA JPL. Uses satellite geodesy (InSAR, GNSS) to study earthquake tectonics, crustal deformation, and landslide processes. AGU Fellow researching active fault systems and seismic hazard assessment.

faculty.name: Caroline Beghein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and composition of Earth's interior using seismic waves. Research focuses on mantle tomography, seismic anisotropy, and constraining the mineralogy and dynamics of the crust and upper mantle through surface wave and normal mode seismology.

faculty.name: Carolina Lithgow-Bertelloni
faculty.title: Professor and Louis B. and Martha B. Slichter Endowed Chair in Geosciences
faculty.summary: Holds the Slichter Endowed Chair in Geosciences. Research in geodynamics and geophysics, studying mantle convection, plate tectonics, and the thermal and chemical evolution of Earth and planetary interiors using numerical modeling and geophysical constraints.

faculty.name: Lars Stixrude
faculty.title: Professor and Leon and Joanne V. C. Knopoff Chair in Geophysics and Physics
faculty.summary: Holds the Knopoff Chair in Geophysics and Physics. Research uses first-principles quantum mechanical computations to study minerals at extreme pressures and temperatures. Investigates the composition, structure, and evolution of Earth's deep interior and other planetary bodies.

faculty.name: Edwin A. Schauble
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research combines theoretical and experimental approaches to stable isotope geochemistry. Studies isotope fractionation mechanisms to develop new geochemical proxies for paleoclimate, biogeochemical cycling, and planetary formation processes.

faculty.name: Edward D. Young
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Vice Chair for Academic Personnel
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor studying the origin and evolution of the solar system and planetary bodies through high-precision isotope geochemistry. Develops novel mass spectrometry techniques including clumped isotope geothermometry applied to Earth and extraterrestrial materials.

faculty.name: Aradhna E. Tripati
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in paleoclimatology and geochemistry using stable and clumped isotope techniques to reconstruct past climate and environmental change. Studies carbonate formation, ocean acidification history, and climate dynamics across geological timescales.

faculty.name: Tina Treude
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geobiology and marine biogeochemistry with focus on seafloor processes, microbial methane cycling, and benthic-pelagic coupling. Investigates the role of marine sediments in global biogeochemical cycles and ocean deoxygenation impacts.

faculty.name: David A. Paige
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Planetary scientist studying the geology and surface processes of solar system bodies, with emphasis on the Moon and Mars. Research uses spacecraft remote sensing data to investigate planetary surface evolution, polar deposits, and regolith properties.

faculty.name: Lingsen Meng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies earthquake physics and seismology, focusing on rupture dynamics of large earthquakes, subduction zone processes, and strong ground motion. Uses seismic imaging techniques to understand fault mechanics and earthquake hazard.

faculty.name: Seulgi Moon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research integrates geomorphology and tectonics to understand landscape evolution. Studies the interactions between tectonic forcing, climate, and surface processes that shape topography, including sediment routing from source to sink and erosion rate quantification using cosmogenic nuclides.

faculty.name: Mackenzie Day
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Sedimentologist studying aeolian (wind-driven) processes on Earth and other planetary bodies. Research focuses on dune dynamics, sedimentary structures, and using sedimentary geology to interpret surface environments and climate history on Earth, Mars, and Titan.

faculty.name: Hao Cao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies planetary magnetism and interior dynamics including Earth's geodynamo. Research bridges geophysics and planetary science to understand the generation and evolution of magnetic fields in planetary cores and the implications for planetary habitability.

faculty.name: John He
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in structural geology and tectonics, focusing on continental deformation and the evolution of orogenic belts. Combines field-based geological mapping with geochronology and numerical modeling to understand mountain building processes.

faculty.name: Stacy Larochelle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Geophysicist studying mechanical interactions between water and the solid Earth. Research spans fault mechanics and earthquake physics, ice sheet dynamics and subglacial hydrology, and hydrology-driven geodetic deformation using satellite geodesy and numerical modeling.

faculty.name: Peng Ni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in experimental petrology and non-traditional stable isotope geochemistry to probe Earth's deep interior and solar system evolution. Uses high-pressure experiments and isotope analysis of diamond inclusions, lunar samples, and meteorites to understand planetary formation and differentiation.

faculty.name: William I. Newman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical and computational geophysicist studying nonlinear processes in Earth sciences. Research includes geophysical fluid dynamics, Earth rotation variations, computational methods for geophysical inverse problems, and applications of statistical physics to geological processes.

faculty.name: Rachael Jones
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Environmental Health Sciences; Director, Southern California Education and Research Center
faculty.sub_field: Occupational exposure science and industrial hygiene
faculty.summary: Dr. Jones specializes in exposure modeling, simulation studies, and field sampling, with emphasis on the assessment and control of workplace exposures to infectious agents such as respiratory viruses. Her recent work explores equity dimensions in occupational health.

faculty.name: Jesus Araujo
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Environmental cardiology and air pollution toxicology
faculty.summary: Dr. Araujo investigates how air pollutant exposure promotes cardiovascular disease through mechanisms involving vascular oxidative stress and gene pathways. His work examines protective genes such as heme oxygenase-1 and their role in vascular inflammatory conditions like atherosclerosis.

faculty.name: Lara Cushing
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Cushing investigates how social and environmental stressors disproportionately affect vulnerable populations, examining exposure inequities related to climate change, energy development, and water quality. Her work partners with community organizations to inform policy.

faculty.name: Feng Gao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Computational environmental health and exposure modeling
faculty.summary: Dr. Gao investigates how environmental contaminants such as metals, pesticides, and PFAS affect human health outcomes using advanced data analytics and computational techniques. His work spans exposure modeling, toxicity prediction, and epidemiological analysis of disease mechanisms.

faculty.name: Oliver Hankinson
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor of Environmental Health Sciences and Pathology & Laboratory Medicine
faculty.summary: Dr. Hankinson investigates how the Aryl Hydrocarbon Receptor mediates the toxic and carcinogenic effects of chemical pollutants found in smog, cigarette smoke, and overcooked foods. He also examines the role of dietary omega-3 fatty acids in inhibiting breast cancer growth and metastasis.

faculty.name: Michael Jerrett
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Geospatial exposure assessment and spatial epidemiology
faculty.summary: Dr. Jerrett investigates population exposures to air pollution and other environmental variables using geographic information science, examining how exposures affect different demographic groups and their health consequences. His recent work explores how the built and natural environment contribute to physical activity, obesity, and health outcomes.

faculty.name: Yifang Zhu
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Zhu's work focuses on measuring and modeling air pollutant emissions, transport, and transformation, as well as assessing and mitigating the associated health effects. Her scholarship has earned recognition including the NSF CAREER Award and the Haagen-Smit Prize.

faculty.name: André Nel
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Nanotoxicology and air pollution health effects
faculty.summary: Dr. Nel investigates how air pollutants contribute to asthma development, specifically examining the mechanisms by which ultrafine particle-induced oxidative stress generates airway inflammation. His work also encompasses nanobiology and the health effects of engineered nanomaterials.

faculty.name: Beate Ritz
