faculty.title: Professor of Epidemiology and Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Ritz investigates how environmental and occupational exposures affect chronic diseases including neurological conditions, cancers, and adverse birth outcomes. Her work uses geographic information systems to model exposures and examines interactions between genetic susceptibility and environmental factors.

faculty.name: Candace Tsai
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Industrial hygiene and aerosol/nanoparticle exposure assessment
faculty.summary: Dr. Tsai investigates aerosol, nanoparticle, and toxic airborne substance exposure assessment in occupational and environmental settings. Her work encompasses particle sampling techniques, toxicological studies, computational modeling of aerosol exposure, and protective equipment efficacy.

faculty.name: Shane Que Hee
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences; Director, UCLA Industrial Hygiene Program
faculty.summary: Dr. Que Hee's work emphasizes glove permeation of chemicals and the development of sampling and analysis methods for workplace chemicals, using advanced analytical techniques such as GC-MS and ICP-MS. His research spans exposure assessment, risk evaluation, and protective equipment performance.

faculty.name: Timothy Malloy
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences; Faculty Director, UCLA Sustainable Technology and Policy Program
faculty.summary: Professor Malloy's work centers on the relationship between regulatory design and implementation and the structure of business organizations. He has written extensively in the area of risk governance and prevention-based regulation of chemicals and emerging technologies.

faculty.name: Miriam Marlier
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Global environmental change and air quality
faculty.summary: Dr. Marlier is an interdisciplinary environmental scientist who examines interactions between environmental change and public health using remote sensing data and modeling techniques. Her work spans fire emissions and air quality in Indonesia and India, climate drivers of western U.S. wildfires, and disaster response.

faculty.name: Yoshira Ornelas Van Horne
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Ornelas Van Horne's work focuses on addressing unequal exposures to harmful contaminants that affect structurally marginalized populations. Her current projects examine novel air pollution methods, community-based VOC exposure characterization, and the health impacts of federal environmental settlements.

faculty.name: Kirsten Schwarz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Dr. Schwarz investigates environmental hazards and amenities in cities and how their distribution impacts minoritized communities, with particular focus on soil lead contamination and tree canopy equity. Her recent work emphasizes participatory design approaches to green infrastructure.

faculty.name: Wendie Robbins
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Health Sciences; Audrienne H. Moseley Endowed Chair, School of Nursing
faculty.summary: Dr. Robbins' research examines relationships between work, environment, and health, including reproductive health impacts and environmentally induced germ cell damage. She combines laboratory and epidemiological studies with clinical nursing experience in pollution-affected communities.

faculty.name: Jian Li
faculty.title: Professor of Work and Health (Environmental Health Sciences and Epidemiology)
faculty.sub_field: Occupational health and work stress epidemiology
faculty.summary: Dr. Li investigates psychosocial factors, work stress, and health, with emphasis on epidemiology and intervention evaluation. His research examines how workplace conditions affect employee wellbeing and disease risk across diverse worker populations.

funding.note: ~5 yrs support (RA/TA/fellowships) incl. tuition, fees, stipend

faculty.name: Matthew Alford
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies ocean dynamics including internal waves, the mixed layer, abyssal overflows, and turbulence, with an emphasis on ocean instrumentation and observations. Co-leads the Multiscale Ocean Dynamics group at Scripps.

faculty.name: Paola Cessi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops models of the oceanic general circulation and of climate dynamics, focusing on how ocean circulation and ocean-atmosphere interactions shape Earth's climate.

faculty.name: Ian Eisenman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates ocean and climate dynamics with particular emphasis on sea ice, climate feedbacks, and large-scale atmospheric and oceanic circulation.

faculty.name: Falk Feddersen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nearshore (surfzone and inner-shelf) physical oceanography, including coastal wave dynamics, mixing, and numerical modeling of coastal processes.

faculty.name: Sarah Giddings
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies estuarine and coastal ocean dynamics and their interactions, including bio-physical interactions and exchange between estuaries, the surfzone, and the coastal ocean.

faculty.name: Sarah Gille
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Southern Ocean dynamics and remote sensing
faculty.summary: Studies Southern Ocean climate and dynamics, including wind forcing of the Southern Ocean and heat transport by the Antarctic Circumpolar Current, along with satellite oceanography and air-sea exchange.

faculty.name: Jennifer MacKinnon
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates small-scale dynamical processes in the ocean, primarily internal waves, submesoscale instabilities, and associated turbulence, combining field observations with modeling to improve climate model representations.

faculty.name: Matthew Mazloff
faculty.title: Researcher
faculty.summary: Investigates ocean circulation and dynamics, specializing in ocean modeling and state estimation with particular focus on the Southern Ocean, including the Southern Ocean State Estimate.

faculty.name: Mark Merrifield
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies coastal oceanography, including surface and internal waves and sea level variability. Directs the Center for Climate Change Impacts and Adaptation at Scripps.

faculty.name: Sarah Purkey
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Abyssal circulation and ocean heat content
faculty.summary: Investigates abyssal ocean circulation and variability and ocean heat and sea level rise budgets through large-scale ocean observations to understand climate impacts.

faculty.name: Daniel Rudnick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical and biological dynamics of the upper ocean, with particular focus on fronts and eddies and air-sea interactions, using sustained observations including underwater gliders.

faculty.name: Lia Siegelman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geophysical fluid dynamics using satellite data, field observations, and computational models, focusing on mesoscale and submesoscale turbulence and air-sea and sea-ice interactions.

faculty.name: Janet Sprintall
faculty.title: Research Oceanographer
faculty.summary: Studies tropical interocean exchanges including the Indonesian Throughflow, Southern Ocean dynamics, and sustained monitoring of large-scale ocean circulation.

faculty.name: Shang-Ping Xie
faculty.title: Professor and Roger Revelle Chair in Environmental Science
faculty.summary: Investigates ocean-atmosphere interactions and their role in climate formation, variability, and change, through diagnostic and modeling studies across the Pacific, Atlantic, and Indian Oceans.

faculty.name: Nicholas Lutsko
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric dynamics, climate sensitivity, and climate impacts and adaptation, using modeling of the atmosphere and climate system.

faculty.name: Amato Evan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies dust storms and mineral dust aerosols, including their dynamics, climate impacts, and radiative forcing, using field measurements, satellite data, and numerical modeling.

faculty.name: Duncan Watson-Parris
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the climate impact of anthropogenic aerosol through interactions with clouds and radiation, and applies machine learning techniques to improve climate modeling across scales.

faculty.name: Amy Waterhouse
faculty.title: Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Investigates internal waves and mixing, including how global patterns of diapycnal mixing relate to internal wave energy sources and how submarine canyons modify internal wave energy fluxes and coastal processes.

faculty.name: Gunnar Voet
faculty.title: Associate Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Investigates dense overflows, internal waves, and turbulent mixing using shipboard and moored observations, examining how these processes impact the large-scale ocean circulation.

faculty.name: Ivana Cerovecki
faculty.title: Researcher
faculty.sub_field: Southern Ocean and water mass formation
faculty.summary: Investigates Southern Ocean circulation and mode water formation, with particular focus on air-sea heat and freshwater fluxes and eddy dynamics.

funding.note: ~5 yrs support (RA/TA/fellowships) incl. tuition, fees, stipend

faculty.name: Octavio Aburto
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Aburto studies marine reserves and commercially exploited species and their fisheries, coordinating the Gulf of California Marine Program with work across Mexico and Latin America. He is also a conservation photographer documenting coastal ecosystems.

faculty.name: Andrew D. Barton
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Barton is a biological oceanographer studying the physiology, ecology, macroecology, and biodiversity of marine microbes. His group maps phytoplankton distributions by integrating ocean models with large observational datasets to understand climate impacts on marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Simone Baumann-Pickering
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Baumann-Pickering specializes in acoustic ecology, using passive acoustic monitoring to study behavioral patterns, geographic distribution, habitat preference, and predator-prey interactions of marine animals such as beaked whales. Her work advances understanding of adaptations to anthropogenic impacts.

faculty.name: Jeff S. Bowman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bowman studies the diversity and function of marine microbial communities, including bacteria and single-celled phytoplankton. His lab explores marine, terrestrial, and host microbial ecology with interests spanning polar ecology, astrobiology, and bioinformatics.

faculty.name: Anela Choy
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Pelagic and deep-sea food web ecology
faculty.summary: Choy is a sea-going biological oceanographer studying open-ocean water-column and deep-sea food web structure and function, focusing on how organic matter flows through and connects species. Her work examines impacts of global environmental change and commercial fishing on pelagic ecosystems.

faculty.name: Moira Decima
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Decima studies plankton food webs and zooplankton communities and curates the Pelagic Invertebrate Collection. Her lab investigates how these communities respond to environmental perturbations including El Nino, global warming, and ocean acidification.

faculty.name: Julia Diaz
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: The Diaz lab explores the biogeochemistry of ocean health and marine ecosystem functioning, focusing on how interactions between ocean geochemistry and microbiology, especially phytoplankton, shape climate and natural resources.

faculty.name: Amro Hamdoun
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Marine cell biology and environmental toxicology
faculty.summary: The Hamdoun lab studies cellular mechanisms of small-molecule transport of drugs, signaling molecules, and environmental toxicants, pioneering study of toxicant interactions with ABCB1/P-glycoprotein and discovery of Transporter Interfering Chemicals.

faculty.name: Ryan Hechinger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Hechinger works to understand the ecology and evolution of parasites and parasitism, using them to advance broader ecological and evolutionary science.

faculty.name: James Leichter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Coastal biological oceanography and marine ecology
faculty.summary: Leichter's research focuses on coastal biological oceanography and marine ecology, including work in cold-water kelp forests, coral reefs, and Antarctic research diving.

faculty.name: Deirdre Lyons
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Evolutionary developmental biology of marine invertebrates
faculty.summary: Lyons studies gene regulatory networks, evolutionary developmental biology, and the development of marine invertebrates, especially molluscs, annelids, and echinoderms.

faculty.name: Paul Ponganis
faculty.title: Research Scientist (Research Marine Biologist)
faculty.sub_field: Diving physiology of marine mammals and birds
faculty.summary: Ponganis studies diving physiology and behavior in marine mammals and birds, including oxygen management and physiological adaptations to breath-hold diving.

faculty.name: Greg Rouse
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Marine invertebrate biodiversity and deep-sea biology
faculty.summary: Rouse specializes in animal biodiversity and deep-sea habitats such as whale falls, hydrothermal vents, and methane seeps, with research on Osedax bone-eating worms, seadragons, and Xenoturbella. He also curates the Scripps Benthic Invertebrate Collection.

faculty.name: Stuart Sandin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Sandin studies the biology and ecology of coral reef ecosystems, including large-scale efforts to characterize reef health across islands worldwide.

faculty.name: Brice Semmens
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Semmens' research spans coastal, conservation, and coral reef ecology with a focus on fisheries biology and management and population and community ecology.

faculty.name: Jennifer Smith
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Marine benthic and coral reef ecology
faculty.summary: Smith investigates the factors that structure marine benthic communities, focusing on how human impacts affect coral reef and kelp forest ecosystems, including seaweed ecology and ocean acidification.

faculty.name: George Sugihara
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Natural Science (McQuown Chair)
faculty.summary: Sugihara integrates mathematical modeling with ecological theory to study ecosystem dynamics and food-web interactions, with applications to climate change and coastal systems.

faculty.name: Martin Tresguerres
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Tresguerres studies the physiology of aquatic organisms with emphasis on pH and CO2 sensing, cellular signaling, ion transport, and acid-base regulation in marine systems.

faculty.name: Jennifer Taylor
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Taylor studies the biomechanics and physiology of marine invertebrates, especially crustacean exoskeletons and molting, integrating functional morphology with behavior, ecology, and evolution.

faculty.name: Daniel Wangpraseurt
faculty.title: Associate Researcher
faculty.sub_field: Coral ecophysiology and reef restoration engineering
faculty.summary: Wangpraseurt studies the ecophysiology of corals and uses that knowledge to develop tools for reef restoration and engineering, including biomaterials and bioprinting.

funding.note: ~5 yrs support (RA/TA/fellowships) incl. tuition, fees, stipend

faculty.name: Sarah Aarons
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses isotope compositions of natural materials such as mineral dust and sediment to reconstruct paleo-environmental conditions and understand Earth's geochemical evolution. Her group studies the evolution of Earth's surface through time as a function of a changing climate.

faculty.name: Tomas Capaldi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Geologist studying mountain belt development and erosion through sedimentary records, combining field work, geochronology and geochemistry, and forward modeling. His research addresses tectonic-climate interactions and sediment transport systems.

faculty.name: Emily Chin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates igneous and metamorphic petrology, high-temperature and isotopic geochemistry, and tectonics, with projects on volcanism-plate interactions, the evolution of continental lithosphere, and the geology of Western North America.

faculty.name: Catherine Constable
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geomagnetism, paleomagnetism, electromagnetism, and mathematical and theoretical geophysics, using numerical modeling to understand the behavior and evolution of Earth's magnetic field.

faculty.name: James Day
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in high-temperature geochemical and volcanological research, including time-series studies of volcanic systems, evolution of the mantle lithosphere, and planetary formation processes traced through meteoritic materials.

faculty.name: Margo Odlum
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies techniques to document thermal, chemical, and mechanical signatures of deformation and fluid-rock interactions in the lithosphere. Her group integrates field geology, microscale observations, geochronology, and geochemistry to study active and ancient fault zones, including strike-slip faults in Alaska and Yukon.

faculty.name: Vashan Wright
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans granular physics, rock physics, reflection seismology, and planetary geophysics, including studies of slope failure, assessment of water on Mars, and the evolution of fault systems.

faculty.name: Ross Parnell-Turner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geodynamics, marine geology and geophysics, and tectonics, including mantle plumes, volcanic eruption cycles, and how plate spreading is accommodated at the seafloor.

faculty.name: Yehuda Bock
faculty.title: Research Geodesist, Senior Lecturer, and Director of the Scripps Orbit and Permanent Array Center
faculty.summary: Applies precise GNSS positioning and observational networks to lithospheric deformation, earthquake physics, and natural hazards, including tsunami early warning, machine learning for crustal processes, and integration of radar and GPS observations.

faculty.name: Steven Constable
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the electrical conductivity of Earth's crust and mantle using marine electromagnetic methods and seafloor instrumentation, with applications to offshore groundwater, gas hydrate, and tectonic plate boundaries.

faculty.name: Wenyuan Fan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational seismologist investigating earthquake source kinematic and dynamic processes, slow earthquakes, and subduction zone processes, as well as ocean-solid earth interactions using array seismology and marine geophysics.

faculty.name: Yuri Fialko
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies crustal deformation and the physics of earthquakes and volcanoes using space geodesy techniques such as InSAR and GPS, combined with mechanical modeling of active tectonics and rock mechanics.

faculty.name: Helen Amanda Fricker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the cryosphere and polar regions using satellite remote sensing, focusing on ice sheet processes and the role of ice in the climate system and environmental change.

faculty.name: Alice Gabriel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in computational and theoretical seismology and earthquake physics, using high-performance computing to model subduction zone earthquakes, tsunami genesis, and ground motion.

faculty.name: Gabi Laske
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Observational seismologist focusing on structural seismology and surface wave modeling of Earth's interior, including the collection and analysis of ocean-bottom seismic data.

faculty.name: Dave May
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate professor at the Institute of Geophysics and Planetary Physics working on computational geodynamics and numerical methods for simulating solid-earth processes.

faculty.name: Matthias Morzfeld
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops mathematical and computational methods for data assimilation and inverse problems, studying how sampling techniques perform in high-dimensional spaces and applying them to merge models with data across Earth science applications.

faculty.name: David Sandwell
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on geodynamics, space geodesy, and global seafloor mapping, improving the resolution of the marine gravity field from satellite altimetry and measuring deformation along the San Andreas Fault system.

faculty.name: Peter Shearer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Seismologist at the Institute of Geophysics and Planetary Physics whose research focuses on earthquake physics and using seismic waves to study the deep structure of the Earth.

faculty.name: Adrian Borsa
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in geodesy and remote sensing, applying satellite and ground-based measurements to lithospheric deformation, polar science, and land surface hydrology.

faculty.name: Lihini Aluwihare
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies organic matter composition and cycling in the ocean, using analytical and isotopic techniques to trace the origins of marine organic matter and the metabolic diversity of aquatic microbial communities.

faculty.name: Ralph Keeling
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Scripps CO2 and O2 programs and studies atmospheric composition and the global carbon cycle, using long-term atmospheric measurements to understand climate change and Earth's biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Jeff Severinghaus
faculty.title: Professor of Geosciences
faculty.summary: Studies trapped bubbles of gases contained in ice cores to track changes in ancient climate, with broader interests in biogeochemistry, greenhouse gases, and past climate change.

faculty.name: Amina Schartup
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates environmental biogeochemistry and toxicology, focusing on how contaminants such as mercury cycle through and interact with marine and aquatic systems.

faculty.name: John Cottle
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies geochronology to solve tectonic problems, combining isotopic dating with field studies of continental tectonics.

faculty.name: Zach Eilon
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Seismologist working on seismic tomography and inverse methods, using anisotropy and attenuation to image Earth structure and tectonic processes.

faculty.name: Tobias Fischer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Volcanologist focusing on volatiles in active volcanic and hydrothermal systems.

faculty.name: Phillip Gans
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies continental structural geology and tectonics, including geochronology of deformed terranes.

faculty.name: Matt Jackson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses stable and radiogenic isotope systems to study the geochemical evolution of the Earth's mantle and crust.

faculty.name: Chen Ji
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Characterizes earthquake sources using seismological and geodetic observations, including finite-fault rupture modeling.

faculty.name: Gen Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the chemical and physical processes that cycle materials and elements in the Earth system, linking surface processes to geochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Lorraine Lisiecki
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Paleoclimatologist known for correlation of paleoclimate records and development of age models, including benthic isotope stacks used to reconstruct glacial cycles.

faculty.name: Carolina Martinez Gutierrez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies genomics and phylogenomics to study the evolution of life, including the early origins of marine bacteria and archaea.

faculty.name: Robin Matoza
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies volcano seismology, acoustics, and eruption dynamics using seismic and infrasound observations of active volcanoes.

faculty.name: Kristin Morell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the dynamics of tectonically active regions, with an emphasis on subduction zones and active faulting.

faculty.name: Susannah Porter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Paleobiologist studying the rise of complex life, from early eukaryotes to the first skeletal animals.

faculty.name: Morgan Raven
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies organic matter preservation and organic sulfur cycling in marine sediments and the modern and ancient carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Roberta L. Rudnick
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Geochemist and petrologist studying the formation and evolution of the continents and the composition of the continental crust and lithosphere.

faculty.name: Alex Simms
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies Quaternary marine settings and the reconstruction of past sea-level change from coastal stratigraphy.

faculty.name: David Valentine
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Combines geochemistry and microbiology to address ocean-related environmental problems, including biogeochemical cycling of methane and petroleum in the ocean.

faculty.name: Syee Weldeab
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the magnitude and pace of past climate change and climatic feedback processes using geochemical proxy records from marine sediments.

faculty.name: Andre Wyss
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Vertebrate paleontologist studying fossil synapsids and mammals, including their phylogenetics and evolutionary history.

faculty.name: Alyson Santoro

faculty.name: Alex Simms

faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Adrian Stier
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Andrew Thurber
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: David Valentine

faculty.summary: Studies the ocean carbon cycle and marine microorganisms using geochemistry and genomics.

faculty.name: Ian Walker

faculty.name: Syee Weldeab
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Lizzy Wilbanks

faculty.summary: Professor researching biogeochemistry with emphasis on toxic metals and emerging pollutants in aquatic and terrestrial environments.

faculty.name: Elliot Douglas
faculty.title: Professor and EES Department Head
faculty.summary: Professor and Head of the Department of Environmental Engineering Sciences whose research addresses engineering problem solving, cultures of inclusion, and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Andrew Altieri
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Interim Director, Center for Coastal Solutions
faculty.summary: Associate Professor studying coastal ecosystem dynamics and human impacts on biogenic habitats, including the role of dead corals in coral reef ecosystem resilience.

faculty.name: Sara Behdad
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching sustainable product design, electronic waste management, and product lifecycle engineering.

faculty.name: Myoseon Jang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in air resources whose research covers atmospheric chemistry, including secondary organic aerosol formation and multiphase partitioning of pollutants.

faculty.name: William (Bill) Properzio
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor with research interests spanning air resources, water systems, and environmental nanotechnology.

faculty.name: Katherine Deliz Quiñones
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working in environmental biotechnology, with research on the remediation of pollutants and pathogens in environmental systems.

faculty.name: Andreia Faria
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor researching nanotechnology for environmental applications, including engineered nanomaterials for water treatment.

faculty.name: Sungyoon Jung
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor studying air pollutants and micro/nanoplastics, and developing advanced nanomaterials for environmental applications.

faculty.name: Elise Morrison
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor researching coastal ecosystem dynamics and environmental microbiology, including biogeochemical cycling in coastal waters.

faculty.name: Chamteut Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor whose research integrates environmental microbiology and geochemistry with applications to public health.

faculty.name: Kathe Todd-Brown
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in computational biogeochemistry studying soil carbon dynamics and carbon dioxide fluxes using data-model integration.

funding.note: College assistantships; funding tied to Dec 5 priority deadline

faculty.name: Maitane Olabarrieta
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in coastal and oceanographic engineering researching storm impacts on the coast and the generation of meteotsunamis.

faculty.name: Alberto Canestrelli
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in coastal and oceanographic engineering focusing on coastal hydrodynamics and morphodynamics, including numerical modeling of coastal and estuarine systems.

faculty.name: Xiao Yu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in coastal and oceanographic engineering studying turbulent mixing and sediment transport processes in coastal environments.

faculty.name: Nina Stark
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor conducting research in coastal and marine geotechnics, including geotechnical characterization of nearshore and seabed sediments.

faculty.name: Alex Sheremet
faculty.title: University Term Professor
faculty.summary: University Term Professor in coastal and oceanographic engineering with research on ocean wave dynamics and nearshore wave processes.

faculty.name: Arnoldo Valle-Levinson
faculty.title: University Term Professor
faculty.summary: University Term Professor researching coastal and estuarine hydrodynamics and sea-level rise.

faculty.name: Andrew Altieri
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Interim Director, Center for Coastal Solutions
faculty.summary: Associate Professor in Environmental Engineering Sciences and Interim Director of the Center for Coastal Solutions, studying coastal ecosystem dynamics and human impacts on biogenic habitats such as coral reefs.

faculty.name: John M. Jaeger
faculty.title: Professor and Chair
faculty.summary: Professor and Chair of the Department of Geological Sciences researching coastal and marine sedimentology, glacial sedimentary processes, and continental margin stratigraphy.

faculty.name: Jonathan Martin
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor whose research spans geochemistry, hydrology, and hydrogeology, including karst systems, coasts, sea level, and glacial landscapes.

faculty.name: Peter N. Adams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor researching quantitative geomorphology and coastal processes, using real-time instrumentation and surface process modeling.

faculty.name: Mark Brenner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor studying limnology and paleolimnology, including lake sediment records of climate change and human disturbance.

faculty.name: Shuo (Echo) Ding
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor researching volcanology, high-temperature geochemistry, experimental petrology, and planetary geochemistry.

faculty.name: Oana Dumitru
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on sea level reconstruction, paleoclimatology, and geochronology.

faculty.name: Stephen M. Elardo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching planetary geochemistry, igneous and experimental petrology, lunar science, and non-traditional stable isotopes.

faculty.name: Alessandro M. Forte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in global geophysics working on computational geodynamics, mantle convection modeling, and seismic tomography.

faculty.name: David A. Foster
faculty.title: Professor and Interim Associate Dean
faculty.summary: Professor researching tectonics, thermochronology, and geochronology, along with volcanic soil amendments and enhanced weathering.

faculty.name: Robert Hatfield
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor specializing in environmental magnetism and paleomagnetism.

faculty.name: Jorge Lorenzo-Trueba
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor studying coastal landscape evolution, geomorphology, and stratigraphy using quantitative approaches.

faculty.name: Emma (Mickey) MacKie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor researching glaciology, geophysics, and geostatistics.

faculty.name: Ellen E. Martin
faculty.title: Professor and Graduate Coordinator
faculty.summary: Professor and Graduate Coordinator working as a paleoceanographer and paleoclimatologist, using radiogenic isotopes to study ocean circulation and chemical weathering.

faculty.name: Joseph Meert
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor researching paleomagnetism and geochronology of the Mesoproterozoic to earliest Paleozoic and ancient plate configurations.

faculty.name: Raymond Russo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor studying tectonics and seismology, including upper mantle flow and lithosphere-asthenosphere interactions.

faculty.name: Courtney J. Sprain
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor specializing in 40Ar/39Ar geochronology and paleomagnetism, with research on mass extinctions and variations of Earth's magnetic field.

faculty.name: Amy J. Williams
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching astrobiology, geobiology, organic geochemistry, and planetary science, including biosignature detection on Mars.

faculty.name: Seonkyoo (Sean) Yoon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor studying hydrogeology, flow in porous media, reactive transport, fluid-rock interaction, and inverse problems.

faculty.name: Andrew Zimmerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor researching organic geochemistry, biogeochemistry, geomicrobiology, and marine science, including carbon cycling and biochar.

funding.note: Research assistantships (tuition waiver + stipend), fellowships, TAships

faculty.name: Matthew V. Bilskie
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Coastal hydrodynamics and storm surge modeling
faculty.summary: Develops computational hydrodynamic models to simulate tides, waves, storm surge, and rainfall-runoff in coastal environments, assessing hurricane impacts under present and future conditions. His work includes real-time flood inundation forecasting and nature-based features for coastal resiliency.

faculty.name: Brian Bledsoe
faculty.title: Professor; UGA Athletic Association Professor in Resilient Infrastructure; Director, Institute for Resilient Infrastructure Systems
faculty.summary: Research spans hydrology, hydraulics, and fluvial geomorphology, ecological engineering and ecosystem restoration, urban streams and floodplain networks, and water-centered planning for sustainability and resilience. He leads interdisciplinary projects funded by NSF, DoD, and USEPA as founding director of the Institute for Resilient Infrastructure Systems.

faculty.name: Mi Geum Chorzepa
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on infrastructure asset management, bridge analysis, monitoring, and engineering, structural analysis and design, and nonlinear finite element analysis. Work spans composite materials, earthquake engineering, and forensic engineering applications.

faculty.name: Stephan Durham
faculty.title: Juneau Construction Company Professor in Civil Engineering; Assistant Dean for Student Success and Outreach
faculty.summary: Investigates concrete materials and structural performance, focusing on optimization of concrete mixtures, sustainable construction, and the beneficial reuse of waste-stream materials in civil infrastructure. His work includes pervious concrete pavements and bridge engineering.

faculty.name: Christina H. Fuller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates air pollution exposure, pollution reduction strategies, health disparities, and social-environmental interactions. She applies expertise in exposure science and epidemiology to advance environmental health and justice research through her Engineering Environmental Equity (E3) Lab.

faculty.name: David Gattie
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on energy policy and integrated energy resource planning for the electric power sector, with emphasis on U.S. national security implications of energy technologies. He directs UGA's Energy Security Studies Program, a collaboration between the College of Engineering and the School of Public & International Affairs.

faculty.name: Alysha Helmrich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates urban resilience and how infrastructure systems navigate periods of stability and instability, working within complexity and social-ecological-technological systems (SETS) frameworks. She coordinates the Future Cities Podcast to catalyze action on urban resilience.

faculty.name: Jenna Jambeck
faculty.title: Regents' Professor; Georgia Athletic Association Distinguished Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Plastic pollution and solid waste management
faculty.summary: Studies solid and hazardous waste management and plastic pollution in the oceans; she co-created the Marine Debris Tracker app and leads the Circularity Informatics Lab. She was named a 2022 MacArthur Fellow for her work on plastic waste.

faculty.name: Sung-Hee "Sonny" Kim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Transportation geotechnics and infrastructure health monitoring
faculty.summary: Employs nondestructive testing, remote sensing, and machine learning for resilient and sustainable infrastructure health monitoring. His work spans transportation geotechnics, pavement design, climate-resilient infrastructure, and saltmarsh restoration.

faculty.name: Ke "Luke" Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the resilience of water systems, with emphasis on water and wastewater treatment and urban sustainability in environmental engineering.

faculty.name: Qianwen Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Cooperative Automated Mobility Innovations (CAMIs) Lab, enhancing transportation safety and efficiency using emerging technologies such as vehicle automation, communication, electrification, and smart infrastructure. Her interests include AI-powered traffic data collection and analytics and traffic flow theory.

faculty.name: Maximilian E. Ororbia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops architectural, engineering, and construction design processes and fabrication technologies for the smart built environment, creating approaches for sustainable and resilient infrastructure using advanced manufacturing, materials, and artificial intelligence. Interests include design space exploration, topology optimization, concrete 3D printing, and experimental structural testing.

faculty.name: Whitney Pagan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates urban hydrology, agricultural water availability and quality, stormwater mitigation, green infrastructure, and circular agricultural systems. She combines field monitoring and modeling to address water quality challenges across natural, urban, and agricultural landscapes.

faculty.name: Rawad Saleh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how atmospheric particulate matter (aerosols) influences air quality and climate, with emphasis on climate warming from light-absorbing aerosols from combustion sources. His Air Quality and Climate Research Laboratory combines laboratory experiments with chemical transport and radiative transfer modeling.

faculty.name: Félix Santiago-Collazo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on compound flood modeling and multi-flood hazard assessments, developing hybrid infrastructure solutions and smart water systems. His Compound Inundation Team for Resilient Applications (CITRA) emphasizes water equity and community flood resilience.

faculty.name: Linbing Wang
faculty.title: Professor; Director, The Sensing and Perception Lab
faculty.summary: Directs the Sensing and Perception Lab, focusing on smart, sustainable, and resilient infrastructures, sensing and perception for safety and security, and digital twin technologies. His work includes pavement testing and design, material genome approaches for multifunctional materials, and data analytics for asset management.

faculty.name: C. Brock Woodson
faculty.title: Professor; Co-Director, Institute for Resilient Infrastructure Systems
faculty.sub_field: Environmental fluid mechanics and coastal oceanography
faculty.summary: Investigates environmental fluid mechanics, mixing and transport processes, and coastal oceanography, with focus on how biophysical interactions influence ocean ecosystems. His work addresses fisheries, climate change, and sustainable use of marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Jidong Yang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies advanced data analytics and AI techniques to transportation and infrastructure challenges, including statistical and econometric models, data mining, and computer vision applications. He leads the Smart Mobility and Infrastructure Lab with emphasis on sustainable and resilient infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: Handong Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops cyber-physical transportation systems using smart infrastructure technologies and machine learning to improve transportation sustainability, safety, and efficiency. He leads the UGA Mobility Lab, with interests in traffic flow theory and electric mobility.

faculty.name: Doug Crowe
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Applies conventional and laser microprobe stable isotope techniques to economic geology studies of ore deposits and fluid systems. Recent collaborative work extends into geobiological and paleoclimatic investigations, including hot spring environments and archaeological applications of isotope geochemistry.

faculty.name: Avishek Dutta
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads a lab exploring microbial life in extreme environments to inform understanding of the origins of life and potential extraterrestrial biology. His applied research develops microbially driven solutions for bioremediation, biomining, and recovery of critical metals from waste streams.

faculty.name: Brennan Ferguson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates biogeochemical processes affecting contaminant fate in subsurface environments using a multi-scale approach from benchtop lab experiments to field-scale experiments. Emphasizes interactions among organic matter, minerals, and radionuclide transport.

faculty.name: Charlotte Garing
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel laboratory methods using rock cores and micromodels to study fluid flow, multiphase transport, and mineral-fluid reactivity in porous media. Her research supports applications in groundwater systems, the vadose zone, and geologic carbon storage.

faculty.name: Steven Holland
faculty.title: Shellebarger Professor of Geology and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates how ecological communities and sedimentary environments respond to climate and sea-level change through fieldwork and computer simulations, applying sequence stratigraphy to understand how the fossil record is constructed. Recent focus is on nonmarine systems such as rivers and lakes. His faculty page states he is not presently accepting new graduate students.

faculty.name: Christian Klimczak
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the growth of faults and other fractures in active and ancient geological settings on Earth and other terrestrial planets. Integrates field observations, laboratory analysis, and remote sensing data to understand deformation mechanisms and planetary tectonic and volcanic evolution.

faculty.name: Adam Milewski
faculty.title: Professor & Department Head
faculty.summary: Uses field-based data and emerging techniques including remote sensing, UAVs, GIS, and machine learning to address water resource and environmental sustainability challenges, particularly in dryland hydrology. Recent work extends to decarbonization solutions such as carbon capture and storage and geologic hydrogen.

faculty.name: Valentine Nzengung
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops innovative environmental remediation technologies including phytoremediation, microbial mat treatment systems, biochar applications, and in-situ bioremediation of soils and groundwater. His work addresses contaminant degradation, metal immobilization, and sustainable treatment of hazardous waste sites; he is a National Academy of Inventors Fellow.

faculty.name: Mattia Pistone
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines experimental, analytical, and field-based geology to investigate how magmas behave during transport and eruption, including viscosity changes, volatile transfer, and melt segregation in volcanic arc systems. He is establishing the MAGMA MIA experimental laboratory at UGA and is co-PI on the ICDP DIVE drilling project. His UGA page advertises that he is seeking a highly motivated PhD-level graduate student to study gas retention and release in magmas.

faculty.name: Bruce Railsback
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates records of climate and environmental change in Earth's history through the petrography, mineralogy, and geochemistry of stalagmites and other carbonate materials. His work combines microscopic analysis with isotopic and chemical methods to reconstruct past climate across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Paul Schroeder
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates clay minerals and their crystal-chemical structures using spectroscopy, isotope analysis, and diffraction techniques. His research explores how these abundant natural materials interact with biological systems and record environmental change on Earth and in extraterrestrial environments.

faculty.name: Sally Walker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how biological, physical, and chemical factors affect the preservation of marine organisms in modern and fossil deposits, with experimental taphonomy work spanning temperate, tropical, and polar regions. Current projects include Antarctic scallops as ecosystem engineers, middle Cambrian taphonomy, and the Middle Miocene climate transition.

faculty.name: David Fastovich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how complex climate processes produce similarly complex ecological dynamics by examining the geologic record across multiple timescales. Uses organic biomarkers, isotope geochemistry, and proxy-based climate reconstructions.

faculty.name: Andrew J. Grundstein
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches climate and human health, heat vulnerability, and environmental monitoring, with emphasis on heat-related hazards including exertional heat illness in athletes, urban heat exposure, and occupational heat stress.

faculty.name: Anna Harper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies interactions between vegetation, people, and climate, with emphasis on land management, carbon sinks, and ecosystem responses to environmental change. Applies satellite data and ground measurements to improve Earth system model climate projections, and contributes to the IPCC 7th Assessment Report.

faculty.name: Weiming Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Geospatial Data Science & Environmental Uncertainty Quantification
faculty.summary: Quantifies and understands uncertainty from multi-source data including remote sensing, model simulations, and observations. Develops accurate and reliable machine learning models for environmental and Earth sciences, applied to renewable energy forecasting, extreme event forecasting, and water resource management.

faculty.name: John A. Knox
faculty.title: Josiah Meigs Distinguished Teaching Professor
faculty.summary: Researches the dynamics of weather and climate, geoscience education, atmospheric hazards, and meteorological applications of social media data. Serves as Associate Director and Undergraduate Coordinator of the Atmospheric Sciences Program.

faculty.name: Gabriel Kooperman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His research group answers critical questions about Earth's changing climate system that impact freshwater availability, sustainability of natural ecosystems, and environmental extremes such as floods, droughts, air quality, and heat waves, using computational climate modeling across multiple scales.

faculty.name: David S. Leigh
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Geomorphologist specializing in late Quaternary climate changes and human impacts throughout the Holocene, with expertise in fluvial and eolian systems, hillslope processes, soils, paleofloods, and geoarchaeology across the Americas and the Pyrenees. His profile states he is currently recruiting students in Quaternary environmental change, geoarchaeology, paleoflood analysis, river channel change, and geomorphic consequences of dam removal.

faculty.name: Paul Miller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Concentrates on tropical meteorology and climatology, with particular emphasis on Saharan dust transport across the north Atlantic Ocean. Also investigates hurricane impacts and drought processes in the U.S. Caribbean and Gulf Coast.

faculty.name: Deepak R. Mishra
faculty.title: Merle C. Prunty, Jr. Professor and Department Head
faculty.summary: Monitors fragile coastal and inland water ecosystems by combining field spectroscopy with satellite remote sensing, including satellite-based decision support tools for salt marsh conservation, cyanobacterial harmful algal bloom mapping, tidal wetland carbon sequestration, and seagrass mapping. Directs the Center for Geospatial Research and the UGA Small Satellite Research Laboratory.

faculty.name: Thomas Mote
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric circulation's influence on precipitation and ice ablation extremes, including over two decades studying the role of atmospheric blocking and atmospheric rivers on the surface mass balance of the Greenland ice sheet. Recent work examines climate, land cover change, and aerosol impacts on Caribbean precipitation and drought.

faculty.name: Lan Mu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on Geographic Information Science and applies geospatial methods to health disparities and environmental challenges, including spatial analysis of healthcare access, geovisualization, computational geometry, and AI applications in geographic problem-solving. Directs the GIScience Certificate Program and the GIS.HEAL lab.

faculty.name: David F. Porinchu
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Broadly trained biogeographer specializing in Quaternary science, paleoclimatology, and paleoecology. Directs the Environmental Change Laboratory, using lake sediment properties to reconstruct long-term and recent patterns of climate and environmental change in arctic and alpine regions of North America, Central America, and Eurasia.

faculty.name: Fausto O. Sarmiento
faculty.title: Professor of Mountain Science
faculty.summary: Research focuses on montology, Neotropical mountains, critical biogeography, political ecology, and socioecological landscapes and seascapes, with current work on climate change adaptation, mountainscape conservation, and restoration of Tropandean landscapes. Directs the Neotropical Montology Collaboratory.

faculty.name: J. Marshall Shepherd
faculty.title: Georgia Athletic Association Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on hydrometeorological extremes, urban climate, and the intersections of atmospheric sciences with society, using remote sensing, weather-climate modeling, and risk-vulnerability approaches applied to urban flooding, the energy-food-water nexus, and risk communication. Directs the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program.

faculty.name: Suzanne Pilaar Birch
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Geography and Anthropology
faculty.summary: Investigates human adaptation and resilience to climate change and natural resource unpredictability in prehistory, combining archaeological and biogeochemical approaches to study diet, mobility, and settlement from the end of the last ice age through early farming. Directs the Quaternary Isotope Paleoecology Laboratory.

faculty.name: Xiaobai (Angela) Yao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in geospatial data science at the intersection of GIScience, human dynamics, and GeoAI, integrating geospatial analytics with machine-learning/AI techniques for urban transportation and public health applications. Her profile states she is always happy to recruit promising graduate students interested in geospatial analytical techniques for urban and human dynamics.

faculty.name: Merryl Alber
faculty.title: Professor, Director of the UGA Marine Institute on Sapelo Island
faculty.summary: Alber studies the marine ecology of nearshore environments, including estuarine food webs and the impacts of freshwater withdrawal on coastal systems. Her work examines human impacts in the coastal zone and salt marsh vulnerability to climate change and sea level rise.

faculty.name: Clark R. Alexander
faculty.title: Director, Skidaway Institute of Oceanography, Professor
faculty.summary: Alexander studies sedimentary processes on continental margins, stratigraphic sequence formation, and coastal and barrier island geology. His research addresses how shorelines change over time, historical sea level patterns, and human effects on coastal environments.

faculty.name: Holly Bik
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Associate Director, Institute of Bioinformatics
faculty.summary: Bik's lab uses high-throughput sequencing and diverse -omics approaches to explore broad patterns in marine microbes, especially microbial eukaryotes in sediment habitats. Her work bridges biology and computer science to understand the relationship between species and environmental parameters.

faculty.name: Jay Brandes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Brandes uses stable isotopes to identify sources and transformations of organic compounds and nutrients in marine ecosystems. He develops X-ray spectromicroscopic methods to analyze nutrient cycling, organic matter breakdown, and particle sequestration in aquatic environments.

faculty.name: Clifton Buck
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Buck investigates how iron and other trace elements enter the marine environment and influence ocean productivity despite their low concentrations. His work covers aerosol transport, elemental solubility, and trace metal distribution in marine systems.

faculty.name: Adrian Burd
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Burd uses mathematical and computer modeling to understand how marine systems function and how they might change under changing environmental and climate conditions. His work focuses on particle flux dynamics in the ocean and coastal ecosystem productivity.

faculty.name: Renato Castelao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Castelao investigates coastal ocean dynamics and their impact on marine ecosystems, including wind-driven mesoscale flows, flow-topography interactions, and buoyancy-driven flows. His work also examines high-latitude oceanography and the fate of Greenland meltwater in the ocean.

faculty.name: Natalie Cohen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Biological oceanography and marine microbial ecology
faculty.summary: Cohen investigates how unicellular eukaryotes (protists) respond to chemical changes in their environment and how this affects ocean nutrient cycling. She uses laboratory experiments, field surveys, and transcriptomic analyses to understand protist metabolic strategies under geochemical variation.

faculty.name: Daniela Di Iorio
faculty.title: Department Head, Professor
faculty.summary: Di Iorio applies physics to understand ocean mixing, transport, buoyancy-driven flows, and circulation from the coastal ocean to deep-sea hydrothermal vents and hydrocarbon seeps. She uses specialized acoustic instrumentation to measure deep-sea hydrothermal plume characteristics.

faculty.name: Catherine R. Edwards
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Edwards investigates coastal ocean responses to atmospheric forcing near critical latitudes for diurnal/inertial resonance. Her work takes a joint observational and modeling approach to describing the coastal ocean's response to near-resonant sea breeze and land breeze forcing.

faculty.name: Nicholas Foukal
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Foukal studies where, when, and how the ocean stores and transports heat, focusing on the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation, the Gulf Stream, and subpolar gyre dynamics. He uses surface drifters and moorings to examine how freshwater from Greenland and the Arctic affects North Atlantic circulation.

faculty.name: Marc E. Frischer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Frischer's lab investigates the role of microbial diversity in marine environments and studies parasites and pathogens affecting marine organisms. His research emphasizes climate change consequences for ocean life and the development of molecular biological methods for marine science.

faculty.name: Adam Greer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Greer investigates how the physical and chemical properties of the ocean influence zooplankton populations. He uses towed camera systems and computational techniques to study spatial relationships among zooplankton taxa and to gain insight into zooplankton interactions and behavior.

faculty.name: Adrienne Hoarfrost
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Microbial oceanography and biological carbon cycle
faculty.summary: Hoarfrost investigates how microorganisms drive the global carbon cycle and affect marine ecosystems. Her work combines machine learning with high-throughput experimental methods to study microbial processes in ocean environments.

faculty.name: Samantha B. Joye
faculty.title: Athletic Assoc. Professor of Arts & Sciences, Regents' Professor
faculty.summary: Joye's group investigates the complex interactions that drive elemental cycling in coastal and open ocean environments. Her research examines the effects of climate change and anthropogenic disturbances on critical environmental processes.

faculty.name: Patricia M. Medeiros
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Medeiros uses molecular biomarkers to investigate the sources, transport, transformations, and fate of organic matter in aquatic and atmospheric environments. She focuses on dissolved organic matter analysis using advanced mass spectrometry techniques.

faculty.name: Christof Meile
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Meile investigates biogeochemical processes in marine and coastal environments, focusing on microbial metabolism, in silico microbial models, nutrient dynamics, and human impacts at the land-ocean interface. His work includes salt marsh carbon dynamics and iron cycling.

faculty.name: Mary Ann Moran
faculty.title: UGA Foundation Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Moran's lab investigates how marine bacteria process and transform organic carbon and sulfur compounds and how bacteria-phytoplankton interactions affect these processes, including climate-relevant gas formation. The research uses tools from molecular microbial ecology and ecological genomics in laboratory and natural systems.

faculty.name: Jimmy Nelson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nelson investigates how changes in food webs alter the function of marine ecosystems. He uses remote sensing, stable isotope analysis, and mathematical modeling to examine energy flow, biogeochemical cycles, and community structure across habitats from saltmarshes to the coastal ocean.

faculty.name: Daniel Ohnemus
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Ohnemus studies how rare trace elements cycle through marine particles, examining the distributions, composition, and behavior of particles as sources and repositories of elements required by ocean organisms. His work integrates field observations, multi-element mass spectrometry, synchrotron analysis, and biogeochemical modeling.

faculty.name: Sara Rivero-Calle
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Biological oceanography and ocean color remote sensing
faculty.summary: Rivero-Calle's research encompasses ocean color remote sensing, CubeSats, phytoplankton ecology, ocean optics, and submesoscale processes. She also works on biogeochemical cycles, Earth System Models, and big data analysis of marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Jaclyn "Jaci" Saunders
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Saunders studies marine microbial ecology and evolution using metaproteomics to discover aspects of microbial biogeochemical processes, including work on metalloenzymes in the ocean. She has developed computational tools and databases for analyzing marine microbial proteomic data.

faculty.name: Amanda Spivak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Spivak's lab examines the processes controlling carbon transformations and fates in coastal environments and how those pathways are changed by natural and anthropogenic stressors. She also directs the NSF-supported Georgia Coastal Ecosystems Long-Term Ecological Research Project.

faculty.name: Patricia L. Yager
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Yager's research focuses on interactions between climate and marine ecosystems, including how melting ice sheets affect Arctic and Antarctic coastal productivity. She also studies carbon cycling in the Amazon River plume region.

faculty.name: Travis C. Glenn
faculty.title: Department Head, Dean Phillip Williams Professor, Director, Institute of Bioinformatics
faculty.summary: Develops and applies genomic tools to study evolutionary biology and address environmental health and infectious disease ecology challenges. Work focuses particularly on tick vectors and disease transmission systems.

faculty.name: Erin K. Lipp
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Academic Affairs, Georgia Power Professor of Environmental Health, Director of Graduate Studies, EHS
faculty.summary: Her lab investigates microbial ecology and environmental microbiology at the interface of public and ecosystem health, spanning water sanitation, climate change, and coral disease. Current work focuses on bacterial pathogen population dynamics in aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Charles A. Easley, IV
faculty.title: Associate Dean for Research, Interim Director, Institute for Disaster Management, Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the impacts of environmental exposures on spermatogenesis and future generations, and on developing stem cell treatments for male factor infertility. Work also includes identifying compounds for male contraceptive drug discovery.

faculty.name: Luke Naeher
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Department Head
faculty.summary: Faculty member in Environmental Health Science focused on exposure science and environmental air pollution. The directory profile does not provide a detailed research summary beyond his departmental affiliation.

faculty.name: Lili Tang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her lab develops high-throughput C. elegans platforms combined with multi-omics and artificial intelligence to investigate neurotoxic mechanisms and host-microbe-xenobiotic interactions. Particular focus on microbial-based interventions for neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson's.

faculty.name: Jia-Sheng Wang
faculty.title: UGA Athletic Association Professor in Public Health
faculty.summary: Research focuses on molecular epidemiology and prevention of human esophageal cancer and hepatocellular carcinoma, examining how environmental and food-borne toxicants affect human health. Work includes developing biomarkers for carcinogen detection in high-risk populations.

faculty.name: Wentao Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His laboratory investigates the role of DNA damage and repair in mutagenesis and carcinogenesis caused by environmental carcinogens. Work involves developing methods to detect environmentally-induced DNA damage and examining how cell types respond to toxic exposures.

faculty.name: Anne Marie Zimeri
faculty.title: Associate Professor, B.S.E.H. Program Director and Undergraduate Internship Coordinator
faculty.summary: Research focuses on environmental agent exposures from produce and water and on undergraduate environmental education. Her scholarly work emphasizes connecting scientific concepts to real-world applications in environmental health.

faculty.name: John Patrick McCracken
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates health effects of air pollution, particularly from use of solid fuels for cooking in low-income settings. Leads the Guatemalan research center within the multi-country Household Air Pollution Intervention Network (HAPIN) and studies respiratory disease in children.

faculty.name: José F. Cordero
faculty.title: Gordhan L. Virginia B. "Jinx" Patel Distinguished Professor of Public Health
faculty.summary: Investigates how endocrine disruptors and oxidative stress influence preterm births and neurodevelopment through large cohort studies in Puerto Rico, including the PROTECT study and CRECE center. Work also examines Zika infection risks during pregnancy via the ZIP consortium.

faculty.name: Seth Wenger
faculty.title: UGA Athletic Association Professor of Ecology; Director of Science, River Basin Center
faculty.summary: Wenger studies stream ecology and conservation biology, connecting science to natural resource management and conservation decision-making, including work on aquatic organism declines and biodiversity enhancement. He directs the science program at UGA's River Basin Center.

faculty.name: James (Jeb) Byers
faculty.title: Martha Odum Distinguished Professor of Ecology
faculty.summary: Byers researches marine community and population ecology, including biological invasions, marine parasites and disease, and ecosystem engineers. His work examines how non-native species impact native marine communities.

faculty.name: Krista Capps
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Odum School of Ecology (joint appointment, Savannah River Ecology Laboratory)
faculty.summary: Capps investigates how human activities alter community structure and ecosystem processes in freshwater systems, focusing on consumer impacts on food resources and nutrient dynamics in streams and wetlands.

faculty.name: Charles B. van Rees
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Odum School of Ecology
faculty.summary: Van Rees studies freshwater biodiversity conservation and waterbird ecology, with focuses on landscape genetics, wetland management, and sustainable water resources. His work integrates connectivity conservation and stakeholder engagement.

faculty.name: Elizabeth King
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Odum School of Ecology (joint appointment, Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources)
faculty.summary: King's research focuses on community and ecosystem ecology and social-ecological systems, with emphasis on dryland vegetation dynamics, pastoralism in Africa, and coastal salt marsh resilience.

faculty.name: Craig W. Osenberg
faculty.title: UGA Athletic Association Professor in Ecology
faculty.summary: Osenberg studies size- and stage-structured population dynamics and species interactions, meta-analysis, environmental impact assessment (BACIPS designs), competition, and predator-prey interactions across aquatic and community ecology.

faculty.name: Jesse Abrams
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Natural Resource Policy and Sustainability, Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources; Director of Graduate Studies, ICON Ph.D. Prog
faculty.summary: Abrams examines adaptive, participatory, and network governance and co-management in forest and rangeland settings, studying how local communities participate in environmental governance and institutional change from the U.S. to Argentina.

faculty.name: James Beasley
faculty.title: Terrell Distinguished Professor of Wildlife Management, Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources (Savannah River Ecology Laboratory)
faculty.summary: Beasley's research addresses wildlife ecology, management, and population dynamics, including game management, non-game and endangered species, wildlife damage, and disease.

faculty.name: Marguerite Madden
faculty.title: Professor of Geography; Associate Director for Education and Research, Center for Geospatial Research
faculty.summary: Madden specializes in GIScience and landscape ecology, using remote sensing and geospatial analysis to examine human-animal-environment interactions and landscape-scale biological and physical processes.

faculty.name: Deepak R. Mishra
faculty.title: Merle C. Prunty, Jr. Professor and Department Head of Geography; Director, Center for Geospatial Research
faculty.summary: Mishra studies coastal and inland water ecosystems using geospatial technologies, focusing on monitoring harmful algal blooms, wetland carbon sequestration, and sea-level rise impacts through satellite remote sensing and field spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Nathan (Nate) Nibbelink
faculty.title: Professor of Spatial Ecology and Geographic Information Science, Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources
faculty.summary: Nibbelink applies spatial theory and technology to ecological and management problems, focusing on spatial patterns in fish and wildlife populations and GIS tools for natural resource decision-making.

faculty.name: John Maerz
faculty.title: Dennis and Sarah Carey Distinguished Professor of Forestry and Natural Resources; Josiah Meigs Distinguished Teaching Professor, Warnell School of Forestry and
faculty.summary: Maerz investigates animal ecology, evolution, and conservation management, emphasizing the effects of terrestrial and aquatic environmental change on amphibians and reptiles and applying ecology to wildlife management.

faculty.name: David F. Porinchu
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Department Head of Geography
faculty.summary: Porinchu directs the Environmental Change Laboratory, using lake sediment properties to reconstruct historical climate and environmental patterns in arctic, alpine, and sub-arctic regions across North America, Central America, and Eurasia.

faculty.name: James Marshall Shepherd
faculty.title: Georgia Athletic Association Distinguished Professor of Geography and Atmospheric Sciences; Director, Atmospheric Sciences Program
faculty.summary: Shepherd investigates hydrometeorological extremes and urban climate dynamics, employing remote sensing and modeling to address challenges such as urban flooding and weather-climate risk communication.

faculty.name: Merryl Alber
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Marine Sciences; Director, UGA Marine Institute (Sapelo Island)
faculty.summary: Alber studies the marine ecology of nearshore environments, estuarine food webs, and the impacts of freshwater withdrawal on coastal systems, examining how human activities affect coastal zones.

faculty.name: Adam Greer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Marine Sciences
faculty.summary: Greer examines how ocean physical and chemical properties influence zooplankton populations, using towed camera systems and computational techniques to understand their ecological roles in marine food webs.

faculty.name: Ted Gragson
faculty.title: Professor and Department Head of Anthropology
faculty.summary: Gragson investigates agropastoralism in the Pyrenees and Iberian regions over the past 2,000 years, integrating archaeological, geomorphic, and socioecological evidence to understand human landscape change.

funding.note: Grainger PhD guaranteed funded appointment (tuition waiver + stipend) first 5 years

faculty.name: Bassem O. Andrawes
faculty.title: Professor and CEE Excellence Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Specializes in earthquake engineering, bridge design, and seismic retrofitting, with pioneering work applying shape memory alloys to civil infrastructure. Leads the Shape Memory and Structures research group and directs the TRANS-IPIC precast innovation center.

faculty.name: Larry A. Fahnestock
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean for Facilities and Capital Planning
faculty.summary: Researches earthquake engineering, steel structures, seismic building design, structural stability, and bridge performance under service and extreme loading, using large-scale laboratory experiments, field monitoring, and numerical simulations.

faculty.name: Paolo Gardoni
faculty.title: Alfredo H. Ang Family Endowed Chair in Civil Engineering; Director, MAE Center; Professor
faculty.summary: Develops probabilistic models and frameworks for assessing infrastructure reliability and resilience, integrating engineering analysis with risk quantification for natural hazard mitigation, infrastructure deterioration, and disaster recovery planning.

faculty.name: C. Armando Duarte
faculty.title: Nathan M. Newmark Distinguished Professor in CEE; CEE Excellence Faculty Scholar; Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced computational methods including generalized finite element and mesh-free methods, focusing on multiscale and multiphysics problems and three-dimensional linear and nonlinear computational fracture mechanics, with applications from structural analysis to hydraulic fracturing.

faculty.name: Ann Sychterz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies kinematic geometrically nonlinear systems such as tensegrity structures and origami for adaptive civil infrastructure. Her SMARTI lab investigates structural dynamics, form-finding, sensor optimization, damage detection, and deployable structures for resilient infrastructure.

faculty.name: Imad Al-Qadi
faculty.title: Grainger Distinguished Chair in Engineering; Director, Illinois Center for Transportation; Professor
faculty.summary: Works on highway and airfield sustainability, rolling resistance and fuel consumption, resiliency, pavement mechanics, advanced modeling, and tire-pavement interaction, with additional research on autonomous vehicles, energy harvesting, and impacts of electric trucks on infrastructure. Directs the Illinois Center for Transportation.

faculty.name: Yanfeng Ouyang
faculty.title: George Krambles Endowed Professor in Rail and Public Transit; Donald B. Willett Faculty Scholar; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches transportation networks and supply chains, logistics systems, sustainable and resilient infrastructure systems, traffic flow theory and operations, sensor networks, and safety, including work on drone delivery, shared electric scooters, and transit systems design.

faculty.name: Eleftheria Kontou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on transportation and energy systems interdependencies, sustainable emerging transportation systems, and transportation electrification planning and policy, including electric vehicle charging infrastructure and the intersection of energy and mobility systems.

faculty.name: Alireza Talebpour
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on human-automated vehicle interactions, artificial intelligence in transportation, traffic flow theory, and quantum computing applications, addressing modern transportation challenges through computational and AI-driven approaches. Leads the Smart City Lab.

faculty.name: Erol Tutumluer
faculty.title: Abel Bliss Professor in Engineering; Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates nonlinear, anisotropic, and stress-dependent behavior of pavement and railroad track foundations, with focus on testing, modeling, and characterization of pavement and railroad geomaterials, soil/aggregate stabilization, and AI and sensing applications to transportation infrastructure.

faculty.name: Lewis J. Lehe
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how pricing mechanisms and traffic dynamics affect urban transportation systems, including congestion pricing policies, effects of parking requirements on housing costs, and mathematical models of traffic flow. Leads the Urban Traffic & Economics Laboratory.

faculty.name: Jeremy Guest
faculty.title: Department Head and Professor; David C. Crawford Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Advances the circular bioeconomy by developing technologies for sustainable sanitation that manage human waste as a renewable resource, and for sustainable agriculture and conversion of plants to bioproducts and biofuels, using a Quantitative Sustainable Design methodology to inform engineering, investment, and policy decisions.

faculty.name: Helen Nguyen
faculty.title: Ivan Racheff Professor of Environmental Engineering in CEE; CEE Excellence Faculty Fellow; Professor
faculty.summary: Researches pathogens and biofilms in drinking water distribution systems, environmental surveillance of pathogens, and water and food safety, including physico-chemical water and wastewater treatment processes, virus inactivation, wastewater-based epidemiology, and microbial risk assessment.

faculty.name: Ashlynn Stillwell
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Elaine F. and William J. Hall Excellence Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Investigates sustainable water and energy systems with policy applications, including technoeconomic analysis, life-cycle assessment, green stormwater infrastructure, and electricity-water resource interactions in urban and rural settings.

faculty.name: Christopher Tessum
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
