faculty.summary: Models air pollution and its health impacts, quantifies inequities in the distribution of those impacts, and develops solutions, creating models that link emissions to the human activities that cause them and to resulting health consequences, with attention to racial-ethnic exposure disparities.

faculty.name: Vishal Verma
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on assessing the health impacts of ambient PM2.5, examining the oxidative potential of particulate matter and its connection to human health effects through chemical and toxicological analyses.

faculty.name: Ximing Cai
faculty.title: Ben Chie Yen Professor; Ven Te Chow Faculty Scholar in Water Resources; Donald Biggar Willett Faculty Scholar; Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in water resources systems analysis focused on interactions between hydrology and human activity, applying systems approaches and data-driven methods to problems including the food-energy-water nexus, river basin management, reservoir operation, drought management, and interdependent infrastructure planning.

faculty.name: Marcelo H. Garcia
faculty.title: M.T. Geoffrey Yeh Endowed Chair in Civil Engineering; Director, Ven Te Chow Hydrosystems Laboratory; Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in hydraulic engineering including sedimentation, sediment transport, fluvial hydraulics, channel stability, meandering, and bank erosion, with environmental applications such as flood management and river naturalization. Directs the Ven Te Chow Hydrosystems Laboratory.

faculty.name: Youssef M. A. Hashash
faculty.title: Grainger Distinguished Chair in Engineering; John Burkitt Webb Endowed Faculty Scholar; Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on deep excavations and tunneling in urban areas, earthquake engineering, continuum and discrete element modeling, and soil-structure interaction, including applications of deep learning and AI to geotechnical problems. His group developed the DEEPSOIL software for seismic site response analysis.

faculty.name: Scott M. Olson
faculty.title: Professor; CEE Excellence Faculty Scholar; Associate Head and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Researches soil-structure interaction of bridge foundations, static and seismic slope stability, ground response analysis, paleoseismology, geohazard identification, liquefaction engineering and mitigation, and tailings behavior, using field testing, laboratory analysis, and centrifuge modeling.

faculty.name: Larry Di Girolamo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Satellite remote sensing of clouds and aerosols
faculty.summary: Studies cloud, aerosol, and radiation processes using satellite remote sensing and 3-D radiative transfer modeling, characterizing their global properties and changes over the satellite era and their impacts on weather, climate, and human health.

faculty.name: Francina Dominguez
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Head, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Her hydroclimatology research examines interactions between the land and the atmosphere, focusing on how climate change and human land modification affect hydrology and the reciprocal effects of surface hydrology changes on climate.

faculty.name: Kelvin K. Droegemeier
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: His work spans severe convective storm prediction and atmospheric remote sensing, including fine-scale computational models for earth system science and numerical prediction methods for severe weather events.

faculty.name: Deanna Hence
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Radar and satellite studies of convective and tropical weather
faculty.summary: Investigates impacts of hazardous weather on human systems, convective storm systems, tropical meteorology, and orographic precipitation, primarily through radar and satellite remote sensing, with an emphasis on bridging science with policy and public engagement.

faculty.name: Atul Jain
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Terrestrial biogeochemical cycles and land-use change modeling
faculty.summary: Leads global model-data integration studies of biogeochemical cycles and water and energy fluxes, investigating the impact of land use and land cover change on biogeophysics and biogeochemistry, greenhouse gas emissions, and climate change effects on agricultural production.

faculty.name: Steve Nesbitt
faculty.title: Professor and Department Head
faculty.sub_field: Radar and satellite meteorology of precipitation systems
faculty.summary: Leads a research group studying radar meteorology, satellite meteorology, tropical meteorology, mesoscale meteorology, and data science, focusing on convective storm processes and precipitation measurement using advanced observational techniques.

faculty.name: Cristian Proistosescu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates coupled atmosphere-ocean dynamics and how clouds and radiative processes shape climate feedbacks and climate sensitivity, combining modeling, observations, paleoclimate, and statistical inference across multiple timescales.

faculty.name: Shiv Priyam Raghuraman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Earth's radiation budget and cloud feedbacks
faculty.summary: Investigates how Earth's planetary radiation balance is altered by clouds, aerosols, and other atmospheric constituents, combining satellite observations with climate models to understand how radiative changes cause Earth to accumulate heat, with focus on cloud feedbacks under surface warming.

faculty.name: Nicole Riemer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her group develops computer models to track how aerosol particles form, move, and change in the atmosphere, combining simulations with observational data to examine aerosol effects on human health, weather, and climate.

faculty.name: Ryan Sriver
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works to develop a deeper understanding of the physical processes influencing variability within Earth's climate system and to quantify uncertainties in future projections, using observations, statistical methods, and numerical models to study extreme weather events and their drivers.

faculty.name: Robert Trapp
faculty.title: Professor and Director of the School of Earth, Society & Environment
faculty.summary: Investigates severe convective storms, examining their dynamics, associated hazards, and relationships to climate change; author of a textbook on mesoscale-convective atmospheric processes.

faculty.name: Joseph Trujillo-Falcón
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines how speakers of multiple languages comprehend and respond to severe weather and climate threats, bridging atmospheric science and communication through collaboration with practitioners and communities to improve risk communication technologies and strategies.

faculty.name: Zhuo Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates tropical meteorology and climate dynamics, including tropical cyclones, intraseasonal variability, tropical-extratropical interaction, and subseasonal to decadal forecasting, aiming to advance understanding of atmospheric dynamics and predictive capabilities.

faculty.name: Gan Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His group investigates climate risks involving physical phenomena such as cyclones and socioeconomic factors such as the energy transition, combining geophysical fluid dynamics with machine learning and real-world applications.

faculty.name: Alison M. Anders
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Examines interactions between climate, erosion, and tectonics, with a focus on orographic precipitation and anthropogenic influences on landscape evolution. Uses field studies in mountain ranges combined with numerical modeling.

faculty.name: James L. Best
faculty.title: Jack C. Threet and Richard L. Threet Professor
faculty.summary: Research concerns the mechanics of sediment transport, the investigation of modern sedimentary environments, and the interpretation of ancient alluvium, spanning flow-sediment interactions, river dynamics, and bedform behavior.

faculty.name: Ellen Buckley
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in geophysics/geodynamics and global change; recent publications focus on sea ice dynamics, satellite remote sensing of ice conditions, and oceanographic processes in polar regions.

faculty.name: Jessica Conroy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates paleoclimate variability on interannual to millennial timescales, paleolimnology, stable isotope hydrology, and stable isotope geochemistry, examining climate patterns and water cycles using geochemical methods.

faculty.name: Jennifer Druhan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on identifying the underlying processes contributing to chemical variability during reactive transport through porous media, measuring stable isotope fractionations and integrating isotope systems into numerical models of reactive flow and transport.

faculty.name: Bruce W. Fouke
faculty.title: Ralph E. Grim Professor
faculty.summary: His laboratory examines the cross-disciplinary intersection of geology, molecular biology (geobiology, astrobiology), and medicine (GeoBioMed), with projects on the subsurface biosphere, microbial evolution, coral reefs, thermophilic bacteria, and human calcification.

faculty.name: Patricia Gregg
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Head
faculty.summary: Uses thermomechanical models to understand the rheological controls on magma reservoir growth, pressurization, and volcanic eruption, and investigates melting processes at mid-ocean ridges through combined fieldwork and computational modeling.

faculty.name: William Guenthner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on low-temperature thermochronology and tectonics, using radiogenic isotopic systems to understand thermal histories of crustal rocks and to determine the dates and rates of mountain creation and destruction.

faculty.name: Tom Johnson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in environmental geochemistry, isotope geochemistry, and hydrogeology, with emphasis on redox-sensitive contaminant elements such as chromium, selenium, uranium, and mercury using stable isotope measurement methods.

faculty.name: Craig C. Lundstrom
faculty.title: Professor, Department Head
faculty.summary: Investigates processes and time scales of igneous rock formation through a combination of experimental simulation and geochemical observations, focusing on continental crust formation and the origins of silicic magmas at convergent margins.

faculty.name: Ross Maguire
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Seismologist whose recent publications on his profile cover seismic phase detection, crustal structure, and deep-learning models for classifying local-distance earthquakes and explosions.

faculty.name: Hung Nguyen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the feedback loop between the water cycle and humanity, using paleoclimate proxies and climate models to examine water cycle behavior across temporal and spatial scales, and collaborates with historians and archaeologists on societal adaptation to hydrological change.

faculty.name: Cristian Proistosescu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies coupled atmosphere-ocean climate dynamics, including climate sensitivity, clouds and radiative feedbacks, stochastic climate variability and extremes, paleoclimate, and statistical inference and machine learning in the earth sciences (per his official UIUC Climate, Meteorology & Atmospheric Sciences profile; joint appointment).

faculty.name: Gillen D'Arcy Wood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Earth Science and Environmental Change (also Professor of English) who studies climate change, disaster studies, environmental history, and the history of the earth sciences, examining major climatic and geological events through historical and cultural lenses.

faculty.name: Robert Schooley
faculty.title: Professor and Department Head
faculty.summary: Schooley investigates how wildlife species and communities respond to human land use, focusing on the effects of habitat fragmentation and landscape connectivity on mammals. He also assesses outcomes of large-scale restoration projects intended to benefit biodiversity.

faculty.name: Yuji Arai
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Arai uses traditional and molecular-scale approaches to understand chemical processes in soils and at the mineral-water interface. This work allows prediction of the biogeochemical fate and cycling of nutrients and contaminants and risk assessment in aquatic and terrestrial environments.

faculty.name: Jennifer M. Fraterrigo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Fraterrigo works to predict how ecosystems and the services they supply respond to environmental change. By integrating processes operating at different spatial scales, she supports decision-making that promotes ecosystem and landscape resilience.

faculty.name: María Alejandra García Otero
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: García Otero studies the social dimensions of sustainable agriculture, including rural development, environmental justice, and women in agriculture and the environment. Her work is grounded in sociology with a focus on Latin American contexts.

faculty.name: Kaiyu Guan
faculty.title: Levenick Professor of Sustainability
faculty.summary: Guan combines Earth system science, satellite data, supercomputing, and AI/ML to monitor and forecast crop growth, water and nutrient needs, yields, and environmental impacts such as greenhouse gas emissions and soil carbon change. His research spans agroecosystem modeling, cross-scale remote sensing, and terrestrial carbon and biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Jon Henn
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Henn leads the Grassland Ecology and Restoration (GEAR) Lab, with research focused on applied grassland plant ecology spanning restoration, fire management, and plant traits. His work aims to inform management practices that maintain grassland diversity under changing environmental conditions.

faculty.name: Robert J.M. Hudson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Hudson models biogeochemical process dynamics, including the fate and transport of metals in the environment, trace metal-phytoplankton interactions, and global cycles of carbon and mercury. His group developed a novel technique for measuring methylmercury applied to quantify mercury pollution across wildlife, waters, and sediments.

faculty.name: McKenzie F. Johnson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Johnson's research and teaching focus on global environmental politics, environmental justice, and human security, examining whether and how global environmental governance initiatives create local opportunities for social justice and sustainability. She has conducted interdisciplinary fieldwork in South Asia, Africa, and South America.

faculty.name: Angela Kent
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kent studies microbial communities that help sustain healthy ecosystems. Her work predicts the impacts of global change and other human forces on microbial ecosystem functions and enhances environmental quality by harnessing microbial processes.

faculty.name: Frances E. Kuo
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Kuo's work examines how urban greening benefits city residents, showing that green environments can reduce aggression and crime, ease ADHD symptoms, and promote self-discipline, academic achievement, and health. She also defines sustainable landscape practices for federal lands.

faculty.name: Eric R. Larson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Larson's research spans conservation science, environmental DNA, freshwater ecology, and invasive species. He applies these tools to understand and conserve freshwater and aquatic organisms.

faculty.name: Jeffrey W. Matthews
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Matthews studies restoration ecology, wetland ecology, and wetland mitigation policy using applied community ecology and ecological assessment. His work includes evaluating the ability of wetland mitigation banks to replace species lost from destroyed wetlands.

faculty.name: James Robert Miller
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Miller advances understanding of strategies for conserving biodiversity in working landscapes comprising private agricultural holdings and protected areas. His research, often focused on avian ecology, provides a scientific basis for reconciling human land use with conservation of native species.

faculty.name: Eric Morgan
faculty.title: Associate Professor, NRES and Agricultural Communications, ALEC
faculty.summary: Morgan's research focuses on how diverse communities come to understand and interact with the environments in which they live, drawing together culture, communication, and environment to examine cultural discourses of the environment. He also studies how science is communicated to broad audiences.

faculty.name: Richard L. Mulvaney
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mulvaney's research relates to soil fertility, with particular emphasis on the fate and behavior of soil and fertilizer nitrogen. He works to increase nitrogen fertilizer uptake in crops to raise profits while reducing the environmental impacts of excessive nitrogen inputs, including through the Illinois Soil Nitrogen Test.

faculty.name: Joy O'Keefe
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Wildlife Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: O'Keefe's research focuses on facilitating coexistence of bats and humans in human-altered landscapes, studying roosting and foraging ecology, bat behaviors, and mitigation practices. She works on distribution and status of imperiled bats and the ecosystem services of forest-dwelling bats.

faculty.name: Cory Suski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Suski integrates laboratory and field studies in animal behavior, physiology, and ecology to determine the mechanisms controlling abundance and distribution of freshwater organisms. He designs conservation strategies to protect aquatic resources against stressors including climate change, angling, and invasive species.

faculty.name: Carmen M. Ugarte
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Ugarte's research focuses on understanding the effects of soil management practices on soil quality and function, with particular interest in the dynamics of soil food webs and their influence on ecosystem services such as nutrient cycling and carbon storage. Her work includes basic and applied research in agronomic and natural systems.

faculty.name: Benjamin M. Van Doren
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Van Doren's Animal Migration Biology Lab investigates the anthropogenic threats migratory animals face and the mechanisms through which they respond to a changing world. The research integrates field, lab, and computational approaches to address questions around animal movement and to guide conservation action and policy.

faculty.name: Carena J. van Riper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Van Riper directs a research program focused on solving social-ecological problems that require an understanding of human behavior. Her team engages community members in natural resource management contexts to understand how factors enable and constrain adaptations to environmental change, particularly through the study of human values.

faculty.name: Michael P. Ward
faculty.title: Professor and Levenick Chair in Sustainability
faculty.summary: Ward studies the ecology and behavior of birds with the overarching goal of conservation, focusing on species of conservation concern. He uses telemetry to monitor the behavior and migration of birds and has developed novel approaches to species conservation.

faculty.name: Chloe Wardropper
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Environmental governance and natural resource policy
faculty.summary: Wardropper's research addresses environmental governance, natural resource policy, and conservation and adaptation in agriculture, including how information is used in water systems and policy processes. Her work examines topics such as risk perception and adoption of conservation practices by agricultural producers.

faculty.name: Christopher D. Wirz
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, NRES and ALEC
faculty.summary: Wirz researches communication among decision makers and publics in the face of environmental risks and technologies. His recent work focuses on weather hazards and the development of use-inspired AI for environmental decision making.

faculty.name: Tony Yannarell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Yannarell uses microorganisms to control weeds and harmful invasive plants, studying the microorganisms that help pest plants succeed and those that can be used to fight them. By understanding plant-microbe interactions, he seeks to improve agricultural productivity and protect natural areas threatened by invasive pests.

faculty.name: Zhongjie Yu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Yu's research resolves the complex dynamics of nitrogen cycling in hydrologic systems, using field, laboratory, and modeling techniques to understand how hydrological and biogeochemical processes control the transport and transformation of reactive nitrogen. His group specializes in stable isotope techniques applied to nitrate and nitrous oxide.

funding.note: Research/teaching assistantships subject to faculty resources; Jan 15 preferred for funding

faculty.name: Nii O. Attoh-Okine
faculty.title: Professor and Chair, Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Specializes in resilient infrastructure with a strong focus on cybersecurity and digital technologies applied to transportation and civil infrastructure. Recognized expert in railway engineering who leads initiatives in digital twins and blockchain applications for infrastructure.

faculty.name: Akua Asa-Awuku
faculty.title: Professor; Associate Dean
faculty.summary: Investigates the formation, composition, and measurement of anthropogenic and biogenic aerosol to explore impacts on climate and health. Her work focuses on droplet formation thermodynamics and organic aerosol characterization relevant to environmental and human health outcomes.

faculty.name: Mark A. Austin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on model-based systems engineering and integration, and systems modeling, analysis, and design of cyber-physical systems. Applications include energy-efficient building simulation and machine learning for urban behavior observation.

faculty.name: Ahmet H. Aydilek
faculty.title: Professor; Distinguished Scholar-Teacher
faculty.summary: Research focuses on sustainable reuse of waste materials in highway construction and environmental remediation. His work spans geoenvironmental analysis, recycled materials evaluation, soil stabilization, and stormwater management solutions.

faculty.name: Bilal M. Ayyub
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Center for Technology and Systems Management
faculty.summary: Applies probabilistic methods to assess and enhance the reliability of civil infrastructure systems across energy, maritime, and defense sectors. Develops methodology for analyzing structural integrity, accounting for uncertainties like corrosion and fatigue in marine vessels and other engineered systems.

faculty.name: Gregory B. Baecher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Work focuses on operational safety of dams and reservoirs, examining how unusual combinations of usual conditions and systems interactions create risks that traditional engineering analysis overlooks. Applies probabilistic risk assessment frameworks to infrastructure safety and flood protection systems; member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Michelle (Shelby) Bensi
faculty.title: Associate Professor; The Deborah J. Goodings Professor in Engineering for Global Sustainability
faculty.summary: Specializes in probabilistic methods for evaluating how infrastructure systems face exposure to natural and human-induced hazards and determining the likelihood of impacts. Her work integrates statistical modeling and Bayesian methods with applications including nuclear power facility risk assessment.

faculty.name: Kaye L. Brubaker
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Maryland Water Resources Research Center
faculty.summary: Research focuses on water resource management and hydrological modeling using computer models, remotely-sensed data, and geographic information systems. Recent NSF-funded work applies game theory to water market models for watersheds including the Anacostia River in Maryland and D.C.

faculty.name: Gang-Len Chang
faculty.title: Professor and A. James Clark Chair
faculty.summary: Leads research on intelligent transportation systems, including real-time traffic monitoring networks and travel time prediction algorithms. His work focuses on optimizing network traffic flow and emergency evacuation capabilities; directs the Traffic Operations and Safety Laboratory.

faculty.name: Peter C. Chang
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Co-Director, Engineers Without Borders
faculty.summary: Focuses on applying sensor technology in infrastructure applications to monitor the health and safety of structural systems. His research includes structural health monitoring and fire and blast protection for structures.

faculty.name: Cinzia Cirillo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Work focuses on transportation modeling and travel behavior analysis, applying advanced statistical methods such as discrete choice analysis and large-scale demand model systems to improve transportation planning and policy evaluation.

faculty.name: Qingbin Cui
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on infrastructure delivery and project management, with emphasis on public-private partnerships, smart construction technologies, and data-driven approaches to infrastructure challenges. Directs the Build America Center addressing innovative financing for transportation and construction projects.

faculty.name: Allen P. Davis
faculty.title: Professor; Charles A. Irish Sr. Chair in Civil Engineering; Associate Chair for Faculty Development
faculty.summary: An international leader in urban stormwater quality characterization and treatment, conducting fundamental and applied research on stormwater control measures. His work develops enhanced stormwater quality improvement media and designs to reduce pollutant runoff into waterways like the Chesapeake Bay.

faculty.name: Jongwan Eun
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Coordinator, Infrastructure Systems Group
faculty.summary: Investigates coupled thermal, hydraulic, and mechanical processes in natural and engineered geosystems, with applications from landfill engineering to geological repositories. His work integrates experimental testing, numerical modeling, and emerging sensing technologies for infrastructure resilience.

faculty.name: Dimitrios G. Goulias
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates non-destructive-testing-based QA/QC systems and machine learning for performance prediction of infrastructure. His work spans materials testing, pavement analysis, recycled materials, and sustainable construction practices.

faculty.name: Birthe Kjellerup
faculty.title: Professor; Chair of Civil Empowerment
faculty.summary: Investigates the diversity and activity of microbial biofilms in complex systems such as sediment, stormwater, soil, and groundwater. Her research covers biodegradation and bioremediation of persistent organic pollutants (PCBs, chlorinated solvents, PAHs) and wastewater-based epidemiology.

faculty.name: Guangbin Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Work focuses on sustainable biological water and wastewater treatment and nutrient removal/recovery. Particular emphasis on the biotransformation and fate of hazardous contaminants and microbial responses to environmental stressors.

faculty.name: Shuna Ni
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on probabilistic structural fire engineering, fire forensics, fire safety of mass-timber buildings, and smart firefighting. Her work combines probabilistic modeling and experiments to assess post-fire structural performance and the impact of fire on civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Deb Niemeier
faculty.title: Clark Distinguished Chair Professor; Director, Center for Disaster Resilience
faculty.summary: Examines connections between infrastructure systems and social equity, with focus on how disasters disproportionately impact vulnerable populations and how transportation investments can advance equitable outcomes. Elected member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Allison Reilly
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Pedro E. Wasmer Professor in Engineering
faculty.summary: Focuses on identifying governance systems that enable resilient infrastructure and strong communities. Her work emphasizes risk equity in vulnerable coastal areas and how transportation infrastructure investments can support just transitions during climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Rachel E. Rosenberg Goldstein
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Work focuses on environmental microbiology and water reuse, with particular interest in water systems and their intersection with public health and urban environments. Holds joint appointments in Civil and Environmental Engineering and Applied Environmental Health.

faculty.name: Alba Torrents
faculty.title: Professor; Ben Dyer Centennial Chair
faculty.summary: Investigates the fate and transport of organic pollutants in the environment, including endocrine disrupting chemicals released from wastewater treatment plants and land application of biosolids. Her work encompasses remediation strategies and methods to reduce bioavailability of persistent pollutants at contaminated sites.

faculty.name: Jianqiang Wei
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops sustainable concrete alternatives and improves concrete durability through novel material composition and internal conditioning techniques. His research spans cement chemistry, cement-free and self-healing concrete, and utilization of solid waste-derived materials in construction.

faculty.name: Xianfeng (Terry) Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director, Maryland Transportation Institute; Clark Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Applies artificial intelligence and machine learning to improve transportation systems, with emphasis on connected automated vehicles, transportation data analytics and privacy, and traffic safety and operations.

faculty.name: Yunfeng Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops resilient self-centering and modular structures and advanced bridge inspection technologies. His work emphasizes machine learning applications for structural assessment and systems that improve structural performance during seismic events.

requirements.admission_model: Not currently accepting applications; directs applicants to Bioengineering (BIOE)

faculty.name: Stephanie Lansing
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lansing leads the Bioenergy and Biotechnology Lab, researching renewable energy, waste treatment, and water quality. Her work converts food and agricultural waste into biofuels and bioplastics via anaerobic digestion and addresses antimicrobial resistance in agricultural systems.

faculty.name: David Tilley
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Tilley integrates ecological engineering and systems ecology to develop solutions for managed environments. His current work focuses on nature-based urban technologies, vegetated systems for thermal and water performance, and generative AI applications in ecological analysis.

faculty.name: Andrew H. Baldwin
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Baldwin studies the plant and ecosystem ecology of natural and restored wetlands. His research examines links between plant regeneration, carbon and nitrogen cycling, invasive species, and global change.

faculty.name: Paul T. Leisnham
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leisnham applies an interdisciplinary Ecology and Health (EcoHealth) approach to environmental challenges, studying the invasion and control of medically important mosquitoes and watershed health and sustainability across population ecology, global change biology, and public health.

faculty.name: Mitchell A. Pavao-Zuckerman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Pavao-Zuckerman investigates how soils, plants, and biogeochemistry function in urban green infrastructure to provide ecosystem services, including tree transpiration effects on stormwater runoff. He also studies agrivoltaics that co-locate solar power with agriculture.

faculty.name: Adel Shirmohammadi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Shirmohammadi's research focuses on watershed-scale modeling and sustainable management of agro-ecosystems, with emphasis on stormwater management in urban and suburban ecosystems and GIS-based assessment of water quality.

faculty.name: Masoud Negahban-Azar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Negahban-Azar leads the Water Security and Sustainability Lab, focusing on watershed-scale hydrological modeling of water quantity and quality and innovative water reuse solutions. He combines mathematical modeling with observational data to address water scarcity and contamination risks.

faculty.name: Gurpal Toor
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Toor's research examines nitrogen and phosphorus flows in soil-plant-water systems to reduce nutrient pollution in the Chesapeake Bay while sustaining agricultural production, developing and evaluating nutrient management tools from waste materials and fertilizers.

faculty.name: Brian A. Needelman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Needelman focuses on soil science, pedology, sustainable agriculture, and coastal wetlands, with specific interests in digital soil mapping, blue carbon dynamics in coastal wetland soils, and climate change resilience in coastal systems.

faculty.name: Stephanie Yarwood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yarwood studies how microbial communities in agricultural, wetland, forest, and urban soils respond to disturbances such as wetland restoration and agricultural management, with implications for nitrogen cycling, greenhouse gas emissions, and carbon storage.

faculty.name: Candice M. Duncan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Duncan's work focuses on the transport and characterization of organic and inorganic contaminants in the vadose zone, particularly using phytoremediation to remove pollutants such as TCE, PFAS, and heavy metals from soil, water, and air.

faculty.name: Hubert Montas
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Montas works in bioenvironmental and water resources engineering, including numerical methods in environmental biodynamics, contaminant transport modeling, and information and decision support systems for watershed management.

faculty.name: Yang Tao
faculty.title: Fischell Family Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Tao's research develops advanced bioimaging, machine vision, and AI-driven robotics technologies for applications in bioengineering, food engineering, biomedical and biopharmaceutical fields, and smart manufacturing.

faculty.name: Birthe Kjellerup
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kjellerup investigates the diversity and activity of microbial biofilms in complex systems, focusing on biodegradation of persistent pollutants such as PCBs and chlorinated solvents, as well as wastewater-based epidemiology.

funding.note: Graduate research/teaching assistantships with competitive stipends; Grad School fellowships

faculty.name: James Carton
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer studying ocean dynamics and the ocean's role in climate variability. Developed the widely used SODA ocean reanalysis. Research spans tropical climate (ENSO), global ocean heat content, Arctic climate and sea ice loss, and Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation. Fellow of the American Meteorological Society.

faculty.name: Russell Dickerson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on atmospheric chemistry and air pollution, including trace gas measurements, satellite remote sensing of atmospheric composition, and the impact of emissions on air quality and climate. Leads field campaigns studying urban and regional air pollution.

faculty.name: Zhanqing Li
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Research on aerosols, clouds, radiation budget, and climate forcing. Uses satellite remote sensing and ground-based measurements to study aerosol-cloud-radiation interactions and their impacts on climate, with extensive work on East Asian and global aerosol systems.

faculty.name: Xin-Zhong Liang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops regional climate models with a focus on land-atmosphere interactions, climate downscaling, and hydrometeorology. Research addresses regional climate predictability, extreme weather events, and climate impacts on water resources and agriculture.

faculty.name: Sumant Nigam
faculty.title: Distinguished University Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies climate dynamics and atmospheric circulation, focusing on climate variability from seasonal to decadal timescales. Research includes monsoon systems, teleconnections, and the mechanisms driving regional hydroclimate variability and change.

faculty.name: Lars Peter Riishojgaard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on data assimilation methods, numerical weather prediction, and the design and impact assessment of Earth observing systems. Previously served as Director of the WMO Integrated Global Observing System.

faculty.name: Ross Salawitch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on stratospheric ozone depletion and recovery, atmospheric chemistry affecting air quality, and the global carbon cycle. Investigates the intersection of atmospheric composition, climate change, and environmental policy.

faculty.name: Ning Zeng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies climate modeling and the global carbon cycle, including land-atmosphere interactions and biogeochemical feedbacks. Research extends to climate intervention strategies and the role of terrestrial ecosystems in climate regulation.

faculty.name: Da-Lin Zhang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading expert in mesoscale meteorology and numerical weather prediction. Research focuses on the dynamics and prediction of tropical cyclones, severe convective storms, and heavy precipitation events through high-resolution modeling.

faculty.name: Timothy Canty
faculty.title: Associate Professor, MEES Director
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry, including stratospheric and tropospheric ozone, photochemical modeling, and the use of satellite observations to understand atmospheric composition and air quality trends.

faculty.name: Sinead Farrell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the cryosphere, using satellite remote sensing (particularly altimetry) to study sea ice thickness, snow on sea ice, and Arctic climate change. Works on satellite mission development for polar observations.

faculty.name: Kayo Ide
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on data assimilation theory and methods, including Lagrangian data assimilation and observing system design. Applies techniques across geophysical disciplines including atmosphere, ocean, and solid Earth.

faculty.name: Jonathan Poterjoy
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Graduate Program Director
faculty.summary: Develops advanced data assimilation methods for ensemble forecasting and numerical weather prediction, with applications to hurricane prediction and high-impact weather. Research bridges theory and operational forecasting.

faculty.name: Jacob Wenegrat
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies physical oceanography and ocean dynamics, focusing on submesoscale processes, air-sea interaction, and the role of ocean turbulence in climate. Combines theory, numerical modeling, and observational analysis.

faculty.name: Christopher Kenseth
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry focusing on aerosol formation and transformation, particularly secondary organic aerosols. Uses advanced mass spectrometry techniques to characterize aerosol chemical composition and reactions.

faculty.name: Maria Molina
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on climate variability and extremes using machine learning and climate modeling approaches. Investigates predictability of extreme weather and climate events, with a focus on improving understanding of atmospheric processes.

faculty.name: Madeleine Youngs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Physical oceanographer studying ocean circulation and its role in climate, with emphasis on the Southern Ocean. Uses numerical models and observational tracers to understand ocean ventilation and transport pathways.

programs.program_name: Marine, Estuarine, and Environmental Sciences, Ph.D.

requirements.admission_model: Interinstitutional program across University System of Maryland

funding.note: University fellowships, research assistantships/traineeships, and TAs cover living/education expenses

faculty.name: Jeremy M. Testa
faculty.title: Professor, Chesapeake Biological Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Investigates how biological, chemical, and physical processes interact in coastal ecosystems, focusing on eutrophication, nutrient and oxygen cycling, and ocean acidification through experiments, data analysis, and computational modeling.

faculty.name: Ming Li
faculty.title: Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Uses numerical modeling to study physical oceanography and estuarine and coastal dynamics, including how physical processes drive hypoxia and harmful algal blooms and how climate change and extreme weather affect coastal systems.

faculty.name: Jeffrey C. Cornwell
faculty.title: Research Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies biogeochemistry and nutrient, metal, and sulfur cycling in estuaries and coastal wetlands, including microbial nitrogen removal, oyster-associated nutrient dynamics, and tidal wetland nutrient balances.

faculty.name: Sairah Malkin
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Works to quantify and predict rates and pathways of geochemical cycling in aquatic systems, emphasizing benthic carbon and nutrient processing and sulfur-oxidizing bacteria in sediments.

faculty.name: Greg Silsbe
faculty.title: Assistant Research Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies phytoplankton physiology and photosynthetic processes in marine environments using bio-optical instrumentation and remote sensing, along with tropical limnology.

faculty.name: William Nardin
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Researches the impact of storms and sea level rise on wetland eco-geomorphology and the coupling between river/estuarine hydrodynamics and coastal processes.

faculty.name: Andrea Pain
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies carbon and nutrient processes across the land-sea interface, including coastal groundwater and Arctic processes, and their implications for coastal ecosystems under climate change.

faculty.name: Cindy Palinkas
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Focuses on geological oceanography and sediment transport and deposition in intertidal, fluvial, and estuarine environments, including how tidal marshes respond to environmental change.

faculty.name: Jacob Cram
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies marine microbial communities and how organisms both shape and are shaped by their environment, with emphasis on marine snow particles and ocean biogeochemistry.

faculty.name: Hongsheng Bi
faculty.title: Professor, Chesapeake Biological Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies fine-scale spatial distributions of pelagic organisms and their trophic interactions, developing underwater plankton imaging systems and deep learning techniques for zooplankton assessment.

faculty.name: Carys Mitchelmore
faculty.title: Professor, Chesapeake Biological Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Focuses on the detection of chemical contaminants and understanding their toxicity and implications for ecosystem health.

faculty.name: Mario Tamburri
faculty.title: Professor, Chesapeake Biological Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies the chemical ecology of aquatic organisms, invasive species, and biofouling, and develops coastal sensor and marine monitoring technologies.

faculty.name: Matthew Gray
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Horn Point Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies the physiological response of marine invertebrates to current and future environmental conditions and the ecological benefits these organisms provide.

faculty.name: Xin Zhang
faculty.title: Professor, Appalachian Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Evaluates how socioeconomic and biogeochemical processes affect the global nutrient cycle, addressing agricultural sustainability and nutrient pollution mitigation.

faculty.name: Zach Zbinden
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Appalachian Laboratory (UMCES)
faculty.summary: Studies biodiversity distribution and dynamics from a 'genes to ecosystems' perspective, emphasizing population genomics of freshwater fish and species resilience to climate change.

funding.note: Competitive graduate assistantships with stipend, tuition, and health benefits

faculty.name: Andrew H. Baldwin
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Studies plant and ecosystem dynamics in natural and restored tidal and inland wetlands, including plant regeneration, carbon and nitrogen cycling, invasive species, and climate change impacts. Applies ecological knowledge to improve wetland restoration outcomes.

faculty.name: William Bowerman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies avian predators, particularly bald eagles, as biological indicators of environmental health. Examines how contaminants, human activities, and habitat degradation affect wildlife populations using long-term Great Lakes eagle datasets.

faculty.name: Frank J. Coale
faculty.title: Professor & Acting Assistant Dean
faculty.summary: Researches soil phosphorus dynamics and the relationships among agronomic production practices and phosphorus over-enrichment of surface waters. Applies nutrient management science to protect Chesapeake Bay water quality while maintaining agricultural productivity.

faculty.name: Robert L. Hill
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how soil management practices affect water movement through soil and the transport of agricultural chemicals. Develops computational tools for nutrient management planning and environmental risk assessment.

faculty.name: Stephanie Lansing
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Bioenergy and Biotechnology Lab, focusing on renewable energy, water quality, and waste treatment. Converts food and agricultural waste into biofuels and bioplastics through anaerobic digestion and thermochemical processes.

faculty.name: Paul T. Leisnham
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies invasion and control of medically important mosquitoes and watershed health using population and community ecology, global change biology, and public health perspectives. Research funded by NSF, USDA-NIFA, EPA-STAR, and NOAA.

faculty.name: Adel Shirmohammadi
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Dean for Research
faculty.summary: Researches watershed-scale environmental modeling and water quality management, including stormwater management in urban and suburban ecosystems and sustainable management of agro-ecosystems. Also studies links between drinking water quality and human cancer incidence via GIS analysis.

faculty.name: Gurpal Toor
faculty.title: Professor and Extension Specialist
faculty.summary: Develops solutions to manage nutrients in the plant root-zone and protect water quality, addressing nitrogen and phosphorus pollution in the Chesapeake Bay by studying release and transport mechanisms in soil-plant-water systems.

faculty.name: Ray Weil
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: International expert in soil management and soil health, especially sustainable agricultural systems in industrial and developing countries. Developed soil microbial biomass and active carbon tests adopted by USDA/NRCS and researchers worldwide.

faculty.name: Stephanie Yarwood
faculty.title: Professor and Graduate Director
faculty.summary: Investigates the microbial ecology of soils across agricultural, wetland, forest, and urban ecosystems, examining how disturbances such as wetland restoration and agricultural management affect microbial communities, nitrogen cycling, greenhouse gas emissions, and carbon storage.

faculty.name: Martin C. Rabenhorst
faculty.title: Ray Weil E-Nnovate Endowed Professor in Soil Science
faculty.summary: Studies the genesis, morphology, and classification of hydromorphic soils in water-saturated environments including non-tidal wetlands and coastal marshes, and develops technology for documenting reducing soil conditions.

faculty.name: Jennifer M. Mullinax
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies threats to wildlife populations and how environmental change affects species, including common tern declines in Maryland, urbanization impacts on bat activity, and habitat modeling for black bears under land-use change.

faculty.name: Brian A. Needelman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Applies pedological concepts to environmental and social problem-solving, including digital soil mapping for precision sustainable agriculture and coastal wetland soil processes related to blue carbon sequestration and methane emissions.

faculty.name: Masoud Negahban-Azar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Water Security and Sustainability Lab, focusing on environmental modeling, innovative water reuse solutions, and diagnostic decision support systems for water resource management, including alternative water sources and best management practices in the Atlantic coastal plain.

faculty.name: Mitchell A. Pavao-Zuckerman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Directs the Urban and Built Environment Sustainability Lab, linking ecology, design, policy, and technology to study green infrastructure ecosystem services, tree transpiration effects on urban stormwater, and agrivoltaics that co-locate solar power with agriculture.

faculty.name: Wendy Peer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Runs the Physiology and Genetics Lab applying biochemistry, genetics, and live cell imaging to study how plants regulate growth and respond to environmental conditions, with focus on specialized compounds such as flavonoids, hormone function, and auxin homeostasis.

faculty.name: David R. Tilley
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates ecological engineering, systems ecology, and innovation to develop solutions for the built and managed environment, including vegetated systems for thermal and water performance, environmental accounting, and use of generative AI for ecological analysis and design.

faculty.name: Lance Yonkos
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts laboratory and field studies on contaminant abundance, transport, fate, and toxicological effects in fresh and estuarine waters and sediments, with focus on microplastics and endocrine-disrupting compounds in the Chesapeake Bay watershed.

faculty.name: Candice M. Duncan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies transport and characterization of organic and inorganic contaminants in the vadose zone and uses phytoremediation to remove pollutants such as chlorinated VOCs, PFAS, and metals from soil, water, and air.

faculty.name: Travis Gallo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how urban environments shape species distributions, populations, communities, and behaviors, providing evidence-based solutions that conserve biodiversity while improving urban residents' lives; work spans urban bat conservation and mammal metacommunity dynamics.

faculty.name: Akash Koppa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Hydrologist and civil engineer working on large-scale, high-resolution atmosphere and land surface modeling; his lab investigates how land-atmosphere feedbacks modulate the formation and expansion of drylands.

faculty.name: Jared Wilmoth
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Soil Chemical Interfaces (W-SCI) Lab investigating mechanistic connections between soil systems, biogeochemical cycles, and climate, examining how soil chemistry and redox transformations affect global carbon cycling.

faculty.name: Yafeng Yin
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Chair of CEE. Research on analysis, modeling, design, and optimization of transportation systems for sustainability and economic efficiency. Former editor-in-chief of Transportation Research Part C.

faculty.name: Sherif El-Tawil
faculty.title: Antoine E. Naaman Collegiate Professor
faculty.summary: Structural engineering and hazard mitigation. Former Editor-in-Chief of ASCE Journal of Structural Engineering (2010-2020). Recipient of 2022 ASCE Shortridge Hardesty Award.

faculty.name: Lutgarde Raskin
faculty.title: Vernon L. Snoeyink Distinguished University Professor and Altarum/ERIM Russell O'Neal Professor
faculty.summary: Elected member of National Academy of Engineering. Focuses on anaerobic waste treatment and drinking water treatment technologies using microbial ecology approaches.

faculty.name: Nancy Love
faculty.title: Borchardt and Glysson Collegiate Professor and JoAnn Silverstein Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water systems sustainability with focus on nutrient-energy-water cycles and circular nutrient economy. Recipient of 2026 Distinguished Faculty Achievement Award.

faculty.name: SangHyun Lee
faculty.title: Robert B. Harris Collegiate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on robotics, AI, and construction safety. Awarded Robert B. Harris Collegiate Professorship in 2025. Focuses on human-robot interaction in construction environments.

faculty.name: Carol Menassa
faculty.title: Professor and John L. Tishman CM Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Construction engineering and management with focus on human-robot collaboration. 2022 Walter L. Huber Civil Engineering Research Prize recipient. Daniel W. Halpin Award for Scholarship in Construction.

faculty.name: Vineet Kamat
faculty.title: Professor and John L. Tishman CM Faculty Scholar
faculty.summary: Construction automation and robotics. 2022 ASCE Construction Management Award recipient. Research on augmented reality and robotics for construction engineering.

faculty.name: Krista Wigginton
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Dean for Graduate and Professional Education
faculty.summary: Environmental engineering with focus on water quality and pathogen detection. Promoted to full professor in 2024. Named Associate Dean for Graduate and Professional Education at Michigan Engineering.

faculty.name: Seth Guikema
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Moved fully to CEE in 2024 to lead the Hazard, Risk, and Resilience program. Research on infrastructure resilience to natural hazards using data-driven approaches.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Scruggs
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Promoted to full professor in 2024. Research on control of ocean wave energy conversion, self-powered cyber-physical systems, and vibratory energy harvesting. Serves as Associate Chair.

faculty.name: Henry Liu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on autonomous vehicle safety, AI-enabled infrastructure innovations, and transportation systems. Testified before U.S. House panel on AV safety funding.

faculty.name: Glen Daigger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Expert in wastewater treatment and integrated urban water management. Co-recipient of the ASCE Wesley W. Horner Award. Focuses on water reuse and resource recovery.

faculty.name: Seymour M.J. Spence
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on wind engineering and structural reliability. PhD student work on AI for disaster-resilient infrastructure won dual international awards in 2025.

faculty.name: Evgueni Filipov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on origami-inspired engineering: deployable, reconfigurable, load-bearing structures using folded thin sheets. Joint appointment with Mechanical Engineering. 2020 NSF CAREER Award.

faculty.name: Jason McCormick
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Arthur F. Thurnau Professor
faculty.summary: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor for outstanding undergraduate education. Research on earthquake engineering and structural dynamics. Herbert Kopf Service Excellence Award recipient.

faculty.name: Branko Kerkez
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Arthur F. Thurnau Professor
faculty.summary: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor. Research on smart water systems, real-time sensor networks, and cyber-physical infrastructure for urban water management.

faculty.name: Ann Jeffers
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on structural fire engineering and multi-hazard resilience. Focuses on performance-based design approaches for extreme loading conditions.

faculty.name: Neda Masoud
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on connected and automated vehicles, shared mobility systems, and transportation data analytics for sustainable urban mobility.

faculty.name: Jeremy Bricker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on coastal infrastructure resilience, tsunami and flood risk assessment, and hydraulic structures subjected to extreme coastal events.

faculty.name: Terese Olson
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair for Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on environmental chemistry and water quality. Serves as Associate Chair for Undergraduate Studies. Jack A. Borchardt Award recipient.

faculty.name: Sabine Loos
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on disaster risk assessment, infrastructure resilience, and community-level risk modeling for natural hazards and climate adaptation.

faculty.name: Rabab Haider
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Joined CEE in Fall 2024. Research on energy and power systems, decarbonization, and AI for grid-edge devices in the context of civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Estéfan Garcia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on resilience modeling for infrastructure systems facing coastal hazards and climate change adaptation challenges.

programs.program_name: Climate and Space Sciences and Engineering PhD

faculty.name: Allison Steiner
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Chair of CLaSP (effective July 2025). Research on atmospheric chemistry and regional climate modeling. Elected president of AGU Atmospheric Sciences section. Studies pollen emissions and microplastics in atmosphere.

faculty.name: Richard Rood
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on applied climate science, climate change adaptation, and the science-policy interface. Graduate advisor for Applied Climate program.

faculty.name: Christiane Jablonowski
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric general circulation models, dynamical cores, and numerical methods for climate and weather prediction. Focuses on adaptive mesh refinement techniques.

faculty.name: Aaron Ridley
faculty.title: Professor and Arthur F. Thurnau Professor
faculty.summary: Named 2025 Arthur F. Thurnau Professor. Developer of the Global Ionosphere Thermosphere Model (GITM). Leads CubeSat missions including CADRE and CYGNSS.

faculty.name: Brian Arbic
faculty.title: Professor and Arthur F. Thurnau Professor
faculty.summary: Named 2025 Arthur F. Thurnau Professor. Research on ocean and climate modeling. Director of Coastal Ocean Environment Summer School in Nigeria and Ghana.

faculty.name: Mark Flanner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on cryosphere-climate interactions, black carbon radiative forcing, snowpack microphysics, and ice sheet surface energy budget.

faculty.name: Xianglei Huang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Co-investigator on NASA Earth Venture Continuity mission (Libera instrument). Research on atmospheric radiation, far-infrared remote sensing, and climate feedback processes. NASA PREFIRE mission honoree.

faculty.name: Chris Ruf
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Graduate advisor for Geoscience and Remote Sensing. Principal Investigator of NASA CYGNSS mission. Research on satellite remote sensing of ocean winds and climate variables.

faculty.name: Tamas Gombosi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Center for Space Environment Modeling (CSEM) and SOLSTICE solar storms center. Research on space plasma physics, solar storms, and terrestrial impacts of space weather.

faculty.name: Michael Liemohn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on magnetosphere-ionosphere coupling, space weather, and planetary atmospheres. Affiliated with CSEM and Space Institute.

faculty.name: Tuija Pulkkinen
faculty.title: George R. Carignan Collegiate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on space weather, magnetosphere dynamics, and solar-terrestrial interactions. Former Chair of CLaSP. Leading authority on geomagnetic storms.

faculty.name: Mark Moldwin
faculty.title: Professor and Arthur F. Thurnau Professor
faculty.summary: Research on space weather and magnetometer networks. Lead for NASA NEMISIS Geospace Dynamics Constellation mission. Director of UM Office of Postdoctoral Affairs.

faculty.name: Susan Lepri
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on heliophysics, solar wind composition, and solar energetic particles. Involved with Solar Orbiter mission studying solar and heliospheric physics.

faculty.name: Justin Kasper
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on heliophysics and space plasma physics. Key scientist on NASA Parker Solar Probe. 2022 APS Fellow and Copernicus Medal recipient.

faculty.name: Nilton Renno
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on planetary atmospheres, Mars science, and atmospheric electricity. Led Phoenix Mars Mission Atmospheric Science Theme Group. Director of MEng Space Engineering program.

faculty.name: Enrico Landi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on heliophysics, solar spectroscopy, and coronal plasma physics. Studies solar corona composition and dynamics using spectroscopic methods.

faculty.name: Gretchen Keppel-Aleks
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research using remote sensing and atmospheric composition data to study climate feedback and ecosystem health. 2024 Harold R. Johnson Diversity Service Award recipient.

faculty.name: Jeremy Bassis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on ice dynamics and sea level rise. Contributing author of the latest IPCC report. Studies glacier and ice sheet response to climate change.

faculty.name: Cheng Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on planetary atmospheres, particularly giant planets. NASA Group Achievement Award honoree for Juno Radio Occultation Team studying Jupiter's atmosphere.

faculty.name: Daniel Welling
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on space weather modeling, magnetosphere-ionosphere coupling, and geospace dynamics. Focuses on predicting geomagnetic storm impacts on technological systems.

faculty.name: Jim Raines
faculty.title: Associate Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Research on space plasma physics and planetary magnetospheres. Involved with MESSENGER mission studying Mercury's magnetosphere.

programs.program_name: Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering PhD

faculty.name: Jing Sun
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair; Michael G. Parsons Collegiate Professor
faculty.summary: Chair of NAME. Leads Real-time Adaptive Control Engineering Lab (RACELab). Research on marine system control, optimization, and power management for autonomous vessels. Elected Fellow of National Academy of Inventors and IFAC.

faculty.name: David Dowling
faculty.title: Professor and ABS Professor of Marine and Offshore Design Performance
faculty.summary: Interim chair of NAME (2021). Research on acoustics and fluid mechanics combining experimental, theoretical, and computational techniques. Recipient of ASME Per Bruel Gold Medal for noise control and acoustics.

faculty.name: Krishnan Mahesh
faculty.title: Professor and Richard B. Couch Professor of Naval Architecture and Marine Engineering
faculty.summary: Joined NAME in 2022 from University of Minnesota. Director of Center for Naval Research and Education. Research on simulation methods for turbulence and CFD for propulsors and hull-propeller interactions. 2026 ASME Freeman Scholar Award.

faculty.name: Kevin Maki
faculty.title: Professor and Richard B. Couch Development Professor of Marine Hydrodynamics
faculty.summary: Director of Aaron Friedman Marine Hydrodynamics Laboratory and Computational Ship Hydrodynamics Laboratory. Research on fluid mechanics algorithms for high-Reynolds number external flows with air-water interfaces.

faculty.name: Pingsha Dong
faculty.title: Professor and Robert F. Beck Collegiate Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: ASME Fellow. Research on marine structures with focus on welding engineering, structural integrity, and fatigue analysis for naval and offshore applications.

faculty.name: Matthew Collette
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads Marine Structures Design Laboratory. Research on structural design and optimization for marine vessels, including rapid strength assessment and lifecycle performance forecasting. Co-leads Great Lakes Maritime Initiative.

faculty.name: Lei Zuo
faculty.title: Professor and Herbert C. Sadler Collegiate Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on marine renewable energy, wave energy conversion, and vibration energy harvesting. Recipient of two 2026 ASME awards for research and mentorship.

faculty.name: David Singer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Advanced Naval Concepts Research Laboratory. Appointed to Department of the Navy Science and Technology Board. Research on set-based design and ship design methodology.

faculty.name: Yulin Pan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: 2020-2021 NAME Outstanding Faculty Award recipient. PhD from MIT (mechanical and ocean engineering). Research on theoretical and computational aspects of ocean engineering and wave mechanics.

faculty.name: Maani Ghaffari
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Joint appointment with Robotics Institute. Leads Computational Autonomy and Robotics Laboratory (CURLY). Research on algorithms for mobile robots in marine environments for autonomous navigation and environmental monitoring.

faculty.name: Anchal Sareen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads Experimental Fluid Dynamics Lab. Research on fluid dynamics with focus on vortex dynamics, flow control, and fluid-structure interactions relevant to marine applications.

faculty.name: Julia Cole
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research on paleoclimate reconstruction using coral records, tropical climate variability, and ocean-atmosphere interactions. Focuses on climate dynamics over the past several centuries.

faculty.name: Brian Arbic
faculty.title: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor; Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on ocean modeling, tides, internal waves, and their role in climate. Leads COESSING summer program training oceanographers in Nigeria and Ghana. Joint appointment with Climate and Space Sciences.

faculty.name: Rose Cory
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair for Curriculum and Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on biogeochemistry with focus on carbon cycling in aquatic ecosystems. Studies dissolved organic matter dynamics and Arctic ecosystem responses to climate change.

faculty.name: Gregory Dick
faculty.title: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on geomicrobiology and harmful algal blooms in the Great Lakes. Leads $6.5M federal grant for Great Lakes Center for Fresh Waters and Human Health studying climate change and algal bloom links.

faculty.name: Matt Friedman
faculty.title: Professor; Director and Curator, Museum of Paleontology
faculty.summary: Director and Curator of the Museum of Paleontology. Research on vertebrate paleontology with focus on fish evolution, phylogenetic systematics, and patterns of diversification through deep time.

faculty.name: Yihe Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair for Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Associate Chair for Graduate Studies. Research on seismology, earthquake source physics, fault mechanics, and induced seismicity using observational and computational approaches.

faculty.name: Rebecca Lange
faculty.title: Alexander N. Halliday Collegiate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on igneous petrology and volcanology. Studies magma generation, evolution, and eruption processes using experimental and thermodynamic approaches.

faculty.name: Jie (Jackie) Li
faculty.title: Rodney C. Ewing Collegiate Professor of Earth and Planetary Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on mineral physics and high-pressure geochemistry of Earth and planetary interiors. Studies deep Earth processes using experimental techniques at extreme conditions.

faculty.name: Adam C. Simon
faculty.title: Arthur F. Thurnau Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on economic geology with focus on ore deposit formation, geochemistry of mineral resources, and critical minerals for energy transition.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Wilson Mantilla
faculty.title: Professor; Curator, Museum of Paleontology
faculty.summary: Curator at Museum of Paleontology. Research on vertebrate paleontology with focus on dinosaur evolution, functional morphology, and Mesozoic terrestrial ecosystems.

faculty.name: Youxue Zhang
faculty.title: James R. O'Neil Collegiate Professor of Geological Sciences
faculty.summary: Elected Geochemistry Fellow of the Geochemical Society (2024). Research on geochemistry of volatiles, multi-species diffusion kinetics, and hydrous species in magmatic systems.

faculty.name: Jessica Fayne
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Joined Fall 2024. Research on hydrology, remote sensing, and spatial data analysis for predicting climate change impacts and natural hazards including wildfire, landslides, and floods. Joint with Climate and Space Sciences.

faculty.name: Zack Spica
faculty.title: Henry Pollack Early Career Professor; Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on geophysics and seismology using distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) with fiber optic cables. Studies near-surface geophysics, urban seismology, and environmental monitoring.

faculty.name: Luke Weaver
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; Assistant Curator, Museum of Paleontology
faculty.summary: Joined Fall 2024. Research on mammalian biodiversity in the fossil record, evolution of mammals, and how climate and tectonics shaped mammalian evolutionary trajectories.

faculty.name: Jena Johnson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on biogeochemistry of early Earth environments, mineral-microbe interactions, and geochemical conditions relevant to the origin and early evolution of life.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Antonio B. Rodriguez
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research on coastal and marine geology, focusing on sedimentology and stratigraphy. Studies how coastal systems such as barrier islands, estuaries, and deltas respond to sea-level rise and climate change over various timescales.

faculty.name: Jaye Cable
faculty.title: Professor, Senior Associate Dean for Natural Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies chemical oceanography and geochemistry, focusing on submarine groundwater discharge and its role in delivering nutrients, carbon, and contaminants to coastal waters. Investigates coastal biogeochemical processes and land-ocean connectivity.

faculty.name: Karl D. Castillo
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on marine ecophysiology and coral reef conservation, examining how corals and reef organisms respond to ocean warming, acidification, and other climate stressors. Uses field experiments and physiological measurements to understand coral resilience.

faculty.name: Drew S. Coleman
faculty.title: Professor, Chair of E3P
faculty.summary: Research on radiogenic isotope geochemistry and geochronology, using isotopic tracers to understand igneous and crustal processes, magma generation, and the tectonic evolution of continental crust.

faculty.name: Jonathan M. Lees
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on seismology and geophysics, including volcano seismology, infrasound, and acoustic methods for studying Earth processes. Investigates links between solid Earth geophysics and climate systems.

faculty.name: Niels Lindquist
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on marine chemical ecology, studying the ecological roles of natural products in marine organisms and their potential applications in antifouling technologies and drug discovery.

faculty.name: Rick Luettich
faculty.title: Alumni Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading expert in coastal hazards and physical oceanography. Lead developer of the ADCIRC coastal circulation and storm surge model used globally for hurricane risk assessment. Research focuses on coastal flooding, sea-level rise impacts, and coastal resilience.

faculty.name: Christopher Martens
faculty.title: William B. Aycock Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on marine biogeochemistry studying carbon and methane cycling in coastal and marine sediments. Pioneer in understanding methane hydrates, benthic biogeochemical processes, and the role of coastal ecosystems in global carbon cycling.

faculty.name: Laura J. Moore
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Chair for Research
faculty.summary: Studies coastal geomorphology and ecomorphodynamics, focusing on how coastal landscapes evolve through interactions between physical processes, ecological dynamics, and human activities. Research includes barrier island and dune system dynamics.

faculty.name: Rachel T. Noble
faculty.title: Mary and Watts Hill Jr. Distinguished Professor of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Research at the land-water interface focusing on water quality, microbial ecology, and public health. Studies pathogen dynamics in coastal waters, stormwater contamination, and the development of rapid molecular methods for water quality monitoring.

faculty.name: Tamlin Pavelsky
faculty.title: Kenan Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on global hydrology using satellite remote sensing, with focus on surface water dynamics of lakes, rivers, and wetlands. Science team member for the NASA/CNES SWOT satellite mission. Studies how surface water responds to climate change globally.

faculty.name: Michael Piehler
faculty.title: Cary C. Boshamer Distinguished Professor, Director of UNC Institute for the Environment
faculty.summary: Studies aquatic ecology and biogeochemistry focusing on how nutrients, particularly nitrogen, affect coastal ecosystem function. Research spans from fundamental biogeochemical processes to applied ecosystem restoration and management.

faculty.name: Harvey Seim
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on observational physical oceanography using shipboard surveys, moored instruments, and underwater acoustics to study coastal ocean circulation, shelf dynamics, and ocean mixing processes.

faculty.name: Donna Surge
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on paleoclimatology and paleoecology using sclerochronology (growth increments in shells) and stable isotope geochemistry to reconstruct past climate and environmental conditions, with focus on Holocene climate variability.

faculty.name: Marc Alperin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on chemical oceanography and aquatic biogeochemical processes, focusing on carbon and nutrient cycling in sediments and the water column. Studies early diagenesis, organic matter degradation, and methane cycling in coastal environments.

faculty.name: Scott Gifford
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on microbial ecology and biogeochemistry in marine systems, using metagenomic and metatranscriptomic approaches to understand microbial community function, diversity, and responses to environmental change.

faculty.name: Xiaoming Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor, McMillan Scholar
faculty.summary: Research in geochemistry focused on trace element and isotope systems. Studies environmental geochemistry, critical mineral resources, and biogeochemical cycling of metals in Earth surface environments.

faculty.name: Janet Nye
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on fisheries oceanography and marine ecology, studying how climate variability and change affect fish populations and marine ecosystems. Uses ecosystem models and statistical approaches to inform fisheries management under environmental change.

faculty.name: Johanna H. Rosman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental fluid dynamics in coastal ocean settings, studying turbulence, mixing, and the hydrodynamic environment of coral reefs, kelp forests, and other coastal ecosystems.

faculty.name: Alecia Septer
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Associate Chair for Academic Programs
faculty.summary: Research on microbiology and symbiosis, studying host-microbe interactions in marine organisms, particularly the Vibrio-squid symbiosis. Uses genomic, genetic, and molecular approaches to understand bacterial colonization and symbiotic relationships.

faculty.name: Kevin G. Stewart
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on structural geology and tectonics with emphasis on Appalachian geology and field-based investigations. Also contributes to geoscience education and public understanding of Earth processes.

faculty.name: Sevan Adourian
