faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on theoretical seismology and solid Earth geophysics, developing methods for seismic wave propagation and imaging to understand Earth's interior structure and dynamics.

faculty.name: Kennet E. Flores
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on petrology, geochemistry, and tectonics, studying the formation and evolution of rocks in subduction zones and other tectonic settings. Investigates metamorphic and magmatic processes in convergent margins.

faculty.name: John Gardner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on river geomorphology and hydrology, studying how physical processes shape river channels and floodplains and how these interact with aquatic ecosystems. Investigates sediment transport, channel migration, and floodplain dynamics.

faculty.name: Sarah W.M. George
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on the interactions between tectonics and climate as recorded in the sedimentary record. Uses basin analysis, sedimentology, and geochronology to understand how mountain building and climate change shape landscapes.

faculty.name: Gilby Jepson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on low-temperature thermochronology to study tectonic processes, exhumation, and landscape evolution. Uses thermochronometric methods to constrain the timing and rates of geological processes in orogenic belts.

faculty.name: Antonia Sebastian
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on natural hazards with focus on flood risk and socio-hydrology. Investigates how urbanization, climate change, and social factors interact to influence flood vulnerability and resilience, particularly in coastal and urban environments.

faculty.name: Ken Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on physical oceanography and the cryosphere, studying ice-ocean interactions in glacier fjords and the role of ocean processes in modulating glacier melt and ice sheet stability.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Drew S. Coleman
faculty.title: Professor of Earth, Marine and Environmental Sciences; Chair of E3P
faculty.summary: Applies radiogenic isotope geochemistry and geochronology to environmental problems, including understanding contaminant transport, watershed processes, and geological controls on environmental systems. Also researches magma generation and crustal evolution.

faculty.name: Angel Hsu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Public Policy and E3P
faculty.summary: Director of the Data-Driven EnviroLab. Research uses data science and AI to analyze climate policy and environmental governance, tracking subnational and non-state climate actions globally and examining their effectiveness and accountability.

faculty.name: Andrew J. Yates
faculty.title: Professor of Economics; Director of Graduate Studies, E3P
faculty.summary: Research on environmental and resource economics, studying the design and evaluation of environmental policies, environmental markets, and economic incentives for pollution control and natural resource management.

faculty.name: John Bruno
faculty.title: Professor of Biology
faculty.summary: Research on marine ecology and conservation, focusing on the impacts of climate change on marine biodiversity, particularly coral reef ecosystems. Studies species interactions, community dynamics, and ecosystem resilience.

faculty.name: Todd BenDor
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Sustainable Community Design, City and Regional Planning
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable community design and environmental planning, focusing on green infrastructure, ecosystem service markets, and the intersection of urban development and environmental protection.

faculty.name: Clark Gray
faculty.title: Professor of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Research on population-environment interactions, studying how environmental change affects human migration and livelihoods, and how demographic change influences environmental outcomes, particularly in developing regions.

faculty.name: Miyuki Hino
faculty.title: Associate Professor of City and Regional Planning
faculty.summary: Research on climate risk, impacts, and adaptation, examining how households and communities respond to flooding and other climate hazards. Studies the effectiveness of policy interventions in reducing climate vulnerability.

faculty.name: Donald T. Hornstein
faculty.title: Thomas F. Taft Distinguished Professor of Law; Director, Center on Risk, Resilience, Energy, and the Environment
faculty.summary: Research spans environmental and energy law, administrative law, and insurance law as applied to climate risk and disaster resilience. Examines legal frameworks for managing environmental risks and promoting clean energy transitions.

faculty.name: Noah Kittner
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Sciences and Engineering & City and Regional Planning
faculty.summary: Research on energy systems and sustainability, including renewable energy integration, energy storage technology deployment, and the nexus of energy policy, public health, and environmental quality.

faculty.name: Charles Mitchell
faculty.title: Professor of Biology
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of disease ecology, community ecology, and microbial ecology. Studies how pathogen dynamics are shaped by species interactions and environmental change in plant and natural systems.

faculty.name: Conghe Song
faculty.title: Professor and Chair of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing and ecosystem modeling to study forest dynamics, land use/land cover change, and human-environment interactions. Research spans from local to global scales examining ecosystem responses to environmental change.

faculty.name: Paige Weber
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Economics
faculty.summary: Research on environmental and energy economics, examining the distributional impacts of environmental policies, energy market design, and the relationship between pollution exposure and socioeconomic outcomes.

faculty.name: Jill Stewart
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Sciences and Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on water quality microbiology, studying the ecology and fate of microbial contaminants in water systems. Investigates pathogen transport, detection methods, and the links between environmental quality and public health.

faculty.name: Jason West
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Sciences and Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on the intersection of air pollution, climate change, and human health using global atmospheric models. Investigates the health impacts of air quality under future climate scenarios and the co-benefits of climate mitigation.

faculty.name: Michael Emch
faculty.title: W.R. Kenan Jr. Distinguished Professor of Geography and Environment; Professor of Epidemiology
faculty.summary: Research on spatial epidemiology and environmental health, using geographic information systems and spatial analysis to understand disease patterns, environmental exposures, and health disparities.

faculty.name: Amanda DelVecchia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Research on aquatic ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry, examining carbon and nutrient cycling in streams, rivers, and wetlands. Investigates how disturbance and environmental change alter ecosystem function.

faculty.name: Caela O'Connell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Anthropology
faculty.summary: Research on environmental anthropology examining human-environment relationships, water governance, and agricultural livelihoods. Studies how communities navigate environmental change and resource management challenges.

faculty.name: Diego Riveros-Iregui
faculty.title: Bowman and Gordon Gray Distinguished Professor of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Research on ecohydrology and carbon cycling, studying the coupling between water and carbon cycles in terrestrial ecosystems with a focus on tropical highland and montane environments.

faculty.name: Erika Wise
faculty.title: Professor of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Research on climatology and dendrochronology using tree rings to reconstruct past climate variability, understand hydroclimate dynamics, and assess water resource vulnerabilities under climate change.

faculty.name: Lindsay L. Dubbs
faculty.title: Research Associate Professor, Institute for the Environment; Director, Outer Banks Field Site
faculty.summary: Research on ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry of coastal systems, and the environmental effects of marine renewable energy development. Integrates field research with sustainability assessment of ocean energy technologies.

faculty.name: Greg Gangi
faculty.title: Teaching Professor, Associate Director for Clean Technology and Innovation, E3P
faculty.summary: Leads the clean technology and innovation curriculum within E3P, focusing on the business, technology, and policy dimensions of clean energy and sustainability transitions. Bridges academic knowledge with entrepreneurial practice.

faculty.name: Paul Taillie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Geography and Environment
faculty.summary: Research on landscape and wildlife ecology, studying how land use and environmental change affect wildlife populations and communities, with focus on coastal ecosystems and conservation planning.

programs.university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

faculty.name: Rebecca Fry
faculty.title: Department Chair and Carol Remmer Angle Distinguished Professor in Children's Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Research uses toxicogenomic and systems biology approaches to understand the molecular mechanisms of metal-induced disease and the factors contributing to inter-individual susceptibility to environmental exposures, with emphasis on children's environmental health.

faculty.name: Barbara J. Turpin
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Leading atmospheric chemist studying air pollution formation and human exposure. Research focuses on atmospheric multiphase chemistry, including aqueous-phase formation of secondary organic aerosol in clouds and fog, and the implications for indoor and outdoor air quality.

faculty.name: Gregory Characklis
faculty.title: W. R. Kenan Jr. Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center on Financial Risk in Environmental Systems. Research addresses water resource management through economic and financial analysis, developing strategies to manage financial risk in water supply systems under growing demand and climate uncertainty.

faculty.name: Orlando Coronell
faculty.title: Philip C. Singer Distinguished Professor and Associate Chair for Academics
faculty.summary: Research on physical and chemical processes for water and wastewater treatment, with focus on membrane-based separations, advanced sorbent materials, and the removal of emerging contaminants including PFAS from water systems.

faculty.name: Jason D. Surratt
faculty.title: Cary C. Boshamer Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry focusing on the formation and evolution of secondary organic aerosols. Investigates the chemical mechanisms by which volatile organic compounds are transformed into particulate matter in the atmosphere.

faculty.name: William Vizuete
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses computational models to study atmospheric chemistry and air pollution. Research focuses on understanding ozone and particulate matter formation in urban atmospheres through the integration of chemical mechanisms and atmospheric models.

faculty.name: Jason West
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research at the nexus of air pollution, climate change, and human health. Uses global atmospheric models to quantify the health impacts of air pollution and assess the co-benefits of climate change mitigation for air quality and public health.

faculty.name: Dale Whittington
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water and sanitation economics with extensive experience in developing countries. Studies household water demand, the economic valuation of water and sanitation improvements, and the planning and financing of water infrastructure.

faculty.name: Joe Brown
faculty.title: Professor, Water Institute Director
faculty.summary: Research on water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) in low-income settings, studying environmental health microbiology and the effectiveness of interventions to reduce enteric infectious disease transmission.

faculty.name: Kun Lu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research uses metabolomics and exposomics approaches to understand how environmental exposures affect human health at the molecular level, developing biomarkers of exposure and effect using advanced mass spectrometry techniques.

faculty.name: Cass T. Miller
faculty.title: Okun Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research on the fate and transport of contaminants in subsurface and porous media systems, developing advanced computational methods to simulate multiphase flow and reactive transport in groundwater environments.

faculty.name: Glenn Morrison
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on indoor air quality and the chemistry of indoor surfaces, studying how buildings influence human exposure to pollutants through interactions between air, surfaces, and occupants. Focus on semi-volatile organic compounds and exposure science.

faculty.name: Aaron Salzberg
faculty.title: Jennifer and Don Holzworth Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Research and practice at the intersection of water, sanitation, and international policy. Previously served as the first Special Coordinator for Water Resources at the U.S. Department of State, directing U.S. foreign policy on drinking water and water resources.

faculty.name: Noah Kittner
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies energy systems planning and policy with a focus on renewable energy integration, battery storage deployment, and the environmental and equity dimensions of energy transitions. Also holds appointment in City and Regional Planning.

faculty.name: Amanda Northcross
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on air pollution exposure assessment with focus on household air pollution in low- and middle-income countries. Develops and evaluates low-cost monitoring technologies to characterize personal exposures and inform interventions.

faculty.name: Julia Rager
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Associate Chair for Strategic Initiatives
faculty.summary: Integrates computational toxicology, bioinformatics, and exposure science to understand the health effects of environmental chemical mixtures. Develops data-driven approaches for chemical risk assessment and high-throughput screening.

faculty.name: Marc Serre
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops geostatistical and spatial analysis methods for environmental exposure assessment. Research focuses on mapping environmental contaminants in water and air to inform epidemiological studies and risk assessment.

faculty.name: Courtney G. Woods
faculty.title: Associate Professor, MPH Program Director
faculty.summary: Research on environmental justice and health disparities, using community-based participatory research approaches to address environmental health inequities and build community capacity for environmental decision-making.

faculty.name: Musa Manga
faculty.title: Joan Heckler Gillings Endowed Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on sustainable water and sanitation systems for low-resource settings, focusing on non-sewered sanitation, resource recovery from waste, and the intersection of sanitation, public health, and environmental quality.

faculty.name: Ryan Cronk
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on global water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) systems, focusing on the sustainability of WASH interventions, monitoring and evaluation methods, and the development of resilient environmental health infrastructure.

faculty.name: Michael B. Fisher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on global drinking water quality and access, developing and evaluating appropriate technologies for water quality monitoring and treatment in low-resource settings, and studying implementation approaches for lasting WASH improvements.

faculty.name: Paul Watkins
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading expert in mechanistic toxicology and drug-induced liver injury. Research bridges preclinical toxicology and clinical translation, developing novel biomarkers and approaches for predicting and understanding hepatotoxicity.

programs.program_name: Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences PhD

funding.note: Full-tuition scholarship + stipend typical ($39k-44k for 2026-27 per Grad School)

faculty.name: Melissa Berke
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies molecular paleoclimatology and paleoenvironmental change using biogeochemical approaches, with work spanning paleolimnology and paleoceanography.

faculty.name: Kyle Bibby
faculty.title: Keating-Crawford Collegiate Professor of Environmental Engineering; Associate Department Chair
faculty.summary: Researches microbiology relevant to water quality and public health, including the detection of human pathogenic viruses in the environment.

faculty.name: Diogo Bolster
faculty.title: Frank M. Freimann Professor of Hydrology and Henry Massman Department Chair
faculty.summary: Works on environmental fluid dynamics, transport and reactive flows in heterogeneous porous media, multiphase flow in porous media, buoyancy driven flows, and sustainable building ventilation.

faculty.name: Patrick Brewick
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches system identification, structural health monitoring, Bayesian model calibration, structural dynamics, condition assessment, and uncertainty quantification.

faculty.name: Peter C. Burns
faculty.title: Dorini Family Professor of Energy Studies
faculty.summary: Specializes in environmental mineralogy and the geochemistry of nuclear materials, including research related to nuclear waste disposal.

faculty.name: Paola Crippa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Focuses on air quality and aerosol dynamics modeling, climate change, and wind energy applications.

faculty.name: Kyle Doudrick
faculty.title: Rooney Family Collegiate Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies physical-chemical environmental processes with a focus on water quality and treatment and emerging contaminants.

faculty.name: Jeremy Fein
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches geomicrobiology and aqueous environmental geochemistry, applying chemical thermodynamics to model bacteria-water-rock systems.

faculty.name: Harindra Joseph Fernando
faculty.title: Wayne and Diana Murdy Endowed Professor of Engineering and Geosciences
faculty.summary: Studies environmental and industrial flows, atmospheric and oceanic processes, wind energy, and contaminant dispersion and air pollution.

faculty.name: Alan Hamlet
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Works on surface water hydrology, hydroclimatology and hydrologic extremes, hydrologic modeling, water resources planning and management, and climate impacts.

faculty.name: Amy Hixon
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches actinide (geo)chemistry, actinide materials, nuclear forensics, and the nuclear fuel cycle.

faculty.name: Ahsan Kareem
faculty.title: Robert M. Moran Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies structural dynamics, wind effects on structures, stochastic systems, fluid-structure interactions, natural hazards, and wind energy.

faculty.name: Andrew Kennedy
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches coastal engineering, experimental techniques for nearshore waves, coastal geomorphology, and remote sensing techniques in the coastal zone.

faculty.name: Kapil Khandelwal
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Works on structural and solid mechanics, computational mechanics, topology optimization, and machine learning and data sciences.

faculty.name: Tracy Kijewski-Correa
faculty.title: Frank M. Freimann Professor of Structural Engineering and Hazard Resilience
faculty.summary: Focuses on disaster risk reduction, resilient and sustainable communities, and post-disaster reconnaissance.

faculty.name: Yahya C. Kurama
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches concrete structures, steel-concrete hybrid and composite structures, and earthquake engineering and structural dynamics.

faculty.name: Wade McGillis
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies ocean, estuary, and river ecosystems, with emphasis on water quality and acidification.

faculty.name: Clive Neal
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies the origin and evolution of the Moon and large igneous provinces, along with space exploration science.

faculty.name: Robert Nerenberg
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Researches biofilms, especially membrane-biofilm reactors (MBfRs) and membrane biofouling, for environmental engineering applications.

faculty.name: David Richter
faculty.title: Frank M. Freimann Collegiate Professor of Environmental Fluid Dynamics
faculty.summary: Studies fluid mechanics and multiphase environmental flows, including turbulence in the atmosphere and ocean, particle-laden turbulence, and computational fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Joshua Shrout
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies bacterial biofilms and the socio-microbiology of bacteria in engineered systems and public health, including bacterial genetics, surface motility, and bioremediation.

faculty.name: Antonio Simonetti
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Develops analytical protocols for isotope measurements and conducts geochemical and radiogenic isotope investigations of rocks, long-range tracing of atmospheric pollution, and isotope studies of archaeological materials.

faculty.name: Alexandros Taflanidis
faculty.title: Professor, Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Natural hazard risk and uncertainty quantification
faculty.summary: Works on computational statistics, uncertainty quantification, natural hazard risk assessment, disaster risk reduction, and optimization under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Ashley Thrall
faculty.title: Myron and Rosemary Noble Collegiate Professor of Structural Engineering; Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches the behavior, design, and optimization of kinetic civil infrastructure such as bridges, shelters, and buildings, including modular systems that are rapidly moveable, erectable, and deployable.

faculty.name: Joannes Westerink
faculty.title: Joseph and Nona Ahearn Professor in Computational Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops computational fluid dynamics and finite element methods for modeling circulation and transport in coastal seas and oceans, including tidal hydrodynamics and hurricane storm surge prediction.

faculty.name: David L. Goldsby
faculty.title: Professor & Department Chair
faculty.summary: Investigates mineral and rock physics with emphasis on the rheological behavior of Earth and planetary materials, using experiments on grain size-sensitive flow and grain boundary sliding in materials such as olivine and ice. His work illuminates how creep behaviors control rheology in glaciers, icy satellites, and planetary interiors.

faculty.name: Joseph S. Francisco
faculty.title: President's Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Applies experimental physical and theoretical chemistry to atmospheric chemistry problems at the molecular level, including new chemistries on cloud surface interfaces, effects of solar radiation on interfacial reactions, and chemistry related to acid rain.

faculty.name: Reto Gieré
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Examines Earth materials, their response to geological forces, and their interaction with the environment and humans, spanning environmental geochemistry, mineralogy, petrology, and the health impacts of atmospheric pollution with a focus on energy, waste, and sustainability.

faculty.name: Jon Hawkings
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Biogeochemist investigating the role of glacial meltwater in downstream biogeochemical cycles, focusing on how meltwater influences ecosystem productivity and nutrient mobilization in freshwater and coastal environments, with fieldwork in Arctic, Patagonian, and Himalayan regions.

faculty.name: Douglas J. Jerolmack
faculty.title: Edmund J. and Louise W. Kahn Endowed Term Professor of Earth and Environmental Science; Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Applied Mechanics
faculty.summary: Conducts experimental geophysics research on the spatial and temporal evolution of patterns that emerge at the interface of fluid and sediment on Earth and planetary surfaces, integrating laboratory experiments, field studies, and theoretical modeling of granular physics and landform dynamics.

faculty.name: Michael Mann
faculty.title: Presidential Distinguished Professor; Director of Penn Center for Science, Sustainability and the Media
faculty.summary: Works on understanding climate science and climate change, with particular focus on climate variability and extremes, paleoclimate, tropical cyclones, and climate education and policy.

faculty.name: Irina Marinov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies Southern Ocean dynamics and how the ocean regulates atmospheric carbon dioxide and global climate, modeling and observing ocean nutrients, carbon cycles, and phytoplankton, including feedbacks between climate change, the ocean carbon cycle, and ocean ecology.

faculty.name: Ileana Pérez-Rodríguez
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geomicrobiology, ecophysiology, bioenergetics, and metabolic ecology to understand microbial processes and their biogeochemical impacts on environmental systems and Earth's history.

faculty.name: Alain F. Plante
faculty.title: Professor & Faculty Director, University Scholars Program
faculty.summary: Studies terrestrial carbon biogeochemistry, focusing on the mechanisms and processes that act to stabilize and de-stabilize organic carbon in surface soils and their connection to the global carbon cycle.

faculty.name: Leigh Stearns
faculty.title: Professor, Graduate Chair
faculty.summary: Investigates how a changing climate impacts the cryosphere and the risks associated with those changes, examining ice sheet dynamics, sea level rise, and Arctic sea ice using satellite remote sensing, machine learning, and geophysical instrumentation.

faculty.name: Hugo N. Ulloa
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Fluid dynamicist studying geophysical and environmental flows, particularly transport and mixing processes in natural waters such as lakes and coastal seas, using theory, numerical modeling, and experimental observations.

faculty.name: Einara Zahn
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how turbulent processes at the Earth's surface interact with the atmosphere across scales through theory, simulations, and field observations, with focus on land-atmosphere interactions in natural and urban environments.

programs.program_name: PhD in Geosciences (Earth and Environmental Sciences)

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition scholarship, annual stipend, health insurance

faculty.name: Erin Black
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Marine carbon cycling and isotope geochemistry
faculty.summary: Investigates marine carbon cycling using isotope geochemistry and uranium-thorium series radionuclides. Her work examines the biological carbon pump and how elements like iron and nitrogen influence phytoplankton productivity in the ocean.

faculty.name: Chiara Borrelli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on paleoceanography and paleoclimatology using foraminifera, stable isotopes, and trace elements, including studies of biomineralization.

faculty.name: Sean Crowell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing and atmospheric transport modeling to study the terrestrial carbon cycle and anthropogenic carbon emissions.

faculty.name: Rachel Glade
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences & Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies geomorphology and landscape evolution, with emphasis on sediment transport, fluid mechanics, granular physics, and pattern formation in Earth surface processes.

faculty.name: John Kessler
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans marine and freshwater chemistry, isotope geochemistry, and analytical chemistry, including studies of oceanic methane dynamics.

faculty.name: Lee Murray
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops and applies 3-D global models of atmospheric chemistry and climate, examining couplings between atmospheric chemistry and the climate system.

faculty.name: Miki Nakajima
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences & Physics and Astronomy
faculty.summary: Studies the dynamics of planetary impacts, the evolution of planetary interiors, and planet formation, including the formation of the early Earth and Moon.

faculty.name: Tolulope Olugboji
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Science & Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Works on Earth imaging with seismic signals and intelligent algorithms, studying the global structure of the lithosphere through seismic signal processing.

faculty.name: Vasilii Petrenko
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research aims at understanding natural and anthropogenic climate and environmental change, particularly through records of atmospheric composition and chemistry such as ice cores.

faculty.name: Kevin Righter
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies igneous petrology, mantle geochemistry, and experimental petrology, with interests in the origin of the Earth and Moon and planetary melting processes.

faculty.name: Dustin Trail
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences, Department Chair
faculty.summary: Uses experimental geochemistry to study the early Earth, planetary geology, astrobiology, and lunar geochemistry, with emphasis on isotope geochemistry.

faculty.name: Thomas Weber
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Studies ocean biogeochemical cycles and trace element oceanography, including how marine ecosystems respond to climate change.

requirements.admission_model: Direct-admit to department; Fall admission only

funding.note: PhD applicants considered for funding (RA/TA/fellowship); verify package

faculty.name: Burcin Becerik-Gerber
faculty.title: Professor and Fred Champion Chair in Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on intelligent infrastructure systems, human-centered design, building informatics, and occupant behavior modeling for sustainable and resilient built environments.

faculty.name: Bora Gencturk
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on performance of reinforced concrete structures under extreme loads, advanced materials for infrastructure, and seismic design and retrofit.

faculty.name: Roger Ghanem
faculty.title: Tryon Chair in Stochastic Methods; Professor of CEE and Aerospace & Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on stochastic modeling, uncertainty quantification in engineering systems, probabilistic methods for structural mechanics and multi-scale materials modeling.

faculty.name: Erik A. Johnson
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on structural dynamics and control, earthquake engineering, semi-active damping systems, and vibration mitigation strategies for civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Behrokh Khoshnevis
faculty.title: Louise L. Dunn Endowed Professor and Distinguished Professor of CEE, Astronautics, and Aerospace & Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on automated construction, large-scale 3D printing (Contour Crafting), robotics in construction, and advanced manufacturing for civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Lucio Soibelman
faculty.title: Fred Champion Chair in Engineering; Professor of CEE and Spatial Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on advanced data acquisition, processing and analysis for civil infrastructure, building information modeling, and construction engineering and management.

faculty.name: Mihailo Trifunac
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake engineering, strong motion seismology, soil-structure interaction, and seismic hazard assessment.

faculty.name: Patrick Lynett
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on coastal and ocean engineering, tsunami generation and propagation, nearshore wave dynamics, and coastal hazard mitigation.

faculty.name: Costas Synolakis
faculty.title: Gordon S. Marshall Professor of Engineering Technology and Professor of CEE
faculty.summary: Research on tsunami science, coastal hazards, wave runup and inundation modeling, and disaster resilience for coastal communities.

faculty.name: Felipe De Barros
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on subsurface hydrology, contaminant transport in heterogeneous porous media, stochastic hydrogeology, and uncertainty quantification in groundwater systems.

faculty.name: Chukwuebuka C. Nweke
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on geotechnical earthquake engineering, seismic site response, basin effects on ground motion, and seismic hazard characterization for infrastructure.

faculty.name: Birendra Jha
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical Engineering & Materials Science and CEE
faculty.summary: Research on geomechanics, poromechanics of subsurface formations, induced seismicity, and coupled processes in energy geotechnics.

faculty.name: Genevieve Giuliano
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Public Policy and CEE
faculty.summary: Research on transportation policy, land use-transportation interactions, urban freight, and metropolitan accessibility and mobility.

faculty.name: Ketan Savla
faculty.title: John & Dorothy Shea Early Career Chair and Associate Professor of CEE, Electrical & Computer Engineering, and Aerospace & Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on control and optimization of transportation networks, autonomous mobility systems, and distributed decision-making in infrastructure systems.

faculty.name: John P. Wilson
faculty.title: Professor of Sociology, Computer Science, CEE, Architecture, and Preventive Medicine
faculty.summary: Research on geographic information science, spatial analysis, terrain modeling, environmental modeling with GIS, and cyberinfrastructure for spatial data.

faculty.name: Mitul Luhar
faculty.title: Henry Salvatori Early Career Chair and Associate Professor of Aerospace & Mechanical Engineering and CEE
faculty.summary: Research on fluid mechanics and turbulence, environmental and geophysical flows, flow through vegetation and urban canopies, and transport processes.

faculty.name: Qiming Wang
faculty.title: Stephen Schrank Early Career Chair and Associate Professor of CEE
faculty.summary: Research on mechanics of architected and bioinspired materials, multi-material additive manufacturing, and multifunctional composites for civil infrastructure.

faculty.name: Audrey Olivier
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on uncertainty quantification in structural engineering, Bayesian model updating, structural health monitoring, and probabilistic inverse problems.

faculty.name: Adam Simpson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on computational mechanics and numerical methods applied to civil infrastructure systems and structural analysis.

faculty.name: Najmedin Meshkati
faculty.title: Professor of CEE, Industrial Systems Engineering, and International Relations
faculty.summary: Research on safety and human factors in complex large-scale infrastructure systems, disaster risk reduction, and organizational reliability of critical infrastructure.

requirements.admission_model: Direct-admit to department; Fall admission only

faculty.name: Amy Childress
faculty.title: Walt Singer Endowed Chair and Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on membrane processes for water and wastewater treatment, desalination, water reuse, osmotic membrane bioreactors, and colloid/interface science in environmental systems.

faculty.name: Constantinos Sioutas
faculty.title: Fred Champion Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on air quality, particulate matter characterization, aerosol measurement technologies, source apportionment, and health effects of airborne pollutants in urban environments.

faculty.name: Massoud Pirbazari
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on physico-chemical processes for water and wastewater treatment, adsorption technologies, environmental fate and transport of contaminants, and advanced oxidation processes.

faculty.name: Kelly Sanders
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on the energy-water nexus, climate change impacts on water and energy systems, sustainable energy transitions, and urban environmental footprints.

faculty.name: Adam Smith
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on anaerobic biotechnologies for water resource recovery, wastewater treatment, environmental antibiotic resistance, and microbial ecology of engineered systems.

faculty.name: Felipe De Barros
faculty.title: Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on subsurface hydrology, contaminant transport in heterogeneous porous media, stochastic hydrogeology, and sustainable groundwater management under uncertainty.

faculty.name: George Ban-Weiss
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on urban climate and air quality, urban heat island mitigation, cool roofs and pavements, atmospheric aerosol-radiation interactions, and climate impacts on air pollution.

faculty.name: Daniel McCurry
faculty.title: Dr. Shiao-Ping Siao Yen Early Career Chair and Associate Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental chemistry of water treatment processes, disinfection byproduct formation, transformation of organic micropollutants, and advanced oxidation for water reuse.

faculty.name: Kandis (Leslie) Gilliard-AbdulAziz
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Pasquale & Adelina Arpea Early Career Chair in CEE and Chemical Engineering & Materials Science
faculty.summary: Research on heterogeneous catalysis for environmental applications, CO2 conversion to value-added products, emissions control, and sustainable chemical processes.

faculty.name: James Moffett
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on speciation and redox chemistry of trace elements in natural waters, marine biogeochemistry, metal-microbe interactions, and environmental cycling of metals.

faculty.name: Sam Silva
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth Sciences and Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry and composition, air quality modeling using machine learning and computational methods, and interactions between climate and atmospheric chemistry.

faculty.name: Jiachen Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of CEE, Population & Public Health Sciences, and Spatial Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on air pollution exposure assessment, spatial modeling of environmental health risks, urban air quality, and linkages between air pollution and public health outcomes.

faculty.name: Thomas Petersen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental engineering with focus on water quality, treatment processes, and environmental chemistry of natural and engineered systems.

faculty.name: Ruolin Li
faculty.title: WiSE Gabilan Assistant Professor of CEE and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of environmental engineering and computer science, applying machine learning and data-driven approaches to environmental and water systems challenges.

faculty.name: Carol Folt
faculty.title: University Distinguished Chair; Professor of CEE, Population & Public Health Sciences, and Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on environmental toxicology, effects of metals on aquatic ecosystems, ecosystem responses to environmental stressors, and the intersection of environmental and public health.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs: tuition + annual stipend via RA/TA/fellowship

faculty.name: Sylvain Barbot
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake mechanics, fault friction, crustal deformation, and the physics of earthquake cycles using geodetic observations and numerical models.

faculty.name: Yehuda Ben-Zion
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake seismology, fault zone structure and dynamics, seismic wave propagation in heterogeneous media, and earthquake source processes.

faculty.name: Thomas H. Jordan
faculty.title: University Professor, William M. Keck Foundation Chair in Geological Sciences and Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake seismology, seismic hazard analysis, geodynamics of plate boundaries, and operational earthquake forecasting and early warning systems.

faculty.name: John Vidale
faculty.title: Dean's Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake seismology, Earth's deep interior structure, earthquake source imaging, and seismic wave propagation through complex structures.

faculty.name: Heidi Houston
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on earthquake source processes, tectonic tremor and slow slip, subduction zone seismicity, and the mechanics of fault slip at plate boundaries.

faculty.name: Ahmed Elbanna
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences and Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on computational geophysics, earthquake rupture dynamics, friction and fracture on faults, and multi-scale modeling of geophysical processes.

faculty.name: John Platt
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on structural geology and tectonics, mechanics of continental deformation, metamorphic core complexes, and tectonic evolution of orogenic belts.

faculty.name: James Dolan
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on active tectonics, earthquake geology, paleoseismology, fault system behavior, and seismic hazard characterization for urban regions.

faculty.name: William Berelson
faculty.title: Paxson H. Offield Professor in Coastal and Marine Systems and Professor of Earth Sciences, Environmental Studies and Spatial Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine and coastal biogeochemistry, carbon cycling at the seafloor, sediment-water interface processes, and geochemical proxies for paleoclimate reconstruction.

faculty.name: Julien Emile-Geay
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on paleoclimate data analysis, climate dynamics and variability, data assimilation methods for climate reconstruction, and tropical Pacific climate over the Holocene.

faculty.name: Sarah Feakins
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on organic geochemistry and paleoclimate, plant wax biomarkers and stable isotopes, terrestrial climate and vegetation change, and hydrological cycle through Earth history.

faculty.name: Lowell Stott
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on paleoceanography and paleoclimatology, stable isotope geochemistry, ocean circulation changes through glacial-interglacial cycles, and abrupt climate change.

faculty.name: Josh West
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on Earth surface processes, chemical weathering and erosion, carbon cycle linkages to landscape evolution, and geochemical cycles at the critical zone.

faculty.name: Frank Corsetti
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on sedimentology and stratigraphy, carbonate depositional systems, microbialite formation, mass extinction events, and the co-evolution of life and environments.

faculty.name: David Bottjer
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences, Biological Sciences and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on paleontology and paleoecology, evolutionary paleobiology of marine invertebrates, mass extinction and recovery dynamics, and the early evolution of animal life.

faculty.name: Steve Lund
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on paleomagnetism, geomagnetic field behavior, environmental magnetism as a paleoclimate proxy, and late Quaternary climate variability from lake and marine sediments.

faculty.name: Seth John
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on trace metal biogeochemistry in the ocean, stable isotope fractionation of metals, marine micronutrient cycling, and the role of trace metals in ocean productivity.

faculty.name: Naomi Levine
faculty.title: Gabilan Distinguished Professorship in Science and Engineering and Professor of Biological Sciences, Quantitative and Computational Biology, and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on interactions between climate and marine microbial ecosystems, using computational and theoretical approaches to model phytoplankton community dynamics and biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Karen Lloyd
faculty.title: Wrigley Chair in Environmental Studies and Professor of Earth Sciences and Marine & Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on microbial life in Earth's subsurface, deep marine sediments and crust, hydrothermal vent microbiology, and biogeochemical cycling of carbon in the deep biosphere.

faculty.name: Sergio Sanudo-Wilhelmy
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine and environmental biogeochemistry, the role of bioactive trace metals and vitamins in ocean productivity, carbon and nitrogen fixation, and nutrient limitation.

faculty.name: Cameron Thrash
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on genomics and physiology of marine bacterioplankton, high-throughput cultivation of marine microbes, and the role of bacteria in ocean biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Caroline Seyler
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on organic geochemistry and microbial ecology, molecular biomarkers for microbial processes, and carbon cycling in modern and ancient environments.

faculty.name: Noah Phillips
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on geophysical processes using computational and numerical methods to understand Earth structure and dynamics.

faculty.name: Drew Steen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on life in low-energy environments, microbial ecology of deep marine sediments, and geomicrobiology of Earth's slowest metabolic environments.

faculty.name: Sam Silva
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth Sciences, Civil & Environmental Engineering, and Population & Public Health Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on atmospheric chemistry and climate interactions, using machine learning and data science approaches to understand air quality and composition-climate feedbacks.

faculty.name: Douglas Capone
faculty.title: William and Julie Wrigley Chair in Environmental Studies and Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Capone studies the role and importance of marine microbes in major biogeochemical cycles, particularly those of nitrogen and carbon.

faculty.name: David Caron
faculty.title: USC Associates Captain Allan Hancock Chair in Marine Science and Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Caron's research addresses marine and freshwater microbial ecology, including the ecological roles of protists and other microbes in aquatic ecosystems.

faculty.name: Suzanne Edmands
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Edmands studies conservation, population, and evolutionary genetics using a combination of fieldwork and molecular analyses, including work on the consequences of interbreeding between divergent populations.

faculty.name: Jed Fuhrman
faculty.title: McCulloch-Crosby Chair in Marine Biology and Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Fuhrman investigates the roles of microorganisms in natural marine ecosystems and the cycling of matter in the ocean.

faculty.name: Andrew Gracey
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Gracey studies how environmental factors such as temperature, oxygen concentration, and salinity affect the physiological processes of marine animals.

faculty.name: John Heidelberg
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Heidelberg's research is in the field of microbial genomics, examining the metabolic potential of common and abundant marine bacteria.

faculty.name: Noelle Held
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Held leads the Proteocean Lab and studies cellular decision-making by marine microbes and how this influences their contributions to ocean biogeochemical cycles, using environmental proteomics.

faculty.name: David Hutchins
faculty.title: Professor and George and Louise Kawamoto Chair of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Hutchins studies marine phytoplankton biology and nutrient and carbon cycling, including how ocean microbes respond to global change.

faculty.name: Carly Kenkel
faculty.title: Wilford and Daris Zinsmeyer Early Career Chair in Marine Studies and Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Kenkel studies the sources of phenotypic variation and how ecology and organism-environment interactions influence the evolutionary trajectories of marine organisms such as corals.

faculty.name: Dale Kiefer
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Kiefer studies the population dynamics of microbial populations in the sea and the material and energy flow within planktonic populations.

faculty.name: Naomi Levine
faculty.title: Gabilan Distinguished Professor of Biological Sciences, Quantitative and Computational Biology, and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Levine's research focuses on understanding the interactions between climate and marine microbial ecosystem composition and function.

faculty.name: Karen Lloyd
faculty.title: Wrigley Chair in Environmental Studies and Professor of Earth Sciences and Marine & Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Lloyd works on discovering and describing life inside Earth's crust, combining bioinformatics with geochemical measurements.

faculty.name: Donal Manahan
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Manahan studies organism-environment interactions spanning diverse environments, with emphasis on the physiology and energetics of marine invertebrate development.

faculty.name: James Moffett
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences, Earth Sciences, and Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Moffett studies the geochemistry of the oceans, including the speciation and redox chemistry of trace elements in natural waters.

faculty.name: Sergio Sanudo-Wilhelmy
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Sanudo-Wilhelmy studies environmental biogeochemistry and how bioactive substances such as vitamins and trace elements influence biological processes and phytoplankton dynamics.

faculty.name: Julia Schwartzman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Schwartzman studies emergent multicellular behaviors in microbial ecosystems using genomics, genetics, and quantitative light microscopy.

faculty.name: Andrew Steen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Steen studies life in low-energy environments and how microbes affect the Earth, particularly microbe-organic matter interactions.

faculty.name: Cameron Thrash
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Thrash investigates the relationships among aquatic microorganisms and their environment through the genomics and physiology of marine bacterioplankton, combining cultivation and multi-omics approaches.

faculty.name: Eric Webb
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Webb studies marine cyanobacterial physiology, ecology, and genomics, including nutrient stress monitoring and secondary metabolite production.

faculty.name: Douglas Capone
faculty.title: William and Julie Wrigley Chair in Environmental Studies, Professor of Biological Sciences; Chair of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on the role of marine microbes in biogeochemical cycles, particularly nitrogen fixation and carbon cycling in ocean ecosystems, spanning from coastal to open-ocean environments.

faculty.name: David Caron
faculty.title: USC Associates Captain Allan Hancock Chair in Marine Science, Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine and freshwater microbial ecology, protistan ecology and diversity, harmful algal bloom dynamics, and the structure and function of microbial food webs.

faculty.name: Jed Fuhrman
faculty.title: McCulloch-Crosby Chair in Marine Biology, Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on microorganisms in marine ecosystems, ocean nutrient cycling, marine viral ecology, and fate of human pathogenic viruses in coastal waters.

faculty.name: David Hutchins
faculty.title: George and Louise Kawamoto Chair in Biological Sciences, Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine phytoplankton biology, nutrient and carbon cycling in the ocean, effects of climate change and ocean acidification on phytoplankton communities, and trace metal biogeochemistry.

faculty.name: Naomi Levine
faculty.title: Gabilan Distinguished Professor, Professor of Biological Sciences, Quantitative and Computational Biology, and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on interactions between climate and marine microbial ecosystem composition and function, using computational modeling to understand phytoplankton community dynamics and their role in global biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: James Moffett
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences, Earth Sciences, and Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on ocean geochemistry, speciation and redox chemistry of trace elements in natural waters, metal-microbe interactions, and the marine biogeochemical cycling of trace metals.

faculty.name: Sergio Sanudo-Wilhelmy
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on environmental biogeochemistry, how bioactive trace metals and vitamins influence carbon and nitrogen fixation, and the role of micronutrients in controlling ocean productivity.

faculty.name: Cameron Thrash
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on genomics and physiology of marine bacterioplankton, high-throughput dilution-to-extinction cultivation of marine microbes, and the functional role of bacteria in ocean ecosystems.

faculty.name: Eric Webb
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Environmental Studies; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on marine cyanobacterial physiology, ecology, and genomics, nutrient and trace metal stress responses, and secondary metabolite production in marine microbes.

faculty.name: Karen Lloyd
faculty.title: Wrigley Chair in Environmental Studies, Professor of Earth Sciences and Marine & Environmental Biology
faculty.summary: Research on microbial life in Earth's crust, subsurface microorganisms in hydrothermal vents and deep marine sediments, and the role of the deep biosphere in global carbon cycling.

faculty.name: Donal Manahan
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on organism-environment interactions in marine organisms spanning warm to polar environments, physiological adaptation, and developmental energetics of marine invertebrates.

faculty.name: Dale Kiefer
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on population dynamics of marine microbial populations, material and energy flow within planktonic ecosystems, and optical approaches to studying marine primary production.

faculty.name: Suzanne Edmands
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on conservation and population genetics, reproductive isolation in marine organisms, environmental stress tolerance, and aging in marine populations.

faculty.name: Carly Kenkel
faculty.title: Wilford and Daris Zinsmeyer Early Career Chair in Marine Studies, Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on phenotypic variation and how ecological factors induce or select for different phenotypes in marine organisms, particularly corals and reef-associated species under climate stress.

faculty.name: Andrew Gracey
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on the effects of temperature, oxygen, and salinity on marine animal physiology, adaptive responses to environmental stress, and the evolutionary development of stress tolerance.

faculty.name: Noelle Held
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on cellular decision-making by marine microbes and their role in biogeochemical cycles, using environmental proteomics to understand microbial function in ocean systems.

faculty.name: Julia Schwartzman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on emergent multicellular behaviors in marine bacteria, how bacteria cycle nutrients in ocean environments, and formation of symbiotic associations in marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: John Heidelberg
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Sciences and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on microbial genomics, genome sequencing of abundant marine bacteria to understand metabolic potential and ecological function, and metagenomic approaches to marine microbial communities.

faculty.name: Seth John
faculty.title: Professor of Earth Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on trace metal biogeochemistry in the ocean, stable isotope fractionation of metals as biogeochemical tracers, and the role of micronutrient metals in ocean productivity and carbon cycling.

faculty.name: William Berelson
faculty.title: Paxson H. Offield Professor in Coastal and Marine Systems, Professor of Earth Sciences, Environmental Studies and Spatial Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on marine and coastal biogeochemistry, carbon cycling at the seafloor, sediment diagenesis, and the role of coastal and marine sediments in global biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Robert Gilbert
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research on geotechnical engineering with focus on reliability-based design, site characterization, and foundation engineering.

faculty.name: Oguzhan Bayrak
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on structural engineering with focus on reinforced and prestressed concrete, bridge engineering, and structural evaluation.

faculty.name: Amit Bhasin
faculty.title: Professor and CTR Director
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Transportation Research. Research on transportation infrastructure materials, asphalt pavement characterization, and sustainable pavement engineering.

faculty.name: Chandra Bhat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on transportation with focus on travel behavior analysis, activity-based modeling, and econometric methods for transportation planning.

faculty.name: Stephen Boyles
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on transportation network modeling, traffic assignment, routing optimization, and network equilibrium analysis.

faculty.name: Carlos Caldas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on construction engineering and project management with focus on construction information technology, automation, and infrastructure delivery systems.

faculty.name: Christian Claudel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on intelligent transportation systems, wireless sensor networks for traffic monitoring, and autonomous vehicle integration into transportation networks.

faculty.name: Kasey Faust
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on infrastructure systems resilience with focus on socio-technical systems, public policy integration, and community-level infrastructure decision-making.

faculty.name: Raissa Ferron
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on construction materials with focus on concrete rheology, sustainable cementitious materials, and advanced characterization of cement-based materials.

faculty.name: Kevin Folliard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on concrete materials durability, alkali-silica reaction mitigation, and long-term performance of concrete infrastructure.

faculty.name: Todd Helwig
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on structural engineering with focus on steel structures, structural stability, and bridge engineering. Expert in lateral-torsional buckling and bracing design.

faculty.name: Ben Hodges
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on environmental fluid mechanics and water resources with focus on hydrodynamic modeling of rivers, lakes, and coastal systems.

faculty.name: Maria Juenger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on cement chemistry and sustainable construction materials. Focuses on alternative cementitious materials and concrete durability for reduced environmental impact.

faculty.name: Loukas Kallivokas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on computational mechanics, wave propagation in solids, inverse problems in elastodynamics, and soil-structure interaction.

faculty.name: Lynn Katz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water quality, environmental chemistry, contaminant fate and transport, and advanced water treatment technologies.

faculty.name: Spyros Kinnas
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on ocean engineering with focus on propeller hydrodynamics, cavitation modeling, and offshore structure hydrodynamics.

faculty.name: Kerry Kinney
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on air quality engineering with focus on indoor air quality, biofiltration for air pollution control, and environmental microbiology applications.

faculty.name: Mary Jo Kirisits
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on drinking water quality with focus on microbiology of water distribution systems, biofilm formation, and water treatment process optimization.

faculty.name: Kara Kockelman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on transportation systems with focus on travel demand modeling, autonomous vehicle adoption impacts, and shared mobility systems.

faculty.name: Krishna Kumar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on geotechnical engineering with focus on AI applications in civil engineering, numerical modeling of granular materials, and multiscale mechanics.

faculty.name: Lance Manuel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on wind engineering, structural reliability, stochastic mechanics, and their applications to buildings, bridges, and offshore structures.

faculty.name: Paola Passalacqua
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water resources and landscape evolution with focus on river delta dynamics, remote sensing of surface water, and eco-hydrological processes.

faculty.name: Ellen Rathje
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on geotechnical earthquake engineering with focus on seismic site response analysis, slope stability during earthquakes, and geotechnical hazard assessment.

faculty.name: Charles Werth
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water quality with focus on contaminant transport in groundwater, advanced remediation technologies, and catalytic treatment of water pollutants.

faculty.name: Lina Sela
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water distribution systems with focus on smart water networks, infrastructure monitoring, and hydraulic modeling for leak detection and water quality management.

faculty.name: Kenneth Dunton
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Dunton investigates coastal ecosystem processes and aquatic plant physiological ecology, focusing on how climate change affects plant species composition, carbon storage, and food web structure across tropical to polar systems. His work emphasizes foundation species such as seagrasses and kelp in productivity and carbon cycling.

faculty.name: Edward Buskey
faculty.title: Professor; Nancy Lee and Perry R. Bass Regents Chair in Marine Science
faculty.summary: Buskey studies the behavioral ecology of marine zooplankton, including how sensory perception guides feeding and predator avoidance. His work examines photobehavior, bioluminescence, predator-prey dynamics, and impacts of crude oil on marine organisms.

faculty.name: Sharon Z. Herzka
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director, Marine Science Institute
faculty.sub_field: Fish ecology & stable isotope trophic ecology
faculty.summary: Herzka investigates marine ecosystem dynamics in the Gulf of Mexico, focusing on early life history, habitat use, and dispersal of marine fishes. She uses stable isotope analysis to trace nutrient cycling and fish migration patterns.

faculty.name: Andrew Esbaugh
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Esbaugh investigates how fish physiology adapts to environmental conditions, focusing on respiratory gas exchange, acid-base balance, and osmoregulation. His work spans evolutionary mechanisms in water-breathing animals and physiological responses to ocean acidification and toxin exposure.

faculty.name: Deana Erdner
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Erdner's laboratory investigates the molecular biology and ecology of marine eukaryotic algae, with emphasis on harmful and/or toxic species, particularly dinoflagellates. Her work spans fundamental biological questions and applied studies of bloom formation.

faculty.name: Zhanfei Liu
faculty.title: Professor; Associate Chair, Department of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Liu studies the source, distribution and diagenesis of biogenic and anthropogenic compounds in the ocean, including natural organic matter, contaminants, and microplastics.

faculty.name: Xinping Hu
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Marine Science
faculty.summary: Hu's work focuses on carbon cycle science in estuarine and coastal environments, quantifying carbonate parameters and investigating how climate variability and anthropogenic pressures affect water chemistry and ecosystem health.

faculty.name: Brett Baker
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Co-director, Center for Planetary Science and Habitability
faculty.summary: Baker investigates microbial genomics and ecology, focusing on uncultured microorganisms and how microbial communities drive biogeochemical cycles. His work reconstructs genomes from natural populations to understand microbial diversity and evolution.

faculty.name: Mark Lever
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Marine Biogeochemistry and Geobiology
faculty.summary: Lever investigates the mechanistic and quantitative influence of microorganisms, humans, and abiotic factors on the short- and long-term fate of organic carbon in aquatic sediments and Earth's crust. His work emphasizes carbon cycling and sustainable carbon sequestration strategies.

faculty.name: Kelly Dorgan
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Marine Science
faculty.summary: Dorgan studies animal-sediment interactions at the intersection of sediment ecology and invertebrate biology. Her lab investigates how burrowing organisms alter their physical and chemical environments and builds mechanistic understanding of bioturbation.

faculty.name: Simon Brandl
faculty.title: Assistant Professor in Marine Science
faculty.summary: Brandl investigates biodiversity, coastal ecosystem processes, and nutrient cycling in oceanic and freshwater environments. His work focuses on fish ecology and the ecosystem functions fishes provide.

faculty.name: Jordan Casey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Casey uses a molecular approach to measure marine biodiversity and trace trophic interactions, focusing on cryptic trophic pathways, fine-scale niche partitioning, and food web resilience under global change. Her work emphasizes tropical coral reefs and the Texas Coastal Bend.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs ($37,080/yr stipend + tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Lawrence Band
faculty.title: Ernest H. Ern Professor
faculty.summary: Band studies eco-hydrology across natural and urban watersheds, including the role of forests and tree canopy on flooding and drought. His current work emphasizes green infrastructure design and computational tools for watershed restoration.

faculty.name: Meghan Blumstein
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Joint Faculty with Landscape Architecture)
faculty.summary: Blumstein studies how forests will survive stress from pests, disease, disturbance, and a changing climate. Her lab combines genomics, quantitative genetics, plant physiology, and spatially explicit modeling to understand drivers of resilience in tree species.

faculty.name: Max C. N. Castorani
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Castorani investigates ecological processes governing species abundance, distribution, and ecosystem stability across scales, focusing on disturbance and biodiversity's role in stabilizing function. His work centers on nearshore marine systems such as kelp forests and seagrass meadows.

faculty.name: Frederick Cheng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Cheng examines how natural and built infrastructure cycle pollutants and protect water resources through nature-based solutions. His group studies interactions between hydrologic processes and biogeochemical cycles across terrestrial-aquatic interfaces using modeling, geospatial analysis, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Robert E. Davis
faculty.title: Director of Undergraduate Programs, Professor
faculty.summary: Davis investigates interactions between weather/climate and human health, emphasizing heat mortality and respiratory morbidity and mortality. His work also covers synoptic climatology, air-quality health linkages, and climate influences on viticulture.

faculty.name: Stephan De Wekker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: De Wekker studies how landscape heterogeneities affect atmospheric boundary layer processes in mountainous terrain. His work integrates field studies, data analysis, and numerical modeling to examine atmospheric circulation and regional transport.

faculty.name: Scott Doney
faculty.title: Joe D. and Helen J. Kington Professor in Environmental Change
faculty.summary: Doney's research spans oceanography, climate, and biogeochemistry, using numerical models, remote sensing, and data analysis. He focuses on how the global carbon cycle and marine ecosystems respond to climate change, including ocean warming, sea-ice loss, and ocean acidification.

faculty.name: Howard E. Epstein
faculty.title: Sidman P. Poole Professor
faculty.summary: Epstein studies Arctic tundra vegetation dynamics in response to climate change and disturbance. He also examines carbon cycling and carbon-water interactions in Mid-Atlantic temperate forests along successional gradients.

faculty.name: Kevin Grise
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Grise's group focuses on atmospheric dynamics and their role in variability and long-term change in the climate system. His work examines clouds and their climate effects, circulation changes under greenhouse gas increases, and how storm tracks vary across timescales.

faculty.name: Huilin Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Huang studies land-atmosphere interactions amid environmental change, with expertise in wildfire and land cover change. His work involves Earth system model development, including fire models, land surface models, and regional and global circulation models.

faculty.name: Manuel Lerdau
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lerdau is an organismal ecologist studying resource acquisition and allocation in plants, herbivory and tri-trophic interactions, atmospheric chemistry and air pollution, and ecosystem impacts of biological invasions. Current work emphasizes genomics, stress tolerance, trace gas exchange, and ecosystem responses to climate change.

faculty.name: Ajay B. Limaye
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Limaye studies the evolution of landscapes on Earth and other planets, focusing on the role of rivers. His work examines river forms and dynamics, sedimentary records of climate on Mars and Titan, and landslide-ecology interactions using remote sensing, modeling, and laboratory experiments.

faculty.name: Antonios Mamalakis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Joint Faculty with School of Data Science)
faculty.summary: Mamalakis is an environmental data scientist who uses statistical and Bayesian analysis, machine/deep learning, and explainable AI to address environmental challenges. His work improves prediction of hydroclimate and extreme events and advances understanding of climate teleconnections, predictability, and causal discovery.

faculty.name: Karen McGlathery
faculty.title: Sherrell J. Aston Professor
faculty.summary: McGlathery examines ecosystem dynamics in shallow coastal systems and how climate change, nutrient enrichment, and invasive species drive change. She investigates blue carbon sequestration in seagrass ecosystems, seagrass restoration, and salt marsh resilience to rising seas.

faculty.name: Lauren Miller
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Miller investigates past changes in coastal, marine, and glacial environments through sedimentological and geomorphological archives. She examines the processes that shaped these systems over time.

faculty.name: Charity Nyelele
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Nyelele explores connections between biodiversity, climate change, and ecosystem services through an environmental justice lens. She focuses on urban forested systems and how planning and management affect the availability and equitable distribution of services such as stormwater management, air quality, and carbon sequestration.

faculty.name: Sally Pusede
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Pusede is an atmospheric chemist investigating air quality and climate change, with focus on reactive nitrogen, chemical oxidation, neighborhood-level variability, and nitrous oxide emissions. She is also Co-Director of the Repair Lab, an interdisciplinary environmental justice initiative at UVA.

faculty.name: Matthew A. Reidenbach
faculty.title: Department Chair, Professor
faculty.summary: Reidenbach studies environmental fluid dynamics with emphasis on physical-biological interactions in coastal environments, including flow and turbulence effects on nutrient exchange in coral reefs, estuarine sediment transport, and wave dynamics. His research also examines how ecosystems such as coral reefs, seagrasses, and oyster beds respond to waves and storms.

faculty.name: Justin Richardson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Richardson investigates soil biogeochemistry, examining how metals and metalloids are sourced from rocks and minerals and move via water and air to and from soils. His work spans natural and human-disturbed ecosystems in the eastern United States using advanced analytical techniques.

faculty.name: Todd Scanlon
faculty.title: Associate Chair, Professor
faculty.summary: Scanlon investigates catchment hydrology and land-atmosphere interactions, examining water, energy, and gas exchange between terrestrial surfaces and the atmosphere. His work integrates field observations, remote sensing, and modeling across multiple scales.

faculty.name: Kathleen Schiro
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Schiro studies atmospheric convection, tropical dynamics, and regional hydroclimatology. Her work aims to improve understanding of clouds, convection, and precipitation in a changing climate by combining field observations, satellite data, and modeling.

faculty.name: Xi Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Yang investigates how vegetation and climate interact as the planet warms, spanning spatial ecology, plant physiology, remote sensing, and climate change. She develops remote-sensing methods for measuring plant traits and uses computational techniques like data assimilation to improve predictive models.

funding.note: Most PhD students fully funded (tuition, fees, health insurance + stipend)

faculty.name: Devin K. Harris
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research on large-scale infrastructure systems with focus on structural health monitoring, condition assessment, and system-level performance evaluation.

faculty.name: Somayeh Asadi
faculty.title: Professor
