faculty.summary: Research on sustainable building materials and energy efficiency in buildings. Leads NSF $1M AI education grant for underserved high school students.

faculty.name: Larry Band
faculty.title: Ernest H. Ern Professor of Environmental Science
faculty.summary: Research on eco-hydrology spanning natural through urban watersheds. Focuses on urban ecosystem restoration and stormwater management for water quality improvement.

faculty.name: Andrés F. Clarens
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Director, UVA Environmental Institute
faculty.summary: Associate Director of UVA Environmental Institute. Research on anthropogenic carbon flows, CO2 manipulation, reuse, and sequestration in engineered systems for infrastructure decarbonization.

faculty.name: Jonathan L. Goodall
faculty.title: Professor and Director, UVA Engineering Link Lab
faculty.summary: Director of UVA Engineering Link Lab. Research on hydroinformatics, data and computational sciences for water systems forecasting and management.

faculty.name: Leidy Klotz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research on design and problem-solving with behavioral science in engineering. Published in Nature and Science on sustainable design and human behavior.

faculty.name: Venkataraman Lakshmi
faculty.title: John L. Newcomb Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: President of AGU Hydrology Section. Elected AAAS Fellow (2024). Research on catchment hydrology, satellite data validation/assimilation, and land-atmosphere interactions for flood/drought monitoring.

faculty.name: James H. Lambert
faculty.title: Janet Scott Hamilton and John Downman Hamilton Professor; Director, Center for Risk Management
faculty.summary: Director of Center for Risk Management. Research on systems engineering, risk analysis, disruption and resilience, and embedded systems security for critical infrastructure.

faculty.name: Osman E. Ozbulut
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Director of Graduate Studies. Research on structural engineering with focus on smart materials, adaptive structures, and structural control for resilient infrastructure.

faculty.name: B. Brian Park
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Traffic Operations Laboratory
faculty.summary: Research on surface transportation efficiency with focus on connected and automated vehicle controls and traffic operations optimization. Joint appointment with Systems and Information Engineering.

faculty.name: Lisa Colosi Peterson
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair for Research and Strategic Initiatives
faculty.summary: Associate Chair for Research. Research on life cycle assessment (LCA), environmental sustainability of energy-water systems, and bioenergy production systems.

faculty.name: James A. Smith
faculty.title: Henry L. Kinnier Professor of Civil Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on environmental engineering and hydrology with focus on flood forecasting, extreme rainfall analysis, and urban flood hydrology.

faculty.name: Brian L. Smith
faculty.title: Professor and Senior Associate Dean for Research and Faculty Affairs
faculty.summary: Senior Associate Dean for Research. Research on advanced technology in surface transportation, connected infrastructure, and intelligent transportation systems.

faculty.name: T. Donna Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on transportation engineering with focus on sustainable mobility, shared mobility systems, and transportation economics for emerging mobility technologies.

faculty.name: Teresa B. Culver
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Associate Chair for Academic Programs
faculty.summary: Associate Chair for Academic Programs. Research on environmental and water resource engineering with focus on sustainable water systems and water quality modeling.

faculty.name: Arsalan Heydarian
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on user-centered design for construction and operation of intelligent infrastructure. Focus on sustainability, resilience, and human-building interaction.

faculty.name: Zhen (Leo) Liu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on soil mechanics and foundation engineering with AI applications, numerical simulations, and multiphysics/multiscale modeling for geotechnical problems.

faculty.name: Julianne Quinn
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research on optimization and simulation for water resources systems with focus on flood and drought protection using advanced sensing and decision methods.

faculty.name: Negin Alemazkoor
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on sensing and computing methodologies for smart interconnected infrastructure with focus on reliability and resilience under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Diana Franco Duran
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; Director of Civil Engineering Undergraduate Program
faculty.summary: Director of Civil Engineering Undergraduate Program and Construction Engineering & Management Track. Research on construction engineering and management with focus on project delivery systems.

faculty.name: Majid Shafiee-Jood
faculty.title: Research Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research on water resources systems with focus on decision-making under uncertainty and hydro-economic modeling for water-food-energy nexus challenges.

requirements.admission_model: Autumn quarter only; preference by deadline

faculty.name: Jeffrey Berman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies seismic performance of steel and heavy timber structures, innovative seismic load resisting systems, and performance-based seismic design, with a focus on Pacific Northwest seismic hazards. Works in the UW Large-Scale Structural Engineering Testing Laboratory.

faculty.name: Paolo Calvi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines large-scale experimental testing with numerical modeling to evaluate structures under earthquakes and other natural hazards. Focuses on passive/semi-active/active response-control technologies, base-isolated buildings, bridge bearings, and effects of corrosion and material degradation on structural resilience.

faculty.name: Marc Eberhard
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches rapid construction and performance of reinforced and prestressed concrete buildings and bridges subjected to gravity loads, earthquakes, and tsunamis.

faculty.name: Dawn Lehman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Conducts large-scale testing of bridge and building components and performance-based earthquake engineering research, including concrete-filled tube components and connections, RC wall design and retrofit, tsunami evacuation structures, and sustainable concrete materials.

faculty.name: Laura Lowes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops tools for modeling the nonlinear response of structural systems subjected to severe loading, including finite element modeling of reinforced concrete components, beam-column joint response under earthquake loading, and fiber optic sensing for structural health monitoring.

faculty.name: Michael Motley
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies fluid-structure interaction of large-scale structural systems, including tsunami wave load prediction, multi-hazard response of coastal infrastructure, and structural response of renewable energy devices and marine composites using computational fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Travis Thonstad
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches earthquake engineering, structural robustness, and precast and prestressed concrete structures. Holds a Ph.D. in Civil and Environmental Engineering from the University of Washington.

faculty.name: Richard Wiebe
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nonlinear structural dynamics of civil, mechanical, and aerospace structures using numerical and experimental methods, including structural response in extreme environments, stability of slender structures, and marine turbine applications.

faculty.name: Michael Brett
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches eutrophication and regulation of algal biomass in lakes, essential fatty acid production and bioaccumulation in aquatic food webs, zooplankton ecology, and nutrient bioavailability and biogeochemical recalcitrance.

faculty.name: David Butman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates global and regional carbon cycling and the biogeochemical connections between land and water systems using advanced field techniques, geospatial modeling, and satellite remote sensing, with a focus on freshwater resource management and climate impacts.

faculty.name: Michael Dodd
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies oxidation and disinfection processes in drinking water and wastewater treatment, using chemical oxidants and photochemically generated reactive species to transform contaminants and inactivate microorganisms, and works on characterization and control of antibiotic resistance dissemination in the environment.

faculty.name: Erica Fuhrmeister
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Civil & Environmental Engineering; Environmental and Occupational Health Sciences)
faculty.summary: Studies waterborne pathogen transmission and antimicrobial resistance proliferation to develop protective barriers for human health at local and global scales, with focus areas in water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH).

faculty.name: Edward Kolodziej
faculty.title: Allan & Inger Osberg Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates water quality and contaminant fate in natural and engineered systems, including characterization and control of roadway runoff and tire rubber-derived chemical pollution, and optimization of engineered systems for trace contaminant removal.

faculty.name: Gregory Korshin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on microscopic-level examination of complex environmental reactions and development of new methods to treat drinking water and wastewater, including natural organic matter and disinfection by-product characterization, heavy metal speciation, and pharmaceutical degradation.

faculty.name: Julian Marshall
faculty.title: Boeing International Professor
faculty.summary: Works on air quality engineering and public health, including mechanistic and empirical modeling of air pollution, measuring and modeling air pollution exposures in low-income countries, and quantifying exposure disparities by race and income.

faculty.name: Rebecca Neumann
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Hydro-biogeochemistry Research Group, investigating how physical, chemical, and biological factors interact in soils, aquifers, and surface water to control chemical fate and transport, with applications to food and water quality and climate change.

faculty.name: Jessica Ray
faculty.title: Robert O. and Irene V. Sylvester Family Endowed Professorship in Water Resources
faculty.summary: Bridges materials science, environmental chemistry, and surface chemistry to develop composite materials for selective contaminant removal in water, including selective adsorption and destruction of PFAS, ferrate-based wastewater treatment media, and engineered soil amendments for urban stormwater.

faculty.name: Mari Winkler
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Examines microbiology, resource recovery, and innovative wastewater and sludge treatment processes, including enhanced nitrogen removal, phosphorus removal and recovery, and microbial ecology of treatment systems.

faculty.name: Xuegang Jeff Ban
faculty.title: William M. and Marilyn M. Conner Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Uses mathematical modeling and computer simulation to address transportation congestion, energy, and emission problems, leveraging vehicle sensor data and studying connected/automated vehicles and shared mobility. Leads the Intelligent Urban Transportation Systems (iUTS) Lab.

faculty.name: Cynthia Chen
faculty.title: Professor (Civil & Environmental Engineering; Industrial & Systems Engineering)
faculty.summary: Directs the THINK Lab, studying the sustainability and resilience of cities through human interaction with the physical environment, including human mobility pattern analysis, bias and uncertainty quantification in big data, and sustainable transportation policy design.

faculty.name: Anne Goodchild
faculty.title: Professor; Associate Chair for External Engagement
faculty.summary: Directs the Urban Freight Lab, researching freight transportation and logistics at the intersection of supply chain management and transportation, including impacts of e-commerce delivery on emissions, air quality, and equity in urban areas.

faculty.name: Don MacKenzie
faculty.title: Allan & Inger Osberg Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Sustainable Transportation Lab, studying how emerging transportation technologies such as vehicle electrification, automation, and new mobility services can make the transportation system more economically viable and environmentally benign while providing equitable access.

faculty.name: Yinhai Wang
faculty.title: Thomas and Marilyn Nielsen Endowed Professor in Engineering
faculty.summary: Directs PacTrans and the Smart Transportation Applications and Research (STAR) Lab, researching traffic detection systems, transportation big data analytics, e-science of transportation, traffic operations and simulation, and connected/automated vehicle impacts.

faculty.name: David Schmidt
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies crustal deformation from earthquakes, slow slip, subduction dynamics, volcanic inflation, and landslides using satellite radar interferometry (InSAR) and geodesy, including Cascadia subduction zone activity.

faculty.name: Harold Tobin
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Pacific Northwest Seismic Network; Paros Endowed Chair in Seismology and Geohazards
faculty.summary: Investigates fault zone properties and processes with a focus on fault mechanics, pore fluid pressure, in situ stress, and seismic structure in subduction zones including Cascadia, Nankai, and Alaska. Directs the Pacific Northwest Seismic Network.

faculty.name: Marine Denolle
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geohazards including earthquakes and landslides and how climate and hydrology influence them, integrating seismology with big data analytics, cloud computing, and AI. Her ESS profile states she is actively recruiting motivated graduate students and postdocs.

faculty.name: Brad Lipovsky
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies environmental geophysics and computation to glaciology, seismology, tectonics, and volcanology, studying fracture at plate boundaries and in ice sheets. Develops fiber optic distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) methods for high-resolution deformation measurement.

faculty.name: Knut Christianson
faculty.title: Associate Professor and Graduate Program Coordinator
faculty.summary: Glaciologist studying ice sheets and glacier dynamics; serves as the department's Graduate Program Coordinator.

faculty.name: Eric Steig
faculty.title: Professor; Ben Rabinowitz Professor in the Department of Earth and Space Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies climate, glaciers, and ice sheets using isotope geochemistry of ice cores and climate modeling; directs IsoLab and works on ice-sheet modeling and paleoclimate data assimilation. AGU and AAAS Fellow, 2024 Guggenheim Fellow.

faculty.name: Gerard Roe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research spans atmospheric dynamics, surface processes, and modern climate and paleoclimate, including glacier response to climate; also adjunct in Atmospheric and Climate Science.

faculty.name: John Stone
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on Quaternary geology, geomorphology, geochronology, and Antarctic glaciation using cosmogenic isotope methods; runs the UW Cosmogenic Nuclide Lab.

faculty.name: David Catling
faculty.title: Professor; Interim Director, UW Astrobiology Program
faculty.summary: Studies planetary atmospheres, biogeochemistry, planetary surface processes, and astrobiology, including atmospheric evolution of Earth and other planets. Adjunct Professor in UW Atmospheric Sciences.

faculty.name: Baptiste Journaux
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Planetary scientist studying the structure and evolution of icy moons, high-pressure mineral physics, and deep extraterrestrial ocean conditions; contributes to the NASA Dragonfly mission to Titan.

faculty.name: Joshua Krissansen-Totton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Models the biogeochemical evolution of terrestrial planets, including atmosphere-surface-interior interactions, and develops testable hypotheses for detecting life via exoplanet atmospheric characterization.

faculty.name: Roger Buick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the origin and earliest evolution of life on Earth as an analogue for life elsewhere, including early bacterial metabolism, Archaean atmospheric composition, and molecular fossils in Precambrian rocks.

faculty.name: Marjorie Cantine
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geochemical and physical records of time and environment preserved in sedimentary rocks, with interests in carbonates, laser ablation geochronology, Precambrian life, and biases in the sedimentary record.

faculty.name: Akshay Mehra
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses quantitative descriptions of form and space, including computational imaging and 3D morphology of the sedimentary rock record, to understand the evolution of life and environment on Earth.

faculty.name: Fang-Zhen Teng
faculty.title: Professor and Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Isotope geochemist studying the composition, formation, and evolution of Earth's crust and mantle and the early solar system; his lab specializes in non-traditional stable isotope analysis of magmatic, subduction, and weathering processes.

faculty.name: Katharine Huntington
faculty.title: Professor; Endowed Professor for the College of the Environment in Earth Systems
faculty.summary: Studies interactions of tectonics, landscapes, and climate using clumped isotope thermometry and detrital geo/thermochronology, with field focus on the Himalaya-Tibet region, western North America, and the Andes.

faculty.name: Drew Gorman-Lewis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies geomicrobiology, astrobiology, low-temperature aqueous geochemistry, and thermodynamics of natural systems, including microbe-mineral interactions and extremophile energy metabolism. His lab site maintains a Research Opportunities page with current recruitment status.

faculty.name: Cailey Condit
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies feedbacks between chemical and mechanical processes in the deep crust and lithosphere, focusing on subduction zone processes and how metamorphism and deformation influence lithospheric evolution.

faculty.name: Michelle Muth
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Combines geochemistry and petrology to study magma generation and evolution, with emphasis on sulfur and other volatile elements in subduction zone volcanism.

faculty.name: Juliet Crider
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director, MESSAGe Program
faculty.summary: Investigates structural geology, neotectonics, crustal deformation, fault zone processes, and the evolution of topography through field studies and computer modeling, with focus on the North Cascades and Columbia Plateau. Directs the applied geosciences masters program.

faculty.name: Alison Duvall
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how mountains are built and how landscapes respond to tectonics, erosion, and climate, including strike-slip faulting and methods for reading fault activity from surface processes.

faculty.name: David Montgomery
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies fluvial, hillslope, tectonic, and planetary geomorphology and the influence of geomorphological processes on ecological systems and human societies, with field work in the Cascades, Andes, Himalaya, and eastern Tibet.

faculty.name: Becky Alexander
faculty.title: Professor and Interim Chair, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Investigates how aerosol formation and the atmosphere's oxidative capacity respond to climate change and human activities, focusing on sulfate and nitrate aerosol formation pathways and changes in atmospheric oxidation under anthropogenic and climate forcing.

faculty.name: Alex Anderson-Frey
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Studies severe weather phenomena including tornadoes and thunderstorms, emphasizing their environmental characteristics and improved warning systems, integrating synoptic meteorology with statistical and machine learning approaches to assess forecast skill.

faculty.name: Kyle Armour
faculty.title: Professor of Atmospheric and Climate Science; Professor of Oceanography
faculty.summary: Investigates climate dynamics, polar oceanography, sea ice, and climate sensitivity, examining how oceanic processes influence polar climate change and the factors governing Earth's climate response to greenhouse gas forcing.

faculty.name: David Battisti
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science; Tamaki Endowed Chair
faculty.summary: Studies large-scale atmosphere-ocean dynamics and tropical circulation patterns, with work spanning climate dynamics, paleoclimate, land-atmosphere interactions, and the physics of natural Arctic and summertime climate variability.

faculty.name: Cecilia Bitz
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Focuses on the role of sea ice in the climate system and high-latitude climate change, including Arctic sea ice predictability through co-leading the Sea Ice Prediction Network and global coupled climate modeling at high resolution.

faculty.name: Shuyi Chen
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Researches air-sea interaction in high-impact weather, with emphasis on tropical cyclones, the Madden-Julian Oscillation, and ENSO, using coupled atmosphere-wave-ocean modeling and Earth system prediction to understand coastal environments and climate extremes.

faculty.name: Dale Durran
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science; Adjunct Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.sub_field: Mesoscale Dynamics and Deep Learning Weather Prediction
faculty.summary: Works on deep learning Earth system modeling, atmospheric predictability, mountain and mesoscale meteorology, and numerical methods, including neural-network weather prediction and the influence of mountain terrain on atmospheric phenomena.

faculty.name: Dargan Frierson
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.sub_field: Atmospheric General Circulation and Climate Change
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric general circulation patterns, water vapor dynamics, and climate change mechanisms, from theoretical modeling of atmospheric motions to how the climate system responds to forcings and perturbations.

faculty.name: Qiang Fu
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric radiation, clouds, and aerosols using remote sensing, with research spanning stratospheric circulation, stratosphere-troposphere exchange, the Brewer-Dobson circulation, tropical tropopause layer dynamics, and aridity responses to warming.

faculty.name: Gregory Hakim
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Works on synoptic and mesoscale meteorology, atmospheric dynamics, stratified turbulence, and paleoclimate, with recent research on hurricane predictability, data assimilation techniques, and ensemble-based methods for analyzing atmospheric phenomena.

faculty.name: Lyatt Jaeglé
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Studies tropospheric and stratospheric photochemistry through chemical modeling of atmospheric observations, examining the influence of human activities on atmospheric composition, including aerosols, sea salt, and pollutant transport.

faculty.name: Daniel Jaffe
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science (UW-Seattle); Professor, School of STEM (UW-Bothell)
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric chemistry, air pollution, long-range transport of pollutants, and photochemistry, with emphasis on sources and transformations of pollutants in the free troposphere and their environmental impacts.

faculty.name: Clifford Mass
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Specializes in synoptic and mesoscale meteorology, regional climate change, wildfire meteorology, and high-resolution numerical weather prediction, particularly for the western United States.

faculty.name: Abigail Swann
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science; Professor of Biology
faculty.summary: Leads research on ecoclimate and biosphere-climate feedbacks using climate and ecosystem modeling, examining how ecological systems and climate interact and influence one another.

faculty.name: Joel Thornton
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science; Interim Dean, College of the Environment
faculty.summary: Investigates atmospheric chemistry and aerosol processes, focusing on boundary layer surface interactions and cloud aerosols, combining in situ and laboratory studies of homogeneous and heterogeneous atmospheric chemical processes.

faculty.name: Alex Turner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.sub_field: Carbon Cycle and Atmospheric Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Works on atmospheric chemistry, carbon cycle dynamics, and remote sensing, employing machine learning to interpret satellite observations of atmospheric composition, with focus on methane emissions and photosynthesis patterns.

faculty.name: Robert Wood
faculty.title: Professor, Atmospheric and Climate Science
faculty.summary: Investigates boundary layer cloud structure, cloud microphysics, and aerosol-cloud-climate interactions, focusing on marine low clouds and how aerosols, clouds, and radiation interact in subtropical stratocumulus regions.

faculty.name: Virginia Armbrust
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Oceanography
faculty.summary: Her lab studies phytoplankton, focusing on molecular ecology, genetic diversity of microbial populations, and bacterial-phytoplankton interactions. Phytoplankton are responsible for roughly 40% of global photosynthesis.

faculty.name: Kyle Armour
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A physical oceanographer studying ocean circulation, climate dynamics, and polar oceanography with an emphasis on climate change.

faculty.name: Randie Bundy
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical Oceanography
faculty.summary: Studies the biogeochemical cycling of trace metals in the marine environment, including hydrothermal iron transport, iron demands of heterotrophic bacteria, and trace metal ligands in the Southern Ocean.

faculty.name: Karen Chan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on biological oceanography, zooplankton ecology, larval ecology, and bio-fluid-mechanics.

faculty.name: Alex Gagnon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses a geochemical approach to study how ocean acidification and other changes to seawater chemistry impact calcifying organisms and biogeochemical cycles.

faculty.name: Alison Gray
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies large-scale ocean circulation and dynamics and the ocean's role in climate.

faculty.name: Anitra Ingalls
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical Oceanography
faculty.summary: Her lab investigates the chemical signatures of microorganisms to understand feedbacks between the global cycles of carbon and nitrogen and climate, using organic chemistry tools.

faculty.name: Richard Keil
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical Oceanography
faculty.summary: His laboratory studies the fate of organic matter in aquatic environments, emphasizing proteins, proteomics, metabolomics, and in situ sensing, focusing on preservation and degradation of organic compounds.

faculty.name: Deborah Kelley
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Geology and Geophysics
faculty.summary: Studies geological and microbiological processes at submarine volcanoes and hot springs, and directs the NSF-funded Regional Cabled Array underwater observatory, working at sites such as Axial Volcano and the Lost City Hydrothermal Field.

faculty.name: Parker MacCready
faculty.title: Professor of Physical Oceanography
faculty.sub_field: Physical oceanography / coastal and estuarine dynamics
faculty.summary: A physical oceanographer working in coastal and estuarine regions, with research on flow over rough topography, estuarine circulation theory, river plumes, and bio-physical interactions.

faculty.name: Georgy Manucharyan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physical Oceanography
faculty.summary: Works on geophysical fluid dynamics, physical oceanography, and climate dynamics, including mesoscale ocean turbulence, sea ice-ocean interactions, remote sensing, and deep learning applications.

faculty.name: Robert Morris
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biological Oceanography
faculty.summary: Studies marine bacteria and their viruses using physiology, genomics, transcriptomics, and proteomics to understand their contributions to carbon and nitrogen cycling in marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Andrea Ogston
faculty.title: Professor of Marine Geology and Geophysics
faculty.summary: Studies the interaction between geological and physical oceanography of the coastal ocean and sediment transport from the surf zone to the continental slope, including instrument development.

faculty.name: Frankie Pavia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: An assistant professor in chemical oceanography at the UW School of Oceanography.

faculty.name: Gabrielle Rocap
faculty.title: Professor of Biological Oceanography
faculty.summary: Studies the ecology, evolution, genetic diversity, and comparative genomics of marine cyanobacteria, using physiological experiments, molecular analyses, and computational genomics.

faculty.name: Julian Sachs
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical Oceanography
faculty.sub_field: Chemical oceanography / paleoclimatology and organic geochemistry
faculty.summary: Works on paleoclimatology and organic geochemistry, developing climate proxies from hydrogen and carbon isotope ratios in molecular fossils and studying natural pre-industrial climate variations.

faculty.name: Stephen Riser
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: A physical oceanographer studying mesoscale mixing and eddies, numerical modeling, and the ocean's role in climate through direct observations of the general circulation and ocean/atmosphere/ice interactions.

faculty.name: Jodi Young
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Studies phytoplankton physiology at high latitudes, focusing on how polar-ocean phytoplankton are adapted to extreme conditions and how their productivity will respond to future environmental change.

faculty.name: Jonathan Bakker
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bakker studies restoration and management of prairies, savannas, and forests, long-term vegetation dynamics, and statistical methods for community ecology. His work spans climate adaptation, fire science, and invasive species ecology.

faculty.name: Laura Prugh
faculty.title: Professor, John C. Garcia Term Professorship
faculty.summary: Prugh researches quantitative multi-species conservation and management, wildlife community ecology, and predator-prey interactions, including conservation of endangered and fragmented populations. Her work addresses human-wildlife interactions and noninvasive genetics.

faculty.name: Brian J. Harvey
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Jack and George Corkery Professorship in Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Harvey studies how forest fires and forest resilience to fire are changing as the climate warms, examining fire ecology patterns, forest structure, and interactions among fires, insect outbreaks, and climate. He works in Pacific Northwest, Rocky Mountain, and coastal California forests.

faculty.name: Gregory Ettl
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Ettl researches sustainable forestry, forest ecology, and silviculture, with focus areas including climate change applications in national forests and the effects of alternative silviculture systems on ecosystem and timber outputs.

faculty.name: David Butman
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Jack and George Corkery Professorship in Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Butman investigates the influence of humans and climate on carbon cycling at the intersection of terrestrial and aquatic systems. His work measures ecosystem capacity to adapt to carbon emissions, landscape alterations, and climate change.

faculty.name: Beth Gardner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gardner develops hierarchical models, spatial capture-recapture and occupancy models, and sampling techniques for wildlife populations using methods such as camera trapping and hair snares. Her research spans conservation science and Bayesian inference.

faculty.name: Sarah J. Converse
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Leader, Washington Cooperative Fish and Wildlife Research Unit
faculty.summary: Converse focuses on quantitative population ecology, demographic estimation and modeling, spatial and movement ecology, conservation biology, and decision analysis. She leads the Washington Cooperative Fish and Wildlife Research Unit.

faculty.name: Aaron J. Wirsing
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Wirsing studies predator ecology, including snow leopard ecology in Kyrgyzstan, wolf-cougar interactions in Washington, and cougar hunting success in human-dominated landscapes. His work examines anti-predator behavior effects.

faculty.name: Alex McInturff
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; Assistant Unit Leader, Washington Cooperative Fish and Wildlife Research Unit
faculty.summary: McInturff studies wildlife science, conservation, and nature-human interactions using landscape ecology and spatial modeling approaches. He directs the People and Wildlife Lab and serves as Assistant Unit Leader of the Washington Cooperative Fish and Wildlife Research Unit.

faculty.name: Joshua Lawler
faculty.title: Professor; Orin and Althea Soest Chair in Urban Horticulture
faculty.summary: Lawler works in landscape ecology and conservation biology, addressing climate change adaptation, nature-human interactions, and wildlife science through spatial analysis and modeling.

faculty.name: Patrick C. Tobin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Invasion ecology and insect population dynamics
faculty.summary: Tobin researches the biology and ecology of biological invasions, the spatial and temporal dynamics of insect populations, and the role of climate change on insect population dynamics.

faculty.name: Soo-Hyung Kim
faculty.title: Professor; Byron and Alice Lockwood Endowed Professorship in Forest Resources
faculty.summary: Kim's Plant Ecophysiology and Modeling Lab studies how plants work and interact with their environments, applying plant science to mitigate and adapt to global change. His research spans ecosystem science and restoration ecology.

faculty.name: Claire Willing
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Willing is an ecologist specializing in microbial ecology and plant physiological ecology, focusing on plant-associated microbial diversity and its functional consequences for plant hosts and communities in a changing climate, with emphasis on mycorrhizal fungi.

faculty.name: Sharon L. Doty
faculty.title: Professor; David R.M. Scott Endowed Professor in Forest Resources
faculty.summary: Doty researches plant microbiology, with focus areas including environmental microbiology, ecosystem science, plant science, restoration ecology, and sustainable forest management.

faculty.name: L. Monika Moskal
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Precision Forestry Cooperative; McLachlan Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Moskal studies multiscale dynamics of landscape change through remote sensing and geospatial tools, directing the Remote Sensing and Geospatial Analysis Laboratory and the Precision Forestry Cooperative. Her work spans ecosystem science, fire science, and sustainable forest management.

faculty.name: Gregory Bratman
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Doug Walker Endowed Professor
faculty.summary: Bratman studies the pathways through which nature experience benefits mental and physical health and the psychological mechanisms underlying these connections. He directs the Environment and Well-Being Lab.

faculty.name: Dan Brown
faculty.title: Director and Professor; Corkery Family Environmental and Forest Sciences Director's Endowed Chair
faculty.summary: Brown works in GIS, remote sensing, and spatial modeling with focus on land-use and land-cover dynamics and linking landscape patterns with ecological and social processes. He is Director of SEFS.

faculty.name: Sergey S. Rabotyagov
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rabotyagov researches environmental economics focusing on water quality, land use change, non-market valuation, and causal inference in complex systems.

faculty.name: Sameer Shah
faculty.title: Assistant Professor; John C. Garcia Professor of Climate Adaptation
faculty.summary: Shah studies how systemic marginalization and climate-related change and disasters interact to create and amplify uneven water, food, and energy insecurities for communities on the frontlines of climate change.

faculty.name: Phillip Levin
faculty.title: Professor of Practice
faculty.summary: Levin works in interdisciplinary conservation science, ecosystem-based natural resource management, and marine and coastal conservation biology. His research spans climate adaptation, ecosystem science, and nature-human interactions.

faculty.name: Ernesto Alvarado
faculty.title: Research Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Wildland fire science and fire ecology
faculty.summary: Alvarado specializes in wildland fire science, fire ecology and management, combustion and fire behavior, carbon emissions, and fire-climate change interactions, including quantitative modeling and traditional ecological knowledge.

faculty.name: Van R. Kane
faculty.title: Associate Research Professor
faculty.summary: Kane studies forest and fire landscape ecology using high-fidelity remote sensing, particularly airborne lidar, examining the causes and effects of these forces on forest resilience.

faculty.name: André Punt
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Punt develops and tests quantitative methods for assessing fish, invertebrate, and marine mammal populations. His work applies population dynamics modeling and Management Strategy Evaluation to inform management decisions.

faculty.name: Trevor Branch
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Branch's lab synthesizes data and develops mathematical models to address ecological questions, with a focus on the global status and future of marine fisheries and research on large whales, especially blue whales.

faculty.name: Timothy Essington
faculty.title: Professor and Director, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Essington applies ecological principles to fisheries and conservation, emphasizing climate change adaptation, oxygen depletion effects on marine ecosystems, and food web interactions from the open ocean to Puget Sound.

faculty.name: Ray Hilborn
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Hilborn focuses on how best to manage fisheries to provide sustainable benefits to human society, through data analysis, fishery management databases, and field research on Pacific salmon biology and conservation.

faculty.name: Daniel Schindler
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Schindler studies how aquatic ecosystems respond to environmental changes such as climate shifts and altered land use, with particular focus on Pacific salmon watersheds in Alaska and freshwater systems in Washington.

faculty.name: Julian D. Olden
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Olden's research spans freshwater ecosystem conservation with principal interests in ecohydrology, community ecology, invasion biology, macroecology, and climate change impacts on aquatic systems.

faculty.name: Mark Scheuerell
faculty.title: Associate Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Scheuerell is an applied ecologist who integrates diverse data and analytical methods for aquatic resource conservation, focusing on statistical approaches for temporal and spatial data related to Pacific salmon and west-coast ecological systems.

faculty.name: Sarah J. Converse
faculty.title: Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences & School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.sub_field: Quantitative population ecology and decision science
faculty.summary: Converse's group develops methods for conserving populations through quantitative population ecology and decision science, working with diverse taxa including birds, mammals, reptiles, amphibians, and fish across multiple continents.

faculty.name: Chelsea Wood
faculty.title: Associate Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Wood studies how parasite and pathogen communities respond to human-driven environmental change, using historical ecology, natural experiments, and manipulative studies across marine and freshwater ecosystems.

faculty.name: Andrew Berdahl
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences
faculty.summary: Berdahl investigates ecological systems with emphasis on collective behavior and movement ecology, using theoretical modeling, simulations, laboratory experiments, and field research on topics like collective navigation and group decision-making in migration.

faculty.name: Beth Gardner
faculty.title: Professor, School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Gardner develops hierarchical and spatial capture-recapture models to estimate wildlife populations and demographic dynamics, applying Bayesian inference and camera trapping to conservation problems including predator-prey interactions and species recolonization.

faculty.name: Laura Prugh
faculty.title: Professor (John C. Garcia Term Professorship), School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Prugh works on quantitative multi-species conservation and management, wildlife community ecology, conservation of endangered and fragmented populations, human-wildlife interactions, and predator-prey dynamics.

faculty.name: Joshua Lawler
faculty.title: Professor (Orin and Althea Soest Chair in Urban Horticulture), School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Lawler works in landscape ecology and conservation biology with emphasis on climate change impacts on species and ecosystems, studying how to plan protected areas and maintain connectivity for wildlife facing climate-driven range shifts.

faculty.name: Aaron Wirsing
faculty.title: Professor, School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Wirsing investigates predator ecology and anti-predator behavior, with current projects examining interactions between wolves and cougars in Washington and snow leopard ecology in Kyrgyzstan.

faculty.name: Sándor Tóth
faculty.title: Associate Professor, School of Environmental and Forest Sciences
faculty.summary: Tóth develops quantitative decision-support tools for forest and natural resource management, focusing on spatially explicit harvest scheduling models, multiple-criteria forest planning, operations research, and non-timber forest economics.

faculty.name: Amy Willis
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Biostatistics
faculty.summary: Willis develops statistical methods for analyzing microbiome and biodiversity data, with expertise in high-dimensional data analysis and phylogenetic methods, addressing nonstandard statistical problems in evolutionary and genomic contexts.

faculty.name: Sunny Jardine
faculty.title: Associate Professor, School of Marine and Environmental Affairs
faculty.summary: Jardine is an environmental economist studying the economics of conservation and management in marine and coastal systems, examining recreational fisheries, salmon conservation, harmful algal blooms, and seafood markets using economic theory and quantitative methods.

faculty.name: Briana Abrahms
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Biology
faculty.summary: Abrahms integrates global change biology with behavioral and spatial ecology to study the effects of environmental variability and change on vertebrate populations across terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems.

faculty.name: Soyoung (Sue) Ahn
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies traffic flow theory and operations, Intelligent Transportation Systems applications, and the operational impacts of traffic on the environment and safety. Her work combines quantitative analysis of traffic dynamics with ITS-based control strategies.

faculty.name: Paul Block
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Addresses water resources management challenges by integrating climate science, hydrology, management, the environment, economics, and policy. His group works on sustainability and adaptation of water systems at local and international scales.

faculty.name: Hannah Blum
faculty.title: Alain H. Peyrot Associate Professor in Structural Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates infrastructure resilience, next-generation structural design, and advanced visualization technologies, with emphasis on metal structural systems including steel, cold-formed steel, and stainless-steel members, as well as extended reality applications in fabrication.

faculty.name: Dante Fratta
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Geological Engineering Program Director
faculty.summary: Investigates the behavior of geomaterials using elastic and electromagnetic waves, with emphasis on geophysical assessment of the onshore and offshore near surface and distributed fiber optic sensing for subsurface characterization.

faculty.name: Matthew Ginder-Vogel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies biogeochemical processes in environmental systems, with emphasis on microbially mediated oxidation of trace elements, manganese oxide chemistry, and contaminant transport in aquifer sediments, including pyrite oxidation, uranium reduction, and radium mobility in groundwater.

faculty.name: Jesse Hampton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates experimental geomechanics, geophysics, and petrophysics, with emphasis on induced seismicity, acoustic emission, fracture mechanics, and applications of artificial intelligence in geological engineering.

faculty.name: Awad Hanna
faculty.title: BOLDT Company Professor and Chair of the Construction Engineering and Management Program
faculty.summary: Specializes in construction productivity at the labor, project, and organizational levels, with expertise spanning integrated project delivery, lean construction, delay management, construction claims, and schedule acceleration. Directs the Construction and Materials Support Center.

faculty.name: Andrea Hicks
faculty.title: Keith and Jane Nosbusch Associate Professor in Engineering Education
faculty.summary: Examines the environmental impacts and sustainability implications of emerging technologies using industrial ecology tools such as life cycle assessment and environmental systems analysis, integrating economic and behavioral dimensions of technology adoption.

faculty.name: Xiaopeng Li
faculty.title: Harvey D. Spangler Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates connected, electric, and automated vehicles and transportation and infrastructure systems analysis, with emphasis on modeling and field experiments spanning autonomous vehicle behavior, energy consumption, and interdependent infrastructure systems. Leads the CATS Lab.

faculty.name: William Likos
faculty.title: Gary Wendt Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in soil mechanics, particularly unsaturated soil behavior, expansive clays, and pore-scale analysis. His work integrates laboratory characterization of soil mechanical, hydraulic, and thermal behavior with field applications and the beneficial reuse of recycled materials.

faculty.name: Steven Loheide
faculty.title: Professor; Environmental Engineering & Science Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Studies the hydroecology and ecohydrology of groundwater-dependent ecosystems, including river restoration, climate change impacts on hydrology, vegetation-water interactions, and green infrastructure.

faculty.name: Trina McMahon
faculty.title: Vilas Distinguished Achievement Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates microbial ecology in natural and engineered systems, with emphasis on phosphorus, nitrogen, and carbon cycling in lakes and wastewater treatment processes, combining molecular tools, metagenomic analysis, and environmental sensor networks.

faculty.name: Daniel Noguera
faculty.title: Wisconsin Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Works on environmental biotechnology, focusing on bioenergy production from renewable resources, biological nutrient removal in wastewater treatment with reduced energy requirements, and anaerobic digestion to improve biogas quality and extract value from organic residues.

faculty.name: David Noyce
faculty.title: Executive Associate Dean; Arthur F. Hawnn Professor of Transportation Engineering
faculty.summary: Investigates operational and behavioral aspects of transportation safety and operations, with emphasis on advanced traffic control, left-turn signalization, connected and automated vehicles, crash analysis, work zone safety, and pedestrian and bicycle operations. Leads the TOPS Laboratory.

faculty.name: Gustavo Parra-Montesinos
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the behavior and design of reinforced concrete, fiber-reinforced concrete, and hybrid steel-concrete structures, with emphasis on earthquake engineering applications and structural connections.

faculty.name: Pavana Prabhakar
faculty.title: Charles G. Salmon Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Manufacturing and Mechanics Lab (MaMeL), focusing on damage-tolerant and resilient lightweight composite materials through mechanics-based material designs and advanced manufacturing techniques for architected polymer composites.

faculty.name: Mohan Qin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Environmental Engineering (Water Treatment and Resource Recovery)
faculty.summary: Develops novel approaches for resource recovery from waste streams and for the concentration and detection of microplastics in the Great Lakes. Her research spans bioelectrochemical systems, electrochemical water treatment, and membrane-based technologies for selective ion removal.

faculty.name: Christy Remucal
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Her group investigates contaminant transformation in aquatic systems, focusing on pesticides, pharmaceuticals, and PFAS, as well as the reactivity of dissolved organic matter to improve water quality.

faculty.name: Hiroki Sone
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in rock mechanics and geomechanics, conducting experimental studies of rock deformation using high-pressure apparatus and applying insights to numerical models for optimizing energy resource recovery and minimizing production hazards.

faculty.name: James Tinjum
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in energy geotechnics, environmental geotechnics, and sustainable engineering, including wind energy site civil, geotechnical, and structural design, campus- and district-scale geothermal heating and cooling systems, industrial byproduct reuse, and contaminated site remediation.

faculty.name: Bu Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Sustainable Materials Innovation Lab (SMIL-CEE), which develops technological solutions for sustainable civil engineering materials, with emphasis on carbon-negative concrete, CO2 utilization, green cement manufacturing, and fundamental behaviors of glassy materials.

faculty.name: Haoran Wei
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced optical analytical tools for detecting emerging contaminants in water supplies, with emphasis on vibrational spectroscopy techniques and point-of-use treatment technologies.

faculty.name: Daniel Wright
faculty.title: Arno Lenz Memorial Associate Professor of Water Resources Engineering; Graduate Program Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Investigates extreme rainfall, floods, and how both are influenced by meteorology, urbanization, and climate change, including satellite rainfall measurement, urban and regional flood modeling, and modernization of stormwater management practices.

faculty.name: Zhenhua Zhu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in construction technology, focusing on data sensing and analysis, visualization, information modeling, and simulation in construction and construction robotics, bridging digital technologies with automation in the built environment.

faculty.name: Dante Fratta
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Geological Engineering Program Director
faculty.summary: Applies geophysical methods including elastic and electromagnetic wave techniques and fiber optic sensing to evaluate geomaterials and characterize near-surface conditions both on land and offshore. Research spans soil and rock behavior, geothermal exchange field performance, and innovative sensing approaches for environmental and ground motion detection. Joint appointment in Civil & Environmental Engineering and Geoscience.

faculty.name: William Likos
faculty.title: Gary Wendt Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on unsaturated soil mechanics, expansive clay behavior, laboratory characterization of soil mechanical/hydraulic/thermal behavior, pore-scale imaging and analysis, field monitoring and instrumentation, and beneficial reuse of recycled materials and industrial by-products.

faculty.name: Hiroki Sone
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Study (Geological Engineering)
faculty.summary: Studies rock mechanics and geomechanics, combining experimental studies of rock properties using high-pressure deformation apparatuses with field data analysis of borehole and seismic information. Applies lab insights to numerical models for energy resource recovery, production hazard reduction, and tectonophysics. Recently focused on long-term ductile properties of clay-rich rocks for waste disposal and reservoir applications. Joint appointment in CEE and Geoscience.

faculty.name: James Tinjum
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Inter-disciplinary research covering geotechnical, geological, environmental, transportation, and sustainable energy engineering. Studies foundation response for wind turbines, fiber-optic monitoring for geothermal installations, landfill liner performance, unsaturated soil thermal conductivity, and contaminated site remediation. Brings 13 years of industry experience to academic research and engineering short course programs.

faculty.name: Jesse Hampton
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Research in experimental geomechanics, geophysics, and petrophysics with focus on induced seismicity, acoustic emission monitoring, fracture behavior, and geothermal systems. PhD from Colorado School of Mines. Teaches rock mechanics (GLE 474) and non-destructive evaluation. Joint appointment in CEE and Geoscience.

faculty.name: Michael Cardiff
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair (Geoscience); Joint appointment in Geological Engineering
faculty.summary: Hydrogeologist specializing in groundwater flow and contaminant transport, aquifer heterogeneity characterization, hydraulic tomography, oscillatory pumping tests, and geothermal energy systems. NSF CAREER Award recipient (2017). PhD from Stanford in Civil and Environmental Engineering. Cross-listed in Geoscience and Geological Engineering (CEE). Teaches GLE 627, 724, 793, and 875.

faculty.name: Lucas Zoet
faculty.title: Professor; Dean L. Morgridge Chair in Geoscience; Joint appointment in Geological Engineering
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of glaciology and glacial geology, focusing on physics of glacier motion through field observation, laboratory experiments, and numerical modeling. Also works on Great Lakes coastal erosion. Leads the Surface Processes Research Group. AGU Cryosphere Early Career Award (2022). Joint appointment in Geoscience and Geological Engineering, with affiliation in American Indian & Indigenous Studies.

faculty.name: Matt Ginder-Vogel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research in environmental geochemistry and biogeochemical processes involving contaminant transport in groundwater and sediments. Studies uranium, arsenic, manganese oxides, and pyrite oxidation. Teaches GLE-prefix graduate courses including GLE 790 Master's Research and GLE 699 Independent Study, indicating affiliation with the Geological Engineering program. Joint appointment in CEE and Geoscience.

faculty.name: Steven Loheide
faculty.title: Professor; Environmental Engineering & Science Associate Chair
faculty.summary: Research at the intersection of hydrology and ecology, focusing on groundwater-surface water interactions, managed aquifer recharge, urban greening ecohydrologic effects, and groundwater-influenced forest mapping. Teaches GLE-prefix graduate courses including GLE 790, 890, 990, and 999. Joint appointment in CEE and Geoscience.

faculty.name: Ken Ferrier
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates topographic and chemical evolution of Earth's surface through numerical modeling, lab measurements, field instrumentation, and analogue experiments. Research spans climate-tectonics influence on mountain evolution and sea-level change impacts on topography. Teaches GLE 350 (Intro Geophysics) cross-listed with Geoscience. PhD from UC Berkeley.

faculty.name: Annie Bauer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how Earth became habitable, focusing on tectonic processes that built the earliest continents and tracking the rise of atmospheric oxygen. Uses geochronologic dating and radiogenic isotope analysis to examine magmatic sources and weathering signals.

faculty.name: Chloë Bonamici
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Bridges geochemistry, petrology, and structural geology to examine deep-Earth fluid movement, thermal histories, and rock deformation. Combines field mapping, electron microprobe, electron-backscatter diffraction, and secondary ion mass spectrometry.

faculty.name: Kristina Butler
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies sedimentology and geochemistry with a focus on critical element cycling (lithium, boron, rare earth elements) and ore deposit formation. Examines how tectonics and hydroclimate control accumulation of critical minerals in sediments and brines.

faculty.name: Michael Cardiff
faculty.title: Professor, Department Chair
faculty.summary: Focuses on understanding aquifer heterogeneity and groundwater flow using multi-physics numerical modeling, stochastic and Bayesian inference methods, optimization, and state-of-the-art hydrologic sensors.

faculty.name: Andrea Dutton
faculty.title: Helen Jupnik Endowed Research Professor
faculty.summary: Geochemist studying paleoclimate and paleoceanography, focusing on sea-level reconstruction over glacial-interglacial timescales to inform understanding of future sea-level rise. Leads the SLEUTH (Sea Level + Environmental Reconstruction using Uranium-Thorium) lab.

faculty.name: Kurt L. Feigl
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on tectonic applications of geodesy, measuring and modeling crustal deformation from earthquake faults, volcanoes, glacier loads, and fluid extraction using GPS surveying, satellite radar interferometry (InSAR), and numerical modeling.

faculty.name: Ken Ferrier
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: His group investigates the topographic and chemical evolution of Earth's surface using laboratory measurements, field instrumentation, and numerical modeling, including how climate and tectonics shape mountains and how sea-level changes affect topography.

faculty.name: Eva Golos
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses seismic data to study Earth's structure and internal dynamics, developing innovative seismic imaging methods to model the crust and upper mantle and investigating melting processes, subduction zone earthquakes, and volcanic activity.

faculty.name: Laurel Goodwin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how brittle-to-ductile fault systems evolve in space and time, including fault mechanics, earthquake recurrence, strain localization, and fluid-rock interactions, using structural analysis, microscopy, and isotopic dating across crustal scales.

faculty.name: Marianne Haseloff
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Examines the physical processes that govern the dynamics of ice sheets and glaciers, with focus on ice stream dynamics, marine ice sheets, ice/ocean interactions, icy moons, and multiphase fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Clay Kelly
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Applies marine microfossils (planktonic foraminifera) to biostratigraphy, paleoceanography, and biotic evolution, studying how oceanic and climatic changes such as past warming episodes and carbon cycle disruptions shaped marine ecosystems.

faculty.name: Shaun Marcott
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on late Quaternary climate change and dating glacial landscapes, using paleoclimate archives including glacial deposits, marine sediment cores, and ice cores to understand interactions between climate and glaciers.

faculty.name: Selva Marroquin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Sedimentary isotope biogeochemist using extreme global warming events and extinctions as proxy events to constrain Earth-system feedbacks, with projects on organic sulfur isotope records, carbonate mud mounds, and organic-rich shales.

faculty.name: Stephen R. Meyers
faculty.title: Vilas Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Works on paleoclimate and paleoceanography with emphasis on statistical analysis, developing quantitative methods for constructing astronomical time scales and analyzing Earth's response to astronomical-insolation changes; author of the Astrochron software.

faculty.name: Athena Nghiem
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies environmental variability in hydrology and redox processes affecting critical water resources, and the release, fate, and transport of trace elements and contaminants, combining lab and field work with data science, spectroscopy, and reactive transport modeling.

faculty.name: Shanan Peters
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantifies the rock record to understand Earth history and Earth-life co-evolution, using macrostratigraphy to study the evolution of marine and terrestrial life, carbon cycling, and climate; leads the Macrostrat lab and field projects on carbonates and stratigraphy.

faculty.name: Eric E. Roden
faculty.title: Albert and Alice Weeks Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the biogeochemistry of aquatic environments, emphasizing in situ rates and controls on microbial metabolism in anaerobic soils and sediments, the physiology of anaerobic respiratory bacteria, and contaminant mobility in groundwater systems.

faculty.name: Bradley S. Singer
faculty.title: Vilas Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Uses high-precision geochronology to resolve fundamental problems in volcano evolution, the history of Earth's magnetic field, and sedimentary basin chronostratigraphy; directs the WiscAr geochronology laboratory and leads work on ice sheet effects on Andean volcanoes.

faculty.name: Basil Tikoff
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Quantitative field geologist studying tectonic deformation at scales from micro to plate, with emphasis on shear zones, granite emplacement, strike-slip tectonism, lithospheric mantle deformation, and western US tectonics; also develops digital geological data systems.

faculty.name: Huifang Xu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in clay and mineral crystal chemistry, nanopore geochemistry, microbe-mineral interactions, carbonate mineralization for carbon sequestration, banded iron formations, dolomitization, and functional minerals for environmental cleanup and renewable energy.

faculty.name: Christopher Zahasky
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Research centers on fluid, gas, and solute transport in heterogeneous porous and fractured media, with applications to contaminant migration, colloid transport, and geologic carbon dioxide storage.

faculty.name: Lucas Zoet
faculty.title: Professor, Dean L. Morgridge Chair in Geoscience
faculty.summary: Focuses on surface processes related to glaciers and coastal environments, including glacial basal processes, glacial erosion mechanics, glacial seismicity, and coastal and ice-cliff wave erosion.

faculty.name: Ángel F. Adames Corraliza
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Ned P. Smith Distinguished Chair of Climatology
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical processes that drive large-scale circulations in the atmosphere, with a particular focus on the tropics, spanning phenomena from inertio-gravity waves to ENSO. His group studies how these circulations affect rainfall and how they respond to rising CO2.

faculty.name: Larissa Back
faculty.title: Professor, Associate Chair of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research focuses on climate and climate change and large-scale atmospheric dynamics, examining atmospheric processes at broad spatial scales and their relationship to the climate system.

faculty.name: Ankur Desai
faculty.title: Vilas Distinguished Achievement Professor, Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies how regional spatial patterning, scale, and human modification of land-atmosphere exchanges of heat, water, and carbon influence the lower atmosphere, and how meteorological and climatic responses modify terrestrial ecosystem functioning.

faculty.name: David Henderson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: remote sensing of clouds and precipitation
faculty.summary: Uses remote sensing of clouds and precipitation to explore how changes in these elements impact local and regional climates, integrating satellite and ground-based observations with numerical modeling to study cloud and precipitation regimes and their effects on atmospheric circulation.

faculty.name: Stephanie Henderson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on subseasonal timescales and atmospheric predictability, studying processes such as the Madden-Julian Oscillation and atmospheric blocking and how they interact with weather and climate patterns across timescales using observations and modeling.

faculty.name: Matt Hitchman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric and oceanic dynamics across scales, currently investigating transport and mixing in the upper troposphere and lower stratosphere, the influence of tropical convection on the extratropics, and dynamical coupling between the troposphere and stratosphere using global datasets, aircraft observations, and numerical models.

faculty.name: Tracey Holloway
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of air quality, energy, climate, and public health, with research emphasizing connections between atmospheric science and environmental health outcomes.

faculty.name: Fraser King
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (Starting Winter 2026)
faculty.summary: Research spans climate and climate change, radiation and remote sensing, synoptic meteorology, atmospheric and cloud physics, and large-scale dynamics. Listed on the AOS active faculty roster as an assistant professor starting Winter 2026.

faculty.name: Tristan L'Ecuyer
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Cooperative Institute for Meteorological Satellite Studies
faculty.summary: Works to improve climate predictions by better understanding the processes that govern atmospheric energy balance and the global water cycle through remote sensing, satellite data analysis, field projects, and modeling, including Arctic climate change, clouds and aerosols in Earth's energy budget, and global snowfall.

faculty.name: Gabrielle "Bee" Leung
faculty.title: Assistant Professor (starting Fall 2026), Anna Julia Cooper Postdoctoral Fellow
faculty.summary: Research interests listed by the department include climate and climate change, radiation and remote sensing, synoptic meteorology, atmospheric and cloud physics, and large-scale dynamics. Listed on the AOS active faculty roster as an incoming assistant professor starting Fall 2026.

faculty.name: Andrea Lopez Lang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Her group investigates scale interactions in the atmosphere from meso- to planetary scales and variability spanning days to weeks, focusing on the drivers and processes leading to extreme weather variability and predictability in the extratropical atmosphere.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Maroon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how the ocean influences climate variability and climate change, applying understanding of coupled ocean-atmosphere processes to the analysis and improvement of interannual to decadal climate predictions.

faculty.name: Jonathan Martin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mid-latitude cyclone structure, precipitation dynamics, and jet stream variability using observational analysis and high-resolution mesoscale numerical model simulations across multiple atmospheric scales.

faculty.name: Michael Morgan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Research focuses on the analysis, diagnosis, prediction, and predictability of mid-latitude and tropical weather systems, with recent emphasis on synoptic interpretations of adjoint-derived forecast sensitivity fields.

faculty.name: Mayra Oyola-Merced
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Ned P. Smith Distinguished Chair of Meteorology
faculty.sub_field: satellite remote sensing and aviation weather
faculty.summary: Emphasizes satellite remote sensing, radiative transfer, and modeling to improve understanding of severe weather and aviation hazards, including aviation turbulence forecasting and aerosol-cloud interactions affecting storm severity.

faculty.name: Brad Pierce
faculty.title: Professor, Director of Space Science and Engineering Center
faculty.sub_field: atmospheric chemistry and air quality modeling
faculty.summary: Works on global atmospheric models and chemical modeling and forecasting; leads the Real-time Air Quality Modeling System (RAQMS), which assimilates satellite observations for air quality prediction.

faculty.name: Angela Rowe
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Her group collects and analyzes data to better understand cloud and mesoscale processes leading to high-impact weather around the world, primarily using weather radar across multiple wavelengths and platforms to study precipitating systems from tropical oceanic convection to frontal systems.

faculty.name: Daniel J. Vimont
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies mechanisms of climate variability and climate change, interactions between weather and climate, and global and regional impacts of climate change, with current work on coupled ocean-atmosphere interactions in the tropics, subseasonal-to-seasonal predictability, and statistical modeling for climate analysis.

faculty.name: Till Wagner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: His group focuses on the physical processes governing Earth's high latitudes, with an emphasis on ice-ocean-atmosphere interactions, including sea-ice/climate feedbacks, glacier-ocean interactions, and iceberg dynamics studied via theory, numerical modeling, and observations.

faculty.name: Hannah Zanowski
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates ocean dynamics across multiple timescales and contexts, spanning polar oceanography, paleoceanography, and exoplanet oceanography, with current focus on Arctic Ocean variability, early Earth ocean circulation, and conditions for habitability on distant planetary oceans.

faculty.name: Jake Vander Zanden
faculty.title: Wayland Noland Distinguished Chair; Professor and Director, Center for Limnology (Department of Integrative Biology)
faculty.summary: Studies the ecology of lakes and rivers with a focus on aquatic food webs, fish ecology, and the impacts of aquatic invasive species. Directs UW-Madison's Center for Limnology.

faculty.name: Emily Stanley
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Integrative Biology; Center for Limnology
faculty.summary: Researches aquatic ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry, including nutrient and carbon dynamics in streams and rivers. Works on how flowing-water ecosystems respond to environmental change.

faculty.name: Hilary Dugan
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Integrative Biology; Center for Limnology
faculty.summary: Investigates the effects of terrestrial and atmospheric changes on biogeochemical fluxes in lakes, including topics such as lake salinization and ice dynamics.

faculty.name: Olaf Jensen
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Integrative Biology; Center for Limnology (FMS Program Chair)
faculty.summary: Studies fish ecology, fisheries science, and management in freshwater and marine systems. Serves as chair of the Freshwater and Marine Sciences program.

faculty.name: Grace Wilkinson
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Integrative Biology; Center for Limnology
faculty.summary: Researches aquatic-terrestrial linkages, lake biogeochemistry, and aquatic food webs, including the dynamics of harmful algal blooms and ecosystem resilience.

faculty.name: Paul Hanson
faculty.title: Distinguished Research Professor, Center for Limnology
faculty.summary: Studies carbon cycling, lake metabolism, and phytoplankton dynamics using modeling and cyberinfrastructure. Leads the Global Lake Ecological Observatory Network (GLEON).

faculty.name: Katherine (Trina) McMahon
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering and Department of Bacteriology
faculty.summary: Studies aquatic microbial ecology, including the microbial communities of freshwater lakes and their roles in nutrient cycling. Uses metagenomics to link microbial diversity to ecosystem function.

faculty.name: Christy Remucal
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering; Environmental Chemistry & Technology Program
faculty.summary: Leads an aquatic chemistry research group studying the fate and transformation of contaminants and dissolved organic matter in natural and engineered water systems. Directs the Aquatic Sciences Center.

faculty.name: Karthik Anantharaman
faculty.title: Associate Professor, Department of Bacteriology
faculty.summary: Studies microbiomes and microbial biogeochemistry, including sulfur metabolism in aquatic and marine environments. Uses genome-resolved metagenomics to characterize microbial functions.

faculty.name: Carol Lee
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Integrative Biology
faculty.sub_field: Evolutionary genomics and physiology of aquatic invaders
faculty.summary: Studies the evolutionary and physiological mechanisms that allow aquatic organisms to invade new environments, using the copepod Eurytemora affinis as a model system. Integrates genomics, physiology, and ecology.

faculty.name: Chin Wu
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering
faculty.sub_field: Physical limnology and environmental fluid mechanics
faculty.summary: Studies physical limnology, environmental fluid mechanics, coastal processes, and air-sea interactions. Works on hydraulics and lake, wetland, and river/stream restoration.

faculty.name: Jessica Hua
faculty.title: Professor, Department of Forest & Wildlife Ecology
faculty.summary: Studies aquatic ecotoxicology and disease ecology, examining how amphibians and other aquatic organisms respond to and evolve in human-modified environments. Also works on science communication and citizen science.

faculty.name: Jesse Weber
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Integrative Biology
faculty.summary: Uses genetic approaches to understand how aquatic vertebrates such as threespine stickleback coevolve with their parasites. Studies adaptation of complex phenotypes in wild fish populations.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Maroon
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences
faculty.summary: Studies climate variability and change, ocean circulation, and the representation of the ocean in global climate models.

faculty.name: Hannah Zanowski
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Department of Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences
faculty.summary: Uses climate models to study polar oceanography, paleoceanography, and exoplanet oceanography. Explores how polar oceans are changing and how ocean processes shape planetary climate.

faculty.name: Alfred Hartemink
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science; Vilas Distinguished Achievement Professor
faculty.summary: Department pedologist specializing in pedology, digital soil morphometrics, and soil mapping. Research on soil carbon dynamics, tropical soils, and GlobalSoilMap initiative. Holds Marv Beaty Professorship in Soil Science. SSSA Fellow and IUSS Honorary Member. One of the world's leading soil scientists with extensive publication record.

faculty.name: Phillip Barak
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in soil chemistry and mineralogy with focus on nutrient recovery from wastewater through struvite/brushite precipitation. Inventor of the commercially-available CalPrex system for phosphate removal. Curates the Virtual Museum of Minerals and Molecules. Educause Medal recipient (1999).

faculty.name: Douglas Soldat
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Department Chair. Research in soil fertility and nutrient management with applications in turfgrass science and urban soil management. Studies nutrient behavior in managed landscapes and develops soil testing recommendations for urban and recreational soil systems.

faculty.name: Margaret Zimmer
faculty.title: Associate Professor; Director of Graduate Studies (Soil Science)
faculty.summary: Research on watershed hydrology with focus on surface water-groundwater interactions, water quality and quantity, from mountain headwaters to coastal wetlands. Director of Graduate Studies for the Soil Science graduate program. Leads the UW Madison Watershed Hydrology Lab.

faculty.name: Thea Whitman
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research on terrestrial carbon biogeochemistry and soil microbial ecology.
