Studies how climate change impacts soil processes, particularly organic matter decomposition, fire effects on soil carbon, and microbial community dynamics. Leads the Whitman Lab focusing on soil microbial ecology and biogeochemistry.

faculty.name: Francisco Arriaga
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Soil Science
faculty.sub_field: Applied Soil Physics, Soil and Water Management
faculty.summary: Research in applied soil physics with focus on soil and water management in agricultural systems. Studies soil hydraulic properties, water infiltration, conservation agriculture practices, and soil compaction effects on crop productivity and environmental quality.

faculty.name: Nicholas Balster
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research at the interface of soil ecology and plant physiological ecology, using stable isotope techniques to understand plant-soil interactions in natural and urban ecosystems. Studies ecosystem restoration, nutrient cycling, and how urbanization affects soil-plant systems.

faculty.name: William Bleam
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.sub_field: Surface and Colloid Chemistry, Soil Colloids
faculty.summary: Research in surface and colloid chemistry applied to soil systems. Studies soil colloids, humic substances, and uses magnetic resonance techniques to understand molecular-scale processes at mineral-water interfaces in soils and sediments.

faculty.name: Zachary Freedman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research on soil microbiology and biogeochemical cycles with focus on forest soil ecology and sustainable agroecosystems. Studies how microbial communities mediate carbon and nutrient cycling in soils under changing environmental conditions and land management practices.

faculty.name: Jingyi Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.sub_field: Soil Physics, Proximal and Remote Sensing
faculty.summary: Research integrating soil physics with proximal and remote sensing technologies for soil monitoring and digital soil mapping. Develops sensor-based approaches for characterizing soil properties at multiple scales and applications in precision agriculture.

faculty.name: Carrie Laboski
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in soil fertility and nutrient management with focus on N/P/K biogeochemistry in agricultural systems. Develops soil testing methodologies and fertilizer recommendations to optimize crop production while minimizing environmental nutrient losses.

faculty.name: Matthew Ruark
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in soil fertility and nutrient management with emphasis on cover crops, dairy cropping systems, and water quality. Studies nitrogen cycling in agricultural systems and develops management practices that balance productivity with environmental protection.

faculty.name: Stephen Ventura
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science; Joint with Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies
faculty.sub_field: Land Use Planning, GIS, Soil Information Systems
faculty.summary: Research applying GIS and spatial analysis to land use planning, biofuels assessment, urban agriculture, and soil information systems. Joint appointment with the Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies. Bridges soil science with environmental policy and planning.

faculty.name: Joel Pedersen
faculty.title: Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in environmental chemistry and biochemistry with focus on fate and transport of organic contaminants, manufactured nanoparticles, and infectious prion proteins in soils and sediments. Studies environmental behavior of emerging contaminants at molecular to field scales.

faculty.name: Xia Zhu-Barker
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in soil biogeochemistry with emphasis on nitrogen and carbon cycling and greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural systems. Studies dairy sustainability issues including composting practices and strategies to reduce agricultural greenhouse gas emissions.

faculty.name: Gregg Sanford
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research on long-term agroecosystem management impacts on soil organic carbon, soil health, and greenhouse gas emissions. Studies ecological intensification in diverse Midwestern agroecosystems from annual to perennial cropping systems. Funded by Dairy Innovation Hub.

faculty.name: Yakun Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.sub_field: AI in Soil Science, Geospatial Analysis
faculty.summary: Integrates AI, data science, sensors, and geospatial analysis with soil and agricultural sciences. Develops tools for sustainable agriculture, water and nutrient management, and climate adaptation. Part of RISE-AI initiative. PhD in Soil Science from UW-Madison. Joined faculty in 2026.

faculty.name: Chaoqun (Crystal) Lu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Soil Science
faculty.summary: Research in global change ecology and biogeochemistry using ecosystem modeling to study agricultural sustainability and resilience, crop nutrient budgets, soil carbon sequestration, and greenhouse gas emissions. Develops nature-based climate solutions. Recently joined the department.

faculty.name: Christopher Kucharik
faculty.title: Professor of Agronomy and Environmental Studies; Chair, Environment and Resources
faculty.summary: Chair of the Environment and Resources PhD program. Research at the intersection of agronomy and environmental studies, focusing on ecosystem modeling, climate change impacts on agriculture, land use change, and sustainability of managed ecosystems.

faculty.name: Paul Robbins
faculty.title: Dean, Nelson Institute; Professor of Environmental Studies and Geography
faculty.summary: Dean of the Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies. Leading scholar in political ecology and environmental governance, examining human-environment interactions, conservation politics, and environmental decision-making across diverse geographical contexts.

faculty.name: Tracey Holloway
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies; Chair, Energy Analysis and Policy
faculty.summary: Chair of Energy Analysis and Policy. Research on air quality, atmospheric chemistry, and energy policy, linking energy systems to environmental and public health outcomes. Leading expert on satellite-derived air quality data for policy applications.

faculty.name: Holly Gibbs
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on global land use change, deforestation, and agricultural supply chain sustainability. Uses remote sensing and spatial analysis to study how commodity markets and policies drive tropical deforestation and land use transitions.

faculty.name: Jonathan Patz
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies and Medicine and Public Health
faculty.summary: Research on the health effects of climate change and environmental degradation, including infectious disease dynamics, health co-benefits of climate mitigation, and environmental health policy. Joint appointment in Medicine and Public Health.

faculty.name: Adrian Treves
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on conservation biology with focus on human-wildlife conflict, large carnivore conservation, and wildlife policy. Studies the ecology and management of predators and explores ethical dimensions of wildlife conservation in human-dominated landscapes.

faculty.name: Annemarie Schneider
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies; Chair, Environmental Observation and Informatics
faculty.summary: Chair of Environmental Observation and Informatics. Research using remote sensing to study urban environmental change, urbanization processes, and land use transitions. Develops earth observation methods for monitoring environmental change at regional to global scales.

faculty.name: Lisa Naughton
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies; Chair, Environmental Professional Programs
faculty.summary: Chair of Environmental Professional Programs. Research on conservation in tropical regions, focusing on the human dimensions of wildlife management, protected areas effectiveness, and community-based conservation approaches that integrate social and ecological perspectives.

faculty.name: Anna Gade
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies; Chair, Undergraduate Programs
faculty.summary: Chair of Undergraduate Programs. Research in environmental humanities examining the intersection of religion, ethics, and ecology. Studies Islamic environmental ethics, religious perspectives on nature, and environmental thought in Southeast Asia.

faculty.name: Chelsea Schelly
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research in environmental sociology focusing on energy transitions, renewable energy adoption, and socio-technical systems. Studies how communities and households adopt sustainable technologies and the social dimensions of energy system transformation.

faculty.name: Asli Gocmen
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies and Geography
faculty.summary: Research in environmental planning and GIS, studying land use planning, urban sustainability, and spatial analysis applications for environmental decision-making. Joint appointment with Geography.

faculty.name: Leah Horowitz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies and American Indian & Indigenous Studies
faculty.summary: Research in political ecology with focus on indigenous environmental governance, community-based natural resource management, and mining conflicts. Studies environmental justice and indigenous sovereignty in Oceania and beyond. Joint appointment with American Indian & Indigenous Studies.

faculty.name: Julie Klinger
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on critical resource geography and political ecology with focus on rare earth elements, mining, and global environmental politics. Studies the political economy of resource extraction and its social and environmental impacts across global supply chains.

faculty.name: Mutlu Ozdogan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Studies and Forest and Wildlife Ecology
faculty.summary: Research on remote sensing and environmental modeling to study land cover and land use change, agricultural systems, and water resources under climate change. Joint appointment with Forest and Wildlife Ecology.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Hennessy
faculty.title: Associate Professor of History and Environmental Studies
faculty.sub_field: Environmental History, Science and Technology Studies
faculty.summary: Research in environmental history and science and technology studies, examining the intersection of science, conservation, and empire with particular focus on the Galapagos Islands. Joint appointment with History.

faculty.name: Rebecca Larson
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research in environmental engineering with focus on sustainable waste management, biogas production, nutrient recovery from organic wastes, and life cycle assessment of waste treatment technologies.

faculty.name: Monica White
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Environmental Justice in Environmental Studies and Community and Environmental Sociology
faculty.summary: Research on environmental justice and food systems, studying community-based agriculture, food sovereignty movements, and the intersection of race, class, and environmental access. Focus on Black agrarianism and cooperative economics as strategies for community resilience.

faculty.name: Grace Bulltail
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on indigenous environmental policy and water resources, examining tribal governance of natural resources, indigenous water rights, and environmental justice in Native American communities. Background in engineering and environmental management.

faculty.name: Zuzana Burivalova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies and Forest and Wildlife Ecology
faculty.summary: Research in conservation biology using bioacoustics to monitor biodiversity in tropical forests. Develops soundscape-based methods for assessing ecosystem health and evaluating conservation intervention effectiveness. Joint appointment with Forest and Wildlife Ecology.

faculty.name: Beth Tellman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research using satellite remote sensing to map and understand flood risk, disaster impacts, and climate adaptation. Studies how environmental hazards disproportionately affect vulnerable populations and develops data-driven approaches for disaster management.

faculty.name: Joel Ferguson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research in environmental economics with focus on sustainability transitions, energy policy, and climate economics. Studies how economic incentives and behavioral factors influence adoption of sustainable technologies and environmental practices.

faculty.name: Jonathan Sullivan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research on environmental policy and climate adaptation, studying governance of natural resources under climate change, risk management strategies, and policy instruments for sustainability transitions.

faculty.name: Tim Portlock
faculty.title: Professor of Environmental Studies and Art
faculty.summary: Research integrating environmental studies with art practice, creating visual representations of urban environments and post-industrial landscapes. Uses digital media and installation art to explore environmental themes and place-making. Joint appointment with Art.

faculty.name: Caglar Oskay
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Cornelius Vanderbilt Professor of Engineering; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Chair of CEE. Directs the Multiscale Computational Mechanics Laboratory. Research on multiscale computational modeling of material and structural systems under extreme environments, failure response of heterogeneous materials, reduced order modeling, quantum computing algorithms for FEM, and process-microstructure-property relationships in additively manufactured materials. Fellow of USACM, ASME, and ASCE/EMI.

faculty.name: Sankaran Mahadevan
faculty.title: John R. Murray Sr. Professor of Engineering; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Co-directs the Laboratory for Systems Integrity and Reliability (LASIR). Research spans risk and reliability analysis, uncertainty quantification, structural health monitoring, digital twins, and optimization under uncertainty. Applications in civil infrastructure, aerospace, automotive, and energy systems. ASCE Distinguished Member, h-index 77 with 22,700+ citations. Author of definitive textbook on probabilistic methods in structural engineering.

faculty.name: Hussam Mahmoud
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Craig E. Philip Endowed Chair in Engineering; Director, Vanderbilt Center for Sustainability, Energy, and Clim
faculty.summary: Leads the SoPHIE group on sustainable and resilient infrastructure. Research on community-scale resilience quantification, multi-hazard structural engineering (earthquakes, fires, wind, blast, flooding), fatigue and fracture of deteriorated infrastructure, and wildfire vulnerability modeling. Fellow of ASCE and SEI. National Geographic Explorer. Previously George T. Abell Professor at Colorado State.

faculty.name: Florence Sanchez
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering; Associate Dean for Graduate Education
faculty.summary: Directs the Multiscale Materials Performance Laboratory. Research on sustainable construction materials with minimal environmental impact, integrating nanotechnology (carbon nanofibers, nano-silica) into concrete, molecular dynamics modeling of chemo-mechanical interfaces, and 3D-printed functionally graded concrete. NSF CAREER awardee. Co-leads $6.7M international project on seawater concrete.

faculty.name: Douglas Adams
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Daniel F. Flowers Professor; Professor of Mechanical Engineering; Executive Director, Vanderbilt
faculty.summary: Co-directs LASIR. Research on structural health monitoring, nonlinear structural dynamics, noise and vibration control. Applications in aerospace, automotive, energy (wind turbines, batteries), and defense. PECASE awardee. Fellow of ASME, SEM, and AAAS. Author of textbook on structural health monitoring and 270+ papers. 10+ patents.

faculty.name: Mark D. Abkowitz
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Director, Vanderbilt Center for Environmental Management Studies (VCEMS)
faculty.summary: Research on enterprise risk management, hazardous materials transportation safety, extreme weather impacts on transportation infrastructure, and pedestrian/bicycle safety using wearable technology. Presidential appointee to Nuclear Waste Technical Review Board (2002-2011). Charles H. Hochman Lifetime Achievement Award from TRB. Elected to European Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Yuri Bazilevs
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Mechanical Engineering; Orin Ingram Endowed Chair in Engineering
faculty.summary: Joined Vanderbilt June 1, 2026. Pioneer in computational mechanics and isogeometric analysis. Previously E. Paul Sorensen Chair at Brown University. Research on high-performance computational mechanics, fluid-structure interaction, aerospace composites, and undersea engineering. Highly Cited Researcher (Clarivate). Will lead the new Vanderbilt Center for Simulation, Computational-modeling, and AI-integrated Learning for Engineering Systems (SCALES). Fellow of USACM, EMI/ASCE, and AIMBE.

faculty.name: Hiba Baroud
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Chair, CEE; Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Research on risk analysis and resilience of critical infrastructure systems, using Bayesian statistical modeling, network science, and machine learning to assess and enhance infrastructure resilience to natural hazards and climate change. A. James and Alice B. Clark Foundation Faculty Fellow. Deputy Director of VSEC. Society for Risk Analysis Distinguished Young Scholar Award.

faculty.name: Ahmad F. Taha
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on control and optimization of cyber-physical urban systems, including smart power grids, drinking water networks, and transportation systems. Develops dynamic network theory for interconnected infrastructure. NSF-funded research on joint control of hydraulics and water quality in drinking water networks. Editor for IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid.

faculty.name: Ravindra Duddu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering; Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Scienc
faculty.summary: Research on computational solid mechanics with emphasis on phase-field fracture models for glacier/ice-shelf dynamics, corrosion/fracture of metal alloys, and delamination of composites. Develops machine learning surrogates (CNN-based) for efficient fracture simulation. NSF CAREER awardee. Fulbright-Kalam Climate Fellow. 2025 Chancellor Faculty Fellow.

faculty.name: Shihong Lin
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering; Chancellor Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Research on advancing water separation processes for sustainability, including desalination, water treatment, resource extraction and recovery (lithium, critical minerals), and brine management. Highly Cited Researcher (Clarivate 2022). Paul L. Busch Award (2020). Walter L. Huber Research Prize from ASCE (2023). Associate Editor of Environmental Science & Technology. Published in Nature Water on electrodialytic crystallization and nanofiltration-based lithium separation.

faculty.name: Eugene J. LeBoeuf
faculty.title: Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Chair, CEE; Director of Undergraduate Studies in Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on fate and transport of contaminants in groundwater, soil, and sediment systems; sustainable-energy water systems; optimization of multi-reservoir hydropower systems using machine learning; and spill management information systems. NSF CAREER awardee. Fellow of ASCE. Board Certified Environmental Engineer. Brigadier General (Ret.) in U.S. Army Reserve.

faculty.name: David S. Kosson
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Cornelius Vanderbilt Professor of Engineering; Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineerin
faculty.summary: Director of the Environmental Engineering Laboratory. PI of CRESP (Consortium for Risk Evaluation with Stakeholder Participation) since 2006, a DOE-funded multi-university consortium for nuclear waste cleanup. Research on nuclear waste management, contaminant mass transfer in groundwater/soil/sediment systems, and leaching assessment (developed the Leaching Environmental Assessment Framework used by US EPA). Served on multiple National Academy of Sciences committees.

faculty.name: Alan R. Bowers
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on adsorption behavior of heavy metals and contaminants at oxide-solution interfaces, treatment of toxic/refractory wastewaters using hydrogen peroxide, and groundwater transport of reactive solutes. Published foundational work on metal-EDTA complex adsorption onto hydrous oxides and activated carbon processes for chromium removal.

faculty.name: Jonathan M. Gilligan
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research at intersection of natural science, social science, and public policy. Studies coupled human-natural systems, climate policy and private governance, water security and environmental migration (Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Southeastern US). Author of Beyond Politics: The Private Governance Approach to Climate Change. Fulbright Scholar. Director of Vanderbilt's Climate and Society Grand Challenge Initiative.

faculty.name: Hiba Baroud
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Chair, CEE; Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Research on risk analysis and resilience of critical infrastructure systems under natural hazards and climate change, using Bayesian statistical modeling, network science, and machine learning. Deputy Director of VSEC. Applies methods to environmental risk assessment, community resilience, and disaster recovery. Society for Risk Analysis Distinguished Young Scholar Award.

faculty.name: Ahmad F. Taha
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on joint control of hydraulics and water quality dynamics in drinking water networks, including real-time water quality regulation, contamination detection and mitigation, optimal sensor placement, and multi-species water quality modeling using control-theoretic approaches. NSF-funded collaborative research on drinking water network control.

faculty.name: Ravindra Duddu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering; Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Scienc
faculty.summary: Research on glacier and ice-shelf fracture mechanics to improve sea-level rise projections. Develops phase-field fracture models for ice dynamics and multi-physics models of material damage. NSF CAREER awardee. Fulbright-Kalam Climate Fellow. Leads NeuCIM project using neural networks for ice-sheet modeling. 2025 Chancellor Faculty Fellow.

faculty.name: John C. Ayers
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Courtesy Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Research on trace element cycling in natural systems, including groundwater arsenic and salinity contamination in Bangladesh coastal zone. Studies heavy metal cycling, salinity, and water quality in coupled human-natural systems. NSF-funded research on multi-scale modeling of Ganges-Brahmaputra delta environmental quality. Fellow of Geological Society of America and Mineralogical Society of America. Author of Sustainability: An Environmental Science Perspective (CRC Press).

faculty.name: John C. Ayers
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on controls on trace element movement in natural chemical systems, spanning high-temperature experimental petrology (zircon, monazite solubility) and low-temperature environmental geochemistry (groundwater arsenic and salinity in Bangladesh coastal zone). NSF-funded work on zircon reactivity and Ganges-Brahmaputra delta environmental quality. Fellow of Geological Society of America and Mineralogical Society of America. Author of Sustainability: An Environmental Science Perspective.

faculty.name: Ralf Bennartz
faculty.title: Stevenson Chair Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on satellite remote sensing and atmospheric radiative transfer, with focus on water vapor, clouds, and precipitation. PI of NASA's PolSIR mission (~$37M), the first NASA satellite mission led by Vanderbilt, studying high-altitude ice clouds using two CubeSats launching in 2027. 158+ publications, 6,200+ citations, h-index 44. Previously at University of Wisconsin-Madison.

faculty.name: Larisa R.G. DeSantis
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Associate Professor of Biological Sciences
faculty.summary: Vertebrate paleontologist and conservation paleobiologist studying fossil mammals to understand responses to ancient climate change and extinction events. Directs the DREAM Lab using dental microwear texture analysis and stable isotope analysis. Extensive work on Rancho La Brea fossils (saber-toothed cats, dire wolves, mammoths). NSF CAREER awardee. 2025 Guggenheim Fellow. 50+ papers, 2,200+ citations.

faculty.name: Jonathan M. Gilligan
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering; Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies
faculty.summary: Research at intersection of natural science, social science, and public policy. Studies coupled human-natural systems using agent-based modeling, climate policy and private governance, water security, and environmental migration. Author of Beyond Politics: The Private Governance Approach to Climate Change. Director of Vanderbilt's Climate and Society Grand Challenge Initiative. Fulbright Scholar. Alexander Heard Distinguished Service Professor.

faculty.name: Steven L. Goodbred, Jr.
faculty.title: Stevenson Chair Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Research on sedimentary systems, Quaternary environments, and coastal/deltaic processes. Major focus on the Ganges-Brahmaputra-Meghna Delta in Bangladesh, examining how climate change, tectonics, and human activities influence delta evolution. Published in Nature Communications on sediment delivery and sea-level rise. Department Chair. Extensive interdisciplinary collaborations with sociologists, archaeologists, and engineers.

faculty.name: Guilherme A. R. Gualda
faculty.title: Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on igneous petrology and volcanology, focusing on super-eruptions and silicic magma systems. Co-developed Rhyolite-MELTS, a widely used thermodynamic model for silica-rich magmas (cited 1,300+ times). Uses X-ray microtomography for 3D imaging of igneous rocks. Discovered that decompression preceding super-eruptions can occur in less than one year. NSF-funded research with collaborations at Argonne National Laboratory.

faculty.name: Maria Luisa Jorge
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on the ecology of interactions and movement of medium and large mammals, working in both urban settings (Nashville, TN) and rural landscapes (Brazil). Studies jaguar conservation in the Brazilian Atlantic Forest, seed dispersal and scatter-hoarding by agoutis in Amazonian forest fragments, and satellite telemetry performance across terrestrial species. 23+ publications, PhD from University of Illinois at Chicago.

faculty.name: Neil Kelley
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Associate Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies; Director of Undergraduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on extinct marine reptiles (ichthyosaurs, plesiosaurs) from the Mesozoic Era, studying how land-dwelling vertebrates repeatedly re-adapted to marine life over 250 million years. Uses photogrammetry and microCT scanning. Led research published in Current Biology on ichthyosaur birthing grounds in Nevada, analogous to modern whale behavior. Teaches oceanography, geology, and field courses.

faculty.name: Lin Meng
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Assistant Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies; Steve and Bunny Fayne Dean's Faculty Fellow
faculty.summary: Research on how climate change and human activities affect terrestrial ecosystems. Studies urban heat island effects and artificial light pollution impacts on tree phenology, and vegetation dynamics using satellite remote sensing. Grand Prize winner of 2021 Science & SciLifeLab Prize for Young Scientists. Green Talents Award (Germany, 2022). PhD from Iowa State University, postdoc at Lawrence Berkeley National Lab.

faculty.name: Susannah Morey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Research on how extreme geomorphic events, particularly outburst floods and landslides, shape Earth's surface across multiple timescales. Combines numerical modeling, field observations, and geochronology. Studies Tibetan megafloods and decadal impacts of the 2013 Colorado Front Range storm that triggered 800+ landslides. PhD from University of Washington (2023), postdoc at CIRES/University of Colorado Boulder. Joined Vanderbilt Fall 2025.

faculty.name: Jessica Oster
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Earth and Environmental Sciences; Associate Professor of Climate and Environmental Studies; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Research on reconstructing past climate change using speleothem geochemistry. Studies western US drought and hydroclimate, Indian monsoon variability, and wildfire history reconstruction using novel techniques (e.g., levoglucosan in stalagmites). Directs the Oster Lab combining fieldwork, wet-lab geochemical analysis, and climate data analysis. NSF CAREER awardee. Chancellor Faculty Fellow. National Geographic Explorer.

programs.program_name: PhD in Energy, Environmental & Chemical Engineering

faculty.name: Joshua Yuan
faculty.title: Lucy & Stanley Lopata Professor; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Chair of EECE. Research focuses on design-based engineering for energy, environment, and health, including carbon capture and utilization, bioplastics (LEAFF), and synthetic/systems biology for biomanufacturing. Director of the NSF CURB Engineering Research Center. AAAS Fellow.

faculty.name: Richard L. Axelbaum
faculty.title: The Stifel & Quinette Jens Professor of Environmental Engineering Science
faculty.summary: Research in combustion science, advanced energy systems, and aerosol processes. Directs the Consortium for Clean Coal Utilization. Studies flame synthesis of materials for batteries and energy applications. Lab: WULACER.

faculty.name: Peng Bai
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Battery Analytical Investigation (BAI) Group. Develops next-generation batteries (lithium-ion, sodium-based, solid-state) using operando microscopy and physics-based mathematical modeling. NSF CAREER awardee (2021). Academy of Science St. Louis Innovation Award (2025).

faculty.name: Rajan Chakrabarty
faculty.title: Harold D. Jolley Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Addresses grand challenges in the aerosol-climate-health nexus using interdisciplinary science. Studies radiative properties of carbonaceous aerosols, non-equilibrium dynamics, and airborne disease transmission. Leads the Aerosol Interdisciplinary Research (AIR) group.

faculty.name: Christopher Cooper
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Jointly appointed in EECE and Mechanical Engineering & Materials Science. Develops responsive soft materials using dynamic polymers for energy storage, environmental sustainability, and human health applications including wearable electronics and recyclable adhesives. Joined Fall 2024.

faculty.name: Jenna Ditto
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Built Environment Air Quality Group. Investigates relationships between indoor and outdoor air quality, chemical transformations of pollutants indoors, and health impacts. Uses gas chromatography and proton transfer reaction mass spectrometry. NSF grantee (2024).

faculty.name: Marcus Foston
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on sustainable conversion of biomass into chemicals and materials. Studies lignocellulosic biomass utilization, lignin depolymerization for renewable chemicals, and cellulose nanocrystals for bioplastics. Co-director of SMARC center. Director of Diversity Initiatives.

faculty.name: Daniel E. Giammar
faculty.title: Walter E. Browne Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for the Environment. Research on water quality, fate and transport of heavy metals (lead, arsenic, chromium, uranium) and radionuclides, corrosion control for drinking water, and geologic carbon sequestration. AEESP Fellow (2024). NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Zhen (Jason) He
faculty.title: Laura and William Jens Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Water Innovation. Develops bioelectrochemical systems for resource recovery from wastewater including microbial fuel cells, microbial desalination cells, and forward osmosis. Editor-in-Chief of Journal of Hazardous Materials. IWA and WEF Fellow.

faculty.name: Feng Jiao
faculty.title: Lauren and Lee Fixel Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Director of the Center for Carbon Management. Advances electrochemical technologies for CO2 and CO electrolysis to produce chemicals and fuels. Develops electro-agriculture via CO2 electrolysis. ACS Fellow (2025). Highly Cited Researcher. Co-founder of Lectrolyst.

faculty.name: Young-Shin Jun
faculty.title: Anna McKelvey Professor
faculty.summary: Studies nanoscale interfacial chemistry and solid nucleation for energy and environmental challenges. Research spans CO2 capture and geologic storage, critical element recovery (rare earths, Li, Ni, Co), and nanomaterial-enabled water purification.

faculty.name: Xinhua Liang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Internationally recognized for gas-phase atomic/molecular layer deposition (ALD/MLD) in fluidized bed reactors. Synthesizes nanostructured catalysts and functionalizes nanomaterials for energy and environmental applications including CO2 conversion to carbon nanotubes.

faculty.name: Fangqiong Ling
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Environmental Microbiology and Genomics Lab. Uses genomics, machine learning, and theory to study microbial ecosystems at the built-natural environment boundary. Develops wastewater-based epidemiology and drinking water microbiome analysis. NSF CAREER awardee (2021). Brilliant 10 by Popular Science.

faculty.name: Randall Martin
faculty.title: Raymond R. Tucker Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Leading expert on atmospheric composition. Leads the open-source GEOS-Chem global atmospheric chemistry model and the SPARTAN global fine particulate matter network. His data is used by WHO, World Bank, and the Global Burden of Disease Project. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Kimberly M. Parker
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates environmental organic chemistry in natural and engineered systems. Studies herbicide volatilization (notably dicamba drift), dsRNA biopesticides, and emerging contaminants. Associate Director of the Center for Water Innovation. ACS Morgan Early Career Award (2024). NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Vijay Ramani
faculty.title: Roma B. and Raymond H. Wittcoff Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Senior Vice Provost for Graduate Education. Leads the Electrochemical Engineering Research Laboratory focusing on energy conversion and storage including low-temperature fuel cells, electrolyzers, and redox-flow batteries. ECS Fellow (2022). NAI Senior Member. 8+ US patents, $17M+ in funding.

faculty.name: Yinjie Tang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in 13C-metabolic flux analysis, fermentation engineering, and yeast/cyanobacterial engineering for bioproduct synthesis. Develops AI/ML approaches for strain design. DARPA Switch program co-investigator for continuous fermentation. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Elijah Thimsen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Expert in plasma science for materials synthesis and environmental applications. Develops gas discharge plasma processing for decarbonizing fuels/chemicals. Studies nonequilibrium plasma aerotaxy of nanocrystals and plasma-catalytic synthesis. NSF and DOE Early Career awardee.

faculty.name: Jay R. Turner
faculty.title: James McKelvey Professor of Engineering Education; Vice Dean for Education
faculty.summary: Research focuses on air quality characterization and management through field measurements, data analysis, and science-based policymaking. Past President of AAAR. Served on U.S. EPA Science Advisory Board (2015-2021). Leads environmental justice air quality monitoring projects.

faculty.name: Jian Wang
faculty.title: Professor; Director, Center for Aerosol Science and Engineering (CASE)
faculty.summary: Director of CASE. Studies aerosol impacts on radiation, clouds, climate, and air quality. Develops advanced instruments and deploys them in field campaigns in key regions (Amazon Basin, Eastern North Atlantic). Published landmark 2024 Science paper on stratospheric air intrusions and new particle formation.

faculty.name: Gang Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in electrochemical science and engineering. Pioneer in PGM-free catalysts for fuel cells (landmark 2011 Science paper). Develops atomically dispersed single-metal-site catalysts for sustainable electrocatalysis. Highly Cited Researcher since 2018. ECS Fellow (2026). H-index > 125.

faculty.name: Lu Xu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies atmospheric chemistry of organic compounds from wildfires, vegetation, and power plants. Investigates impacts on air quality and climate. Develops mass spectrometry instrumentation. Research on stratospheric aerosol injection geoengineering (Simons Foundation grant). AIChE Early Career Award (2025).

faculty.name: Fuzhong Zhang
faculty.title: Francis F. Ahmann Professor
faculty.summary: Uses synthetic biology to produce biofuels, chemicals, and high-performance materials from engineered microbes. Co-director of SMARC center. Develops synthetic biology tools for sustainable production of structurally-defined chemicals. Researches zero-waste circular economy.

funding.note: Fully funded 5+ yrs (~$52k stipend + full tuition)

faculty.name: Eric Altman
faculty.title: Roberto C. Goizueta Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies fundamental chemical processes at surfaces at the atomic scale using scanning tunneling microscopy and spectroscopy. Research spans heterogeneous catalysis, 2D silicate membranes for gas separations, ferroelectric surface chemistry, and molecular systems for CO2 conversion.

faculty.name: John Fortner
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Leads the Fortner Lab focused on engineering interfacial processes and materials for environmental health and energy challenges. Key projects include superparamagnetic nanoparticles for PFAS removal from water, graphene-based membranes, and CO2 sorption/transformation materials.

faculty.name: Drew Gentner
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies air pollutant emissions and atmospheric chemistry using advanced analytical techniques. Research has revealed major gaps in petrochemical emissions reporting (Science, 2024) and characterized wildfire smoke health risks across outdoor and indoor environments.

faculty.name: Peijun Guo
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads a group focused on synthesizing nanostructured materials for energy conversion and optoelectronics, using ultrafast optical spectroscopy to resolve energy flow in functional materials including 2D metal halide perovskites and transition-metal dichalcogenides.

faculty.name: Amir Haji-Akbari
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Uses advanced molecular simulation and path sampling techniques to study rare events in soft matter, including atmospheric ice nucleation, colloidal self-assembly, ion transport through nanoporous membranes, and protein folding.

faculty.name: Shu Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Leads the Lightcatalysis Lab developing solar-driven chemical conversion systems. Recently demonstrated a floating reactor that uses sunlight and seawater to produce syngas, achieving 0.71% solar-to-fuel efficiency and >1000 hours stability.

faculty.name: Jaehong Kim
faculty.title: Henry P. Becton Sr. Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Advances water quality engineering through nanotechnology and electrochemistry. Develops single-atom catalysts for advanced oxidation processes, solar water disinfection using edible photosensitizers, low-cost water quality sensors, and point-of-use treatment for developing regions.

faculty.name: David Kwabi
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of electrochemistry, photochemistry, and energy systems. Develops aqueous organic redox flow batteries for grid-scale storage and electrochemical methods for atmospheric and marine CO2 capture, including hydrogen-looping systems for seawater.

faculty.name: Michael Loewenberg
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies dynamics of complex fluids including emulsions, foams, colloidal suspensions, and polymer solutions. Research on microstructural distortions under flow and fields, with applications in microfluidics, drug delivery, and microcirculation.

faculty.name: Jordan Peccia
faculty.title: Thomas E. Golden, Jr. Professor of Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Integrates biotechnology, microbial genetics, and engineering to address environmental and public health challenges. Pioneered wastewater-based epidemiology for SARS-CoV-2 surveillance and studies indoor air microbiology, tree microbiomes, and airborne pathogen inactivation.

faculty.name: Lisa Pfefferle
faculty.title: C. Baldwin Sawyer Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Expert in combustion kinetics and chemical reaction engineering. Research spans catalytically stabilized hydrocarbon fuels, soot formation mechanisms, nanomaterial synthesis and functionalization, and aligned carbon nanotube applications in biomedicine.

faculty.name: Paul Van Tassel
faculty.title: Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Studies biomolecules at interfaces and nanofilm biomaterials for biomedical applications. Research combines theory, simulation, and experiment to develop layer-by-layer assembled films for tissue engineering, antimicrobial coatings, and controlled drug delivery.

faculty.name: T. Kyle Vanderlick
faculty.title: Thomas E. Golden Jr. Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering; Dean Emeritus of SEAS
faculty.summary: Research focuses on interfacial forces, membrane biophysics, antimicrobial peptides, and engineering synthetic membrane structures (vesicles, polymersomes, lipid discs) using DNA-based assembly. Former Dean of SEAS (2008-2017).

faculty.name: Lea Winter
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering
faculty.summary: Develops electrified processes at the food-energy-water-climate nexus, combining electrocatalysis and nonthermal plasma to convert CO2 into chemicals/fuels, fix nitrogen to fertilizers, and transform wastewater contaminants into useful products.

faculty.name: Mingjiang Zhong
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering; Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Specializes in precision polymer synthesis and microstructure engineering. Develops controlled radical polymerization methods using rare earth-cobalt bimetallic catalysts and designs hierarchically structured nanocomposites from bottlebrush block copolymers for energy and environmental applications.

faculty.name: Julie Zimmerman
faculty.title: Liliane and Christian Haub Professor of Chemical & Environmental Engineering and the Environment; Vice Provost for Planetary Solutions
faculty.summary: Joint appointment between SEAS and YSE. Pioneers green chemistry and engineering approaches for sustainable design. Research integrates life cycle assessment, material flow analysis, and systems thinking to evaluate environmental impacts of products and processes across water, energy, and materials.

faculty.name: Paul Anastas
faculty.title: Teresa and H. John Heinz III Professor in the Practice of Chemistry for the Environment
faculty.summary: Widely recognized as the father of green chemistry, with secondary appointment in CEE. Founded the Center for Green Chemistry and Green Engineering at Yale. Develops molecular design principles that reduce or eliminate hazardous substances in chemical products and processes.

faculty.name: Michelle Bell
faculty.title: Mary E. Pinchot Professor of Environmental Health
faculty.summary: Secondary appointment in CEE. Studies the health impacts of air pollution, extreme weather, and climate change. Research combines epidemiology, exposure assessment, and biostatistics to inform environmental health policy, with focus on vulnerable populations.

funding.note: Fully funded 5+ yrs (~$52k stipend + full tuition)

faculty.name: Mark S. Ashton
faculty.title: Morris K. Jesup Professor of Silviculture and Forest Ecology; Senior Associate Dean of The Forest School; Director of Yale Forests
faculty.summary: Studies forest dynamics, silviculture, and ecosystem restoration across temperate and tropical systems. Directs Yale Forests and leads research on carbon sequestration, deforestation, forest resource management, and native uses of forests and grasslands.

faculty.name: Karl M. Aspelund
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental and Natural Resource Economics
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of environmental economics and policy, studying environmental markets, valuation of ecosystem services, and the design of market-based instruments for sustainable resource management and climate mitigation.

faculty.name: Michelle L. Bell
faculty.title: Mary E. Pinchot Professor of Environmental Health; Senior Associate Dean of Research and Director of Doctoral Studies
faculty.summary: Investigates the health impacts of air pollution, extreme weather events, and climate change using epidemiology, exposure assessment, and biostatistics. Research informs environmental health policy with emphasis on vulnerable populations and environmental justice.

faculty.name: Mark Bradford
faculty.title: E.H. Harriman Professor of Soils and Ecosystem Ecology
faculty.summary: Studies soil carbon cycling, biogeochemistry, and ecosystem ecology. Research focuses on how land management and climate change affect soil organic matter dynamics, carbon sequestration potential, and ecosystem services across forests and grasslands.

faculty.name: Paulo Brando
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Ecosystem Carbon Capture
faculty.summary: Studies tropical forest ecosystems, focusing on the impacts of drought, fire, and land-use change on carbon cycling and biodiversity. Research spans the Amazon and other tropical regions, examining forest dynamics, degradation, and resilience to climate change.

faculty.name: Craig Brodersen
faculty.title: Howard and Maryam Newman Professor of Plant Physiological Ecology
faculty.summary: Investigates plant hydraulic function, physiological responses to environmental stress, and anatomical adaptations of plants to drought and other climate extremes. Uses advanced imaging techniques to visualize water transport and carbon dynamics within plants.

faculty.name: Indy Burke
faculty.title: Carl W. Knobloch, Jr. Dean; Professor of Ecosystem Ecology
faculty.summary: Studies ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry, with emphasis on carbon and nitrogen cycling in grasslands and managed ecosystems. Research addresses ecosystem restoration, land-use change impacts, and conservation strategies in terrestrial systems.

faculty.name: Brianna Castro
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Urban Sustainability
faculty.summary: Examines urban sustainability and climate adaptation through an environmental social science lens. Research investigates how communities and governance systems respond to climate risks including extreme heat, flooding, and migration in urban contexts.

faculty.name: Liza Comita
faculty.title: Davis-Denkmann Professor of Tropical Forest Ecology
faculty.summary: Researches tropical forest ecology, focusing on the mechanisms that maintain species diversity, the drivers of plant species distributions, and the impacts of global change on tropical forest ecosystems and their carbon dynamics.

faculty.name: Michael R. Dove
faculty.title: Margaret K. Musser Professor of Social Ecology; Professor of Anthropology
faculty.summary: Integrates anthropology and ecology to study human-environment relationships. Research examines indigenous environmental knowledge, deforestation, climate adaptation, environmental governance, and the social and cultural dimensions of resource management in tropical regions.

faculty.name: Daniel C. Esty
faculty.title: Hillhouse Professor of Environmental Law and Policy, jointly with Yale Law School; Director of the Center for Environmental Law and Policy
faculty.summary: Focuses on environmental law, policy, and governance at the intersection of trade, corporate strategy, and sustainability. Research addresses climate change policy design, ESG, environmental performance measurement, and international environmental negotiations.

faculty.name: Justin Farrell
faculty.title: Professor of Sociology
faculty.summary: Studies the cultural, moral, and social roots of environmental conflict using a blend of ethnographic fieldwork and computational methods. Research on climate change denial networks, wealth and the American West, and corporate influence on environmental policy has been cited in U.S. Senate proceedings.

faculty.name: Eli Fenichel
faculty.title: Knobloch Family Professor of Natural Resource Economics
faculty.summary: Develops frameworks for natural capital valuation and accounting, treating natural resources as economic assets. Former White House OSTP Assistant Director who led the national strategy for environmental-economic accounts and updated federal benefit-cost analysis guidelines.

faculty.name: Kenneth Gillingham
faculty.title: Grinstein Class of 1954 Professor of Environmental and Energy Economics; Senior Associate Dean of Academic Affairs
faculty.summary: Studies energy and environmental economics, focusing on clean energy technology adoption, carbon pricing, energy efficiency, and climate policy design. Research combines economic theory with empirical analysis to inform energy and environmental policy.

faculty.name: Nyeema Harris
faculty.title: Knobloch Family Associate Professor of Wildlife and Land Conservation
faculty.summary: Investigates wildlife ecology and conservation across natural and human-modified landscapes. Research spans carnivore ecology, human-wildlife conflict, urban wildlife dynamics, and conservation planning, with field sites in Africa and North America.

faculty.name: David Keiser
faculty.title: Professor of Water Policy and Management
faculty.summary: Economist focused on quantifying the benefits of clean water and the social costs of water pollution. Research evaluates water quality policies and informs water resource management using economic frameworks. Joins YSE faculty July 2026.

faculty.name: Matthew Kotchen
faculty.title: Langdon K. Storm Professor of Economics
faculty.summary: Researches environmental and public economics, analyzing energy systems, climate mitigation costs, and environmental policy instruments. Work bridges economic theory and empirical policy evaluation on issues including renewable energy, electricity markets, and conservation.

faculty.name: Xuhui Lee
faculty.title: Sara Shallenberger Brown Professor of Climate Science
faculty.summary: Studies climate science and biosphere-atmosphere interactions, focusing on land surface processes, carbon and water cycles, urban climatology, and greenhouse gas exchange. Pioneers micrometeorological methods for measuring ecosystem-atmosphere fluxes.

faculty.name: Anthony Leiserowitz
faculty.title: JoshAni-TomKat Professor of Climate Communication; Director, Yale Program on Climate Change Communication
faculty.summary: Founding director of the Yale Program on Climate Change Communication. Researches public opinion, risk perception, and behavior related to climate change. His work on Global Warming's Six Americas has been influential in climate communication strategy.

faculty.name: Sparkle L. Malone
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Ecosystem Carbon Capture
faculty.summary: Studies ecosystem carbon dynamics, focusing on how disturbance events (fire, drought, hurricanes) affect carbon cycling and ecosystem resilience across wetlands, freshwater systems, and coastal ecosystems in a changing climate.

faculty.name: Narasimha Rao
faculty.title: Professor of Energy Systems
faculty.summary: Examines the intersection of energy systems, human well-being, and climate change. Lead author on IPCC AR7 and the UN Emissions Gap Report. Develops the Decent Living Energy framework quantifying energy needs for poverty eradication within climate constraints.

faculty.name: Luke Sanford
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Environmental Policy and Governance
faculty.summary: Studies environmental policy and governance, focusing on the political economy of land use decisions, climate policy design, and international environmental agreements. Research examines how political institutions shape environmental outcomes.

faculty.name: Oswald J. Schmitz
faculty.title: Oastler Professor of Population and Community Ecology
faculty.summary: Studies population and community ecology with emphasis on how species interactions (particularly predator-prey dynamics) influence ecosystem functioning, carbon cycling, and biodiversity. Research links animal ecology to ecosystem processes and nature-based climate solutions.

faculty.name: Karen C. Seto
faculty.title: Frederick C. Hixon Professor of Geography and Urbanization Science; Director of the Hixon Center for Urban Sustainability
faculty.summary: Studies the global impacts of urbanization on land use, ecosystems, and climate. Uses satellite remote sensing and geospatial analysis to quantify urban expansion and its environmental consequences, contributing to IPCC assessments on urban mitigation pathways.

faculty.name: Dorceta Taylor
faculty.title: Wangari Maathai Professor of Environmental Justice
faculty.summary: Leading environmental justice scholar studying environmental racism, institutional diversity in environmental organizations, and food insecurity. Her landmark research on diversity in environmental institutions and environmental grantmaking disparities has shaped national conversations on environmental equity.
